philosophy.en: Its intellectual outlook was deeply shaped by Indian cosmology, sacral kingship, and concepts of merit, but in practice these were blended with Southeast Asian ancestor cults, ideas of sacred power, and pragmatic politics.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用梵文书写于铭文和王室宗教场合，日常行政可能依赖口头传达及尚未充分保存的地方书写实践。
writingSystem.en: Sanskrit was used chiefly in inscriptions and royal-religious contexts, while everyday administration likely relied heavily on oral transmission and local writing practices that are poorly preserved.

culturalAchievements.zh: 扶南在东南亚早期国家形成、海上贸易组织、运河与水利建设、印度宗教与礼仪传播方面成就突出；其港口文化促进了南亚、中国与群岛世界的交流。
culturalAchievements.en: Funan was notable for early state formation in Southeast Asia, organization of maritime trade, canal and hydraulic works, and the transmission of Indian religion and ritual; its port culture fostered exchange between South Asia, China, and the island world.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言可能属南亚语系高棉支，精英宗教与外交场合广泛使用梵语
languageFamily.en: The main spoken language was likely Austroasiatic, probably early Khmer-related, while Sanskrit was widely used in elite religious and diplomatic contexts

gdpDescription.zh: 扶南经济以稻作农业、渔业、手工业和海上转口贸易为核心。其财富并非完全来自高人均生产，而是来自对湄公河三角洲交通节点、港口税收和国际商路的控制。
gdpDescription.en: Funan’s economy was centered on rice agriculture, fishing, craft production, and maritime entrepôt trade. Its wealth derived less from exceptionally high per capita output than from control of Mekong delta transport nodes, port revenues, and international trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、盐业、木材与林产采集、造船、珠宝加工、金属手工业、陶器生产、港口贸易与转运
mainIndustries.en: Rice farming, fishing, salt making, timber and forest extraction, boatbuilding, gem working, metal crafts, pottery production, port trade, and transshipment

tradeGoods.zh: 输出稻米、鱼干、盐、象牙、犀角、沉香、香料、木材、龟甲、珠宝与奴婢；输入印度布匹、珠饰、金属器、宗教用品，以及来自中国的丝织品与奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, dried fish, salt, ivory, rhinoceros horn, agarwood, spices, timber, tortoiseshell, gems, and slaves; imports included Indian textiles, beads, metal goods, religious items, and silks and luxuries from China.

name.zh: 金银称量与实物并用体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为稻田使用权、舟船、渔具、牲畜、粮食储备与亲族网络；港口商人和地方贵族则拥有更多金银、珠宝、奴仆和仓储资产。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly in rice-field use rights, boats, fishing gear, livestock, grain stores, and kinship ties; port merchants and regional nobles held greater amounts of bullion, gems, servants, and warehoused goods.

economicSystem.zh: 王权主导下的港口贸易经济与农业贡赋经济并存，市场交换、实物税和地方再分配共同构成整体体系。
economicSystem.en: A royal port-trade economy coexisted with an agrarian tribute economy, with market exchange, in-kind taxation, and local redistribution forming an integrated system.

source.zh: 农业实物税与仓储折值
source.en: Agrarian in-kind taxes and granary valuation
Market dues, extraction income, and miscellaneous taxes

category.en: Hydraulic works, canals, and public construction
Provincial gifts and maintenance of dependencies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、珠宝、进口奢侈品、稻谷储备和可随时征发的贡品构成。由于财政很大程度依赖港口收入和实物储备，其流动性强于纯农业王国，但对航运波动较敏感。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of bullion, gems, imported luxuries, grain reserves, and tribute goods that could be mobilized when needed. Because finances relied heavily on port income and stored goods, liquidity was greater than in purely agrarian kingdoms, but also more sensitive to fluctuations in shipping.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏稳健，重视维持港口繁荣、保障水利与交通，并通过礼物政治和宗教资助来巩固王权；在稳定时期倾向保留适度结余以应对贸易中断和地方叛离。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively prudent, emphasizing prosperous ports, maintenance of hydraulic and transport infrastructure, and the reinforcement of kingship through gift politics and religious patronage; in stable times the court aimed to retain a modest surplus against trade disruption and provincial defection.

description.zh: 由王廷直属卫队、长矛兵、弓手和持盾步兵组成，是防守都城、镇压叛乱和参与地方征讨的核心力量。
description.en: Composed of the royal guard, spearmen, archers, and shield-bearing infantry, this was the core force for defending the capital, suppressing unrest, and conducting regional campaigns.

description.zh: 以轻便舟船为主，负责河道巡逻、护航、运输和对三角洲水网的机动作战，是扶南军事特色所在。
description.en: Centered on light craft, this branch handled river patrols, convoy protection, transport, and mobile warfare across the delta’s waterways, forming a distinctive element of Funan’s military.

description.zh: 由地方首领在战时提供，装备参差不齐，但熟悉地方地形，适合守备、护运和短期作战。
description.en: Raised by local chiefs in wartime, these troops were unevenly equipped but familiar with local terrain and useful for garrison duty, escort work, and short campaigns.

description.zh: 数量不多但具有威慑作用，多用于仪式展示、冲击和指挥中枢护卫，体现王权与贵族地位。
description.en: Limited in number but highly prestigious, they were used for display, shock action, and protection of command centers, symbolizing royal and noble status.

commanderInChief.zh: 阇耶跋摩王兼最高统帅

technology.zh: 军备以铁制长矛、短剑、弓箭、藤盾、皮甲和木制舟船为主，善用河网机动、季风航行经验及象兵威慑，但缺乏重装甲与攻城技术。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, short swords, bows, rattan shields, leather armor, and wooden craft, with effective use of river mobility, monsoon navigation, and war elephants, but limited heavy armor and siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自对三角洲港口控制的内部竞争、周边沿海政体尤其占婆势力的增长、附属地区离心，以及海贸线路受季风、海盗或区域战争影响。
threats.en: The main threats came from internal competition over control of delta ports, the rise of neighboring coastal polities especially Champa, centrifugal tendencies among dependencies, and disruption of maritime routes by monsoons, piracy, or regional conflict.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于湄公河三角洲稻作区、河道聚落和港口节点，族群与职业构成复杂，包含本地南亚语族居民、印度商人与宗教人士，以及往来于海路的多族群社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Mekong delta rice zone, river settlements, and port nodes, with a complex ethnic and occupational composition including local Austroasiatic-speaking inhabitants, Indian merchants and religious specialists, and multiethnic maritime communities.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族居上层，婆罗门和僧侣享有宗教威望，商人与港口中介因贸易而富裕；其下为工匠、船民、农民、渔民与采集者，另有依附劳工、奴仆和战俘群体。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and nobility formed the upper stratum, Brahmins and monks enjoyed religious prestige, and merchants and port intermediaries gained wealth from trade; below them were artisans, boat people, farmers, fishers, and gatherers, along with dependent laborers, servants, and captives.

allies.zh: 扶南没有近代意义上的固定盟国，但与南印度商人网络、部分马来群岛海商集团及若干属地首领保持互利关系，并通过向中国南朝遣使获取声望与贸易便利。
allies.en: Funan did not maintain fixed allies in the modern sense, but it had mutually beneficial ties with South Indian merchant networks, some maritime groups of the Malay archipelago, and several subordinate chiefs, while embassies to the Southern Dynasties of China brought prestige and commercial advantages.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺沿海与转口贸易的占婆诸政体，以及可能挑战王廷控制的地方首领和外围附庸。
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were the Cham polities competing for coastal and entrepôt trade, as well as local chiefs and outer dependencies that might challenge royal control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制商路、维护港口安全、吸纳外商、通过朝贡外交提升合法性，并在必要时以武力或婚盟维持对周边节点的影响力为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling trade routes, protecting ports, attracting foreign merchants, enhancing legitimacy through tributary diplomacy, and maintaining influence over surrounding nodes by force or marriage alliances when necessary.

era.zh: 古典晚期至东南亚早期国家形成时代
era.en: Late Antiquity and the era of early Southeast Asian state formation

keyInnovations.zh: 运河开凿与水利整治、河海结合航运、季风海路知识、港口仓储组织、珠饰与金属工艺、印度化宗教礼仪与铭文传统的在地化应用。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included canal excavation and hydraulic management, integrated river-sea navigation, monsoon-route knowledge, organized port warehousing, bead and metal craftsmanship, and the localized adoption of Indianized ritual and inscriptional traditions.

strengths.zh: 扶南最大的优势在于其地理位置与海陆结合的交通体系，既控制三角洲农业基础，又掌握国际贸易中转；王权能够借助宗教仪式、港口财富和属地网络维系统治。
strengths.en: Funan’s greatest strengths lay in its geography and combined land-water transport system, controlling both the delta’s agrarian base and international commercial transit; kingship could be sustained through religious ritual, port wealth, and a network of dependencies.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于中央对外围控制较间接，财政对港口贸易依赖较高，政治整合程度不如后世成熟帝国；一旦航路转移、地方离叛或邻近海上竞争者崛起，国力容易受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the relatively indirect control of the periphery, heavy fiscal dependence on port trade, and a lower degree of political integration than later mature empires; if trade routes shifted, provinces defected, or nearby maritime rivals rose, its power could quickly be weakened.

outlook.zh: 476年时扶南总体稳定，仍是东南亚最重要的海贸王国之一；但从长期看，其未来将取决于是否能维持对湄公河三角洲和海上网络的双重控制，并应对占婆及内陆新兴高棉势力的挑战。
outlook.en: In 476 CE Funan was broadly stable and remained one of Southeast Asia’s most important maritime kingdoms; over the longer term, however, its future would depend on preserving dual control over the Mekong delta and maritime networks while meeting challenges from Champa and rising inland Khmer powers.

description.zh: 扶南掌控湄公河三角洲与海上商路，是东南亚最重要早期国家之一。
description.en: Funan controlled the Mekong delta and maritime trade routes, one of the most important early states of Southeast Asia.

name.zh: 斯里兰卡阿努拉德普勒王国

ruler.zh: 国王姓名不确定（传统上常归于拘陀迦那·提沙或近似时期君主）
ruler.en: Uncertain king (traditionally associated by some reconstructions with Kutakanna Tissa or a near-period ruler, though uncertain)

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为中心、依托宫廷与佛教寺院网络的古典南亚王国
governmentForm.en: A centralized classical South Asian monarchy supported by court administration and Buddhist monastic networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王、宫廷贵族、地方首领、僧团、灌溉农业村社、工匠与商人为核心，兼有劳役人口与边缘森林部族；佛教僧团在社会组织、教育与土地控制中具有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on the king, court nobles, local chiefs, the Buddhist sangha, irrigated farming villages, artisans, and merchants, alongside laboring groups and peripheral forest peoples; the Buddhist monastic order held major influence in social organization, education, and landholding.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、宫廷官僚、地方贵族与大型佛寺住持集团
rulingClass.en: The royal family, court officials, provincial aristocracy, and the leadership of major Buddhist monasteries

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，原则上偏向父子或近亲承袭，但实际常受宫廷派系、贵族支持与武力控制影响。
succession.en: The throne generally passed within the royal house, ideally from father to son or among close kinsmen, but in practice succession was often shaped by court factions, aristocratic backing, and military strength.

structure.zh: 政体以阿努拉德普勒宫廷为中心，国王通过王室内廷、财政与仓储官员、灌溉管理者、地方长官以及与僧团合作的土地管理体系统治全国。行政制度尚未形成后世高度文书化的官僚国家，但已具备稳定的王室指挥与地方统治层级。
structure.en: The polity was centered on the court at Anuradhapura, where the king ruled through palace officials, revenue and granary administrators, irrigation supervisors, provincial authorities, and land-management arrangements closely linked with the sangha. It was not yet a fully documentary bureaucracy in the later imperial sense, but it possessed a stable hierarchy of royal command and local administration.

function.zh: 负责宫廷、仪礼、王室护卫、诏令传达与王室财产管理。
function.en: Managed the palace, ceremony, royal guard, transmission of orders, and the king's personal estates.

function.zh: 征收谷物、劳役与实物税，管理王室仓库、贡赋和再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, labor, and goods in kind, and managed royal granaries, tribute, and redistribution.

function.zh: 维护水库、堤坝、渠道、道路和其他公共工程，是农业稳定的核心机构。
function.en: Maintained tanks, bunds, canals, roads, and other public works, forming the core institution for agricultural stability.

function.zh: 监督地方长官、村社、边地据点与治安事务，协调地租征收和劳役。
function.en: Supervised local governors, village communities, frontier posts, and policing, while coordinating rents and corvée obligations.

function.zh: 处理王室裁判、土地争议、赋役纠纷与寺院特许文书。
function.en: Handled royal judgments, land disputes, tax and labor conflicts, and monastic grants.

function.zh: 监管港口、市舶、商税、外来商人及与南印度和印度洋的海贸联系。
function.en: Oversaw ports, maritime customs, merchant taxes, foreign traders, and seaborne exchange with South India and the Indian Ocean.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠地区贵族、村长、税吏与水利管理者共同运作。王权在核心灌溉区最强，在边缘森林地带与鲁胡纳等地区更多依赖附属首领和地方合作。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on regional nobles, village headmen, tax collectors, and irrigation supervisors. Royal authority was strongest in the core irrigation zone, while in frontier forest regions and areas such as Ruhuna it depended more on subordinate chiefs and negotiated local cooperation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、土地权属传统及佛教道德规范为基础。重大案件由国王或其代表裁决，村社与寺院也在日常争端中发挥仲裁作用。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on royal order, customary law, traditions of landholding, and Buddhist moral norms. Major cases were judged by the king or his delegates, while villages and monasteries also played arbitral roles in routine disputes.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地出产、稻谷实物征收、劳役、灌溉区附加义务、手工业供纳与港口关税为主。寺院常享有免税或特许地，减少了王室直接税基但强化了宗教与政治联盟。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested chiefly on land produce, grain collected in kind, labor service, irrigation-related obligations, artisanal dues, and port customs. Monasteries often enjoyed tax-exempt or privileged lands, narrowing the direct royal tax base but reinforcing the alliance between kingship and religion.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教居主导地位，同时并存本地神灵崇拜、护国仪式与部分婆罗门祭祀传统。阿努拉德普勒是南亚佛教学习、舍利崇拜与寺院建筑的重要中心。
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism was dominant, alongside local deity cults, protective rites, and some Brahmanical ritual traditions. Anuradhapura was a major center of Buddhist learning, relic devotion, and monastic architecture in South Asia.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以佛教戒律、布施、王权护法观念和僧团经学为主，也吸收了来自印度次大陆的政治伦理与宇宙秩序观。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped chiefly by Buddhist discipline, merit-making, the idea of kingship as protector of the faith, and monastic scholasticism, while also drawing on broader South Asian ideas of political ethics and cosmic order.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期僧伽罗-婆罗米系文字书写铭文和行政性题记，宗教经典以巴利语传承，并受梵语文化影响。
writingSystem.en: Early Sinhala-Brahmi derived scripts were used for inscriptions and administrative records, while religious texts were transmitted in Pali and influenced by Sanskritic culture.

culturalAchievements.zh: 大型佛塔、寺院建筑群、灌溉水库工程、石刻题铭、佛教史传传统与印度洋佛教网络构成其主要文化成就。阿努拉德普勒的圣树崇拜、舍利信仰与宏大宗教建筑在当时极具声望。
culturalAchievements.en: Its principal cultural achievements included massive stupas, monastic complexes, reservoir engineering, stone inscriptions, Buddhist chronicle traditions, and participation in the Indian Ocean Buddhist world. The sacred Bodhi tree cult, relic devotion, and monumental religious architecture of Anuradhapura enjoyed great prestige.

languageFamily.zh: 僧伽罗语属印度-雅利安语支；巴利语为重要宗教语言，泰米尔语在贸易与族群接触中亦具影响。
languageFamily.en: Sinhala belonged to the Indo-Aryan branch; Pali was the major liturgical language, and Tamil was also influential in trade and interethnic contact.

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克/人
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person, c. 476 CE

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作灌溉农业为基础，辅以寺院地产、手工业与海上转口贸易。其总量在岛屿政权中相当可观，但仍远小于同时代大型欧亚帝国；财富集中于灌溉核心区、都城及寺院网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated rice agriculture, supplemented by monastic estates, craft production, and maritime transit trade. Its overall scale was considerable for an island kingdom, though far smaller than that of the major Eurasian empires of the age; wealth was concentrated in the irrigation heartland, the capital, and monastic networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米农业、水利工程、寺院地产经营、宝石采集、金属与陶器制造、象只与林产利用、港口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, hydraulic engineering, monastic estate management, gem extraction, metalworking and pottery, elephant and forest-resource exploitation, and port trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、宝石、珍珠、象只、肉桂等香料、象牙、木材、纺织品、金属器、僧院所需宗教用品；输入品包括马匹、奢侈布料、金属、盐和部分工艺品。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, gems, pearls, elephants, cinnamon and related aromatics, ivory, timber, textiles, metal goods, and religious supplies for monasteries; imports included horses, luxury cloth, metals, salt, and selected manufactured items.

name.zh: 铜币与实物赋税并用体系
name.en: Copper coin and in-kind tax system

unitName.zh: 卡哈帕那等地方币值单位

householdWealth.zh: 普通农村家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、灌溉水权、谷物储备、役畜和宗族关系；城市商人与寺院掌握更高价值的金属器、宝石和进口品。
householdWealth.en: The wealth of ordinary rural households lay chiefly in access to land, irrigation water, grain stores, draft animals, and kinship ties; urban merchants and monasteries controlled more valuable metal goods, gems, and imported wares.

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克/人/年
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year, c. 476 CE

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

economicSystem.zh: 王权、寺院地产和村社经济并存的农业主导型再分配经济，兼具印度洋商业联系。国家财富更多来自控制土地、水利、劳役与港口，而非纯粹货币征税。
economicSystem.en: An agriculture-led redistributive economy combining royal authority, monastic estates, and village production, with active Indian Ocean commercial ties. State wealth derived more from control of land, water, labor, and ports than from purely monetary taxation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

source.en: Corvée labor and public works levies

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物储备、铜币、贵金属器、宝石和王室物资库存共同构成，并不完全表现为可流通货币。稳定年份中，较强的灌溉农业使其具备一定抗灾能力。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of grain reserves, copper coinage, precious metal objects, gems, and royal stockpiles rather than only liquid money. In stable years, the strong irrigation agrarian base gave the kingdom a meaningful capacity to absorb shocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持水利、支持佛教机构、保障王都与军队供给。王权通常通过实物征收和劳役调度维持国家运作，而在丰年积蓄仓储与珍贵物资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintenance of irrigation, support for Buddhist institutions, and provisioning of the capital and army. The monarchy ordinarily sustained the state through collection in kind and labor mobilization, while storing grain and valuables in prosperous years.

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、弓箭、盾牌和刀剑的步兵组成，负责守卫都城、道路、仓储和主要灌溉区。
description.en: The core of the army, composed of spearmen, archers, shield-bearers, and swordsmen, tasked with guarding the capital, roads, granaries, and main irrigation districts.

description.zh: 由地方首领和村社按需征发，战时用于地区防御、平乱和补充主力。
description.en: Raised by local chiefs and village communities as needed, these forces were used for regional defense, suppression of unrest, and reinforcement in wartime.

description.zh: 数量不大但象征性强，用于冲击、威慑和王室出征场合，通常配属精锐护卫和侍从。
description.en: Small in number but highly prestigious, they were used for shock, intimidation, and royal campaigns, usually accompanied by elite guards and attendants.

description.zh: 负责港口安全、税关保护和海上来犯预警，规模有限，难称真正海军。
description.en: Responsible for port security, customs protection, and warning against seaborne threats; limited in scale and not a true navy in later terms.

technology.zh: 军备以铁制近战武器、弓箭、防盾、战象和堡垒守备为主，具备古典南亚水准。其优势在本土机动、补给与要地防御，而非远洋作战或重装骑兵。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron melee weapons, bows, shields, war elephants, and fortified defense, broadly in line with classical South Asian standards. Its strength lay in home-territory mobility, supply, and defense of key zones rather than blue-water warfare or heavy cavalry.

unit.zh: 公元476年银第纳尔等值克
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent, c. 476 CE

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南印度政治势力的海上干预、岛内地方叛乱、王位争夺以及边缘地区对王权控制的波动。与西方罗马世界并无直接军事对抗，其外部安全更受南亚海域格局影响。
threats.en: The main threats came from seaborne intervention by South Indian powers, internal provincial rebellion, succession struggles, and fluctuating royal control over peripheral regions. It had no direct military confrontation with the western Roman world; its external security was shaped far more by South Asian maritime politics.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于北中部灌溉农业区和王都周边，其余分布在南部、东部沿海和森林边缘地带。人口结构受大型水利系统承载能力、季风条件和地方安全状况影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the north-central irrigation zone and around the capital, with the remainder spread across the south, eastern coasts, and forest margins. Demographic distribution was strongly shaped by the capacity of large hydraulic systems, monsoon conditions, and regional security.

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为王室与贵族、僧团、地方首领、地主与灌溉管理者、农民、工匠、商人、港口居民、劳役人口及森林边缘族群。社会流动有限，但通过军功、宗教捐献和宫廷服务仍存在上升路径。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included the royal family and nobility, the sangha, local chiefs, landholders and irrigation managers, peasants, artisans, merchants, port populations, laboring groups, and forest-margin peoples. Social mobility was limited, but advancement remained possible through military service, religious patronage, and court employment.

allies.zh: 没有意义上固定的远程正式盟友；现实合作对象主要是南印度部分港口商人集团、佛教僧团网络以及与印度洋贸易相关的友好商帮。
allies.en: It had no fixed long-distance formal allies in the later diplomatic sense; practical partners included certain South Indian port merchant groups, Buddhist monastic networks, and friendly commercial communities tied to Indian Ocean trade.

enemies.zh: 主要潜在敌手为南印度可能入侵或干预的王国、海上掠夺者以及岛内挑战王权的地方势力。
enemies.en: Its main potential enemies were South Indian kingdoms capable of invasion or intervention, maritime raiders, and local forces within the island challenging royal authority.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持海上贸易、保障港口安全、管理与南印度的竞争及联系、并通过佛教声望提升国际地位为主。其对西方世界的联系多经由中间商和印度洋航线，而非直接大使政治。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving maritime trade, securing ports, managing both rivalry and connection with South India, and enhancing status through Buddhist prestige. Its links to the western world operated mostly through intermediaries and Indian Ocean routes rather than direct embassy politics.

era.zh: 古典晚期南亚水利与寺院文明时代
era.en: Late Classical South Asian hydraulic and monastic age

keyInnovations.zh: 大型蓄水池与渠道网络、堤坝工程、成熟的稻作灌溉体系、佛塔和石造宗教建筑技术、铭文行政传统、宝石与金属加工技术。
keyInnovations.en: Large storage tanks and canal networks, embankment engineering, mature irrigated rice cultivation, stupa and stone religious construction techniques, inscription-based administrative practices, and gem and metalworking skills.

strengths.zh: 王国稳定，首都与灌溉核心区控制力较强；佛教中心地位提升了合法性与文化影响；农业基础扎实，能支撑人口、寺院和军队；海上贸易使其接入更广阔的印度洋世界。
strengths.en: The kingdom was stable, with strong control over the capital and irrigation heartland; its status as a Buddhist center enhanced legitimacy and cultural influence; the agrarian base was robust enough to support population, monasteries, and army; maritime trade connected it to the wider Indian Ocean world.

weaknesses.zh: 对大型水利系统依赖极高，若失修或遇灾则影响深远；继承不稳时易引发宫廷争斗；地方与边缘地区整合有限；对马匹和部分奢侈品依赖外来贸易。
weaknesses.en: It was highly dependent on large hydraulic systems, so neglect or environmental stress could have far-reaching effects; unstable succession could trigger court conflict; integration of outlying regions remained limited; and horses and some luxury goods depended on external trade.

outlook.zh: 在476年前后，阿努拉德普勒王国前景总体稳健，只要维持水利、寺院与王权之间的平衡，便能继续作为斯里兰卡主要政权和印度洋佛教重镇存在。但其长期命运仍取决于岛内继承政治与南印度力量的周期性介入。
outlook.en: Around 476 CE, the outlook of the Anuradhapura Kingdom was broadly solid. If it maintained the balance among irrigation, monastic institutions, and royal authority, it could continue as Sri Lanka's principal state and a major Buddhist center of the Indian Ocean. Its long-term fate, however, still depended on internal succession politics and periodic intervention from South India.

description.zh: 阿努罗陀补罗王国统治斯里兰卡北中部，是南亚海上佛教中心之一。
description.en: The Anuradhapura Kingdom ruled north-central Sri Lanka and was a major Buddhist center of the Indian Ocean.

ruler.zh: 范當根據系譜推定的後林邑／占婆國王
ruler.en: A later Linyi/Champa king of the Bhadravarman line, name uncertain in surviving records

governmentForm.zh: 印度化海岸王國君主制，王權結合地方酋長、宗教贊助與港口控制
governmentForm.en: Indianized coastal monarchy in which royal authority was reinforced through local chiefs, religious patronage, and control of ports

socialStructure.zh: 社會由王室、婆羅門與祭司、地方貴族與部族首領、商人與海員、工匠、農民與漁民，以及依附勞動人口所構成；不同河口與沿海盆地具有相當地方性
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal family, Brahmins and ritual specialists, local nobles and clan chiefs, merchants and sailors, artisans, peasants and fishers, and dependent laborers; individual river valleys and coastal basins retained strong local identities

rulingClass.zh: 王族、婆羅門祭司、地方貴族與控制港口與土地的氏族首領
rulingClass.en: The royal house, Brahmin ritual elites, local nobles, and clan leaders who controlled ports and land

succession.zh: 以王族世襲為主，但實際繼承常取決於王室分支、軍事支持、地方首領承認與宗教合法性
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within the royal line, though actual succession often depended on support from royal branches, military backing, recognition by local chiefs, and religious legitimacy

structure.zh: 王國統治以宮廷為核心，透過首都、宗教中心與各海岸河谷據點向外延伸；中央直接控制有限，地方酋長、港口首長與宗教機構在行政中地位重要
structure.en: Rule centered on the royal court and extended outward through the capital, religious centers, and coastal river-valley nodes; direct central control was limited, and local chiefs, port authorities, and religious institutions played major administrative roles

function.zh: 管理宮廷、王室土地、禮儀、使節接待與王室工坊
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremonies, reception of envoys, and royal workshops

function.zh: 徵收稻米、林產、海產、港口稅與附庸貢納，並管理倉儲與再分配
function.en: Collected rice, forest products, marine produce, port duties, and tributary payments, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 監督港口、商舶停泊、關稅、度量與外商往來
function.en: Supervised ports, docking of merchant vessels, customs dues, weights and measures, and dealings with foreign merchants

function.zh: 維護印度教神廟、祖先祭祀、王室奉獻與宗教土地
function.en: Maintained Hindu sanctuaries, ancestor rites, royal donations, and temple lands

function.zh: 統籌王室親軍、港口守備、地方徵發與戰船調度
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, port garrisons, local levies, and deployment of war boats

function.zh: 聯繫地方首領、處理朝貢、仲裁邊地與內部河谷關係
function.en: Liaised with local chiefs, handled tribute, and mediated relations in frontier and interior river valleys

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以河谷與沿海據點為單位，由王室任命或承認的地方首領、港口官、稅吏與神廟管理者共同治理；實際統治深受地域、氏族與航路條件影響
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through river valleys and coastal nodes, governed jointly by royal appointees or recognized chiefs, port officers, tax collectors, and temple managers; effective rule was strongly shaped by regional, clan, and maritime conditions

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、習慣法、部族慣例與受印度影響的王權—宗教秩序並行，土地、貢賦、婚姻、繼承與商業糾紛常由地方首領、婆羅門與王廷共同裁決
legalSystem.en: Law combined royal decree, customary practice, clan norms, and Indian-influenced ideas of sacred kingship; disputes over land, tribute, marriage, inheritance, and trade were often judged jointly by local chiefs, Brahmins, and the court

taxationSystem.zh: 稅制以實物與勞役為主，包括稻米徵收、港口關稅、森林香木與象牙貢納、漁鹽收入以及對附屬社群的定期進貢
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based mainly on goods and labor service, including rice collection, port customs, tribute in aromatic woods and ivory from upland zones, fisheries and salt revenues, and periodic contributions from subordinate communities

religion.zh: 以濕婆崇拜為主的印度教為王室核心信仰，同時存在毗濕奴崇拜、祖先祭祀、地方靈神信仰，並可能有少量佛教影響
religion.en: Court religion centered on Shaivite Hinduism, alongside Vaishnavite worship, ancestor rites, local spirit cults, and probably limited Buddhist influence

philosophy.zh: 思想以婆羅門教宇宙觀、王權神聖性、功德奉獻與地方祖靈傳統交織，重視王室祭祀、神廟建置與維持宇宙秩序
philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined Brahmanical cosmology, sacred kingship, merit through donation, and local ancestral traditions, emphasizing royal ritual, temple foundations, and the maintenance of cosmic order

writingSystem.zh: 使用梵文作為碑銘與王室宗教語言，並已出現早期占語書寫傳統，文字系統受南印度婆羅米系影響
writingSystem.en: Sanskrit was used for inscriptions and royal religious expression, while an early Cham writing tradition was emerging; the scripts were influenced by southern Indian Brahmi-derived forms

culturalAchievements.zh: 建立印度化王權與碑銘傳統、發展沿海神廟與聖地、吸收南亞藝術母題、形成善航海與港市交流的海洋文化，並在中部越南沿岸塑造具地方特色的占人文明
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included the establishment of Indianized kingship and inscriptional culture, development of coastal temples and sanctuaries, adoption of South Asian artistic motifs, creation of a seafaring port culture, and the formation of a distinct Cham civilization along the central Vietnamese coast

languageFamily.zh: 占語屬南島語系，占—亞齊群；上層宗教與政治文化大量借用梵語
languageFamily.en: Cham belonged to the Austronesian family, specifically the Chamic-Acehnese grouping, while elite religious and political culture borrowed heavily from Sanskrit

gdpDescription.zh: 經濟以稻作、漁鹽、林產採集、手工業與轉口海貿結合而成。整體規模不及中國或印度大帝國，但在南海沿岸屬於富於商業活力的中等海洋王國
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined rice agriculture, fishing and salt production, forest extraction, handicrafts, and entrepôt maritime trade. It was smaller than the major empires of China or India, but it was a commercially active medium-sized maritime kingdom in the South China Sea world

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻種植、漁業、鹽業、造船、木材與香木採集、象牙與犀角轉運、紡織、陶器、金屬加工、港口服務與海外貿易
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice cultivation, fishing, salt making, boatbuilding, logging and aromatic wood collection, transit trade in ivory and rhinoceros horn, textiles, pottery, metalworking, port services, and overseas commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口沉香、檀木、象牙、犀角、龜甲、珍禽異獸、奴婢、海產與可能的樹脂、蜂蠟與香料；進口布匹、金屬器、珠寶、玻璃珠、宗教用品與印度文化奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included agarwood, sandalwood, ivory, rhinoceros horn, tortoiseshell, exotic birds and animals, slaves, marine products, and likely resins, beeswax, and spices; imports included textiles, metal goods, jewelry, glass beads, ritual items, and Indian luxury goods

name.zh: 貴金屬秤量與實物交換
name.en: Bullion by weight and barter exchange

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭財富主要體現在稻田使用權、牲畜、漁具、小舟、鹽、陶器與宗族關係；沿海商人、港口中介者與神廟相關精英掌握較高財富
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in access to rice land, livestock, fishing gear, small boats, salt, pottery, and kin networks; much higher wealth was concentrated among coastal merchants, port intermediaries, and temple-linked elites

economicSystem.zh: 以農業與地方貢納為基礎、由王室與港口精英主導的海洋交換經濟；市場存在但未完全貨幣化，長途交易依賴船主、外商與政治保護
economicSystem.en: A maritime exchange economy grounded in agriculture and local tribute, dominated by the royal court and port elites; markets existed but were not fully monetized, and long-distance trade depended on shipowners, foreign merchants, and political protection

source.en: Tribute from subordinate communities and local chiefs

source.zh: 王室土地、鹽業與林產收入
source.en: Royal estates, salt, and forest-product income

category.en: Court, royal household, and elite patronage

treasuryDescription.zh: 國庫並非純以鑄幣保存，而是由貴金屬、珠寶、稻穀、香木、進口奢侈品與可供再分配的貢品共同構成；對海洋王國而言，實物儲備與聲望財同樣重要
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not held purely in coinage but in a mixed store of bullion, jewelry, rice, aromatic woods, imported luxury goods, and tribute suitable for redistribution; for a maritime kingdom, physical reserves and prestige goods were as important as specie

fiscalPolicy.zh: 財政策略偏向保守穩定，優先維持港口收益、王室賞賜網絡與宗教合法性，在貿易繁榮年份積累儲備，以應對戰事、歉收或航路波動
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and stability-oriented, prioritizing port income, royal patronage networks, and religious legitimacy, while building reserves in prosperous trading years to withstand war, poor harvests, or disruptions in maritime routes

description.zh: 以長矛、盾牌、弓與短兵器為主，負責首都、防禦工事與內陸河谷作戰
description.en: Equipped mainly with spears, shields, bows, and short weapons, they defended the capital, fortifications, and inland river valleys

description.zh: 由戰船、武裝商船與港口守軍組成，用於護航、巡查海岸、打擊海盜與控制河口
description.en: Composed of war boats, armed merchant craft, and port garrisons, used for convoy protection, coastal patrol, anti-piracy, and estuary control

description.zh: 由地方首領提供，戰時集結，適合山地、林地與地方守備，但訓練與裝備不如王室親軍整齊
description.en: Supplied by local chiefs and assembled in wartime, they were suited to upland, forest, and local defense but were less uniformly trained and equipped than the royal troops

description.zh: 數量有限但具威懾力，用於決戰衝擊、儀仗與王權象徵，配屬精銳護衛
description.en: Limited in number but highly prestigious, they were used for shock action in battle, ceremonial display, and royal symbolism, accompanied by elite guards

commanderInChief.en: The King of Champa as supreme commander

technology.zh: 軍事技術以鐵製兵器、弓矛、木質或竹質防具、戰象運用、河海船隻與土木城防為主；海上機動力對沿岸王國特別重要
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, bows and spears, wooden or bamboo armor, the use of war elephants, riverine and seagoing craft, and earthen or timber fortifications; maritime mobility was especially important for a coastal kingdom

threats.zh: 主要威脅來自與中國南朝相關的邊境衝突、北方與鄰近沿海政權競逐、地方首領叛服不定，以及海盜與航路安全問題
threats.en: Primary threats came from frontier tensions linked to the Southern Chinese states, rivalry with northern and neighboring coastal polities, uncertain loyalty of local chiefs, and piracy and insecurity along maritime routes

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中於中部越南沿海平原、河口與河谷，並與山地族群保持密切交換關係；王國人口密度沿海高、內陸低，具有分散而節點化的海岸聚落格局
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the coastal plains, estuaries, and river valleys of central Vietnam, while maintaining close exchange ties with upland peoples; density was higher on the coast and lower inland, producing a dispersed but nodal settlement pattern

socialClasses.zh: 上層為王族、婆羅門、地方貴族與富商；中層為小地主、工匠、船主、港口中介者與神廟從屬人員；下層為農民、漁民、搬運與依附勞動人口，另有山地互市社群與被擄掠者
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal family, Brahmins, local nobles, and wealthy merchants; the middle strata included lesser landholders, artisans, shipowners, port brokers, and temple dependents; the lower strata included peasants, fishers, porters, and dependent laborers, alongside upland exchange communities and captives

allies.zh: 無固定同盟體系；實際上與南朝中國、南印度商人、扶南等下湄公河海洋勢力及區域港口保持依時局變化的通商與使節關係
allies.en: There was no fixed alliance system; in practice the kingdom maintained shifting commercial and diplomatic relations with the Southern Chinese states, South Indian merchants, Lower Mekong maritime powers such as Funan, and regional ports

enemies.zh: 主要敵對對象為北方中國政權在南方邊境的軍事壓力，以及與周邊海岸與內陸首領之間的競爭；並無與西方地中海諸國直接敵對
enemies.en: Its main adversaries were military pressure from the northern Chinese sphere on the southern frontier and competition with neighboring coastal and inland chiefs; it had no direct hostility with the western Mediterranean polities listed in the broader world context

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以維護海上貿易、向大國輸誠以換取承認、在區域內保持自主並利用港口中介地位為核心；必要時以劫掠、示威或使節互訪並用
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on protecting maritime trade, offering submission or embassies to great powers when useful for recognition, preserving regional autonomy, and exploiting the kingdom's intermediary port position; raiding, displays of force, and embassy exchange could all be used as circumstances required

era.zh: 古典晚期南海海洋時代
era.en: Late Antique South China Sea Maritime Age

keyInnovations.zh: 南海季風航海知識、沿海與河口港口經營、受印度影響的碑銘與宗教建築技術、磚石神廟工藝、造船與沿岸遠航能力、稻作與鹽業結合的區域生產體系
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included monsoon navigation knowledge for the South China Sea, management of coastal and estuarine ports, Indian-influenced inscriptional and religious architectural techniques, brick sanctuary construction, boatbuilding and coastal long-distance sailing, and a regional production system combining rice agriculture with salt making

strengths.zh: 占婆的優勢在於地處南海航路節點、善於吸收印度文化以鞏固王權、海上貿易活躍、港口靈活且能整合沿海與山地物產。其政治雖非高度集中，卻具備適應地形與商路的彈性
strengths.en: Champa's strengths lay in its position on South China Sea routes, its successful adoption of Indian culture to legitimize kingship, its lively maritime trade, and its ability to integrate coastal and upland products. Though not highly centralized, its political order was flexible and well adapted to terrain and commerce

weaknesses.zh: 弱點在於地方分散、中央控制有限、繼承易生爭議、對港口貿易與季風環境依賴較深，且面對北方大國壓力時難以持久進行大規模陸戰
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included regional fragmentation, limited central control, succession disputes, heavy dependence on port trade and monsoon conditions, and difficulty sustaining large-scale land warfare against stronger northern states

outlook.zh: 476年前後的占婆前景整體穩定且具擴張商機。若能維持王室與地方首領合作、鞏固港口與宗教中心、避免與北方政權爆發過度消耗的戰爭，則可在接下來數世代繼續作為南海重要的印度化海洋王國
outlook.en: Around 476 CE, Champa's outlook was broadly stable and commercially promising. If the court could preserve cooperation with local chiefs, strengthen ports and religious centers, and avoid excessively draining wars with northern states, it could continue for generations as a major Indianized maritime kingdom of the South China Sea

description.zh: 占婆沿今越南中部海岸发展，是南海贸易中的重要印度化王国。
description.en: Champa developed along the central Vietnamese coast as a key Indianized kingdom in South China Sea trade.

capital.zh: 室利差呾罗及其他城邦

governmentForm.zh: 以有城墙的佛教城邦为核心的君主制联盟，诸城各自统治并保持文化联系
governmentForm.en: A constellation of walled Buddhist city-states ruled by monarchs, each governed independently while maintaining close cultural ties

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、宗教团体、城市贵族、书吏与工匠、农民及劳役人口构成，城市与灌溉农业区联系紧密
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around royal households, religious communities, urban elites, scribes and artisans, cultivators, and dependent laborers, with close ties between cities and irrigated agricultural zones

rulingClass.zh: 城邦王族、地方贵族、宫廷官员以及与寺院相关的高级僧俗精英
rulingClass.en: City-state royal families, local nobles, court officials, and high lay and monastic elites associated with religious establishments

succession.zh: 通常为王族世袭，但常受地方贵族、宗教权威与宫廷集团影响，具体规则因城邦而异
succession.en: Generally hereditary within royal lineages, though often influenced by local nobles, religious authorities, and court factions, with practices varying by city-state

structure.zh: 骠诸城邦并无统一帝国官僚体系，而是各主要城市设王廷、宗教机构与地方行政网络；室利差呾罗可能为最强中心之一，对周边聚落与灌溉区施加较大影响
structure.en: The Pyu did not possess a single imperial bureaucracy; instead, major cities maintained their own courts, religious establishments, and local administrative networks. Sri Ksetra was likely among the strongest centers, exercising notable influence over nearby settlements and irrigation zones

function.zh: 管理王宫、礼仪、使节接待、仓储与王室土地
function.en: Managed the palace, ceremonies, reception of envoys, storage, and royal lands

function.zh: 维护水渠、水塘、堤坝与农时征发，监督灌溉农业生产
function.en: Maintained canals, reservoirs, embankments, and labor obligations tied to the agricultural calendar, overseeing irrigated production

function.zh: 征收谷物、手工业贡赋、贸易税及储备粮食
function.en: Collected grain dues, craft levies, trade tolls, and maintained food reserves

function.zh: 记录土地、裁断纠纷、保管铭文与宫廷文书
function.en: Recorded land matters, judged disputes, and preserved inscriptions and court documents

function.zh: 协调寺院供养、节庆、捐献确认与宗教建筑维护
function.en: Coordinated monastery support, festivals, confirmation of donations, and upkeep of religious buildings

function.zh: 监管商队、道路、渡口与市集税收，联络远方贸易者
function.en: Supervised caravans, roads, ferries, and market tolls, and dealt with long-distance merchants

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托城墙城市、附属市镇与乡村聚落。王廷通过地方首领、村社长老、书吏和水利管理者控制田地、劳役与贡赋；边远地区的控制较为间接
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on walled cities, subordinate towns, and village settlements. Courts governed fields, labor, and dues through local chiefs, village elders, scribes, and irrigation managers; control over more distant areas was comparatively indirect

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于王命、习惯法、宗教伦理与地方调解。土地、水权、赋役、婚姻及商业纠纷多由官员、长老或宗教权威处理，严重案件由王廷裁断
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on royal orders, customary practice, religious ethics, and local mediation. Disputes over land, water rights, taxation, marriage, and commerce were handled by officials, elders, or religious authorities, while grave cases were judged at court

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以谷物、芝麻、棉布、手工业品和劳役为主，并辅以市场税、关卡税、渡口费及对贸易商队征收的通行税；部分王室与寺院土地享有减免
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on grain, sesame, cotton cloth, craft goods, and labor services, supplemented by market taxes, tolls, ferry dues, and caravan transit charges; some royal and monastic lands likely enjoyed exemptions

religion.zh: 以佛教为核心，可能兼具上座部、部派与大乘因素，同时持续吸收本地精灵崇拜、祖先祭祀与婆罗门礼仪元素
religion.en: Religion centered on Buddhism, likely incorporating Theravada, other early Buddhist sectarian elements, and some Mahayana influence, while continuing to absorb local spirit cults, ancestor rites, and Brahmanical ritual forms

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以佛教功德观、王权护法观念、因果伦理与僧俗供养关系为主，也受印度宇宙观、占星与礼制影响
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Buddhist ideas of merit, righteous kingship, karma, and monastic-lay patronage, alongside Indian cosmology, astrology, and court ritual concepts

writingSystem.zh: 使用骠文与印度系文字传统，铭文中亦可见巴利语或梵语影响，书写主要服务于宗教、纪念和行政记录
writingSystem.en: The Pyu used the Pyu script within the broader Indic writing tradition, with Pali or Sanskrit influence visible in inscriptions; writing served primarily religious, commemorative, and administrative purposes

culturalAchievements.zh: 骠文明以大型城墙都市规划、佛塔与寺院建筑、青铜与陶器工艺、石刻铭文、珠饰制作及以音乐和平和风俗著称，是早期缅甸地区最重要的城市文明之一
culturalAchievements.en: The Pyu are noted for large walled urban planning, stupas and monastic architecture, bronze and ceramic craftsmanship, stone inscriptions, bead production, and a reputation for music and peaceful customs, making them one of the most important early urban civilizations in Myanmar

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系骠语支
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Pyu branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉稻作农业为基础，辅以芝麻、豆类、棉花、畜养、陶器与金属手工业，并通过连接印度次大陆、云南与下缅甸的陆路贸易获利。就476年区域标准而言，属于稳定而中等发达的内陆城市经济
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated rice agriculture, supported by sesame, pulses, cotton, livestock, pottery, and metal crafts, and profited from overland trade linking the Indian subcontinent, Yunnan, and Lower Myanmar. By regional standards in 476 CE, it was a stable and moderately developed inland urban economy

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、稻米种植、棉纺织、陶器烧造、珠饰制作、青铜与铁器加工、寺院建筑、商队贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, rice cultivation, cotton textiles, pottery production, bead making, bronze and iron working, religious construction, and caravan trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、芝麻、棉布、盐、陶器、珠饰、木材、漆器、宝石、象牙及转口的印度奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, sesame, cotton cloth, salt, pottery, beads, timber, lacquerware, gemstones, ivory, and Indian luxury goods moved through transit trade

name.zh: 混合实物与金属货币体系
name.en: Mixed commodity and metal currency system

unitName.en: Pyu silver coin and grain valuation

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷物储备、牲畜、农具、棉布与陶器；城市富户和寺院则积累金属器、珠宝、库存与劳动力支配权
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of land-use rights, grain stores, livestock, tools, cotton cloth, and pottery, while urban elites and monasteries accumulated metal goods, jewelry, stockpiles, and command over labor

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余、王室再分配、寺院供养与受监管的市场交换并存的早期城市经济
economicSystem.en: An early urban economy combining agrarian surplus, royal redistribution, monastic endowment, and regulated market exchange

source.zh: 劳役与公共工程征发折值
source.en: Labor dues and public works obligations

source.zh: 王室地产与手工业收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要不是纯粹铸币金银，而是由银、铜器、谷仓储备、贡品、纺织品与可再分配库存共同构成，足以支撑常规工程和短期危机应对
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not composed solely of coined precious metal, but of silver, copper goods, granary reserves, tribute items, textiles, and redistributable stockpiles, sufficient for routine works and short-term crisis response

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持水利、稳固城防、支持宗教威望与保证商路安全，税赋总体温和，依赖实物征收与季节性劳役
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized irrigation upkeep, secure defenses, religious prestige, and safe trade routes; taxation was relatively moderate and relied heavily on payment in kind and seasonal labor

description.zh: 以持矛、刀、弓和藤盾的步兵为主，是守城、巡逻和战时征发的核心力量
description.en: Infantry armed with spears, blades, bows, and wicker or hide shields formed the core force for city defense, patrol, and wartime mobilization

description.zh: 负责城门、城墙、仓储、道路节点和渡口的守卫，平时兼具警备职能
description.en: Guarded gates, walls, storehouses, road junctions, and ferries, while also performing routine security duties

description.zh: 规模不大，但具威慑与指挥价值，战象和骑乘人员多用于冲击、仪仗与机动联络
description.en: Small in number but valuable for intimidation and command presence; elephants and mounted personnel were used for shock action, ceremony, and mobile communications

description.zh: 由附属聚落和乡村临时征发，战斗力有限，但在守卫粮道、水利与周边聚落时十分重要
description.en: Temporarily raised from subordinate settlements and villages; of limited battlefield quality but important for guarding food routes, irrigation works, and nearby communities

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦之王兼最高统帅

technology.zh: 军备技术以铁制兵器、弓矢、长矛、刀剑、木土城防与战象运用为主，缺乏重甲骑兵与高度复杂攻城器械，整体适合区域性防御与城邦战争
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, bows, spears, swords, timber-and-earth fortifications, and the use of war elephants, lacking heavy cavalry and highly sophisticated siege machinery; it was well suited to regional defense and city-state warfare

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近山地与平原部族冲突、商路争夺、城邦间竞争及上缅甸区域势力变化，而非远距离帝国入侵
threats.en: Principal threats came from neighboring hill and plain groups, competition over trade routes, rivalry among city-states, and shifting power balances in Upper Myanmar rather than invasion by distant empires

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约六十万，主要分布在上缅甸灌溉农业带、城墙城市及其附属村社。人口密度在主要河谷与交通节点较高，其他地区相对分散
populationDescription.en: The population was about 600,000, concentrated in the irrigated agricultural zones of Upper Myanmar, the walled cities, and their dependent villages. Density was highest in major river valleys and transport nodes, and lower elsewhere

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为王族与贵族、僧侣与宗教人员、官员与书吏、商人和工匠、自耕农、佃作者与劳役人口，以及边缘附属部落群体
socialClasses.en: Society may be broadly divided into royal and noble lineages, monks and religious personnel, officials and scribes, merchants and artisans, peasant cultivators, tenant and laboring populations, and marginal subordinate tribal groups

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的正式盟友体系，但与印度次大陆诸政权、南诏前身地区商路群体、下缅甸与东南亚内陆贸易网络保持友好往来
allies.en: There was no formal alliance system in the strict sense, but the Pyu maintained friendly relations with polities of the Indian subcontinent, trade communities toward the Yunnan corridor, and networks in Lower Myanmar and mainland Southeast Asia

enemies.zh: 无明确长期宿敌；现实对手多为区域性竞争城邦、争夺商路的地方势力及不稳定边地部族
enemies.en: There was no clearly defined long-term archenemy; practical rivals were regional competing city-states, local powers contesting trade routes, and unstable frontier groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保障商道、安全通行、维持宗教声望与避免大规模战争为主，通过馈赠、通婚、使节往来和贸易联系扩展影响力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing trade routes, ensuring safe passage, maintaining religious prestige, and avoiding large-scale war, with influence extended through gifts, marriage ties, envoys, and commerce

era.zh: 古典晚期至后古典早期过渡
era.en: Late Antique to Early Post-Classical transition

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的城墙都市规划、灌溉池渠系统、印度系文字采用、佛教建筑扩展、铁器与青铜工艺、珠饰与陶器专业化生产
keyInnovations.en: Mature walled urban planning, irrigated reservoir-and-canal systems, adoption of Indic scripts, expansion of Buddhist architecture, iron and bronze craftsmanship, and specialized production of beads and ceramics

strengths.zh: 骠文明的优势在于稳定的灌溉农业基础、发达的城墙城市、连接印度与东南亚的陆路贸易位置、较高的宗教文化凝聚力，以及相对温和的统治与社会整合能力
strengths.en: The strengths of the Pyu lay in a stable irrigated agrarian base, developed walled cities, an overland trade position connecting India and Southeast Asia, strong religious-cultural cohesion, and a relatively moderate capacity for social integration and rule

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于缺乏统一中央政权、城邦之间协调有限、军事扩张能力较弱、对商路和水利维护高度依赖，并容易受到区域人口迁徙与新兴势力冲击
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the absence of a unified central state, limited coordination among city-states, modest military expansion capacity, heavy dependence on trade routes and irrigation maintenance, and vulnerability to regional migrations and rising powers

outlook.zh: 在476年前后，骠诸城邦前景总体稳定，仍将依靠农业与贸易继续繁荣；但从长期看，若无法形成更强的政治整合，未来可能在面对外来民族迁入和新王朝兴起时逐渐失去主导地位
outlook.en: Around 476 CE, the Pyu city-states had a generally stable outlook and were likely to continue prospering through agriculture and trade; over the longer term, however, without stronger political integration they could gradually lose primacy when faced with incoming peoples and the rise of new dynasties

description.zh: 上缅甸骠人诸城邦依托灌溉农业与陆路贸易长期繁荣。
description.en: The Pyu city-states of Upper Myanmar prospered through irrigated agriculture and overland trade.

governmentForm.zh: 游牧君主制，兼具部落联盟与王廷官署特征
governmentForm.en: Nomadic monarchy combining a tribal confederation with a royal court administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会由慕容王族、诸部贵族、部落民众、附属羌胡部众、商旅与少量定居农户构成，兼具草原部落等级与绿洲定居社会成分
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Murong royal clan, tribal nobles, common pastoral tribespeople, subordinate Qiang and other subject groups, merchants, and a small number of settled farmers, blending steppe hierarchy with oasis-based sedentary elements

rulingClass.zh: 慕容氏王族与各部首领、军政贵族共同构成统治集团
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed by the Murong royal house together with tribal chiefs and military-political nobles

succession.zh: 以王族世袭为主，通常由慕容氏宗室继承，但需获得主要部族贵族支持
succession.en: Succession was principally hereditary within the royal Murong clan, though effective accession required support from leading tribal nobles

structure.zh: 中央以可汗王廷为核心，下设近臣、军政首领与使者机构；地方依诸部、牧地与交通要道分区治理，边地由部帅、镇守首领和受封酋长管理
structure.en: The state centered on the khan’s royal court, assisted by close retainers, military-political chiefs, and envoys; locally it was governed through tribes, pasture zones, and transport corridors, with frontier areas managed by tribal commanders, garrison chiefs, and enfeoffed local leaders

function.zh: 处理诏令、朝会、部族协调与王族事务
function.en: Handled decrees, court audiences, tribal coordination, and royal clan affairs

function.zh: 统筹骑兵征发、边境防务、将领任命与战时调度
function.en: Oversaw cavalry levies, frontier defense, appointment of commanders, and wartime mobilization

function.zh: 分配牧场、统计部众、调解部族争端并监督附属部落
function.en: Allocated pastureland, recorded tribal populations, mediated intertribal disputes, and supervised subordinate tribes

function.zh: 管理南丝路通道、关卡征税、商队保护与市易
function.en: Managed the southern Silk Road corridors, customs collection, caravan protection, and market exchange

function.zh: 接待北魏、南朝及西域使者，办理朝贡、盟约与婚姻外交
function.en: Received envoys from Northern Wei, the Southern Dynasties, and Inner Asia, and managed tribute, alliances, and marriage diplomacy

function.zh: 执行习惯法、仲裁案件、监督赔偿、誓盟与惩罚
function.en: Applied customary law, arbitrated cases, supervised compensation, oath-taking, and punishments

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依部落编制与交通节点展开。青海湖周边、河湟走廊与柴达木东缘设有王族或贵族代理人，负责征集贡赋、维持道路安全与调停部落关系。较远地区保留附属首领的相当自治权。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through tribal organization and key transit nodes. Around Qinghai Lake, the Hehuang corridor, and the eastern rim of the Qaidam Basin, royal or noble agents supervised tribute collection, road security, and tribal mediation. More distant areas retained substantial autonomy under subordinate chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以鲜卑—草原习惯法为核心，辅以对羌、氐及绿洲居民的地方惯例。重视血亲责任、赔偿、誓言与首领裁断；涉及商旅与外交事务时也会采用书面契约和口头盟誓并行方式。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was rooted in Xianbei-steppe customary law, supplemented by local practices among Qiang, Di, and oasis populations. It emphasized kin responsibility, compensation, oaths, and chieftain adjudication; for commerce and diplomacy, written agreements and oral sworn compacts were both used.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自部众贡赋、牧畜抽贡、商道关税、市场税、附属部落进献及战利品分配。定居农业区可能征收谷物、布帛和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from tribal tribute, levies on livestock, customs duties on trade routes, market taxes, tribute from subordinate tribes, and shares of war booty. In settled farming areas, grain, textiles, and labor service could also be demanded.

religion.zh: 以萨满式草原信仰、祖先崇拜与天命观念为主，同时受佛教逐渐影响；与河西、凉州及西域接触使佛教僧侣和法物流入王廷与城镇。
religion.en: Religion was primarily steppe shamanic belief, ancestor veneration, and a heaven-legitimacy concept, while Buddhism was increasingly influential; contact with Hexi, Liangzhou, and the Western Regions brought monks and Buddhist objects into the court and towns.

philosophy.zh: 政治理念重在王族威望、部族忠诚、盟誓伦理与实用统治；对中原王朝的礼制与名号有一定吸收，但尚未形成系统文官儒学政治。
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized royal prestige, tribal loyalty, oath ethics, and pragmatic rule; it selectively absorbed titles and ceremonial ideas from Chinese courts, but had not developed a full Confucian bureaucratic ideology.

writingSystem.zh: 本族核心统治仍多依口传与多语行政，正式外交与贸易记录常借用汉字书写，也可能使用相关地区通行的文书语言。
writingSystem.en: Core governance remained heavily oral and multilingual, while formal diplomatic and commercial records commonly employed Chinese characters and other documentary languages current in adjacent regions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 吐谷浑在476年前后的文化成就在于把鲜卑草原传统、青海羌地文化与丝路商道网络结合起来，形成兼具游牧机动性与关道控制力的政治文化；其乐舞、服饰、马具和金属装饰体现出草原与西域风格交融。
culturalAchievements.en: Tuyuhun’s cultural achievement around 476 lay in combining Xianbei steppe traditions, the cultures of Qinghai’s Qiang lands, and Silk Road commercial networks, producing a political culture that fused nomadic mobility with control of strategic corridors; its music, dress, horse gear, and metal ornaments reflected a mix of steppe and Inner Asian styles.

languageFamily.zh: 以鲜卑语系成分为主的多语环境，并广泛使用汉语及羌、氐等地方语言进行交流
languageFamily.en: A multilingual environment centered on Xianbei-related speech, with Chinese and local Qiangic and Di languages also widely used

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 吐谷浑经济以畜牧业为基础，以青海草场和高原交通优势支撑王国运转。其总体财富不及大型农耕帝国，但凭借对南丝路要道、马匹贸易、盐产与中转贸易的控制，形成区域性强国的中等经济规模。
gdpDescription.en: The Tuyuhun economy was based on pastoralism, supported by Qinghai’s grasslands and the plateau’s transit advantages. Its total wealth was far below that of major agrarian empires, but control of southern Silk Road routes, horse trading, salt, and transit commerce gave it a solid regional middle-scale economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、马匹与牦牛饲养、羊毛与皮革加工、盐产、有限农业、商队护送与转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse and yak raising, wool and leather production, salt extraction, limited agriculture, caravan escorting, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牦牛、羊毛、皮革、盐、麝香、毛织品，以及转口的丝绸、金属器、珠饰与药材
tradeGoods.en: Horses, yaks, wool, leather, salt, musk, woolen textiles, and re-exported silk, metalware, beads, and medicinal goods

name.en: Cloth and metal currencies in mixed use

householdWealth.zh: 普通牧民家庭的财富主要表现为牲畜数量、帐幕、马具、皮毛与亲族互助关系；上层贵族则拥有大群马牛羊、附属人口、贸易收益与战利品分配权。
householdWealth.en: For ordinary pastoral households, wealth was chiefly measured in livestock, tents, horse gear, hides, and kin-based mutual support; elite nobles held large herds, dependent people, trade income, and rights to shares of tribute and booty.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部落贡赋和畜牧生产为基础、以商道税收和转口贸易补充的混合型草原—丝路经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed steppe–Silk Road economy based on tribal tribute and pastoral production, supplemented by trade-route taxation and transit commerce

source.zh: 盐产、特产与王室牧群收益
source.en: Salt, special products, and royal herd income

category.en: Road posts, checkpoints, and relay services

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非纯粹以铸币形式存在，而是由金银器、布帛、牲畜、盐货、丝绸馈赠和可供再分配的储备物资共同构成。稳定时期王廷能够保持一定积储，但遇到大战、灾年或部族动荡时消耗很快。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury did not exist purely as coinage; it consisted of precious metal objects, cloth, livestock, salt goods, silk gifts, and redistributable stores. In stable times the court could maintain a modest reserve, but major wars, bad years, or tribal unrest could deplete it quickly.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持王廷再分配能力与草原政治忠诚，尽量保护商路畅通以确保关税收入，同时避免对核心部众过度压榨。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining the court’s redistributive capacity and political loyalty on the steppe, while keeping trade routes secure to preserve customs income and avoiding excessive pressure on core tribes.

description.zh: 由可汗直属精锐骑兵组成，负责王廷护卫、快速突击与镇压叛乱
description.en: Elite cavalry directly attached to the khan, responsible for court protection, rapid strikes, and suppression of rebellions

description.zh: 来自各部的主要野战力量，以弓骑与机动突袭见长，是吐谷浑军队的核心
description.en: The principal field force drawn from the tribes, excelling in mounted archery and mobile raids, and forming the core of Tuyuhun military power

description.zh: 部署于要道、关卡和聚落周边，承担守卫、巡逻与护商任务
description.en: Stationed at passes, checkpoints, and settlements to provide guard duty, patrols, and caravan protection

description.zh: 由附属羌、氐及地方部族提供，战时补充侦察、山地作战与后勤
description.en: Provided by subordinate Qiang, Di, and local tribes, supplementing reconnaissance, mountain warfare, and logistics in wartime

technology.zh: 以5世纪草原骑战技术为主，装备复合弓、长矛、环首刀或直身刀、皮甲与部分铁甲，善于高原行军、骑射、侦察与截击商道。攻城技术有限，通常依赖围困、诱降或借助外来工匠。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of 5th-century steppe warfare, using composite bows, spears, ring-pommel sabers or straight blades, leather armor, and some iron armor. Tuyuhun forces excelled in plateau campaigning, mounted archery, reconnaissance, and interception along trade routes. Siege capabilities were limited, often relying on blockade, negotiated surrender, or outside craftsmen.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方与东北的柔然势力、河西与北魏方向的压力、西域通道上的竞争，以及内部部族离心与附属部落叛服不定。
threats.en: Main threats came from the Rouran in the north and northeast, pressure from the Hexi and Northern Wei direction, competition along Inner Asian corridors, and internal centrifugal tendencies among tribes and subject groups.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约50万，其中多数为分散于青海湖周边、高原牧地与河谷地带的游牧或半游牧部众，定居人口主要集中在交通据点、市场聚落及少数农业区。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 500,000, most of them dispersed as nomadic or semi-nomadic communities around Qinghai Lake, plateau pastures, and river valleys; settled populations were concentrated in transit points, market settlements, and a few farming districts.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为慕容王族、部落贵族与高级将领；中层包括普通部民首领、富裕牧户、商人、使者与宗教人物；下层为一般牧民、农户、手工业者、附属部落成员及战俘奴仆。社会流动性部分取决于军功、婚姻联盟与王廷赏赐。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the Murong royal clan, tribal nobles, and senior commanders; the middle included lesser tribal headmen, prosperous pastoralists, merchants, envoys, and religious figures; the lower comprised ordinary herders, farmers, artisans, subordinate tribal members, and war captives or servants. Social mobility depended partly on military merit, marriage alliances, and royal patronage.

allies.zh: 吐谷浑通常不维持现代意义上的固定盟友体系，而是通过与南朝、北方政权及邻近部族之间的临时通好、互市和婚姻关系维持均势。476年前后可视为与部分青海、河湟地方部族及丝路商团保持合作。
allies.en: Tuyuhun did not maintain a modern-style fixed alliance system; instead it preserved balance through temporary amity, trade, and marriage relations with southern Chinese courts, northern regimes, and neighboring tribes. Around 476 it may be regarded as cooperating with some local Qinghai and Hehuang groups and Silk Road merchant networks.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括争夺草原支配权的柔然、可能在河西与西北边界形成压力的北魏势力，以及不时冲突的周边部族与西域通道竞争者。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries included the Rouran, who competed for steppe predominance, Northern Wei influence pressing from the Hexi and northwestern frontier, and neighboring tribes or corridor rivals that clashed intermittently with Tuyuhun.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以控制通道、维持贸易、在强邻之间周旋并保存王国独立为核心。吐谷浑善于利用高原地理优势和机动骑兵，在北魏、南朝、西域商路与草原诸强之间实行务实均衡政策。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling routes, preserving trade, maneuvering among powerful neighbors, and maintaining the kingdom’s independence. Tuyuhun used plateau geography and mobile cavalry to pursue a pragmatic balancing strategy among Northern Wei, the Southern Dynasties, Inner Asian trade routes, and steppe powers.

era.zh: 5世纪晚期欧亚古典晚期与草原王国时代
era.en: Late 5th-century Late Antique and steppe kingdom era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非原创性大发明，而是成熟运用高原畜牧管理、复合弓骑射、长距离骑乘、皮革与毛织加工、盐产开发以及多区域商路组织能力。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths were not original inventions but the mature use of plateau pastoral management, composite-bow cavalry warfare, long-distance riding, leather and wool processing, salt extraction, and the organization of multi-regional trade routes.

strengths.zh: 吐谷浑的优势在于控制青海高原与南丝路要道，骑兵机动性强，能够在农耕世界与草原世界之间充当中介；王族出自慕容鲜卑，具有较强的政治凝聚力与区域号召力。
strengths.en: Tuyuhun’s strengths lay in controlling the Qinghai plateau and key southern Silk Road corridors, fielding highly mobile cavalry, and acting as an intermediary between the agrarian and steppe worlds; the Murong Xianbei royal lineage also provided notable political cohesion and regional prestige.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是人口与资源总量有限，行政制度较为松散，对部族忠诚和首领合作依赖较大；一旦遭遇持续外压、商路受阻或内部继承争议，国家整合力容易下降。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited population and resource depth, a relatively loose administrative structure, and heavy reliance on tribal loyalty and noble cooperation; under sustained external pressure, disrupted trade, or succession disputes, state cohesion could weaken quickly.

outlook.zh: 476年前后吐谷浑整体处于稳定且具有扩张余地的阶段，只要能维持商道控制并避免被北方草原强权或中原北朝持续压制，仍可在青海—河湟—西域交界长期保持重要地位。
outlook.en: Around 476 Tuyuhun was generally stable and still had room to expand; if it could maintain control over trade routes and avoid prolonged domination by northern steppe powers or the northern Chinese states, it could continue to hold an important position across the Qinghai–Hehuang–Inner Asian frontier for a long period.

description.zh: 吐谷浑控制青海与丝路南道要冲，是中国西北与中亚之间的重要政权。
description.en: Tuyuhun controlled Qinghai and key corridors of the southern Silk Road, linking northwest China and Central Asia.

governmentForm.zh: 部落联盟式游牧政体，在柔然宗主压力与北魏招抚之间维持高度分散的酋邦结构
governmentForm.en: A tribal confederational nomadic polity, highly decentralized and balancing between Rouran overlordship and Northern Wei patronage

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部落与牧营为基本单位，上层为贵族酋长及其亲族，中层为自由牧民与战士，下层包括附属部众、俘虏与奴婢
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, tribes, and pastoral camps, with noble chieftains and their kin at the top, free herders and warriors in the middle, and dependent followers, captives, and slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 各大部落首领、氏族贵族、战功显赫的骑兵首领，以及与柔然或北魏往来密切的中介贵族
rulingClass.en: Major tribal chiefs, clan nobility, distinguished mounted war leaders, and intermediary elites tied to the Rouran or Northern Wei

succession.zh: 以部落内部推举、家族世袭与军事声望相结合，联盟层面的领袖地位常因战争、分裂或外部支持而变动
succession.en: Succession combined internal tribal election, hereditary claims, and military prestige, while confederational leadership often shifted through war, factionalism, or external backing

structure.zh: 高车并非统一帝国，而是由多个车轮高大的游牧部落组成的松散联盟；重大事务由强势酋长、部落长老和军事首领协商决定，对外常以临时盟主代表全体或大部行动
structure.en: The Gaoche were not a centralized empire but a loose federation of wagon-using nomadic tribes; major affairs were settled by powerful chiefs, tribal elders, and military leaders, while external action was often represented by a temporary paramount leader

function.zh: 协调诸部关系、商议迁徙、战争、贡赋与对柔然或北魏的外交立场
function.en: Coordinated intertribal relations and deliberated migration, war, tribute, and diplomatic posture toward the Rouran or Northern Wei

function.zh: 管理各自部众、征集骑兵、维持草场秩序并处理部落间纠纷
function.en: Managed tribal followers, mustered cavalry, maintained pasture order, and handled disputes among groups

function.zh: 负责与柔然、北魏及邻近绿洲地区联络、议和、通商和递送人质或贡物
function.en: Handled communication, truces, trade, and the transfer of hostages or tribute with the Rouran, Northern Wei, and nearby oasis regions

function.zh: 监督牲畜分配、贡品征集、战利品分配及贵族营帐后勤
function.en: Oversaw livestock allocation, tribute collection, distribution of spoils, and logistics for noble encampments

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依附于部落与氏族首领，按季节迁徙路线和草场范围组织，不设固定州县；绿洲边缘或贸易节点可能设临时营地首领
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on tribal and clan chiefs, organized by seasonal migration routes and pasture zones rather than fixed districts; temporary camp leaders could appear near oasis edges or trade nodes

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，强调血亲责任、赔偿、牲畜所有权、婚姻交换和对盗窃、叛离、私斗的惩治；重大案件由酋长与长老裁断
legalSystem.en: The legal system was primarily customary, stressing kin responsibility, compensation, livestock ownership, marriage exchange, and punishment for theft, desertion, and private feuding; major cases were judged by chiefs and elders

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一定额税制，主要通过牲畜贡纳、战利品分成、对附属小部的勒索式供给，以及从与北魏、柔然往来中获得赏赐与互市利益
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform fixed tax system; resources came chiefly from livestock tribute, shares of war booty, exactions from subordinate groups, and rewards and market gains from dealings with the Northern Wei and Rouran

religion.zh: 以草原萨满信仰、祖先崇拜、天神观念和自然灵崇拜为主，个别部众受北方佛教传播或周边胡汉宗教习俗影响
religion.en: Religion centered on steppe shamanism, ancestor veneration, sky-god beliefs, and reverence for natural spirits, though some groups were influenced by Buddhism and neighboring Inner Asian and Chinese practices

philosophy.zh: 无成文哲学传统，价值观重勇武、忠诚、部族荣誉、长老经验和对天命与草场秩序的敬畏
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophical tradition; core values emphasized martial prowess, loyalty, tribal honor, elder wisdom, and respect for heavenly mandate and pastoral order

writingSystem.zh: 本部缺乏稳定自有文字，外交与贸易中常借用鲜卑、汉文或其他中亚书写传统，由通译和外来书吏处理文书
writingSystem.en: The Gaoche lacked a stable indigenous writing system and often relied on Xianbei, Chinese, or other Inner Asian written traditions in diplomacy and trade, using interpreters and foreign scribes

culturalAchievements.zh: 以高轮车营、牧歌传统、骑射技艺、皮毛与毡帐工艺、口头史诗和灵活的大范围季节迁徙组织见长
culturalAchievements.en: Their cultural achievements included high-wheeled wagon camp culture, pastoral song traditions, mounted archery, felt and fur craftsmanship, oral epic memory, and highly adaptive long-range seasonal migration systems

languageFamily.zh: 可能属早期突厥语相关草原语言群，内部方言差异较大，并夹杂鲜卑、蒙古高原与西域接触语成分
languageFamily.en: They likely spoke an early Turkic-related steppe language group, with considerable internal dialect variation and elements from Xianbei, Mongolian steppe, and Inner Asian contact languages

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 高车经济以畜牧业为绝对核心，财富主要体现在马、牛、羊、驼、皮毛与可移动家庭资产上。总体货币化程度低，但通过与北魏边市、柔然宗主关系及西域绿洲交换形成可观的物资流通。
gdpDescription.en: The Gaoche economy was overwhelmingly pastoral, with wealth concentrated in horses, cattle, sheep, camels, furs, and mobile household assets. Monetization was low, but meaningful circulation existed through Northern Wei frontier markets, relations with the Rouran, and exchange with Inner Asian oases.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧畜牧、马匹繁育、羊毛与皮革加工、毡帐制造、狩猎、战利品掠夺、边境互市运输
mainIndustries.en: Nomadic pastoralism, horse breeding, wool and leather processing, felt-tent production, hunting, raiding for spoils, and transport for frontier exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牛羊、皮毛、毡毯、乳制品、奴隶、角骨制品，换取谷物、铁器、丝织品、茶前代替饮料、奢侈品与兵器
tradeGoods.en: Horses, cattle, sheep, furs, felt textiles, dairy products, slaves, and horn or bone goods were exchanged for grain, iron tools, silk, beverage substitutes, luxury items, and weapons

name.zh: 以物易物兼用外来钱币

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显，贵族家庭拥有大量马群、羊群、战车和附属人口，普通牧户则以维持迁徙、越冬和婚姻交换所需牲畜为主
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal: noble families controlled large horse and sheep herds, wagons, and dependents, while ordinary herding households mainly held enough livestock to sustain migration, winter survival, and marriage exchange

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部落控制草场和牲畜为核心的游牧再分配经济，辅以掠夺、贡纳、婚姻交换和边市贸易
economicSystem.en: A pastoral redistributive economy centered on tribal control of pasture and livestock, supplemented by raiding, tribute, marriage exchange, and frontier market trade

source.zh: 牲畜贡纳与附属部供给
source.en: Livestock tribute and subordinate tribal contributions
Raiding and shares of war booty
Rewards from the Northern Wei or Rouran

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在固定国库，财富分散保存在强大酋长的牲群、毡帐储藏、金属器、外来丝织品和俘获财物中；危机时可迅速转移但也容易因内部分裂而流失
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed state treasury; wealth was dispersed among powerful chiefs in herds, tent stores, metal goods, imported silks, and captured valuables. It was highly mobile in crisis but also vulnerable to fragmentation

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持部众忠诚和战争动员为中心，优先将收入转化为馈赠、宴飨、婚姻联盟和军事资源，而非长期积储
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining follower loyalty and military mobilization, prioritizing gift distribution, feasting, marriage alliances, and war resources over long-term accumulation

description.zh: 高车武力核心，依靠机动性、复合弓、骑射与分散袭扰作战，适于草原追击和快速撤离
description.en: The core of Gaoche power, relying on mobility, composite bows, mounted archery, and dispersed harassment tactics suited to steppe pursuit and rapid withdrawal

description.zh: 由贵族亲兵和精锐战士组成，装备较好的甲胄、长矛和刀剑，用于突击、护卫酋长和决定性冲锋
description.en: Composed of noble retainers and elite warriors with better armor, spears, and blades, used for charges, chieftain protection, and decisive attacks

description.zh: 多在守营、护送车队、保卫牲群和近距离冲突中使用，战斗力低于骑兵但对维持部落运作重要
description.en: Used mainly to guard camps, escort wagons, protect herds, and fight in close local engagements; less formidable than cavalry but essential to tribal operations

commanderInChief.zh: 阿伏至罗等盟主级酋长

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、角骨箭、皮甲、部分铁甲、长矛、马镫早期形式或稳固骑乘技术、高轮车营和草原侦察通讯为主
technology.en: Military technology centered on the composite bow, horn-and-bone arrows, leather armor, some iron armor, spears, early stirrup-related or otherwise stable riding techniques, high-wheeled wagon camps, and steppe scouting communications

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自柔然的压制与征发、联盟内部诸部叛离，以及北魏在漠南和边塞地区的招抚与军事干预；在西部还需警惕与西域绿洲和其他草原集团的冲突
threats.en: Major threats came from Rouran domination and demands, internal defections among the tribes, and Northern Wei intervention and incorporation along the southern steppe frontier; in the west there was also danger of conflict with oasis polities and rival steppe groups

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于蒙古草原、阿尔泰周边及北疆通道地带，多以季节性迁徙营地形式存在，难以形成稳定统计；人口中相当比例具备骑乘和放牧能力
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the Mongolian steppe, the Altai zone, and corridors of northern Xinjiang, mostly in seasonal migrating camps rather than stable settlements; a significant share possessed riding and herding skills

socialClasses.zh: 社会层级包括部落贵族、酋长亲兵与随从、自由牧民、工匠与通译、附庸部众、俘虏和奴婢；女性在家庭财产管理、营地组织和婚姻联盟中具有重要作用
socialClasses.en: Social strata included tribal nobility, chieftain retainers, free herders, artisans and interpreters, subordinate followers, captives, and slaves; women played important roles in household property management, camp organization, and marriage alliance building

allies.zh: 无稳定盟友，部分部落在特定时期依附或联合北魏以抗衡柔然，其他部落则被迫追随柔然；与西域若干绿洲势力存在机会性合作
allies.en: There were no permanent allies; some tribes aligned with the Northern Wei at times to counter the Rouran, while others were compelled to follow the Rouran; opportunistic cooperation also existed with certain oasis powers

enemies.zh: 主要对手为柔然统治集团，同时也与争夺草场的其他草原部众及个别边塞守军发生冲突
enemies.en: The principal adversary was the Rouran ruling power, along with other steppe groups competing for pastures and occasional frontier garrisons

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以生存和机动平衡为目标，在臣属、反叛、投附和互市之间反复转换，力求通过分散结盟避免被单一强权完全控制
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at survival through flexible balance, shifting repeatedly among submission, revolt, alignment, and trade in order to avoid complete domination by any single great power

era.zh: 古代晚期草原铁器时代
era.en: Late Antique Steppe Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟游牧马政、复合弓骑射体系、高轮车迁营、毡帐保温工艺、远距离草场利用和草原口头情报传递网络
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature nomadic horse management, a composite-bow mounted warfare system, high-wheeled wagon mobility, insulated felt-tent craft, long-distance pasture exploitation, and oral intelligence networks across the steppe

strengths.zh: 机动性强，适应严酷草原环境，骑射传统成熟，社会组织能迅速集结作战，且在柔然与北魏之间具有外交回旋空间
strengths.en: Its strengths were high mobility, strong adaptation to the harsh steppe environment, mature mounted archery traditions, social structures capable of rapid mobilization, and diplomatic room to maneuver between the Rouran and Northern Wei

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏稳固中央权力、固定财政和定居后勤基础，易受严寒、疫病、牲畜灾损和内部离散影响，对外部强权的赏赐和市场依赖明显
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the lack of firm central authority, fixed fiscal institutions, and settled logistical bases; the tribes were vulnerable to severe winters, disease, livestock losses, and internal fragmentation, and depended significantly on outside rewards and markets

outlook.zh: 476年前后，高车诸部仍将作为草原政治的重要力量存在，但短期内难以形成持久统一国家。其前途取决于能否摆脱柔然压制、整合诸部，并利用北魏与西域局势扩展自主空间。
outlook.en: Around 476 CE, the Gaoche tribes would remain an important force in steppe politics, but were unlikely in the short term to form a durable unified state. Their future depended on escaping Rouran domination, integrating the tribes, and exploiting Northern Wei and Inner Asian conditions to widen their autonomy.

description.zh: 高车诸部活跃于蒙古草原与新疆北缘，受柔然与北魏双重影响。
description.en: The Gaoche tribes were active across the Mongolian steppe and northern Xinjiang under both Rouran and Northern Wei influence.

governmentForm.zh: 以郁久闾氏可汗为核心的草原部落联盟政体
governmentForm.en: A steppe tribal confederation centered on the khagan of the Yujiulü clan

socialStructure.zh: 社会以游牧部落为基本单位，分为可汗王族、贵族首领、附属部众、工匠与俘获人口；政治关系依附于血缘、婚姻、军事追随和贡赋网络。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around nomadic tribes, divided into the khagan's royal clan, aristocratic chiefs, dependent tribal followers, artisans, and captive populations; political ties rested on kinship, marriage, military followership, and tribute networks.

rulingClass.zh: 郁久闾王族及其下属部落贵族、军政首领
rulingClass.en: The Yujiulü royal clan together with subordinate tribal nobles and military-political chiefs

succession.zh: 名义上由郁久闾氏王族内部继承，通常由贵族会议、实力平衡与军功共同决定，兄终弟及与父子相承并存。
succession.en: In principle succession remained within the Yujiulü royal clan, but in practice it was shaped by aristocratic consensus, power balance, and military prestige; both fraternal succession and father-to-son inheritance occurred.

structure.zh: 中央权力围绕可汗幕府展开，下辖左右翼部众、宗王、部落酋长与受封将领；对定居边地与附属部落则通过监领、质子和贡赋关系实施间接统治。
structure.en: Central authority revolved around the khagan's mobile court, under which stood left and right wing tribal groupings, royal princes, tribal chieftains, and appointed commanders; sedentary frontier zones and subordinate tribes were ruled indirectly through supervisors, hostages, and tribute obligations.

function.zh: 处理军政决策、部落调度、使节接待与王族事务
function.en: Handled military-political decisions, tribal coordination, reception of envoys, and royal clan affairs

function.zh: 征收附属部族贡物，登记对北魏贸易所得与战利品分配
function.en: Collected tribute from subordinate tribes and recorded distributions of trade gains from Northern Wei and war booty

name.en: Military Orders and Tribal Muster Office

function.zh: 传达可汗军令，统计骑兵、畜群与战时动员能力
function.en: Issued the khagan's military orders and kept counts of cavalry, herds, and wartime mobilization capacity

function.zh: 管理与北魏、吐谷浑、高车及西域势力的外交和互市
function.en: Managed diplomacy and exchange markets with Northern Wei, Tuyuhun, Gaoche, and Inner Asian powers

name.en: Royal Camp Crafts and Logistics Office

function.zh: 负责王庭车辆、毡帐、马具、武器修造与畜产供给
function.en: Oversaw wagons, felt tents, harnesses, weapon repair, and livestock supply for the royal camp

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依赖部落首领、宗王和受封大人。草原腹地几乎无固定文官系统，服属部落按畜群、骑兵与贡物承担义务；边缘定居地区则由代理人和军事监护者管控。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on tribal chiefs, royal princes, and enfeoffed magnates. In the steppe core there was little fixed civil bureaucracy; subordinate tribes owed livestock, cavalry, and tribute, while peripheral sedentary zones were controlled through agents and military overseers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以草原习惯法为主，强调王命、部落惯例、赔偿、血亲责任与军纪；叛乱、违命和逃散常受严厉惩处，普通纠纷多由首领仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on steppe customary practice, emphasizing royal command, tribal precedent, compensation, kin responsibility, and military discipline; rebellion, disobedience, and desertion were punished harshly, while ordinary disputes were usually arbitrated by chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制并非定额田税，而是以部落贡赋、牲畜征发、战利品分配、对外勒索和边贸收益为核心；附属集团需贡献马匹、羊群、皮革、毡毯和兵员。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system was not a fixed land tax but was centered on tribal tribute, livestock levies, distribution of booty, external exactions, and frontier trade revenue; subordinate groups contributed horses, sheep, hides, felt, and manpower.

religion.zh: 以草原萨满信仰、天神崇拜、祖先祭祀和自然灵信仰为主，同时在与北魏和西域接触中吸收部分佛教与其他外来宗教观念。
religion.en: Religion was primarily steppe shamanism, sky-god worship, ancestor rites, and reverence for natural spirits, while some Buddhist and other foreign religious ideas entered through contact with Northern Wei and Inner Asia.

philosophy.zh: 没有成体系的成文哲学传统，政治伦理重视勇武、忠诚、王族正统、盟誓与部落秩序；与中原接触使部分统治层理解礼制、封号与王朝政治语言。
philosophy.en: There was no fully systematized written philosophical tradition; political values stressed valor, loyalty, royal legitimacy, oath-keeping, and tribal order, while contact with China exposed parts of the ruling elite to ritual hierarchy, titulature, and dynastic political language.

writingSystem.zh: 柔然自身缺乏广泛使用的成熟本族文字，政务与外交多借助口传、记号以及与汉地交往时使用汉文文书。
writingSystem.en: The Rouran lacked a widely used mature native script of their own; administration and diplomacy relied on oral transmission, marks or tallies, and Chinese written documents in dealings with the Chinese states.

culturalAchievements.zh: 柔然在政治史上的重要成就在于发展出“可汗”这一具有广泛影响的草原至尊称号，并建立覆盖蒙古高原的大型游牧联盟；其骑射文化、毡帐生活、金属饰物、马具与草原礼仪对后世突厥—蒙古传统影响深远。
culturalAchievements.en: The Rouran's major cultural achievement in political history was the development and prestige of the title 'Khagan' as a supreme steppe rulership term, along with the creation of a large nomadic confederation spanning Mongolia; their mounted-archery culture, felt-tent life, metal ornaments, horse gear, and steppe ceremonial traditions influenced later Turkic-Mongolian patterns.

languageFamily.zh: 语言归属存在争议，通常视为蒙古高原游牧语群之一，可能与早期蒙古语或相关草原语言有联系。
languageFamily.en: Their language affiliation is debated, but it is generally treated as belonging to one of the nomadic language groupings of the Mongolian steppe, possibly related to early Mongolic or a connected Inner Asian language.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 柔然经济以畜牧业为核心，财富主要体现为马、羊、牛、驼等牲畜，以及对附属部落的贡赋、边境互市和军事掠夺所得。由于缺乏大规模农业与城市手工业，总量低于大型农耕帝国，但其机动财富与战时动员能力较强。
gdpDescription.en: The Rouran economy was centered on pastoralism, with wealth mainly embodied in horses, sheep, cattle, camels, and in tribute from subordinate tribes, frontier exchange, and military raiding. Lacking large-scale agriculture and urban crafts, its total output was below that of major agrarian empires, but its mobile wealth and wartime mobilization capacity were strong.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧畜牧、马匹繁育、皮革与毛毡加工、骨角木器制作、金属修造、边境贸易、贡赋征收
mainIndustries.en: Pastoral herding, horse breeding, leather and felt production, bone-horn-wood craftwork, metal repair and manufacture, frontier trade, and tribute extraction

tradeGoods.zh: 输出马匹、牲畜、皮毛、毡毯、奴隶和草原特产；输入丝绸、谷物、金属器、铁料、奢侈品和手工业品。
tradeGoods.en: They exported horses, livestock, furs, felt goods, slaves, and steppe products; they imported silk, grain, metalwares, iron, luxury goods, and manufactured items.

name.zh: 以物易物与金属称量并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由牲畜数量决定。普通牧户以羊群和少量马牛维持生计，贵族则拥有大规模畜群、随从、战马和来自附庸的贡物。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was determined chiefly by herd size. Ordinary pastoral households lived from sheep flocks and a limited number of horses and cattle, while nobles controlled large herds, retainers, warhorses, and tribute from dependents.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧家庭生产为基础、由部落与王庭再分配调节的贡赋—掠夺—互市混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribute-raiding-exchange economy based on pastoral household production and regulated by tribal and royal redistribution

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 对北魏和边境互市收益
source.en: Returns from trade with Northern Wei and frontier markets

source.zh: 王庭直属畜群与手工业

category.zh: 贵族赏赐与部落再分配

treasuryDescription.zh: 柔然国库并非固定城库，而是分散于王庭贵重物、金银、丝绸、牲畜和贵族掌握的再分配资源之中。其财富高度流动，便于迁徙，但也易因内乱和失利而迅速消耗。
treasuryDescription.en: The Rouran treasury was not a fixed urban hoard but was dispersed across royal valuables, precious metals, silk, livestock, and redistributive resources held by the court and nobles. This wealth was highly mobile and suited to migration, but it could also be rapidly depleted by internal conflict or military setbacks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王族权威和部落忠诚为核心，通过赏赐分配稳定贵族、以互市和威胁并用向北魏索取利益，并尽量保持可汗幕府收支平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving royal authority and tribal loyalty by stabilizing nobles through gift redistribution, extracting benefits from Northern Wei through a combination of trade and coercion, and keeping the khagan's court broadly in balance.

description.zh: 直属可汗的精锐骑兵，负责护卫王庭、执行突击和监督各部。
