languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族戈伊德尔支
languageFamily.en: Goidelic branch of the Celtic language family

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为核心，财富更多以牛群、土地使用权、附庸关系和宴饮再分配衡量，而非货币总量。跨海贸易规模不大但政治意义上升。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on subsistence agriculture and pastoralism, and wealth was measured more in cattle, land-use rights, client ties, and feast redistribution than in money. Overseas trade remained modest in scale but was increasing in political importance.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、谷物种植、乳制品加工、铁器锻造、木工、皮革加工、船只建造与手工织造
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, grain cultivation, dairy processing, ironworking, woodworking, leatherworking, boatbuilding, and household textile production

tradeGoods.zh: 牛、皮革、羊毛、奴隶、盐渍食品、猎犬、金属制品、磨石，以及输入的葡萄酒、玻璃器、饰物和部分地中海奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, hides, wool, slaves, preserved foods, hunting dogs, metal goods, and quernstones, with imports including wine, glassware, ornaments, and some Mediterranean luxuries

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为牛群数量、耕地与牧场使用权、房舍、亲族支持以及携带武器与首饰等可移动财产
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was chiefly expressed through cattle holdings, rights to arable and pasture land, dwellings, kin support, and movable property such as weapons and ornaments

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族控制土地、畜群财富、附庸义务和礼物再分配为基础的部族经济，货币化程度较低，但海上交换和教会网络正在促进更广泛流通
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on kin-controlled land, cattle wealth, client obligations, and gift redistribution, with low monetization but growing circulation encouraged by maritime exchange and ecclesiastical networks

source.en: Port dues, ferry charges, and trade shares

source.zh: 战争战利品与袭掠所得
王室地产与手工业收入

category.zh: 王室宴饮与馈赠再分配

category.en: Support for religious and learned specialists

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓国库并非集中统一金库，而是分散于各王室据点、牛群、谷仓、金属器物、船只和可供再分配的奢侈品之中。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a centralized state vault but was dispersed among royal sites, cattle herds, granaries, metalwork, boats, and prestige goods available for redistribution.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持王权威望与联盟稳定，通过定期宴饮、赠礼和保护贸易航线来巩固忠诚，同时避免过度榨取自由民。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving royal prestige and alliance stability by sustaining feasts, gift-giving, and protection of trade routes while avoiding excessive burdens on free households.

name.en: Royal household troops and warrior retinues

description.zh: 由国王直属战士、贵族扈从和精选勇士组成，是机动性最强的常备核心力量
description.en: Composed of the king's direct warriors, noble retainers, and selected champions, this was the most mobile core standing force

description.zh: 主要由自由民征召，装备长矛、短剑、盾牌和少量投射武器，适于地方冲突、伏击与防御
description.en: Raised mainly from free commoners, they were equipped with spears, short blades, shields, and some missile weapons, suitable for local warfare, ambush, and defense

description.zh: 利用轻便船只在爱尔兰海两岸行动，执行袭掠、护航、联络和快速登陆任务
description.en: Using light vessels, these forces operated on both sides of the Irish Sea for raiding, escorting, liaison, and rapid landing operations

commanderInChief.en: Each local king commanded his own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制长矛、剑、盾、皮甲或简易护具、轻型船只和山地湿地作战经验为主，缺乏晚期罗马式攻城器械与重装建制
technology.en: Military technology relied on iron spears, swords, shields, leather or simple protective gear, light boats, and experience in fighting across hills and wetlands, lacking late Roman siege machinery and heavy formations

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近盖尔王国间竞争、不列颠西部与皮克特地区冲突、海上袭掠报复，以及罗马撤退后不列颠权力真空引发的跨海争霸
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry among neighboring Gaelic kingdoms, conflict with western British and Pictish regions, retaliatory sea raids, and cross-sea competition created by the post-Roman power vacuum in Britain

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于乡村农庄、环形堡、湖上居址周边与小型王室中心，整体密度较低，沿海与东部海峡联系地区更活跃。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across rural farmsteads, ringforts, crannog zones, and small royal centers, with generally low density and greater activity in coastal and Irish Sea contact regions.

name.zh: 阿马克地区早期宗教聚落
name.en: Early ecclesiastical settlement of Armagh region

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王族与贵族、战士贵人、法学家与诗人、神职人员、自由农牧民、依附佃户、工匠与奴隶。社会身份具有明确等级，赔偿标准和政治权利随身份而异。
socialClasses.en: Main social strata included royal and noble families, warrior elites, jurists and poets, clergy, free farming and pastoral households, dependent tenants, craftsmen, and slaves. Status hierarchy was explicit, and legal compensation and political rights varied by rank.

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系；通常与不列颠西部的盖尔社群、部分不列颠凯尔特政权及通婚对象保持临时联盟
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; temporary alliances were commonly maintained with Gaelic communities in western Britain, some Brittonic Celtic polities, and marriage partners

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级宿敌；敌对对象多为竞争性的盖尔小王国、部分皮克特势力、海峡对岸争地政权及袭掠对手
enemies.en: There was no single state-level archenemy; opponents were usually rival Gaelic petty kingdoms, some Pictish forces, competing polities across the sea, and raiding adversaries

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持海上通道、拓展婚姻与附庸网络、争夺不列颠西部影响力及获取贸易与战利品为核心，呈现灵活而高度地方化的特点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining sea-lanes, expanding marriage and client networks, competing for influence in western Britain, and acquiring trade and booty, in a flexible and highly localized manner.

era.zh: 古代晚期至后罗马早期边缘海洋社会
era.en: Late Antique to early post-Roman maritime frontier society

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度包括欧甘铭文、环形堡与乡村防御形态、轻型渡海船只、成熟的口传法律记忆体系，以及随基督教传播而来的拉丁书写与教会组织雏形。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and developments included Ogham inscriptions, ringfort-based rural defensive patterns, light seagoing craft, a mature orally preserved legal system, and early forms of Latin literacy and church organization arriving with Christianity.

strengths.zh: 优势在于强韧的亲族网络、适应海洋环境的机动能力、灵活的政治联盟、稳固的口传法律传统，以及在罗马秩序瓦解后扩大的跨海活动空间。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in resilient kin networks, maritime mobility, flexible political alliances, a durable oral legal tradition, and expanding room for cross-sea activity after the collapse of Roman order.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治高度分裂、缺乏统一财政与官僚体系、城市和书写能力薄弱、军事动员规模有限，且内部继承争斗频繁。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, the absence of unified finance and bureaucracy, weak urban and literate infrastructure, limited large-scale military mobilization, and frequent succession disputes.

outlook.zh: 476年前后的盖尔世界总体稳定，并因罗马撤退后的不列颠形势而获得新机遇。未来最可能的发展是王权整合缓慢推进、基督教网络加深、爱尔兰海联系更紧密，但统一国家短期内仍不现实。
outlook.en: Around 476, the Gaelic world was broadly stable and gained new opportunities from conditions in post-Roman Britain. The most likely trajectory was gradual strengthening of kingship, deeper Christian networks, and tighter Irish Sea connections, though a unified state remained unlikely in the near term.

description.zh: 爱尔兰海两岸的盖尔人部族政权逐步形成更稳定的跨海联系。
description.en: Gaelic polities on both sides of the Irish Sea were developing more stable transmarine ties.

name.zh: 玛雅文明（卡拉克穆尔城邦）

ruler.zh: 卡安王朝圣主（具体名号未能确证）
ruler.en: Kaan dynasty Holy Lord (specific personal name uncertain)

governmentForm.zh: 玛雅世袭王权城邦，依靠王室、贵族与附庸网络统治
governmentForm.en: Hereditary Maya monarchic city-state governed through the royal court, nobles, and a network of subordinate allies

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化，最高为王室与祭司贵族，其下为书记官、武士与地方首领，再下为工匠、商人、农民与劳役人口
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly hierarchical, with the royal family and priestly nobility at the top, followed by scribes, warriors, and local lords, then artisans, merchants, farmers, and laboring dependents

rulingClass.zh: 王室卡安王朝、宫廷贵族、祭司集团与受封地方领主
rulingClass.en: The Kaan royal house, court nobility, priestly elites, and invested local lords

succession.zh: 以父系王室世袭为主，通常由王子继位，但亦受宫廷贵族、联姻与军事声望影响
succession.en: Succession was primarily patrilineal within the royal house, usually passing to a prince, though court nobles, marriage alliances, and military prestige also influenced the outcome

structure.zh: 卡拉克穆尔的统治核心为圣主宫廷，结合宗教仪式权威、军事领导与对附属城邦的宗主权；政务依靠王室近臣、书记官、祭司和地方贵族分层执行
structure.en: Calakmul’s government centered on the Holy Lord’s court, combining ritual authority, military leadership, and overlordship over subordinate polities; administration was carried out by royal retainers, scribes, priests, and local nobles in layered fashion

function.zh: 管理宫廷日常、接见使节、安排仪式与王室财产
function.en: Managed daily court affairs, received envoys, organized ceremonies, and oversaw royal property

function.zh: 负责碑铭书写、王朝记述、历法计算与贡赋记录
function.en: Handled inscriptions, dynastic records, calendrical calculations, and tribute accounting

function.zh: 主持祭祀、管理神庙财产、维持宗教合法性
function.en: Conducted rituals, managed temple holdings, and maintained religious legitimacy

function.zh: 征收玉米、可可、棉布、玉石与劳役，并管理储藏设施
function.en: Collected maize, cacao, cotton cloth, jade, and labor obligations, and managed storage facilities

function.zh: 组织战事、协调盟友、监督附庸忠诚与战俘处置
function.en: Organized warfare, coordinated allies, monitored vassal loyalty, and managed captives

localAdmin.zh: 核心城区由王室直接控制，周边聚落由地方贵族、宗族首领和附庸统治者管理，向卡拉克穆尔提供贡品、劳役与军事支持
localAdmin.en: The urban core was directly controlled by the royal court, while surrounding settlements were managed by local nobles, lineage heads, and subordinate rulers who supplied tribute, labor, and military support to Calakmul

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断与宗教规范为基础，重视土地、贡赋、婚姻、贵族义务及战争俘虏的处置；判决多由地方领主与宫廷权威共同执行
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, royal judgment, and religious norms, with emphasis on land, tribute, marriage, noble obligations, and the treatment of war captives; rulings were enforced by both local lords and court authority

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自实物贡赋与劳役，而非统一货币税；附庸与乡村家庭按身份和土地负担上缴玉米、豆类、棉布、可可、盐、羽毛及公共工程劳役
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied mainly on in-kind tribute and labor service rather than a uniform monetary tax; vassals and rural households owed maize, beans, cotton cloth, cacao, salt, feathers, and corvée for public works according to status and landholding

religion.zh: 多神信仰为主，崇拜雨神、玉米神、太阳神、战争与祖先神灵，王权被视为沟通人神世界的神圣中介
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, venerating the rain god, maize god, sun deity, war powers, and ancestral spirits, with kingship seen as a sacred mediator between human and divine realms

philosophy.zh: 思想核心在于宇宙循环、时间神圣性、祖先合法性与王权仪式责任，政治、天文与宗教几乎不可分割
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core emphasized cosmic cycles, the sacred nature of time, ancestral legitimacy, and the ritual obligations of kingship, with politics, astronomy, and religion deeply intertwined

writingSystem.zh: 使用成熟的玛雅象形文字体系，结合音节与语词符号，广泛用于碑铭、祭祀纪年和王朝宣传
writingSystem.en: A mature Maya hieroglyphic writing system was used, combining syllabic and logographic signs for inscriptions, ritual dating, and dynastic propaganda

culturalAchievements.zh: 卡拉克穆尔在古典期玛雅世界中以纪念碑、金字塔建筑、复杂历法、天文观测、精美陶器与宫廷艺术著称，并不断扩展其王朝记忆与盟友体系
culturalAchievements.en: In the Classic Maya world, Calakmul was noted for its monuments, pyramid architecture, complex calendrics, astronomical observation, fine ceramics, and courtly art, while continuously extending its dynastic memory and alliance system

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 卡拉克穆尔经济以农业剩余、附庸贡赋、手工业生产与区域交换为基础。对外贸易活跃于玛雅低地内部及与中部美洲的接触圈，但经济结构仍以宫廷再分配和农村生产为主
gdpDescription.en: Calakmul’s economy rested on agricultural surplus, tribute from subordinate polities, craft production, and regional exchange. External trade was active within the Maya lowlands and wider Mesoamerican contact zones, but the overall structure remained centered on court redistribution and rural production

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、可可生产、棉纺织、陶器制作、石器与玉器加工、建筑营造、盐与林产采集
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cacao production, cotton textiles, pottery making, stone and jade working, construction, and the extraction of salt and forest products

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、棉布、玉石、黑曜石、羽毛、陶器、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、海贝与仪式性奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cacao, cotton cloth, jade, obsidian, feathers, pottery, salt, honey, wax, shell goods, and ritual luxury items

name.zh: 实物贡赋与可可交换体系
name.en: In-kind tribute and cacao exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、住房、储粮、纺织品、工具与亲族网络；贵族则控制更大土地、劳役、奢侈品与祭祀资源
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed through access to farmland, housing, stored grain, textiles, tools, and kinship ties; nobles controlled larger lands, labor obligations, luxury goods, and ritual resources

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per worker per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农村家庭生产为基础、由宫廷与贵族主导再分配的贡赋经济，并辅以区域市场交换和长距离奢侈品贸易
economicSystem.en: A tribute economy based on rural household production and elite court redistribution, supplemented by regional market exchange and long-distance trade in prestige goods

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 贸易、奢侈品与市场征收
source.en: Trade, prestige goods, and market levies

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

category.zh: 行政记录与神庙人员供养
category.en: Administrative record-keeping and temple personnel support

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非现代金属货币储备，而是由可可、布匹、玉石、羽毛、陶器、储粮和王室奢侈品构成的综合财富，另有用于重大祭典和战争的动员能力
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a modern metallic reserve but a composite stock of cacao, cloth, jade, feathers, pottery, stored grain, and royal prestige goods, along with the ability to mobilize resources for major rituals and wars

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持宫廷威望、宗教仪式、公共工程和对附庸的控制；在稳定时期倾向积累贡物与劳役余力，以备建筑、战争和旱季风险
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining court prestige, religious ceremonies, public works, and control over subordinate polities; in stable periods it favored accumulating tribute and labor reserves for construction, warfare, and dry-season risk

description.zh: 负责宫廷护卫、仪式展示与精锐近战作战，由王室直接统辖
description.en: Responsible for palace protection, ceremonial display, and elite close combat under direct royal control

name.en: Noble Warriors and Local War Bands

description.zh: 由贵族家族和附庸首领召集，是战争中的主体兵力，擅长伏击、突袭与俘获敌方贵族
description.en: Raised by noble families and subordinate lords, they formed the main field force and specialized in ambush, raids, and the capture of enemy elites

description.zh: 使用标枪、投石器、长矛与木盾，承担侦察、骚扰和支援任务
description.en: Used javelins, slings, spears, and wooden shields, serving in scouting, skirmishing, and support roles

technology.zh: 军备以石刃、黑曜石武器、木矛、标枪、投石器、棉甲与木盾为主，缺乏冶铁和大型畜力运输，但在丛林地形机动、围困威慑和象征性战争方面经验丰富
technology.en: Military equipment centered on stone-bladed and obsidian weapons, wooden spears, javelins, slings, quilted cotton armor, and wooden shields, lacking ironworking and large draft transport but highly experienced in jungle mobility, siege intimidation, and symbolic warfare

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自蒂卡尔等竞争性玛雅强权、忠诚不稳的附庸城邦、区域贸易路线争夺以及因王朝声望下降引发的联盟瓦解
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Maya great powers such as Tikal, wavering subordinate city-states, competition over regional trade routes, and alliance collapse if dynastic prestige weakened

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括卡拉克穆尔核心城区、周边农业聚落及受其直接控制的近郊住民。作为古典期低地玛雅强权之一，其政治影响力超过单一城市人口规模
populationDescription.en: The population included the urban core of Calakmul, surrounding agricultural settlements, and nearby residents under direct control. As one of the major Classic lowland Maya powers, its political influence extended beyond the population of the central city alone

socialClasses.zh: 王室与高贵族、祭司与书记官、武士阶层、工匠与商人、普通农民、劳役者与战俘构成主要社会层级
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were the royal house and high nobility, priests and scribes, warrior elites, artisans and merchants, common farmers, laborers, and war captives

allies.zh: 卡拉克穆尔通过婚姻、军事保护和宗主关系维系一系列玛雅低地盟友与附庸城邦，并积极扩展蛇王朝影响网络
allies.en: Calakmul maintained a series of Maya lowland allies and vassal city-states through marriage, military protection, and overlordship, actively expanding the Kaan or Snake dynasty influence network

enemies.zh: 最主要对手是蒂卡尔及其潜在盟友，此外还有可能反复倒向敌对阵营的边缘城邦
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was Tikal and its potential allies, along with frontier polities that might repeatedly shift into hostile camps

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以建立广泛联盟网、扶植附庸统治者、通过仪式与婚姻确认等级秩序，并在必要时以战争重塑地区权力平衡
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to build a broad alliance network, sponsor subordinate rulers, confirm hierarchy through ritual and marriage, and, when necessary, use warfare to reshape the regional balance of power

era.zh: 中美洲古典期
era.en: Mesoamerican Classic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的长纪历与历法计算、精细玛雅文字、石灰灰泥与金字塔建筑技术、蓄水设施、堤道系统、精美陶器与玉石工艺
keyInnovations.en: Mature Long Count and calendrical calculation, refined Maya writing, lime-plaster and pyramid construction techniques, water storage systems, causeways, and sophisticated ceramic and jade craftsmanship

strengths.zh: 卡拉克穆尔拥有强大的王朝合法性、成熟的宫廷文化、广泛的盟友与附庸网络，以及在古典期低地玛雅政治中的重要战略地位
strengths.en: Calakmul possessed strong dynastic legitimacy, an advanced court culture, a broad network of allies and vassals, and an important strategic position in Classic lowland Maya politics

weaknesses.zh: 其统治高度依赖王室威望与附庸忠诚，农业基础受生态与降水波动影响明显，缺乏统一货币和重装技术，长期争霸容易消耗资源
weaknesses.en: Its rule depended heavily on royal prestige and vassal loyalty, its agricultural base was sensitive to ecology and rainfall fluctuation, it lacked unified currency and heavy military technology, and prolonged rivalry could drain resources

outlook.zh: 476年时卡拉克穆尔总体稳定且处于扩张联盟阶段，若能持续压制蒂卡尔并维持附庸忠诚，其地区霸权有望增强；但一旦王朝声望受挫，联盟体系也可能迅速松动
outlook.en: In 476 CE Calakmul was broadly stable and in a phase of alliance expansion; if it could continue to check Tikal and preserve vassal loyalty, its regional hegemony might strengthen, but any serious blow to dynastic prestige could loosen the alliance system quickly

description.zh: 卡拉克穆尔是玛雅低地另一强大政治中心，正在扩展其同盟网络。
description.en: Calakmul was another powerful Maya political center, expanding its alliance network.

governmentForm.zh: 以都市贵族、祭司集团与军事领袖共同主导的城邦政体，可能带有寡头统治色彩，而非明确的世袭君主制
governmentForm.en: A city-state polity led jointly by urban nobles, priestly institutions, and military leaders, likely oligarchic in character rather than a clearly defined hereditary monarchy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由统治精英、祭司、武士、行政管理者、工匠、商人、农民与附属劳役人口构成，并包含来自中部高原与外地社区的多族群居民
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of ruling elites, priests, warriors, administrators, artisans, merchants, farmers, and dependent laborers, including multiethnic communities from the central highlands and beyond

rulingClass.zh: 城市宫殿复合体中的贵族家族、祭司集团及军事首领
rulingClass.en: Noble lineages, priestly groups, and military leaders based in the city's palace compounds

succession.zh: 继承方式不明，可能由精英家族内部继任、祭司认可与军政集团协商共同决定
succession.en: Succession is unclear, likely determined through elite family inheritance, priestly sanction, and negotiation among military and political factions

structure.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎在476年已处衰落阶段，但仍保持大型都市的组织能力。政权核心可能由宗教仪式中心、宫殿行政单位、工匠管理网络与军事首领联合构成，统治周边谷地聚落并维持对贸易线路的有限控制。
structure.en: By 476 CE Teotihuacan was in decline, yet it still retained the organizational capacity of a large urban center. Its political core was likely formed by ritual institutions, palace-based administrative units, craft-management networks, and military leaders, ruling surrounding valley settlements and maintaining limited control over trade routes.

function.zh: 主持国家祭祀、天象与历法安排、神庙维护及合法性仪式
function.en: Oversaw state ritual, astronomical and calendrical scheduling, temple maintenance, and ceremonies of legitimacy

function.zh: 征收周边聚落贡物，管理玉米、豆类、辣椒、龙舌兰制品及手工业物资储备
function.en: Collected tribute from surrounding settlements and managed reserves of maize, beans, chili, maguey products, and craft materials

name.zh: 工坊与黑曜石管理机构

function.zh: 监管黑曜石加工、陶器生产、石器和装饰品工坊，并协调原料分配
function.en: Supervised obsidian processing, pottery production, stone-tool and ornament workshops, and coordinated raw material distribution

function.zh: 管理城市交换活动、商旅住宿区、外来社群联系以及奢侈品流通
function.en: Managed urban exchange, merchant lodging districts, relations with foreign communities, and circulation of luxury goods

function.zh: 负责城内秩序、道路巡逻、要地守备与对周边威胁的军事动员
function.en: Handled internal order, road patrols, guarding strategic sites, and military mobilization against surrounding threats

localAdmin.zh: 城市以大型住宅复合体和功能区为基本管理单元，周边村镇通过附属首领、贡赋代理人与宗教联系纳入统治体系
localAdmin.en: The city was administered through large apartment compounds and functional districts, while surrounding towns and villages were integrated through subordinate local leaders, tribute agents, and religious ties

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典留存，秩序主要依赖习惯法、宗教禁忌、精英裁断与社区层级管理；盗窃、叛乱和扰乱贡赋秩序可能受到严厉惩罚
legalSystem.en: No written legal code survives; order relied mainly on customary law, religious taboo, elite judgment, and hierarchical community discipline; theft, rebellion, and disruption of tribute likely faced harsh punishment

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括粮食、纺织品、陶器、黑曜石制品、建筑劳役与祭祀供品，而非统一货币税制
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base relied chiefly on tribute in kind and labor service, including foodstuffs, textiles, pottery, obsidian goods, construction labor, and ritual offerings, rather than a uniform monetary tax system

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以雨神、羽蛇神、风暴与生育相关神祇、山岳与水源崇拜为核心，并重视人祭、献血和公共仪式
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on rain deities, the Feathered Serpent, storm and fertility gods, and sacred mountains and springs, with strong emphasis on sacrifice, bloodletting, and public ritual

philosophy.zh: 其思想体系以宇宙秩序、历法循环、祭祀义务和城市神圣空间观念为核心，不存在如欧亚古典文明那样独立成文的哲学传统
philosophy.en: Its intellectual system centered on cosmic order, calendrical cycles, ritual obligation, and the sacred layout of urban space, rather than a separate written philosophical tradition like those of classical Eurasia

writingSystem.zh: 尚未形成成熟的完整表音文字，主要使用象征图像、壁画符号、地名记号和仪式图像传达权威与宗教信息
writingSystem.en: A fully developed phonetic writing system was absent; authority and religious meaning were conveyed mainly through symbolic imagery, mural signs, place glyphs, and ritual iconography

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括美洲古代最宏大的城市规划之一、太阳金字塔与月亮金字塔、亡灵大道、精致壁画艺术、标准化公寓式住宅布局以及高度发达的黑曜石手工业
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included one of the grandest urban plans in the ancient Americas, the Pyramids of the Sun and Moon, the Avenue of the Dead, refined mural art, standardized apartment-compound housing, and a highly developed obsidian industry

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言未能确定，可能涉及中部墨西哥多种语言群体，学界常推测与乌托-阿兹特克语系、托托纳克语系或米赫-索克语系相关，但无定论
languageFamily.en: The principal language remains uncertain, possibly involving several central Mexican language groups; scholars have proposed links with Uto-Aztecan, Totonacan, or Mixe-Zoquean families, but no consensus exists

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎476年的经济规模已明显低于其鼎盛期，但仍是中部墨西哥最重要的都市经济体之一。经济以农业剩余、黑曜石工艺、地方交换、区域贡赋和有限的长途贸易为支柱，城市影响力仍大于其实时政治控制范围。
gdpDescription.en: Teotihuacan's economy in 476 CE was far below its peak, but it remained one of the most important urban economies in central Mexico. It rested on agricultural surplus, obsidian craft production, local exchange, regional tribute, and limited long-distance trade, with influence extending beyond its immediate political control.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、黑曜石开采与加工、陶器制造、建筑、纺织、石雕、宗教祭祀相关手工业与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, obsidian extraction and processing, pottery manufacture, construction, textiles, stone carving, ritual crafts, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石刀片、陶器、石制装饰物、玉米、豆类、辣椒、龙舌兰制品、羽毛、贝壳、玉石与其他奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian blades, pottery, stone ornaments, maize, beans, chili, maguey products, feathers, shells, jade, and other luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 财富差距显著。精英控制大型住宅、仪式器物和进口奢侈品；普通家庭以住房、工具、衣物、储粮和有限手工业资产为主要财富形式。
householdWealth.en: Wealth inequality was marked. Elites controlled large residences, ritual goods, and imported luxuries, while ordinary households held wealth mainly in housing, tools, clothing, stored grain, and modest craft assets.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以国家引导的贡赋分配、家庭生产、专业工坊和市场交换并存的再分配经济，货币化程度很低
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy combining state-directed tribute allocation, household production, specialized workshops, and market exchange, with very low monetization

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非以铸币形式储存，而主要表现为粮仓储备、工艺原料、黑曜石成品、祭祀物资和可再分配的贡品。衰落时期储备能力已不及鼎盛时代。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not stored as coinage but mainly as granary stocks, craft raw materials, finished obsidian goods, ritual supplies, and redistributable tribute. Its reserve capacity had diminished from the city's peak.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持祭祀中心、都市秩序和对附属地区的剩余提取为核心；在衰落背景下，政权更依赖强制性贡赋和存量消耗
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining the ritual center, urban order, and extraction of surplus from dependent areas; under decline, the regime relied more heavily on compulsory tribute and depletion of stored reserves

description.zh: 负责神庙区、主要大道、仓储区和政治核心的防卫，装备以投枪、矛、木盾和黑曜石刃武器为主
description.en: Defended temple precincts, the main avenue, storage areas, and the political core, using darts, spears, wooden shields, and obsidian-edged weapons

description.zh: 由周边聚落与附属社区在危机时提供，战斗力不一，主要承担外围防御与示威性出征
description.en: Provided by surrounding settlements and dependent communities in times of crisis, uneven in quality, mainly used for outer defense and demonstrative campaigns

description.zh: 与宗教象征和政治威望密切相关的小规模精锐力量，可能参与护卫、镇压和对外军事行动
description.en: A small elite force closely tied to religious symbolism and political prestige, likely used for guard duty, suppression, and external military action

commanderInChief.zh: 不详的统治集团或最高军事祭司
commanderInChief.en: Unknown ruling council or supreme military priest

technology.zh: 军事技术属于前铁器时代美洲体系，使用投枪器、石刃武器、棉甲或厚织物护具、木盾与要地工事；无骑兵、铁器和攻城机械
technology.en: Military technology belonged to the pre-iron Mesoamerican tradition, employing atlatls, stone-edged weapons, cotton or padded textile armor, wooden shields, and fortified positions; there was no cavalry, iron weaponry, or siege machinery

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部权力分裂、附属地区离心、谷地竞争城邦、供应网络衰退以及可能的社会动乱，而非跨大陆外敌
threats.en: The chief threats came from internal political fragmentation, centrifugal tendencies in dependent regions, competing valley polities, decline of supply networks, and possible social unrest rather than transcontinental enemies

populationDescription.zh: 476年的特奥蒂瓦坎虽已衰落，人口仍约10万，是当时美洲最大的城市之一，但较其早期鼎盛阶段明显收缩，部分城区可能已废弃或人口稀疏。
populationDescription.en: In 476 CE, Teotihuacan, though in decline, still had about 100,000 inhabitants, making it one of the largest cities in the Americas, though significantly reduced from its earlier height, with some districts likely abandoned or thinly populated.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司、贵族与军事精英；中层为专业工匠、商人、管理者；下层为普通农民、劳役者和附属社区居民。外来族群社区在城市中占有重要地位。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included priests, nobles, and military elites; middle groups included specialized artisans, merchants, and administrators; lower strata consisted of common farmers, laborers, and residents of dependent communities. Foreign ethnic enclaves also held an important place in the city.

allies.zh: 无可确认的正式盟友；与中部墨西哥若干附属或友好聚落维持松散联系，并与部分玛雅地区保有残余交流传统
allies.en: No firmly attested formal allies; it maintained loose ties with some subordinate or friendly settlements in central Mexico and residual traditions of contact with parts of the Maya world

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括争夺谷地资源与贸易线路的中部墨西哥地方势力，以及脱离控制的附属聚落
enemies.en: Potential enemies included local central Mexican powers competing for valley resources and trade routes, as well as dependent settlements slipping from control

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持宗教威望、保住黑曜石贸易影响力、稳定周边贡赋网络和防止进一步解体为主，已不具备早期大规模对外扩张能力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving religious prestige, sustaining influence in the obsidian trade, stabilizing nearby tribute networks, and preventing further disintegration, no longer capable of the large-scale outward expansion of earlier centuries

era.zh: 中部美洲古典期晚期
era.en: Late Mesoamerican Classic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模网格化城市规划、公寓式复合住宅、黑曜石标准化刀片生产、壁画颜料技术、金字塔平台建筑和复杂的仪式景观设计
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale grid urban planning, apartment-compound housing, standardized obsidian blade production, mural pigment techniques, pyramid-platform construction, and complex ritual landscape design

strengths.zh: 仍拥有巨大的城市规模、深厚的宗教声望、成熟的工匠体系、黑曜石资源优势和对周边文化的持续影响力
strengths.en: It still possessed great urban scale, deep religious prestige, mature craft systems, advantages in obsidian resources, and continuing influence over surrounding cultures

weaknesses.zh: 政治中心权威衰退、经济网络收缩、附属地区控制下降、财政紧张、社会分裂加剧，且缺乏稳定而清晰的王朝继承结构
weaknesses.en: The authority of the political center was declining, economic networks were contracting, control over dependent regions was weakening, finances were strained, social divisions were deepening, and it lacked a stable and clearly defined dynastic succession structure

outlook.zh: 前景偏向持续衰落。特奥蒂瓦坎在476年仍是中部墨西哥的重要象征性都市，但其作为主导政治中心的时代已接近终结，未来更可能向碎片化和区域权力替代发展。
outlook.en: The outlook points toward continued decline. In 476 CE Teotihuacan remained a major symbolic metropolis of central Mexico, but its age as a dominant political center was nearing its end, with fragmentation and replacement by regional powers increasingly likely.

description.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎虽已过鼎盛，但仍是中部墨西哥最具影响力的都市中心之一。
description.en: Though past its peak, Teotihuacan remained one of the most influential urban centers of central Mexico.

governmentForm.zh: 古典期玛雅世袭神权君主制城邦，统治核心由王室、贵族家族、祭司与军事首领共同构成
governmentForm.en: A Classic Maya hereditary sacral-monarchical city-state, with rule centered on the royal house, noble lineages, priests, and military leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度分层，上层为王室与贵族，中层为祭司、书记员、工匠和商人，下层为普通农民、劳役者与附庸社群
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the royal family and nobles at the top, priests, scribes, artisans, and merchants in the middle, and common farmers, laborers, and subordinate communities below

rulingClass.zh: 王室贵族、地方领主、祭司集团与高级武士
rulingClass.en: Royal nobles, local lords, priestly elites, and senior warriors

succession.zh: 通常实行王室父系世袭，但亦可能因战争、摄政、婚姻联盟或王族内争而出现继承复杂化
succession.en: Succession was generally patrilineal within the royal dynasty, though warfare, regencies, marriage alliances, and dynastic disputes could complicate accession

structure.zh: 蒂卡尔由圣主居于顶端，以下为王族、贵族议政圈、祭司书记官僚以及附属聚落首领。政权通过宫廷网络、祭祀中心、贡赋体系和对周边附庸的宗主权来维系统治
structure.en: Tikal was headed by the Holy Lord, beneath whom stood the royal kin, a noble governing circle, priest-scribal administrators, and leaders of subordinate settlements. Authority was maintained through court networks, ritual centers, tribute arrangements, and overlordship over nearby vassals

function.zh: 负责君主近侍、宫廷礼仪、王室地产管理、接待使节与重大决策协调
function.en: Handled royal attendants, court ceremony, royal estate management, reception of envoys, and coordination of major decisions

function.zh: 征收粮食、可可、棉布、羽毛、玉石与劳役，管理仓储并调配给宫廷、工匠与军队
function.en: Collected food, cacao, cotton cloth, feathers, jade, and labor dues, managed storage, and distributed resources to the court, artisans, and military forces

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维护神庙礼仪、解释历法并为战争、加冕和建筑工程择日
function.en: Conducted rituals, maintained temple observances, interpreted the calendar, and selected auspicious dates for war, accession, and construction

function.zh: 记录王朝谱系、战争胜利、朝贡关系和天文历法，并制作碑铭、彩绘器与行政记录
function.en: Recorded dynastic genealogy, military victories, tributary relations, and astronomical-calendrical information, and produced inscriptions, painted vessels, and administrative records

function.zh: 组织王室武士、协调附庸城镇军役、巡察边界并处理对外战争与臣属关系
function.en: Organized royal warriors, coordinated military service from subordinate towns, patrolled frontiers, and managed warfare and overlord-vassal relations

localAdmin.zh: 蒂卡尔直接控制核心城区、周边农田与若干卫星聚落；更远地区多由附庸领主在宗主关系下自治，按期贡献物资、劳役和军事支持
localAdmin.en: Tikal directly controlled its urban core, surrounding farmland, and several satellite settlements; more distant areas were often governed by vassal lords under overlordship, providing periodic goods, labor, and military support

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断与宗教规范为基础。贵族和附庸纠纷多由宫廷裁决，普通民众则受地方首领与社区规范约束，惩罚可包括赔偿、劳役、羞辱仪式或处决
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal judgment, and religious norms. Noble and vassal disputes were often settled by the court, while commoners were subject to local chiefs and community standards; punishments could include compensation, labor service, ritual humiliation, or execution

taxationSystem.zh: 没有近代意义上的统一货币税制，财政主要依靠实物贡赋、强制劳役、附庸进献、战争掠获和王室地产产出
taxationSystem.en: There was no modern-style uniform monetary tax system; public finance relied mainly on tribute in kind, compulsory labor, vassal offerings, war booty, and the output of royal estates

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以天体、雨神、玉米神、祖先神与王权神圣性为核心，祭祀、献血与节庆维系宇宙秩序
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on celestial bodies, the rain god, the maize god, ancestor cults, and the sacred nature of kingship, with ritual, bloodletting, and festivals sustaining cosmic order

philosophy.zh: 思想重在宇宙循环、时间神圣性、祖先合法性与统治者作为天地中介者的角色，并通过历法与神话解释政治秩序
philosophy.en: Its worldview emphasized cosmic cycles, the sacred nature of time, ancestral legitimacy, and the ruler’s role as mediator between heaven and earth, using calendars and myth to explain political order

writingSystem.zh: 玛雅象形文字体系，结合表意与音节成分，用于碑铭、陶器、宫廷文书与历法记录
writingSystem.en: The Maya hieroglyphic writing system, combining logographic and syllabic elements, was used on monuments, ceramics, court documents, and calendrical records

culturalAchievements.zh: 在476年前后的古典期，蒂卡尔以金字塔神庙、王宫建筑群、纪年碑铭、复杂历法、天文观测和精致彩绘陶器著称，是低地玛雅最重要的文化与政治中心之一
culturalAchievements.en: In the Classic period around 476, Tikal was renowned for its pyramid temples, palace complexes, dated monuments, complex calendars, astronomical observations, and finely painted ceramics, making it one of the foremost cultural and political centers of the Maya lowlands

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系；宫廷环境可能使用与低地玛雅相关的书写与礼仪语言，民间则存在多种地方玛雅语变体
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family; the court likely used a lowland Maya-related written and ceremonial language, while various local Mayan vernaculars were spoken among the population

gdpDescription.zh: 蒂卡尔经济以玉米农业为基础，辅以豆类、南瓜、可可、林产、手工业和区域贸易。其“国内生产”主要反映农业剩余、建筑动员能力、宫廷消费和附庸贡赋，而非货币化市场总量
gdpDescription.en: Tikal’s economy was based on maize agriculture, supplemented by beans, squash, cacao, forest products, craft production, and regional trade. Its effective “GDP” chiefly reflects agricultural surplus, construction mobilization, court consumption, and vassal tribute rather than a monetized market total

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、园圃栽培、石灰与石材开采、建筑营造、陶器制作、玉石与贝饰加工、羽毛与纺织品生产、可可与奢侈品交换
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, garden cultivation, lime and stone extraction, construction, pottery making, jade and shell ornament production, featherwork and textile production, and exchange in cacao and luxury goods

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、玉石、黑曜石、燧石器、盐、棉布、羽毛、陶器、贝壳、蜂蜜、染料和祭祀用品
tradeGoods.en: Cacao, jade, obsidian, flint tools, salt, cotton cloth, feathers, pottery, shells, honey, dyes, and ritual goods

name.zh: 无统一铸币；以可可豆与布匹等作为交换标准
name.en: No unified coinage; cacao beans and cloth served as common media of exchange

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以耕地使用权、房舍、农具、储粮、家族劳力与少量装饰品体现；贵族则拥有更大地产、附庸人口、礼器与奢侈品网络
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was expressed through access to farmland, dwellings, tools, stored food, family labor, and a few ornaments; nobles possessed larger estates, dependent populations, ritual objects, and luxury-goods networks

economicSystem.zh: 以农业自给、宫廷再分配、附庸贡赋和精英控制的长途交换相结合的古典玛雅再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A Classic Maya redistributive economy combining agricultural subsistence, court redistribution, vassal tribute, and elite-controlled long-distance exchange

source.zh: 贸易、市场征收与战争掠获
source.en: Trade, market exactions, and war booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中金银储备，而是由仓储粮食、可可、布匹、仪式用品、玉器、羽饰和可调动劳役能力构成的复合性储备
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a centralized stock of precious metal, but a composite reserve of stored grain, cacao, cloth, ritual goods, jade objects, feathers, and mobilizable labor capacity

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持宫廷威望、神庙祭祀、工程建设和战争能力；在稳定时期倾向积累仓储并通过馈赠巩固附庸关系
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining court prestige, temple ritual, construction, and war-making capacity; in stable periods it tended toward storage accumulation and the use of gifts to reinforce vassal ties

description.zh: 由王室直接控制的精锐战士，负责宫廷护卫、仪式展示和关键战斗
description.en: Elite fighters directly controlled by the royal house, responsible for palace security, ceremonial display, and decisive combat roles

description.zh: 由贵族领主和地方社群动员的主力，装备长矛、投枪、木盾、石刃武器与棉甲
description.en: The main force raised by noble lords and local communities, equipped with spears, javelins, wooden shields, stone-bladed weapons, and padded cotton armor

description.zh: 来自卫星聚落和附庸首领的支援兵力，多用于边境巡逻、突袭和扩大战时兵源
description.en: Support troops from satellite settlements and vassal chiefs, used for frontier patrol, raiding, and wartime expansion of manpower

commanderInChief.en: The Holy Lord as supreme commander

technology.zh: 军事技术以步兵作战、棉甲、防盾、石刃与黑曜石刃兵器、投枪和攻取仪式中心为主，不具备冶铁武器、骑兵或大型攻城机械
technology.en: Military technology was centered on infantry warfare, padded cotton armor, shields, stone and obsidian-edged weapons, javelins, and the seizure of ritual centers, without iron weaponry, cavalry, or large siege engines

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自低地玛雅 rival 城邦竞争，尤其是卡拉克穆尔体系形成前后的区域争霸、附庸倒向敌对势力，以及贸易路线和农业腹地安全问题；与特奥蒂瓦坎的间接政治文化联系仍具影响
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry with other lowland Maya city-states, especially regional competition in the era before and around the rise of the Calakmul system, the risk of vassals shifting allegiance, and insecurity along trade routes and agricultural hinterlands; indirect political-cultural connections with Teotihuacan still mattered

populationDescription.zh: 此人口大致包括蒂卡尔核心城区、近郊居住群与受其直接支配的周边农业社群，并不等于整个更广泛政治网络的人口总和
populationDescription.en: This population estimate broadly includes Tikal’s urban core, nearby residential groups, and surrounding agricultural communities under its direct control, rather than the full population of its wider political network

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为王室、贵族领主、祭司与书记、专业工匠与商人、普通农民、劳役人口和附庸社群；社会地位与血统、祭祀职能、军事功绩和对宫廷的接近程度密切相关
socialClasses.en: Society may be broadly divided into the royal house, noble lords, priests and scribes, specialized artisans and merchants, common farmers, laboring populations, and subordinate communities; status was closely tied to lineage, ritual function, military achievement, and proximity to the court

allies.zh: 以周边附庸聚落、婚姻盟友和临时同盟城邦为主；对外关系高度流动，联盟常随王朝婚姻、战争胜负与宗主权变化而调整
allies.en: Its allies consisted mainly of nearby vassal settlements, marriage partners, and temporary allied city-states; external relations were highly fluid, with alliances shifting according to dynastic marriage, military outcomes, and changes in overlordship

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者为竞争性玛雅城邦与反叛附庸；在5世纪的区域格局中，蒂卡尔需持续防范低地政治中心间的争霸
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were rival Maya city-states and rebellious vassals; in the fifth-century regional order, Tikal had to guard continuously against competition among lowland political centers

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护区域霸权、控制朝贡网络、通过婚姻与礼物稳固关系、并在必要时以战争惩罚敌对城邦为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining regional hegemony, controlling tributary networks, reinforcing ties through marriage and gift exchange, and punishing hostile city-states by war when necessary

era.zh: 中美洲古典期
era.en: Mesoamerican Classic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的玛雅文字与长纪历系统、精确历法计算、纪年碑铭传统、金字塔神庙工程、灰泥建筑技术、蓄水设施与复杂城市仪式景观
keyInnovations.en: Mature Maya writing and Long Count calendrical systems, precise calendar calculation, dated monument traditions, pyramid-temple engineering, stucco architectural techniques, water management facilities, and complex ceremonial urban landscapes

strengths.zh: 蒂卡尔拥有强大的王朝合法性、成熟的宫廷文化、卓越的建筑与书写传统、可观的人口基础，以及在低地玛雅政治网络中的重要号召力
strengths.en: Tikal possessed strong dynastic legitimacy, a mature court culture, distinguished architectural and scribal traditions, a substantial population base, and major influence within the lowland Maya political network

weaknesses.zh: 政权依赖个人君主威望、附庸忠诚和农业剩余；生态压力、战争失利、贸易受阻或王朝继承危机都可能迅速削弱其统治
weaknesses.en: The polity depended heavily on the prestige of individual rulers, vassal loyalty, and agricultural surplus; ecological stress, military defeat, disrupted trade, or dynastic succession crises could quickly weaken its rule

outlook.zh: 476年的蒂卡尔总体稳定且仍具扩张潜力，但未来数世纪其命运将取决于与其他玛雅强邦的争霸、对附庸的控制能力，以及对环境与人口压力的适应程度
outlook.en: In 476, Tikal was broadly stable and still had expansion potential, but in the coming centuries its fate would depend on rivalry with other major Maya powers, its ability to control vassals, and its adaptation to environmental and demographic pressures

description.zh: 提卡尔是古典期玛雅低地最重要的城邦之一。
description.en: Tikal was one of the leading Classic Maya city-states in the lowlands.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭神权君主制城邦，以王室、贵族宗族与祭祀权威共同支撑统治
governmentForm.en: Hereditary sacral monarchic city-state, with rule supported by the royal house, noble lineages, and ritual authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，上层为王室、贵族、祭司与高级书记官，中层为工匠、商人和地方首领，下层为农民、劳役者与附庸社群
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the royal family, nobles, priests, and high scribes at the top; artisans, merchants, and local headmen in the middle; and farmers, laborers, and dependent communities below

rulingClass.zh: 王室贵族、仪式精英与控制碑铭、历法和贡赋的宫廷官僚
rulingClass.en: Royal nobles, ritual elites, and court officials who controlled inscriptions, calendrics, and tribute

succession.zh: 通常由王朝内部父系世袭继承，继承合法性通过祖先崇拜、仪式表演和王室血统确认
succession.en: Usually patrilineal dynastic succession within the royal house, with legitimacy confirmed through ancestor veneration, ritual performance, and royal bloodline

structure.zh: 科潘由王宫核心直接统治，王权通过宫廷、祭祀机构、贵族住宅群及附属聚落首领向外延伸；政治中心兼具行政、宗教与司法功能
structure.en: Copán was ruled directly from the palace core, with royal authority extending outward through the court, ritual institutions, noble residential groups, and the headmen of subordinate settlements; the political center combined administrative, religious, and judicial functions

function.zh: 管理宫廷事务、贡赋分配、劳役征发和附庸首领联络
function.en: Managed court affairs, tribute allocation, labor levies, and relations with subordinate leaders

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、维护历法、择定纪念碑树立和王权神圣活动日期
function.en: Conducted religious ceremonies, maintained the calendar, and fixed dates for monument erection and sacred royal events

function.zh: 记录王朝谱系、战争、盟约和仪式事件，制作碑文与象形文字文献
function.en: Recorded dynastic genealogy, wars, alliances, and ritual events, and produced inscriptions and hieroglyphic texts

function.zh: 组织金字塔、神庙、广场、梯道、水利与石雕工程
function.en: Organized pyramids, temples, plazas, stairways, water management, and stone carving works

function.zh: 征收玉米、可可、纺织品、黑曜石、羽毛及其他贡物，并管理储藏
function.en: Collected maize, cacao, textiles, obsidian, feathers, and other tribute goods, and managed storage

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠附属聚落首领、贵族家族和宗族长者执行王命；核心谷地控制较强，外围地区则多通过宗主关系与婚姻联盟维系
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on subordinate settlement headmen, noble houses, and lineage elders to carry out royal commands; control was stronger in the core valley, while outer areas were maintained through overlordship and marriage alliances

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典留存，司法主要依据习惯法、王室裁断、赔偿原则与宗教誓约；严重罪行、政治叛逆和贡赋违抗由宫廷处理
legalSystem.en: No surviving formal law code is known; justice was based mainly on customary law, royal judgment, compensation principles, and sacred oath-taking; serious crimes, political rebellion, and refusal of tribute were handled by the court

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋和劳役为主，而非统一货币税制；农产、工艺品、建筑劳工和仪式供奉是主要征收形式
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based primarily on in-kind tribute and labor service rather than a uniform money tax; agricultural produce, crafted goods, construction labor, and ritual offerings were the main forms of extraction

religion.zh: 多神信仰与祖先崇拜并存，强调宇宙周期、神圣山水、王权通神及流血献祭；主要神祇体系与玉米、生育、雨水、太阳和冥界相关
religion.en: Polytheistic religion coexisted with ancestor veneration, emphasizing cosmic cycles, sacred landscape, the ruler’s communion with the gods, and bloodletting sacrifice; major deities were associated with maize, fertility, rain, the sun, and the underworld

philosophy.zh: 世界观建立在历法时间、宇宙层次、祖先记忆与王权神圣责任之上，重视周期循环而非线性历史观
philosophy.en: Its worldview rested on calendrical time, layered cosmology, ancestral memory, and the sacred obligations of kingship, valuing cyclical recurrence rather than a linear view of history

writingSystem.zh: 使用成熟的玛雅象形文字体系，结合音节符号与表意符号，用于碑铭、陶器、祭祀记述和王朝历史
writingSystem.en: It used the mature Maya hieroglyphic writing system, combining syllabic and logographic signs for inscriptions, ceramics, ritual records, and dynastic history

culturalAchievements.zh: 科潘以精美石碑、祭坛、象形文字长篇碑铭、复杂历法知识、天文观测传统和高度发展的宫殿—神庙建筑闻名，是古典期玛雅艺术与王朝纪念文化的重要中心
culturalAchievements.en: Copán was renowned for finely carved stelae, altars, long hieroglyphic inscriptions, sophisticated calendrical knowledge, traditions of astronomical observation, and highly developed palace-temple architecture, making it a major center of Classic Maya art and dynastic commemorative culture

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系，科潘地区可能主要使用乔尔—奇奥蒂支相关语言，同时宫廷书写采用超地方化的古典玛雅书面语传统
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family; the Copán region likely used a Ch’olan-related language, while court writing employed the supralocal tradition of Classic Maya written language

gdpDescription.zh: 科潘经济以农业剩余、王室再分配、工艺生产和区域交换网络为基础。总量不大，但对一座古典期玛雅城邦而言较为繁荣，尤其体现在纪念性建筑与艺术制作能力上。
gdpDescription.en: Copán’s economy was based on agricultural surplus, royal redistribution, craft production, and regional exchange networks. Its total scale was modest, but for a Classic Maya city-state it was relatively prosperous, especially in its ability to support monumental building and artistic production.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、可可与果园生产、石工与雕刻、陶器制造、纺织、羽饰加工、黑曜石与玉石制品、建筑营造
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cacao and orchard production, stoneworking and carving, pottery manufacture, textiles, featherwork, obsidian and jade goods, and construction

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、玉石、可可、盐、羽毛、陶器、纺织品、贝壳、雕刻石器和仪式用品
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, jade, cacao, salt, feathers, pottery, textiles, shells, carved stone goods, and ritual items

name.zh: 无统一铸币；以可可豆与实物计值
name.en: No unified coinage; value reckoned in cacao beans and goods

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、粮食储备、陶器、工具、纺织品和亲属网络中；贵族则集中拥有玉器、羽饰、精制器物和劳役支配权
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth consisted of land-use rights, grain stores, pottery, tools, textiles, and kin networks; nobles concentrated wealth in jade, featherwork, fine goods, and control over labor

economicSystem.zh: 农业基础上的宫廷再分配经济，结合市场式交换、附庸贡赋和精英控制的长距离贸易
economicSystem.en: A court-redistributive economy grounded in agriculture, combined with market-style exchange, subordinate tribute, and elite-controlled long-distance trade

source.en: Labor levies and public works service

category.zh: 公共建筑与纪念碑营建

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非现代意义上的现金储备，而是宫廷仓储、祭器、纺织品、可可、玉石、羽饰与可动员劳役能力的综合积累
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a modern cash reserve but a combined accumulation of palace stores, ritual goods, textiles, cacao, jade, featherwork, and mobilizable labor capacity

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重心在于维持王权威望与宗教秩序，通过周期性仪式、建筑工程和再分配来巩固忠诚；平衡依赖农业稳定和附庸持续进贡
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining royal prestige and sacred order, using periodic rituals, building projects, and redistribution to reinforce loyalty; balance depended on stable agriculture and continued tribute from subordinates

description.zh: 负责王宫、宗教中心和统治者安全的精锐武装，由忠于王室的战士组成
description.en: Elite troops responsible for the palace, ritual center, and the ruler’s safety, composed of warriors loyal to the royal house

description.zh: 由贵族随从和核心居民中的战斗人员构成，用于突袭、俘虏作战和近距离冲突
description.en: Made up of noble retainers and fighting men from the core population, used for raids, captive-taking warfare, and close combat

description.zh: 来自周边聚落和附庸社群的临时征召力量，主要承担守备、搬运和战时支援
description.en: Temporary levies from surrounding settlements and dependent communities, mainly serving in defense, transport, and wartime support

technology.zh: 以黑曜石刀剑、投矛器、长矛、木盾、棉甲和石刃武器为主，无冶铁和骑兵；作战重视地形、仪式与俘虏获取
technology.en: Weapons centered on obsidian-edged swords, atlatls, spears, wooden shields, quilted cotton armor, and stone blades, with no iron metallurgy or cavalry; warfare emphasized terrain, ritual, and taking captives

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近玛雅政体的竞争、附庸离心、贸易路线争夺及区域声望战争，而非大规模帝国入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Maya polities, centrifugal tendencies among subordinates, competition over trade routes, and regional prestige warfare rather than large imperial invasions

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括科潘核心仪式中心、周边谷地农业居民以及受其影响的附属聚落。作为东南玛雅边疆重镇，其人口规模在区域内相当可观。
populationDescription.en: The population included the Copán ceremonial core, agricultural residents of the surrounding valley, and dependent settlements within its orbit. As a major center on the southeastern Maya frontier, its population was substantial by regional standards.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与高级贵族、祭司和书记官、地方首领与专业工匠、普通农民、劳役者与附庸群体构成主要社会层次
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were the royal house and high nobility, priests and scribes, local headmen and specialist artisans, common farmers, and laboring dependent groups

allies.zh: 与部分周边玛雅城邦和附庸聚落保持婚姻、宗教和政治联盟，但联盟通常短暂且以王朝利益为核心
allies.en: It maintained marriage, religious, and political alliances with some neighboring Maya city-states and subordinate settlements, though such alliances were often temporary and centered on dynastic interest

enemies.zh: 主要对手是竞争性的玛雅政体，尤其是在洪都拉斯高地、危地马拉东部和更广泛低地网络中的 rival 王朝；也需防范附庸反叛
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were rival Maya polities, especially competing dynasties in the Honduran highlands, eastern Guatemala, and the wider lowland network; subordinate rebellion was also a concern

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以王朝联姻、仪式互访、碑铭宣示、附庸整合和有限战争并用，目标是维持科潘在东南边疆的文化与政治中心地位
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined dynastic marriage, ritual visits, inscriptional display, subordinate integration, and limited warfare, aiming to preserve Copán’s position as a cultural and political center on the southeastern frontier

era.zh: 美洲古典期玛雅时代
era.en: Classic Maya era in the Americas

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟象形文字、长纪年系统、复杂历法、精确石雕技术、纪念性建筑规划、灰泥装饰、天文观测和高强度热带农业管理
keyInnovations.en: Mature hieroglyphic writing, the Long Count system, complex calendrics, precise stone carving, monumental architectural planning, stucco decoration, astronomical observation, and intensive tropical agricultural management

strengths.zh: 科潘拥有强大的王朝合法性、发达的艺术与书写传统、稳定的农业基础、区域贸易联系和高水平纪念性建筑能力，是古典期玛雅世界的重要文化中心之一
strengths.en: Copán possessed strong dynastic legitimacy, advanced artistic and scribal traditions, a stable agricultural base, regional trade links, and a high capacity for monumental construction, making it one of the important cultural centers of the Classic Maya world

weaknesses.zh: 其政治整合依赖个人王权、附庸忠诚和农业生态平衡，面对长期干旱、人口压力、王朝断裂或外部竞争时较为脆弱
weaknesses.en: Its political integration depended heavily on personal kingship, subordinate loyalty, and agricultural ecological balance, making it vulnerable to prolonged drought, demographic pressure, dynastic rupture, or external competition

outlook.zh: 在476年左右，科潘总体稳定且仍具扩张和营建能力，短期前景良好；但从长期看，若环境压力和区域竞争加剧，其高度仪式化的王权结构可能面临挑战
outlook.en: Around 476 CE, Copán was broadly stable and still capable of expansion and construction, so its short-term outlook was favorable; in the longer term, however, intensified environmental stress and regional competition could challenge its highly ritualized royal order

description.zh: 科潘是玛雅东南边缘的重要礼仪与王权中心。
description.en: Copán was an important ceremonial and dynastic center on the southeastern Maya frontier.

name.zh: 蒙特阿尔班萨波特克政权

governmentForm.zh: 以王室与贵族为核心的萨波特克山地城邦政体，带有神权合法性与附属聚落网络
governmentForm.en: A Zapotec highland city-state centered on a royal and noble court, with sacred legitimacy and a network of subordinate settlements

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈明显等级化，顶层为王室、祭司与世袭贵族，其次为地方首领、武士与行政人员，再下为工匠、商人、农民与劳役人口；乡村社群仍维持亲族与地方共同体组织
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the royal family, priests, and hereditary nobles at the top; below them stood local chiefs, warriors, and administrators; beneath them were artisans, merchants, farmers, and laborers, while rural communities retained kin-based and local corporate organization

rulingClass.zh: 蒙特阿尔班王室、祭司集团与控制山谷土地和贡赋的世袭贵族
rulingClass.en: The Monte Albán royal house, priestly elite, and hereditary nobles who controlled valley lands and tribute

succession.zh: 大体为王室内部的世袭继承，可能结合贵族与祭司对继承人的认可；在晚期衰落阶段，地方贵族影响力上升，继承稳定性可能减弱
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary within the ruling line, likely requiring recognition by nobles and priests; in the late declining phase, powerful local elites probably gained influence, making succession less stable

structure.zh: 政府以蒙特阿尔班宫廷为核心，由统治者、宗教祭司、贵族议事圈和地方代理人构成。其控制方式主要通过仪式中心、婚姻联盟、贡赋征收和对周边村镇首领的间接支配来实现。
structure.en: Government was centered on the Monte Albán court and consisted of the ruler, religious priests, a circle of nobles, and local agents. Control was exercised mainly through the ceremonial center, marriage alliances, tribute collection, and indirect authority over surrounding town and village chiefs.

function.zh: 管理宫殿、王室土地、贵族接待、仪式筹备与宫廷命令传达
function.en: Managed the palace, royal lands, elite reception, ritual preparation, and transmission of court orders

function.zh: 征收玉米、豆类、纺织品、陶器、黑曜石器和劳役，负责储藏与再分配
function.en: Collected maize, beans, textiles, pottery, obsidian goods, and labor tribute, and oversaw storage and redistribution

function.zh: 主持神庙祭典、祖先崇拜、历法记录、占卜与王权神圣化
function.en: Conducted temple rites, ancestor worship, calendar keeping, divination, and sacral legitimation of rule

function.zh: 联络山谷及周边聚落首领，监督贡赋、调解纠纷并组织公共工程
function.en: Liaised with valley and nearby settlement chiefs, supervised tribute, mediated disputes, and organized public works

function.zh: 组织城邦守军、战士阶层、边地警戒和战时动员
function.en: Organized the city-state garrison, warrior class, frontier watch, and wartime mobilization

function.zh: 调配石匠、陶工、建筑劳役与道路、平台、墓葬和宗教建筑维修
function.en: Directed stonemasons, potters, construction labor, and maintenance of roads, platforms, tombs, and religious buildings

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠山谷村镇的世袭首领与蒙特阿尔班派遣或认可的代理人。中央对远离核心区的控制力较早期明显减弱，更像是一个仪式与政治中心对周边社区的松散网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on hereditary chiefs in valley towns and villages, along with agents appointed or recognized by Monte Albán. Central control over areas beyond the core had weakened significantly from earlier centuries, functioning more as a loose network around a ceremonial and political center.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系未形成成文法典，而是由习惯法、王室裁决、贵族仲裁与宗教规范共同构成。土地、婚姻、贡赋义务和人身冒犯等事务多由地方首领和祭司处理，重大案件由宫廷裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was not based on a formal written code but on customary law, royal judgment, noble arbitration, and religious norms. Matters involving land, marriage, tribute obligations, and personal offenses were usually handled by local chiefs and priests, while major cases went to the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要是实物贡赋与劳役，而非普遍货币税。附属社区按期缴纳粮食、纺织品、陶器、家禽、手工制品和建筑劳役，贵族与祭司往往享有减免或优先分配权。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base rested mainly on tribute in kind and labor service rather than general monetary taxation. Dependent communities periodically delivered food, textiles, pottery, poultry, craft goods, and construction labor, while nobles and priests often enjoyed exemptions or privileged access to redistribution.

religion.zh: 宗教为多神信仰，重视与雨、水、丰产、祖先和山地圣所相关的神灵崇拜。祭祀、墓葬礼仪、神庙平台和历法仪式继续维持政治合法性，尽管蒙特阿尔班晚期宗教影响范围已较早期收缩。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, emphasizing deities associated with rain, water, fertility, ancestors, and sacred mountains. Rituals, funerary ceremonies, temple platforms, and calendrical observances continued to sustain political legitimacy, though Monte Albán’s religious reach had narrowed from its earlier height.

philosophy.zh: 其思想传统并非抽象哲学体系，而是以宇宙循环、祖先权威、历法秩序、祭祀义务和统治者与神灵之间的中介关系为核心。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual tradition was not an abstract philosophical system but one centered on cosmic cycles, ancestral authority, calendrical order, ritual obligation, and the ruler’s mediating role between people and the divine.

writingSystem.zh: 使用萨波特克文字与历法记号，主要见于碑铭、墓葬、纪念性建筑与精英语境。到5世纪时，其公共铭文传统已不如早期活跃，但书写与符号记录仍具有礼仪和身份意义。
writingSystem.en: They used Zapotec writing and calendrical notation, chiefly seen in inscriptions, tombs, monuments, and elite contexts. By the 5th century, the public inscriptional tradition was less active than in earlier periods, but writing and symbolic record keeping still retained ritual and status significance.

culturalAchievements.zh: 蒙特阿尔班长期形成了宏大的平台式建筑、精致墓葬、石雕纪念物、彩绘与灰陶传统、历法记录和区域性的都市规划遗产。至476年，其文化成就更多体现为延续性的传统中心，而非新的扩张性创新中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Monte Albán had long developed monumental platform architecture, elaborate tombs, carved stone monuments, painted and gray-ware pottery traditions, calendrical records, and a regional urban planning legacy. By 476, its cultural achievement lay more in preserving an enduring traditional center than in generating a new expansionist florescence.

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系，萨波特克语支
languageFamily.en: Oto-Manguean language family, Zapotec branch

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地农业、地方手工业和区域交换为主，整体规模中等偏低。蒙特阿尔班仍是重要的山谷中心，但已不再像古典盛期那样主导广泛的外部贡赋网络，因此总产出更多依赖本地农产、工匠生产和与邻近地区的有限贸易。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on highland agriculture, local craft production, and regional exchange, with a modest overall scale. Monte Albán remained an important valley center, but it no longer dominated a wide external tribute network as in its Classic peak, so output depended more on local farming, artisan production, and limited trade with nearby regions.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜和龙舌兰等农业生产；陶器、纺织、石器加工、建筑维修、墓葬工艺和少量长途交换中介
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture in maize, beans, squash, and maguey; pottery, textiles, stone tool production, construction maintenance, tomb craftsmanship, and limited participation in longer-distance exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、纺织品、陶器、石器、壳饰、玉石类奢侈品、小规模黑曜石与地方手工制品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, textiles, pottery, stone tools, shell ornaments, jade-like prestige goods, small quantities of obsidian, and local manufactured crafts

name.zh: 无统一铸币，主要为贡赋与易货
name.en: No unified coinage, mainly tribute and barter

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、玉米储备、住房、陶器、纺织工具和亲族关系。精英财富则集中在土地控制、劳役调配权、墓葬随葬品和奢侈装饰品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed chiefly in access to farmland, maize stores, housing, pottery, textile tools, and kinship ties. Elite wealth was concentrated in land control, command over labor, tomb offerings, and prestige ornaments.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person/year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系以村社农业、自给生产、精英再分配和实物贡赋为核心，辅以区域市场交换。它不是市场资本型经济，而是由政治与宗教中心调动剩余产品的再分配体系。
economicSystem.en: The economic system centered on village agriculture, household subsistence, elite redistribution, and in-kind tribute, supplemented by regional market exchange. It was not a market-capital economy but a redistributive system in which political and religious centers mobilized surplus goods.

source.en: Labor levy and public works service

source.zh: 手工业与宫廷作坊产出
区域交换与附属首领献纳
source.en: Regional exchange and subordinate elite offerings

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是金银储备，而是仓储中的玉米、豆类、纺织品、陶器与可调配劳役能力。以银值换算仅为比较尺度。由于政治衰退与控制范围收缩，财政余裕有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly not of precious metal reserves but of stored maize, beans, textiles, pottery, and mobilizable labor capacity. The silver valuation is only a comparative measure. Because of political decline and shrinking control, fiscal slack was limited.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策趋向保守，重点在于维持宗教中心、王室体面和基本防务，同时通过再分配稳住附属社群。由于衰落中的权威下降，强行增加贡赋的空间有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, focused on maintaining the religious center, royal prestige, and basic defense, while using redistribution to stabilize dependent communities. Since authority was weakening in decline, there was limited scope for sharply increasing tribute demands.

description.zh: 常驻蒙特阿尔班及周边要点的核心守军，负责宫廷、仓储和宗教建筑防护
description.en: Core troops stationed at Monte Albán and nearby key points, responsible for guarding the court, storage facilities, and religious buildings

description.zh: 由贵族家族及其随从组成的战斗力量，兼具地位象征与实际作战功能
description.en: A fighting force drawn from noble families and their retainers, combining status display with practical combat roles

description.zh: 战时从周边聚落征集的农民兵，装备较简陋，主要用于守御、巡逻和短期集结
description.en: Peasant levies raised from surrounding settlements in wartime, lightly equipped and used mainly for defense, patrol, and short-term mobilization

commanderInChief.zh: 蒙特阿尔班统治者兼最高军事首领
commanderInChief.en: The ruler of Monte Albán as supreme military leader

technology.zh: 军事技术以传统中美洲武备为主，包括投掷武器、矛、棍棒武器、盾牌、棉甲或厚织物防护以及山地据点防御。476年的蒙特阿尔班军力明显偏向防御和仪式化武士传统，而非大规模远征能力。
technology.en: Military technology was based on traditional Mesoamerican arms, including projectile weapons, spears, club weapons, shields, cotton armor or thick textile protection, and hilltop defensive positions. By 476, Monte Albán’s forces were clearly oriented toward defense and ritualized warrior traditions rather than large-scale expeditionary capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自瓦哈卡地区内部竞争中心的崛起、附属聚落离心化、贸易路线控制权下降以及与米斯特克等邻近高地势力的摩擦，而非跨大陆敌人。
threats.en: The main threats came from the rise of rival centers within Oaxaca, centrifugal tendencies among dependent settlements, declining control over trade routes, and friction with neighboring highland powers such as Mixtec groups, rather than from any transoceanic enemies.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约4万，主要分布在瓦哈卡高地核心区及其附属村镇。与早期霸权时期相比，蒙特阿尔班中心人口与吸附能力均已下降，人口重心更趋分散于区域聚落网络。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 40,000, concentrated in the Oaxaca highland core and its dependent towns and villages. Compared with the earlier hegemonic period, Monte Albán’s central population and pull had declined, with demographic weight becoming more dispersed across the regional settlement network.

name.zh: 特拉科卢拉谷地聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室、祭司、世袭贵族、地方首领、武士、工匠、商贩、农民与劳役者。社会流动有限，政治权力与宗教权威多集中于少数血缘和地位群体。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included the royal family, priests, hereditary nobles, local chiefs, warriors, artisans, traders, farmers, and laborers. Social mobility was limited, and political power and religious authority were concentrated in a small number of status and kin groups.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的远距离正式盟邦；主要依靠与瓦哈卡山谷内部分地方首领、亲族贵族和附属社群维持关系
allies.en: There were no stable formal long-distance allies; diplomacy relied mainly on relations with local chiefs, kin-based nobles, and dependent communities within the Oaxaca valleys

enemies.zh: 主要对手是瓦哈卡地区内争夺影响力的邻近中心和可能扩张的高地族群，而非欧亚非诸文明
enemies.en: Its principal opponents were neighboring centers competing for influence within Oaxaca and potentially expanding highland groups, not the civilizations of Eurasia or Africa

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域生存和维持威望为目标，重视婚姻联系、贡赋关系、礼仪交流与有限军事威慑。476年的蒙特阿尔班已难以恢复早期广域霸权，更现实的目标是保住核心高地和宗教中心地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at regional survival and prestige maintenance, emphasizing marriage ties, tribute relations, ritual exchange, and limited military deterrence. By 476, Monte Albán was no longer capable of restoring its earlier broad hegemony; the realistic goal was to preserve the core highlands and its status as a sacred center.

era.zh: 中美洲古典期晚段向后古典过渡期
era.en: Late Classic to Early Postclassic transition in Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度遗产包括平台式石建筑、山地都市规划、灌溉与坡地农业经验、历法记录、墓葬营造和成熟的陶器与纺织工艺。到476年，更重要的是这些既有传统的持续运用，而不是全新突破。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional legacies included platform stone architecture, hilltop urban planning, irrigation and terraced farming experience, calendrical record keeping, tomb construction, and mature pottery and textile crafts. By 476, the greater significance lay in continued use of these established traditions rather than radical new breakthroughs.

strengths.zh: 优势在于蒙特阿尔班作为历史悠久的宗教与政治中心仍具象征权威；瓦哈卡高地农业基础稳固；社会组织、历法礼仪和工艺传统延续性强；山地地形有利防御。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in Monte Albán’s continuing symbolic authority as an ancient religious and political center; the solid agricultural base of the Oaxaca highlands; the durability of social organization, calendrical ritual, and craft traditions; and defensible mountain terrain.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于区域霸权衰退、附属聚落离心、财政和军力有限、城市中心人口下降，以及难以像早期那样整合广泛贸易和贡赋网络。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were declining regional hegemony, centrifugal subordinate settlements, limited fiscal and military resources, declining central urban population, and an inability to integrate broad trade and tribute networks as it had in earlier times.

outlook.zh: 前景总体偏弱。蒙特阿尔班在476年仍可作为具有历史威望的高地中心延续一段时间，但其主导地位已不可逆地减退，未来更可能让位于瓦哈卡地区新的政治格局与地方化权力结构。
outlook.en: The outlook was generally poor. Monte Albán in 476 could still persist for some time as a highland center of historical prestige, but its dominant position had declined irreversibly, and it was likely to give way to new political configurations and more localized power structures in Oaxaca.

description.zh: 瓦哈卡高地的萨波特克中心仍存，但其区域霸权已经衰退。
description.en: The Zapotec center in the Oaxaca highlands survived, though its regional hegemony had faded.

ruler.zh: 地方祭司首领与世俗酋长

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教仪式中心为核心的地方酋邦联盟，祭司与世俗首领共享权威
governmentForm.en: A decentralized confederation of local chiefdoms centered on ritual authority, with priestly and secular leaders sharing power

socialStructure.zh: 社会由地方精英家族、祭司、工匠、农民、渔民与劳役群体构成，权力依托血缘、灌溉控制与宗教威望分配
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of local elite lineages, priests, artisans, farmers, fishers, and laboring communities, with power distributed through kinship, irrigation control, and ritual prestige

rulingClass.zh: 地方祭司贵族、灌溉管理者与世袭酋长家族
rulingClass.en: Local priestly nobles, irrigation managers, and hereditary chiefly families

succession.zh: 通常在地方宗族内部世袭，但需获得精英家族、祭司团体与社区认可
succession.en: Usually hereditary within local lineages, but requiring recognition from elite families, priestly groups, and the community

structure.zh: 纳斯卡晚期并无统一国家官僚体系，而是由多个河谷聚落与仪式中心构成的分散政治网络。卡瓦奇虽已衰落，仍保有象征性宗教地位；各地首领通过祭祀、治水、劳役组织与婚姻联盟维持统治。
structure.en: Late Nazca did not possess a unified state bureaucracy but rather a dispersed political network of valley settlements and ritual centers. Though Cahuachi had declined, it retained symbolic religious importance; local chiefs ruled through ritual, water management, labor organization, and marriage alliances.

function.zh: 主持祭典、管理圣地、保存祖先与神灵崇拜传统，并协调与农业周期相关的仪式
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, managed sacred sites, preserved ancestral and divine cults, and coordinated rituals tied to the agricultural cycle

function.zh: 维护地下水道、渠道与蓄水设施，分配耕地用水，并组织季节性公共劳役
function.en: Maintained puquios, canals, and water facilities, allocated irrigation water, and organized seasonal communal labor

function.zh: 征收粮食、纺织品、陶器与劳役贡赋，管理地方储备以供祭祀、再分配与灾荒应对
function.en: Collected tribute in crops, textiles, ceramics, and labor, and managed local reserves for ritual use, redistribution, and famine response

function.zh: 组织陶器、纺织、染色、羽饰与木器制作，保障精英需求与交换网络供应
function.en: Organized pottery, textile, dyeing, featherwork, and wooden craft production for elite demand and exchange networks

function.zh: 调解村社纠纷、保护灌溉设施和仓储点，并在冲突时动员武装随从
function.en: Mediated village disputes, protected irrigation works and storehouses, and mobilized armed retainers in times of conflict

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以河谷聚落、宗族领地和灌溉共同体为基础。酋长、长者与祭司共同主持事务，村落层面依赖习惯法与集体协商。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on valley settlements, lineage territories, and irrigation communities. Chiefs, elders, and priests jointly managed affairs, while village-level governance relied on customary law and collective deliberation.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、宗教禁忌与首领裁决为主，没有成文法典。案件多涉及土地边界、用水权、婚姻、贡赋与仪式义务。
legalSystem.en: The legal system relied on customary law, religious taboo, and chiefly judgment, without a written legal code. Cases mainly concerned land boundaries, water rights, marriage, tribute, and ritual obligations.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要以实物贡赋和劳役征发为主，包括玉米、棉花、葫芦、鱼干、纺织品和公共工程劳作，而非统一货币税。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation consisted primarily of tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, cotton, gourds, dried fish, textiles, and work on public projects, rather than standardized monetary taxes.

religion.zh: 以多神信仰、祖先崇拜和自然神灵崇拜为核心，祭仪与农业、水源、丰产及头颅战利品传统密切相关
religion.en: Religion centered on polytheistic worship, ancestor veneration, and nature deities, with rituals closely linked to agriculture, water, fertility, and the trophy-head tradition

philosophy.zh: 纳斯卡没有成体系的书面哲学，其世界观强调人与祖先、神灵、土地与水脉之间的平衡，以及通过祭祀维持宇宙秩序
philosophy.en: Nazca had no formal written philosophy; its worldview emphasized balance among people, ancestors, deities, land, and watercourses, and the maintenance of cosmic order through ritual

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，信息通过口述传统、图像符号、纺织纹样、陶器图像与地画表达
writingSystem.en: No writing system; information was expressed through oral tradition, visual symbolism, textile motifs, ceramic imagery, and geoglyphs

culturalAchievements.zh: 以多彩彩绘陶器、精细纺织、地下水道工程、地画传统和复杂仪式建筑闻名。到476年，其经典艺术高峰已过，但地方工艺和宗教传统仍延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Nazca was renowned for polychrome painted ceramics, fine textiles, underground waterworks, geoglyph traditions, and complex ritual architecture. By 476 CE its classical artistic peak had passed, but local craft and religious traditions endured.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未有定论，可能属于安第斯南海岸的已消失地方语言群
languageFamily.en: Language affiliation is uncertain, likely belonging to now-lost local language groups of the south-central Andes

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业为基础，辅以渔业、畜产品交换、陶器与纺织手工业。由于晚期生态压力、区域分散与仪式中心衰落，整体生产能力较纳斯卡鼎盛期下降。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on irrigated agriculture, supplemented by fishing, exchange in camelid products, pottery, and textile crafts. Owing to late ecological stress, regional fragmentation, and the decline of ritual centers, overall productive capacity was lower than at the Nazca peak.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、棉纺织、彩陶制作、渔业、葫芦与果类种植、地方交换贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, cotton textiles, painted ceramics, fishing, gourd and fruit cultivation, and local exchange trade

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、棉花、豆类、辣椒、葫芦、鱼干、贝壳、羽毛、羊驼制品、陶器、纺织品与染料
tradeGoods.en: Maize, cotton, beans, chili peppers, gourds, dried fish, shells, feathers, camelid products, ceramics, textiles, and dyes

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、灌溉接入、储粮、棉纺工具、陶器和亲族网络，而非金属货币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to fields, irrigation rights, stored grain, cotton tools, ceramics, and kinship ties rather than in accumulated metal currency.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、精英再分配、实物贡赋和区域互惠交换为核心的农业经济
economicSystem.en: An agrarian economy centered on community production, elite redistribution, in-kind tribute, and regional reciprocal exchange

source.zh: 劳役与公共工程征发折值
source.en: Labor service and public works levy

category.zh: 精英再分配与仓储供养

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金属财富，而是分散于各河谷仓储、祭司控制的贮藏点和精英家族手中的粮食、棉料、手工品与牲产资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized stock of metal wealth but a dispersed reserve of food, cotton, crafted goods, and livestock resources held in valley storehouses, priestly depots, and elite households.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政依靠实物征收和劳役动员，优先保障水利、祭祀与再分配功能；在衰退时期更依赖地方储备与紧缩分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy depended on in-kind collection and labor mobilization, prioritizing water management, ritual, and redistribution; in a period of decline it relied increasingly on local reserves and restrained allocation.

description.zh: 由地方酋长与精英家族维持的常备随从，负责护卫圣地、仓储与首领住所，并在冲突中担任核心战力。
description.en: A small standing body maintained by local chiefs and elite lineages, guarding sacred sites, storehouses, and chiefly residences and serving as the core in conflict.

description.zh: 由各聚落在危机时征召的成年男子组成，主要用于防守灌溉设施、田地和村社边界。
description.en: Composed of adult men mobilized by settlements in times of crisis, mainly for defending irrigation works, fields, and community boundaries.

description.zh: 负责联络河谷、监视通道和护送交换队伍的小规模武装人员，兼具侦察与地方治安职能。
description.en: Small armed groups responsible for linking valleys, watching routes, and escorting exchange caravans, with scouting and local security roles.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地方酋长分别统领
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; authority was exercised separately by local chiefs

technology.zh: 武器以投石索、木矛、棍棒、石制或铜制刀具、盾牌和简易防护为主；缺乏重装部队和复杂攻城能力。
technology.en: Weaponry relied on slings, wooden spears, clubs, stone or copper blades, shields, and simple protection; there were no heavy troops or advanced siege capabilities.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近河谷竞争、资源紧张、灌溉系统争夺以及安第斯南海岸其他群体的局部冲突，而非远距离帝国入侵。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival valleys, resource stress, competition over irrigation systems, and localized conflict with other south-coastal Andean groups rather than long-range imperial invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 476年的纳斯卡人口主要分布于南秘鲁海岸多个河谷与绿洲聚落中。虽然经典纳斯卡中心已衰落，但农业村社和地方宗教中心仍维持相当人口规模。
populationDescription.en: In 476 CE the Nazca population was concentrated in multiple valleys and oasis settlements on the south coast of Peru. Although the classical Nazca centers had declined, agrarian communities and local ritual sites still sustained a substantial population.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司与酋长家族，其次为地方管理者、工匠与专业仪式人员；主体人口为农民、渔民与牧养交换群体，底层为承担重劳役与附属义务的人群。
socialClasses.en: At the top were priestly and chiefly families, followed by local managers, artisans, and specialist ritual personnel; the majority were farmers, fishers, and pastoral exchange communities, with lower strata bearing heavy labor and dependent obligations.

allies.zh: 无正式远程盟国，主要依赖与南海岸及邻近高地群体的地方性互惠关系、婚姻联盟和交换网络
allies.en: There were no formal long-distance allies; relations depended mainly on local reciprocity, marriage alliances, and exchange networks with south-coastal and nearby highland groups

enemies.zh: 无固定的文明级宿敌，但与邻近河谷社群和部分高地群体时有围绕土地、水源与贸易通道的冲突
enemies.en: There were no fixed civilization-scale enemies, but periodic conflict occurred with neighboring valley communities and some highland groups over land, water, and trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持区域交换、避免破坏灌溉秩序和通过祭祀联系巩固地方网络为核心，整体偏向防御与协调而非扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining regional exchange, preventing disruption to irrigation order, and strengthening local networks through ritual ties; it was defensive and coordinative rather than expansionist.

era.zh: 安第斯晚期地方传统阶段
era.en: Late local Andean tradition phase

keyInnovations.zh: 地下引水系统、干旱地带灌溉农业、精细彩陶烧制、复杂纺织与大型地画营造技术仍是其核心技术成就，但到476年创新速度已明显放缓。
keyInnovations.en: Subterranean water-capture systems, irrigation agriculture in arid lands, sophisticated polychrome ceramics, intricate textiles, and large-scale geoglyph construction remained its key technological achievements, though by 476 CE the pace of innovation had clearly slowed.

strengths.zh: 擅长在极端干旱环境下利用灌溉与地下水道维系农业；拥有强烈的宗教凝聚力、成熟的地方交换网络和高水平工艺传统。
strengths.en: Nazca excelled at sustaining agriculture in extreme aridity through irrigation and puquio systems; it retained strong ritual cohesion, well-developed local exchange networks, and high craft traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一军政核心；生态脆弱，易受干旱与洪水影响；卡瓦奇衰落后区域整合能力下降，资源竞争加剧。
weaknesses.en: Its politics were highly fragmented and lacked a unified military-administrative core; the ecology was fragile and vulnerable to drought and floods; after the decline of Cahuachi, regional integration weakened and resource competition intensified.

outlook.zh: 476年的纳斯卡已处于衰退后期，传统仍在但整体文明整合力减弱。未来更可能被地方化延续、与周边安第斯文化融合，或被新兴区域力量取代，而非恢复早期辉煌。
outlook.en: By 476 CE Nazca was in a late phase of decline: traditions persisted, but overall civilizational integration had weakened. Its future lay more in localized survival, fusion with neighboring Andean cultures, or replacement by new regional powers than in any return to its earlier florescence.

description.zh: 纳斯卡南海岸文化虽趋衰微，但地区聚落与宗教传统仍延续。
description.en: The Nazca culture on the south coast was waning, but local settlements and ritual traditions persisted.

name.zh: 安第斯中部高地诸部文明

governmentForm.zh: 由多个高地地方政体组成的部族酋邦网络，权力分散，依托亲族、祭祀中心与地方灌溉共同体而运作。
governmentForm.en: A network of multiple highland tribal polities and chiefdoms with decentralized authority, operating through kinship groups, ritual centers, and local irrigation communities.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族群体、村社与地方祭祀组织为核心，上层为酋长家族与祭司，中层为自由农牧民与工匠，下层为依附家庭、战俘与服役者。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, village communities, and local ritual organizations, with chief lineages and priests at the top, free farmers, herders, and artisans in the middle, and dependent households, war captives, and laborers below.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、祭司精英与控制梯田、牧场和祭祀中心的世袭首领。
rulingClass.en: Local chief lineages, priestly elites, and hereditary leaders controlling terraces, pasturelands, and ritual centers.

succession.zh: 通常为家族世袭并辅以长老认可，实际继承常受婚姻联盟、地方武力与祭祀威望影响。
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families and validated by elders, though actual succession was often shaped by marriage alliances, local force, and ritual prestige.

structure.zh: 无统一中央国家机构，各谷地与高地盆地由独立或半独立酋邦治理；政务通过家族长老会议、祭司集团及酋长随从执行。
structure.en: There was no unified central state apparatus; valleys and highland basins were governed by independent or semi-independent chiefdoms, with administration carried out through councils of elders, priestly groups, and retainers of local chiefs.

function.zh: 主持地方神庙、祖先祭祀、农业时序与公共仪式，维护酋长合法性。
function.en: Oversaw local temples, ancestor worship, agricultural timing, and public ceremonies, sustaining chiefly legitimacy.

function.zh: 组织梯田修筑、渠道维护、播种收获协调及粮食储存。
function.en: Organized terrace construction, canal maintenance, coordination of sowing and harvest, and food storage.

function.zh: 管理羊驼、美洲驼牧群、季节性放牧与山间运输路线。
function.en: Managed llama and alpaca herds, seasonal pasturage, and mountain transport routes.

function.zh: 征集劳役、织物、农产和牲畜贡品，并监管公共仓储分配。
function.en: Collected labor service, textiles, farm produce, and livestock tribute, and supervised redistribution from communal storehouses.

function.zh: 在冲突时期动员战士，平时调解土地、水源与婚姻争端。
function.en: Mobilized warriors in times of conflict and mediated disputes over land, water, and marriage in peacetime.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社、灌溉共同体和山谷联盟为基础；每一地区由酋长、长老和祭司共同决策，行政边界通常与生态区和水利系统相重合。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on village communities, irrigation groups, and valley alliances; each area was governed jointly by chiefs, elders, and priests, with boundaries often following ecological zones and water systems.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，重视亲族赔偿、公共调解与宗教誓约；严重罪行可能导致逐出共同体、劳役惩罚或祭祀性制裁。
legalSystem.en: Customary law predominated, emphasizing kin compensation, public mediation, and sacred oaths; serious offenses could bring expulsion from the community, labor penalties, or ritual sanctions.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，主要以劳役、农产、纺织品、牲畜及节庆供奉形式承担公共义务。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; public obligations were met chiefly through labor service, agricultural produce, textiles, livestock, and ceremonial offerings.

religion.zh: 以安第斯多神信仰、祖先崇拜、山岳神灵与地方圣地祭祀为核心，祭祀与农业周期紧密相连。
religion.en: Religion centered on Andean polytheistic belief, ancestor worship, mountain spirits, and local sacred places, with ritual closely tied to agricultural cycles.

philosophy.zh: 思想观念强调人与山川、水源、祖灵之间的互惠关系，社会秩序建立在共同劳动、仪式交换与群体责任之上。
philosophy.en: Its worldview emphasized reciprocal relations among people, mountains, water sources, and ancestors; social order rested on collective labor, ritual exchange, and communal obligation.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，信息主要通过口述传统、图像符号、结绳式记忆辅助及仪式表演传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, visual symbols, mnemonic cord-like aids, and ritual performance.

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长高海拔梯田农业、石砌聚落、纺织工艺、陶器制作、祖先墓葬传统以及适应多生态带的资源整合。
