culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included high-altitude terrace agriculture, stone-built settlements, textile production, pottery making, ancestor-oriented mortuary traditions, and the integration of resources across multiple ecological zones.

languageFamily.zh: 以中安第斯诸语言为主，可能包括早期盖丘亚化与艾马拉化之前的多种地方语言群。
languageFamily.en: Languages belonged mainly to central Andean groups, likely including several local language families before later wider Quechua and Aymara expansions.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业和牧业为基础，市场交换规模有限，但通过高地、山谷与沿海之间的互补交换维持较强韧性。估值仅为现代比较意义上的折算，不代表当时存在统一货币经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence farming and herding, with limited market exchange but considerable resilience through complementary exchange between highlands, valleys, and the coast. This estimate is only a modern comparative conversion and does not imply a unified monetary economy at the time.

mainIndustries.zh: 梯田农业、块根作物种植、玉米栽培、羊驼与美洲驼牧养、纺织、陶器、石工与地方性长途驮运。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included terrace agriculture, tuber cultivation, maize farming, llama and alpaca pastoralism, textile production, pottery, stonework, and regional caravan transport.

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、马铃薯类块根、藜麦、羊驼毛与羊毛织物、陶器、盐、黑曜石、铜制品、干肉和高地药用植物。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included maize, potato-like tubers, quinoa, llama and alpaca wool textiles, pottery, salt, obsidian, copper goods, dried meat, and highland medicinal plants.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、亲族劳动力、牲畜数量、储粮、纺织品和与祭祀中心的联系，而非铸币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to fields, kin labor, herd size, stored food, textiles, and ties to ritual centers rather than in coined money.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、贡赋再分配与生态互补交换为核心的部族经济；劳役和礼物交换比纯粹市场交易更重要。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on community production, tribute redistribution, and ecological complementarity; labor obligations and gift exchange were more important than pure market transactions.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金属储备，而主要表现为各地仓储中的粮食、织物、盐、牲畜和可调动劳力。少量贵金属主要具有礼仪价值。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not primarily a centralized stock of metal but consisted mainly of stored food, textiles, salt, livestock, and mobilizable labor in local storehouses. Small amounts of precious metal had chiefly ritual value.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏重维持灌溉、祭祀和地方稳定，通过再分配缓冲歉收风险，整体政策谨慎而保守。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining irrigation, ritual life, and local stability, using redistribution to buffer poor harvests; overall it was cautious and conservative.

description.zh: 由各地酋长维持的核心武装，平时兼任护卫、执法与征收劳役监督，战时担任先锋。
description.en: The core armed force maintained by local chiefs, serving in peacetime as guards, enforcers, and supervisors of labor levies, and acting as vanguards in war.

description.zh: 由村社成年男子按需征集，主要用于守卫梯田、水源、通道和参与地方冲突。
description.en: Drawn as needed from adult males of village communities, mainly used to defend terraces, water sources, routes, and to take part in local conflicts.

description.zh: 熟悉山路与生态带转换的轻装人员，承担侦察、联络、运输补给和突袭任务。
description.en: Lightly equipped personnel familiar with mountain routes and ecological transitions, used for scouting, liaison, supply transport, and raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地方酋长分别统领
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; each local chief commanded his own forces

technology.zh: 使用石制、木制与铜制武器，装备以棍棒、矛、投石索、飞石器和简易盾牌为主，善于山地防御但缺乏攻城能力。
technology.en: Weapons were made of stone, wood, and copper, with clubs, spears, slings, thrown stones, and simple shields predominating; forces were effective in mountain defense but lacked siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近高地与山谷政体之间对土地、水利、牧场和贸易通道的竞争，以及沿海强势文化势力的影响扩张。
threats.en: The main threats came from competition among neighboring highland and valley polities over land, irrigation, pasture, and trade routes, as well as expanding influence from powerful coastal cultures.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于秘鲁中部和南部高地的山谷、盆地与台地，多数为农村聚落，依托梯田、灌溉与牧场生存。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across valleys, basins, and upland plateaus of the central and southern Peruvian highlands, with most people living in rural settlements sustained by terraces, irrigation, and pasture.

name.zh: 蒂蒂喀喀北缘高地中心

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括酋长家族与祭司、地方武士与随从、自由农牧民、工匠与驮运者，以及依附家庭与战俘。阶层流动有限，但亲族关系和婚姻联盟具有重要作用。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included chiefly families and priests, local warriors and retainers, free farmers and herders, artisans and caravan handlers, and dependent households and war captives. Mobility was limited, though kinship ties and marriage alliances were important.

allies.zh: 无统一对外同盟体系，常见的是相邻山谷酋邦之间基于婚姻、共同祭祀和互助防御形成的临时联盟。
allies.en: There was no unified foreign alliance system; more common were temporary alliances among neighboring valley chiefdoms based on marriage, shared ritual, and mutual defense.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是周边竞争性高地政体，以及在资源和通道上发生冲突的沿海与山间群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly rival highland polities and coastal or intermontane groups with which they clashed over resources and routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以地方自保、控制交换路线、维持祭祀声望和缔结婚姻关系为主，并未形成统一扩张战略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on local self-preservation, control of exchange routes, maintenance of ritual prestige, and marriage ties rather than any unified expansionist strategy.

era.zh: 前瓦里形成期的安第斯区域复杂社会阶段
era.en: Andean Regional Complex Society stage before Wari consolidation

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括高海拔梯田建设、石砌挡墙、水渠灌溉、羊驼驮运网络、精细纺织、储藏技术与适应多生态带的生产组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included high-altitude terracing, stone retaining walls, canal irrigation, llama caravan transport networks, fine textile production, storage techniques, and production systems adapted to multiple ecological zones.

strengths.zh: 优势在于对高地生态的深度适应、强韧的村社组织、跨生态带资源互补、稳定的仪式权威以及较高的人口承载能力。
strengths.en: Strengths included deep adaptation to the highland environment, resilient village organization, resource complementarity across ecological zones, stable ritual authority, and relatively high carrying capacity for population.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治高度分散、缺乏统一指挥、地方冲突频繁、交通成本高、技术和行政整合程度有限，容易被更具组织力的新兴高地国家吸纳。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included extreme political fragmentation, lack of unified command, frequent local conflict, high transport costs, and limited technological and administrative integration, making the region vulnerable to absorption by more organized rising highland states.

outlook.zh: 476年前后的局势总体稳定，但区域整合趋势正在增强；这些高地酋邦为随后数代内瓦里势力的扩张与国家形成提供了人口、道路、祭祀中心和组织经验基础。
outlook.en: The situation around 476 CE was broadly stable, but regional integration was strengthening; these highland chiefdoms provided the demographic base, routes, ritual centers, and organizational experience that would support Wari expansion and state formation in the following generations.

description.zh: 秘鲁高地分布众多区域性政体，为后续瓦里扩张提供政治背景。
description.en: Numerous regional highland polities in Peru formed the political backdrop for later Wari expansion.

capital.zh: 莫切祭祀中心（莫切月亮金字塔与太阳金字塔）及其他区域中心
capital.en: Huacas de Moche and other regional centers

governmentForm.zh: 分裂的神权军事诸侯制王国体系
governmentForm.en: Fragmented theocratic-militarized kingdom system

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度分层，由祭司—武士贵族统治，之下为工匠、农民、渔民、仆役与战俘。地区政权各自控制灌溉谷地，并通过宗教仪式、再分配与武力维系统治。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, ruled by priest-warrior elites above artisans, farmers, fishers, retainers, and war captives. Regional polities controlled irrigated valleys and maintained rule through ritual authority, redistribution, and force.

rulingClass.zh: 祭司贵族、军事首领与世袭地方精英
rulingClass.en: Priestly nobility, military chiefs, and hereditary local elites

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，但常受地方贵族、军事力量与宗教合法性影响；在文明晚期，各谷地政权继承方式并不统一。
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within elite lineages, but shaped by local nobility, military strength, and ritual legitimacy; in the late period, succession varied among valley polities.

structure.zh: 莫切在476年已非统一国家，而是由北秘鲁沿海多个谷地政权构成。每个中心由领主、祭司集团和军事随从共同统治，以神庙、宫殿和仓储设施为行政核心。
structure.en: By 476 CE the Moche were no longer a unified state but a set of valley-based polities along the north Peruvian coast. Each center was governed by a lord, priestly establishment, and military retainers, with temples, palaces, and storage compounds serving as administrative hubs.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、祖先崇拜、历法仪式、供品管理与神庙财产分配
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, ancestor cults, calendrical rites, offerings, and allocation of temple property

function.zh: 组织运河维护、农田分配、劳役征发与粮食征收
function.en: Managed canal maintenance, field allocation, labor mobilization, and grain collection

function.zh: 监督陶器、纺织、金属与贝饰作坊，并征收地方贡品
function.en: Supervised pottery, textile, metal, and shell workshops and collected local tribute

function.zh: 指挥战士、守卫中心、组织突袭与俘虏押解
function.en: Commanded warriors, guarded centers, organized raids, and handled captives

function.zh: 管理粮仓、供给精英与工匠，并在灾荒时进行有限分配
function.en: Managed storehouses, provisioned elites and specialists, and carried out limited famine redistribution

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以谷地为单位，各河谷中心控制周边村落与灌溉网。基层并非文书官僚制，而是依靠宗族首领、监督者和神庙管理人员执行命令。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by valley polities, each controlling nearby villages and irrigation networks. At the base, rule relied less on literate bureaucracy than on kin leaders, overseers, and temple managers.

legalSystem.zh: 莫切没有已知成文法典。秩序主要依据习惯、领主裁决、宗教规范和威慑性惩罚维持，战争俘虏、反抗者与失范者可能遭受严厉处置，且祭祀暴力具有政治功能。
legalSystem.en: The Moche had no known written law code. Order was maintained through custom, lordly judgment, religious norms, and deterrent punishments; war captives, rebels, and deviants could face severe treatment, and ritual violence had political functions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政征收主要以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、豆类、棉花、鱼干、纺织品、陶器与建筑、运河修缮等强制劳动，而非货币税。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal extraction was based chiefly on in-kind tribute and labor service, including maize, beans, cotton, dried fish, textiles, pottery, and compulsory work on buildings and canals rather than monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 莫切宗教为多神且强烈仪式化，崇拜与山海、丰产、祖先、战争和祭祀相关的神灵。神庙仪式、献酒、流血与人祭在精英政治中占据核心地位。
religion.en: Moche religion was polytheistic and intensely ritualized, centered on deities linked to mountains, sea, fertility, ancestors, warfare, and sacrifice. Temple rites, libations, bloodletting, and human sacrifice stood at the core of elite politics.

philosophy.zh: 莫切并无如欧亚文明那样独立成文的哲学传统，其世界观体现为宗教宇宙秩序、祖先权威、自然循环与统治合法性的结合。
philosophy.en: The Moche did not possess a separate textual philosophical tradition like those of Eurasia; their worldview was expressed through a fusion of sacred cosmic order, ancestral authority, natural cycles, and political legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 未发现被普遍承认的成熟文字系统；行政与象征传播主要依靠图像叙事、仪式表演、建筑空间和口传传统。
writingSystem.en: No widely accepted mature writing system is known; administration and symbolic communication relied mainly on visual narratives, ritual performance, architectural space, and oral tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 莫切以写实而复杂的陶器艺术、金银铜工艺、精细纺织、巨型神庙建筑、墓葬随葬体系和高水平灌溉工程著称。其图像叙事对后世安第斯文化影响深远。
culturalAchievements.en: The Moche were renowned for realistic and complex ceramics, gold-silver-copper metallurgy, fine textiles, monumental temple architecture, elaborate funerary assemblages, and sophisticated irrigation works. Their visual narrative tradition deeply influenced later Andean cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能与北秘鲁沿海早期语言群有关；后世该地区曾分布奎宁南语和穆奇克语系相关语言，但476年无法精确确定。
languageFamily.en: Their linguistic affiliation is uncertain, probably linked to early north Peruvian coastal language groups; later the region included languages associated with Quingnam and Muchik, but 476 CE cannot be identified precisely.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 莫切经济以灌溉农业、渔业、手工业和地区再分配为核心。476年因政治碎片化、环境压力与部分中心衰退，总体经济低于鼎盛期，但沿海谷地仍保有相当产出能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Moche economy was centered on irrigated agriculture, fishing, craft production, and regional redistribution. In 476 CE, political fragmentation, environmental stress, and the decline of some centers reduced output from its peak, though the coastal valleys still retained substantial productive capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、海洋渔业、陶器制作、纺织、金属工艺、贝壳与石器加工、建筑营造
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, marine fishing, pottery production, textiles, metallurgy, shell and stone working, and construction

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜、辣椒、棉花、鱼干、纺织品、彩陶、铜器、金银饰品、热带羽毛、贝壳与高地交换品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, squash, chili peppers, cotton, dried fish, textiles, painted ceramics, copper goods, gold and silver ornaments, tropical feathers, shells, and exchange goods from the highlands

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、灌溉份额、棉纺工具、渔具、储粮与家用陶器。精英财富则集中于金属器、华服、祭仪器物和劳动力控制权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of access to land, irrigation shares, cotton tools, fishing gear, stored food, and domestic pottery. Elite wealth was concentrated in metal goods, fine clothing, ritual objects, and control over labor.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为宫庙再分配与地方贡赋并存的农业手工业经济，市场交换存在但不居主导；生产和资源调配高度依赖谷地首领、神庙和季节性劳役。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined temple-palace redistribution with local tribute in an agrarian-craft economy; market exchange existed but was not dominant. Production and resource allocation depended heavily on valley lords, temples, and seasonal labor obligations.

source.en: Labor dues and public works obligations

source.zh: 手工业与神庙作坊产出
长途交换与附属首领献纳

treasuryDescription.zh: 莫切并无统一国库，各中心财富分散储存在神庙仓库、贵族墓葬储备、金属与纺织珍品及粮仓中。476年因地区割据和气候冲击，财政储备较脆弱。
treasuryDescription.en: The Moche had no single central treasury; wealth was dispersed among temple storehouses, elite reserves, metal and textile valuables, and grain depots. In 476 CE, fragmentation and climatic shocks made fiscal reserves relatively fragile.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持神庙威望、灌溉系统和武装随从。面对衰退，各地区可能加重劳役与贡纳，但这也削弱了基层社会稳定。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving temple prestige, irrigation systems, and armed retainers. In response to decline, regional rulers likely intensified labor and tribute demands, but this also weakened social stability at the base.

description.zh: 由贵族武士和核心随从组成，装备较好，负责作战、仪式护卫和俘虏展示。
description.en: Composed of noble warriors and core retainers, comparatively well equipped, responsible for combat, ceremonial protection, and the display of captives.

description.zh: 从各谷地农民与附属社群中征发，主要用于突袭、守卫灌溉设施与地区冲突。
description.en: Raised from valley farmers and subordinate communities, used mainly for raids, guarding irrigation works, and local conflicts.

description.zh: 承担物资运输、武器搬运、俘虏押解和战时营地保障，不全为前线战斗人员。
description.en: Handled supply transport, movement of weapons, captive escort, and wartime camp support, not all of them front-line fighters.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地区领主兼最高军事首领
commanderInChief.en: Regional lords as supreme military leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以木棍棒、矛、投石索、飞镖、盾牌、棉甲与铜制礼仪或实战武器为主，无骑兵、铁器体系或攻城技术。战争多为地区性冲突、突袭与俘虏获取。
technology.en: Military technology relied on clubs, spears, slings, darts, shields, cotton armor, and copper ceremonial or practical weapons, with no cavalry, iron system, or advanced siegecraft. Warfare was largely regional, involving raids, clashes, and the taking of captives.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部碎片化、邻近谷地政权竞争、北方与高地势力压力，以及干旱、洪水和灌溉系统失修引发的社会动荡。
threats.en: The main threats came from internal fragmentation, rivalry among neighboring valley polities, pressure from northern and highland groups, and social unrest caused by droughts, floods, and deteriorating irrigation systems.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在北秘鲁沿海河谷、灌溉农业区与近海聚落。476年总体人口仍然可观，但部分中心衰落、聚落迁移与区域分化明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the river valleys, irrigated agricultural zones, and coastal settlements of northern Peru. In 476 CE the total remained substantial, but some centers had declined and settlement shifts and regional differentiation were pronounced.

name.zh: 圣何塞-德莫罗地区中心
name.en: San José de Moro regional center

socialClasses.zh: 上层为领主家族、祭司、武士贵族；中层为工匠、管理者、专业祭仪人员；下层为农民、渔民与劳役者；最底层包括战俘、奴役人口和被迫献祭者。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood lordly families, priests, and warrior nobles; the middle strata included artisans, administrators, and ritual specialists; below them were farmers, fishers, and laborers; at the bottom were captives, servile populations, and sacrificial victims.

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友；各地区领主可能与相邻谷地、部分高地社群或海岸中心建立临时联盟。
allies.en: There were no durable unified allies; regional lords may have formed temporary alliances with neighboring valleys, some highland communities, or coastal centers.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者是其他竞争性莫切政权、邻近沿海与高地群体，以及在资源紧张时期出现的地方叛乱势力。
enemies.en: The principal enemies were rival Moche polities, neighboring coastal and highland groups, and local rebellious forces emerging during times of resource stress.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制河谷资源、维护交换路线、获取俘虏和保持仪式威望为主。由于缺乏统一中央政权，外交更像地方首领间的关系网络而非系统国家外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed chiefly at controlling valley resources, maintaining exchange routes, obtaining captives, and preserving ritual prestige. Because there was no unified central state, diplomacy resembled a network of local chiefly relations rather than a systematic state foreign policy.

era.zh: 安第斯前哥伦布晚期早期国家时代
era.en: Late Early State era of pre-Columbian Andes

keyInnovations.zh: 大型灌溉渠系、干旱海岸农业适应、写实陶塑与叙事图像、铜金银冶炼与镀金技术、复杂纺织与神庙建筑技术。
keyInnovations.en: Large irrigation canal systems, adaptation of agriculture to arid coasts, realistic modeled ceramics and narrative iconography, copper-gold-silver metallurgy and gilding techniques, and advanced textile and temple-building methods.

strengths.zh: 莫切仍拥有深厚的宗教文化传统、成熟的灌溉农业、精湛手工业和强烈的地区认同。其艺术、礼仪和社会组织在北秘鲁沿海仍具广泛影响力。
strengths.en: The Moche still possessed a deep religious-cultural tradition, mature irrigated agriculture, highly skilled crafts, and strong regional identities. Their art, ritual, and social organization continued to exert broad influence on the north Peruvian coast.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治分裂、中心权威衰退、环境波动脆弱性高，以及对劳役和仪式暴力的高度依赖。缺乏统一整合能力使其难以应对长期危机。
weaknesses.en: Its greatest weaknesses were political fragmentation, declining central authority, high vulnerability to environmental fluctuations, and heavy reliance on labor extraction and ritual violence. The lack of unified integration made it difficult to respond to prolonged crises.

outlook.zh: 476年后的莫切世界很可能继续区域化并逐步转化为新的后莫切政治文化格局。作为统一文明的复兴前景有限，但其技术、宗教图像和精英传统将延续并影响后继的北海岸社会。
outlook.en: After 476 CE, the Moche world was likely to continue regionalizing and gradually transform into new post-Moche political and cultural formations. Prospects for revival as a unified civilization were limited, but its technologies, religious imagery, and elite traditions would endure and shape successor societies on the north coast.

description.zh: 秘鲁北海岸的莫切文明已分裂为多个地区性政权，但仍保持高度文化影响力。
description.en: The Moche civilization on the north Peruvian coast had fragmented into regional polities but retained strong cultural influence.

name.en: Eastern South American Forest Tribal Civilization

governmentForm.zh: 以村社与部族联盟为基础的分散酋邦体制，权力依赖亲族、威望、战功与馈赠网络
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chieftainship system based on villages and tribal alliances, with authority resting on kinship, prestige, war leadership, and gift-exchange networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩大家族、氏族与村社为核心，数个村落可在战争、迁徙或祭仪中形成较松散的部族联合体；地位差异存在，但尚未形成官僚国家式的严格等级制度
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on extended families, clans, and villages; several settlements could form looser tribal groupings for warfare, migration, or ritual. Status differences existed, but not a rigid bureaucratic class order of a state

rulingClass.zh: 部族酋长、战士首领、长老与具有宗教声望的祭仪人物
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs, war leaders, elders, and ritual specialists with religious prestige

succession.zh: 继承通常不固定，多由亲族网络、长老共识、个人战功、演说能力与馈赠声望决定，部分首领职位可在家族内延续
succession.en: Succession was usually flexible, shaped by kin consensus, elder approval, personal war prestige, oratorical ability, and gift-giving reputation, though some leadership roles could continue within prominent families

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央政府；政治结构由众多村社、区域性部族群体与临时联盟组成。酋长负责调解纠纷、组织迁徙、主持迎战与协调大型仪式，而非通过成文法令进行统治
structure.en: There was no unified central government; the political structure consisted of many villages, regional tribal groupings, and temporary alliances. Chiefs mediated disputes, organized movement, led defense and raiding, and coordinated major rituals rather than ruling through written decrees

function.zh: 由长老、家族首领与酋长协商婚姻、迁居、狩猎区域、对外关系与内部纠纷
function.en: Elders, lineage heads, and chiefs deliberated on marriage, relocation, hunting zones, external relations, and internal disputes

function.zh: 组织防御、报复性袭击、护卫村落与年轻男性的战斗训练
function.en: Organized defense, retaliatory raids, village protection, and martial training for young men

function.zh: 主持葬礼、成年礼、集体宴会、祈福仪式、治疗与灵性实践
function.en: Conducted funerals, coming-of-age rites, communal feasts, blessing rituals, healing, and spiritual practices

function.zh: 协调木薯、玉米、鱼类、陶器、羽饰、石材与地方性特产的分配和交换
function.en: Coordinated the distribution and exchange of manioc, maize, fish, pottery, feather ornaments, stone materials, and local specialties

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由村社自行处理，村长或酋长在长老与亲族支持下行使权威；较大的聚落群可形成区域性首领网络，但缺乏常设行政层级
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by each village, where a headman or chief exercised authority with elder and kin support; larger settlement clusters could form regional leadership networks, but there was no permanent administrative hierarchy

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以口头传统、祖先规范、和解、赔偿、婚姻安排与报复原则为基础；严重冲突有时通过分离聚落、决斗或部族冲突解决
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, based on oral tradition, ancestral norms, reconciliation, compensation, marriage arrangements, and principles of retaliation; serious disputes could sometimes be resolved by village separation, duels, or intertribal conflict

taxationSystem.zh: 无国家税制；酋长通过馈赠、公共劳动、战利品分配、婚姻联盟义务和节庆宴会中的再分配来维持权威
taxationSystem.en: There was no state tax system; chiefs maintained authority through gift exchange, communal labor, distribution of spoils, marriage alliance obligations, and redistribution during festival feasts

religion.zh: 以万物有灵信仰、祖灵观念、自然精灵崇拜与萨满式祭仪为主，宗教生活与狩猎、农业、战争、疾病治疗和葬俗紧密相连
religion.en: Religion was centered on animism, ancestral spirits, nature beings, and shamanic-style ritual practices, closely tied to hunting, agriculture, warfare, healing, and burial customs

philosophy.zh: 世界观强调亲族互助、部落荣誉、与森林河流环境的平衡、祖先传统以及勇武与慷慨的社会价值
philosophy.en: Their worldview emphasized kin solidarity, tribal honor, balance with forest and river environments, ancestral tradition, and the social values of bravery and generosity

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字体系，知识依赖口述传统、歌谣、仪式语言、身体装饰图案与物质文化符号传承
writingSystem.en: There was no developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, ritual speech, body decoration patterns, and material cultural symbols

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括热带森林环境下的村社营建、木薯加工技术、独木舟与河流交通、陶器制作、羽饰艺术、编织、集体长屋传统以及复杂的口头史诗和仪式歌曲
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included village construction in tropical forest settings, manioc processing techniques, canoe and river transport, pottery production, featherwork art, weaving, communal longhouse traditions, and complex oral epics and ritual songs

languageFamily.zh: 以图皮-瓜拉尼语族及其他东部南美相关语言群为主
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Tupi-Guarani languages and other related eastern South American language groups

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、渔猎采集和地方交换为主，市场化程度低，但在河流与森林走廊中存在稳定的区域交换网络。财富主要体现在食物储备、亲族劳动力、独木舟、装饰品与社会关系，而非货币积累
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, fishing, hunting, gathering, and local exchange, with low market integration but stable regional exchange networks along rivers and forest corridors. Wealth was expressed mainly in food stores, kin labor, canoes, ornaments, and social ties rather than monetary accumulation

mainIndustries.zh: 刀耕火种农业、木薯与玉米种植、渔业、狩猎、采集、陶器、木器、编织、羽饰制作和独木舟制造
mainIndustries.en: Slash-and-burn agriculture, manioc and maize cultivation, fishing, hunting, gathering, pottery, woodworking, weaving, featherwork, and canoe making

tradeGoods.zh: 木薯粉、玉米、干鱼、盐类替代品、陶器、羽毛与羽饰、木制器具、石制工具材料、染料、贝壳装饰物和地方药用植物
tradeGoods.en: Manioc flour, maize, dried fish, salt substitutes, pottery, feathers and feather ornaments, wooden tools, stone tool materials, dyes, shell ornaments, and local medicinal plants

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常体现为耕地使用权、劳动成员数量、粮食与木薯储备、渔猎工具、陶器、独木舟和婚姻联盟关系
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was typically measured in access to cultivated plots, number of working family members, food and manioc stores, fishing and hunting equipment, pottery, canoes, and marriage alliance ties

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族生产、公共劳动、季节性迁移和礼物交换为基础的部族再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal redistributive economy based on kin production, communal labor, seasonal mobility, and gift exchange

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

source.zh: 公共劳动与首领再分配贡献
source.en: Communal labor and chief redistribution contributions

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

source.zh: 战争战利品与报复性掠获

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

category.zh: 迁居、营建与独木舟制造

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库；“财政储备”更准确地表现为酋长与村社掌握的粮食、木薯制品、工具、装饰物、独木舟与可用于宴饮和结盟的剩余物资
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; the 'treasury' is better understood as reserves of food, manioc products, tools, ornaments, canoes, and surpluses held by chiefs and villages for feasting and alliance-building

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以维持社会凝聚、应对冲突和支持迁徙与仪式为主，不追求货币盈余，而强调再分配与首领慷慨
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining social cohesion, responding to conflict, and supporting movement and ritual, emphasizing redistribution and chiefly generosity rather than monetary surplus

description.zh: 由成年男性组成的主要战斗力量，平时从事农业与渔猎，战时集结使用木棍、石斧、长矛、弓箭与木盾作战
description.en: The main fighting force, composed of adult men who normally farmed, fished, and hunted, but assembled in wartime with clubs, stone axes, spears, bows, arrows, and wooden shields

description.zh: 依靠独木舟进行河流巡逻、突袭与快速转移，适合森林与水网环境中的短程作战
description.en: Used canoes for river patrols, raids, and rapid movement, suited for short-range warfare in forest and riverine environments

name.en: Chiefly Retinues and Elite War Bands

description.zh: 围绕声望较高的首领形成，承担先导攻击、护卫和复仇行动，在部族冲突中具有较高士气
description.en: Formed around more prestigious leaders, these bands undertook leading assaults, protection duties, and revenge expeditions, often with higher morale in intertribal conflict

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各部族酋长与战首分别领军
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; each tribal chief and war leader commanded his own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术处于石器、木器与有机材料并用阶段，擅长伏击、林地机动、河道行动和短促袭击，但缺乏金属武器、攻城技术与重装防护
technology.en: Military technology combined stone, wood, and organic materials; it was effective in ambush, woodland maneuver, river operations, and short raids, but lacked metal weaponry, siege methods, and heavy protection

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯折算）

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族竞争、报复性袭击、迁徙引发的土地争夺以及局部资源压力，而非外来帝国入侵
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal competition, retaliatory raids, land disputes driven by migration, and local resource pressure rather than invasion by external empires

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛而分散，集中于大西洋沿岸森林、巴西高地边缘、河谷和可耕作林间空地。多数居民生活在中小型村落，随土壤消耗、战争或联盟变化而迁移
populationDescription.en: The population was widely dispersed across Atlantic forests, margins of the Brazilian plateau, river valleys, and cultivable forest clearings. Most people lived in small and medium-sized villages and shifted location with soil exhaustion, warfare, or alliance changes

name.zh: 无固定城市，最大聚落为区域性大村社
name.en: No Fixed Cities; Largest Settlements Were Regional Large Villages

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致可分为酋长与显贵家族、战士与成年男性、普通农渔家庭、祭仪与治疗者、婚入或附属成员以及战俘或边缘人群；整体仍以亲族共同体而非严格阶级制为主
socialClasses.en: Social strata may be broadly divided into chiefs and prestigious families, warriors and adult men, ordinary farming and fishing households, ritual specialists and healers, affinal or attached members, and war captives or marginal dependents; overall society remained based more on kin communities than rigid class hierarchy

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟国，通常与语言相近或通婚频繁的邻近部族结成临时联盟
allies.en: There were no permanent unified allies; temporary alliances were usually formed with nearby tribes sharing similar speech or frequent marriage ties

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是周边竞争部族与长期存在报复关系的邻近群体
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly rival neighboring tribes and nearby groups bound in long-standing cycles of retaliation

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持婚姻网络、交换通道与安全边界为核心；外交多通过使者、婚配、馈赠、和解宴会与战争威慑共同实现
foreignPolicy.en: External relations centered on maintaining marriage networks, exchange routes, and secure frontiers; diplomacy was conducted through envoys, marriages, gifts, reconciliation feasts, and deterrence through warfare

era.zh: 新石器晚期至早期园艺村社阶段
era.en: Late Neolithic to Early Horticultural Village Stage

keyInnovations.zh: 木薯脱毒加工、林地刀耕火种、独木舟制造、热带陶器烧制、渔猎工具改良、羽饰与纤维编织技术
keyInnovations.en: Detoxification processing of manioc, forest slash-and-burn cultivation, dugout canoe construction, tropical pottery firing, improved fishing and hunting gear, and advanced featherwork and fiber weaving

strengths.zh: 优势在于对森林与河流环境的高度适应、灵活迁徙能力、稳固的亲族组织、强韧的口述文化传统以及在热带环境中维持人口与村社网络的能力
strengths.en: Strengths included strong adaptation to forest and river environments, flexible mobility, resilient kin organization, durable oral cultural traditions, and the ability to sustain population and village networks in tropical conditions

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分散、缺乏统一指挥、没有文字和国家行政体系、金属技术不足，以及在大规模长期战争和集约化积累方面能力有限
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, lack of unified command, absence of writing and state administration, limited metallurgy, and reduced capacity for sustained large-scale warfare and intensive accumulation

outlook.zh: 476年时此文明整体处于稳定状态，区域人口与村落网络能够延续并扩展；未来的发展仍将主要依赖迁徙、部族融合与环境利用，而非快速形成集中式国家
outlook.en: In 476 CE this civilization was broadly stable, with regional populations and village networks able to continue and expand; future development would likely depend on migration, tribal fusion, and environmental adaptation rather than rapid formation of a centralized state

description.zh: 南美东部与内陆广大地区由图皮—瓜拉尼等族群的村社与部族政体分布其间。
description.en: Much of eastern and interior South America was occupied by village societies and tribal polities of Tupi-Guarani and related peoples.

capital.zh: 杰内杰诺及其他区域中心

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族与酋邦为基础的分散部族统治，若干河谷聚落形成区域性首领联盟
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal rule based on clans and chiefdoms, with several riverine settlements forming regional alliances under local leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、村社、手工业群体、农民、牧民与地方首领为核心，部分地区已出现较稳定的阶层差异，但尚未形成高度集权的国家贵族体系
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on kin groups, village communities, craft groups, farmers, pastoralists, and local chiefs; some regions showed stable social differentiation, but not yet a highly centralized aristocratic state system

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长、宗族长老、祭司性权威人物与控制贸易节点的家族
rulingClass.en: Local chiefs, clan elders, ritual authorities, and families controlling trade nodes

succession.zh: 多为宗族内部继承与长老认可相结合，常见兄终弟及、父子继承或母系亲属影响下的推举
succession.en: Succession usually combined lineage inheritance with elder approval, including brother-to-brother transfer, father-to-son inheritance, or selection influenced by matrilineal kin in some areas

structure.zh: 政治结构松散，由众多村社、市场聚落与小型酋邦组成。杰内杰诺等大型聚落充当区域交换与协调中心，但并无覆盖整个萨赫勒—尼日尔盆地的统一中央政权
structure.en: The political structure was loose, consisting of many villages, market settlements, and small chiefdoms. Large settlements such as Jenne-Jeno served as regional centers of exchange and coordination, but there was no single central authority over the entire Sahel-Niger basin

function.zh: 协调村社关系、调解争端、组织防务与季节性劳役
function.en: Coordinated village relations, mediated disputes, organized defense, and supervised seasonal labor

function.zh: 征集谷物、牲畜与手工业贡品，管理公共储备以应对旱灾与饥荒
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, and craft tribute, and managed communal reserves against drought and famine

function.zh: 维护集市秩序、规范度量交换、向商旅收取通行与市场费用
function.en: Maintained market order, oversaw exchange measures, and collected passage and market fees from traders

function.zh: 主持祭祀、祖先崇拜、土地与水域仪式，并为首领权威提供神圣合法性
function.en: Conducted rituals, ancestor worship, and ceremonies for land and water, providing sacred legitimacy for chiefly authority

function.zh: 组织村社卫队、召集战士、保护农田、牧道与河道贸易
function.en: Organized village guards, mustered warriors, and protected fields, grazing routes, and river trade

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社长老、宗族首领和酋长代理人为核心。大型聚落周边常形成若干附属村落网络，通过贡赋、婚姻与市场联系维系
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on village elders, lineage heads, and agents of chiefs. Around large settlements there were often networks of dependent villages tied by tribute, marriage, and market exchange

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，依赖长老会议、酋长裁决与赔偿原则。案件多涉及土地、水源、婚姻、牲畜与贸易纠纷，处罚通常为赔偿、牲畜罚没、驱逐或血亲调解
legalSystem.en: Law was mainly customary, relying on councils of elders, chief's judgment, and principles of compensation. Cases commonly involved land, water, marriage, livestock, and trade disputes, with punishments usually consisting of compensation, livestock seizure, exile, or kin-based settlement

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要表现为实物贡赋与劳役，包括谷物、鱼干、牲畜、布料、陶器、铁器及集体工程劳动；贸易节点还可能征收市场费和渡口费
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute in kind and labor obligations, including grain, dried fish, livestock, cloth, pottery, iron goods, and communal work; trade centers could also levy market tolls and ferry fees

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然灵崇拜和地方祭祀传统为主，重视河流、洪泛平原、树木、土地与祖灵的神圣性
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and local ritual traditions, emphasizing the sacred power of rivers, floodplains, trees, land, and ancestral beings

philosophy.zh: 思想传统主要体现为口述伦理、长老智慧、家族责任、互助义务与人与土地平衡的观念，而非成文哲学体系
philosophy.en: Its intellectual tradition was expressed through oral ethics, elder wisdom, family duty, mutual obligation, and ideas of balance between people and land rather than through formal written philosophy

writingSystem.zh: 无本土广泛使用的文字系统，知识主要通过口述、记忆、仪式、谱系传承与工艺传统保存
writingSystem.en: There was no widely used indigenous writing system; knowledge was preserved mainly through oral transmission, memory, ritual, genealogy, and craft traditions

culturalAchievements.zh: 杰内杰诺等城市型聚落的发展、先进的陶器传统、区域性铁器生产、渔猎与农耕复合经济、长距离交换网络以及复杂的口述谱系与仪式文化，均显示出尼日尔内三角洲地区高度适应环境的文明形态
culturalAchievements.en: The growth of urban-style settlements such as Jenne-Jeno, advanced pottery traditions, regional iron production, mixed fishing-farming economies, long-distance exchange networks, and complex oral genealogical and ritual culture all demonstrate a highly adaptive civilization in the Inner Niger Delta

languageFamily.zh: 以尼日尔-刚果语系诸语言为主，并与尼罗-撒哈拉语系及撒哈拉边缘语言群发生接触
languageFamily.en: Languages were predominantly from branches of the Niger-Congo family, with contact zones involving Nilo-Saharan and Saharan fringe languages

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、渔业、畜牧业和区域交换为基础。尼日尔河洪泛平原提供高生产力粮食与渔产，杰内杰诺等中心则促进工艺品和远程商品的流通。整体财富水平不高，但生态多样性使经济具有较强韧性
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, fishing, pastoralism, and regional exchange. The Niger floodplains provided highly productive grain and fish supplies, while centers such as Jenne-Jeno promoted the circulation of crafts and long-distance goods. Overall wealth levels were modest, but ecological diversity gave the economy strong resilience

mainIndustries.zh: 洪泛农业、珍珠粟与高粱种植、稻作试验性种植、渔业、畜牧业、陶器制作、铁器冶炼与锻造、纺织与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Floodplain agriculture, pearl millet and sorghum cultivation, limited rice cultivation, fishing, pastoralism, pottery production, iron smelting and smithing, weaving, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、鱼干、盐、牲畜、皮革、陶器、铁器、铜饰品、珠饰、布料与少量跨撒哈拉转运奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dried fish, salt, livestock, leather, pottery, iron tools, copper ornaments, beads, cloth, and small amounts of trans-Saharan luxury goods

name.zh: 实物交换与金属衡量并行

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为粮仓储备、牲畜数量、陶器与铁器工具、渔具、织物及亲属网络，而非大量金属货币积累
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through grain stores, livestock, pottery and iron tools, fishing gear, textiles, and kinship networks rather than large accumulations of coin

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、家族控制资源、地方市场交换和酋长贡赋再分配相结合的混合经济体系
economicSystem.en: A mixed economic system combining village production, family control of resources, local market exchange, and chiefly redistribution through tribute

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要不是金银，而是分散于各中心与附属村社的粮仓、牲畜、盐、铁器和礼品库存。此类储备有助于在旱季、洪水异常或局部冲突时维持稳定
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly not of gold or silver but of distributed reserves of grain, livestock, salt, iron goods, and gift stockpiles held in major centers and dependent villages. Such reserves helped maintain stability during drought, irregular flooding, or local conflict

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守与再分配导向，重视储粮、礼物交换和社会稳定，而非高强度榨取性征税
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and redistribution-oriented, emphasizing grain reserves, gift exchange, and social stability rather than highly extractive taxation

description.zh: 由各地酋长维持的常备武装，负责护卫聚落、市场与首领，装备长矛、标枪、弓箭、木盾及部分铁制武器
description.en: Permanent forces maintained by local chiefs to guard settlements, markets, and leaders, equipped with spears, javelins, bows, wooden shields, and some iron weapons

description.zh: 由农民、渔民与牧民在战时或冲突期征召，主要执行防御、巡逻和保护收获任务
description.en: Raised from farmers, fishers, and pastoralists during war or periods of tension, mainly for defense, patrol, and crop protection

description.zh: 来自萨赫勒边缘牧业群体，擅长远程侦察、突袭和护送牲畜及商队
description.en: Drawn from pastoral groups on the Sahelian margins, skilled in scouting, raiding, and escorting livestock and caravans

description.zh: 部署于河道、渡口和市场航线，保障运输、征收通行费并防范盗匪
description.en: Stationed along waterways, ferries, and market routes to secure transport, collect tolls, and deter banditry

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由诸酋长分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; forces were led separately by local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制矛头、刀具、箭镞、木盾、皮甲和简单防御工事为主，缺乏大规模骑兵与攻城技术，但熟悉河谷、沼泽与草原地形作战
technology.en: Military technology relied on iron spearheads, blades, arrowheads, wooden shields, leather protection, and simple field fortifications. It lacked large cavalry forces and siege techniques but was well adapted to warfare in river valleys, wetlands, and grasslands

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺牧场和水源、河道贸易冲突、盗匪袭扰以及气候波动引发的人口迁移与资源竞争，而非外部帝国直接入侵
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal competition over pasture and water, conflict along trade routes, banditry, and migration driven by climatic fluctuation rather than direct invasion by distant empires

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于尼日尔河内三角洲、萨赫勒农牧混合带及若干集市聚落。多数人口居于村社和小型聚落中，少数生活在杰内杰诺等较大的城市型中心
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across the Inner Niger Delta, mixed farming-pastoral zones of the Sahel, and various market settlements. Most people lived in villages and small communities, with a minority in larger urban-style centers such as Jenne-Jeno

name.zh: 尼日尔内三角洲区域聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会可分为酋长与首领家族、长老与祭司、自由农民与牧民、专业工匠、商旅群体以及依附者。阶层差异存在，但在多数地区仍受亲属关系与村社共治结构缓和
socialClasses.en: Society can be divided into chiefs and leading families, elders and ritual specialists, free farmers and pastoralists, specialist artisans, trading groups, and dependents. Social differences existed, but in most areas they were moderated by kinship ties and communal village structures

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友体系，通常与邻近村社、河谷聚落和部分萨赫勒牧业群体保持婚姻联盟、互市协定与临时防务合作
allies.en: There was no unified external alliance system; relations were usually maintained through marriage alliances, market agreements, and temporary defense cooperation with neighboring villages, river settlements, and some Sahelian pastoral groups

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为争夺资源与贸易路线的邻近部族、盗匪团体及在旱灾时期南下或北上的竞争群体
enemies.en: Primary enemies were neighboring tribes contesting resources and trade routes, bandit groups, and competing populations moving southward or northward during times of drought

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护区域贸易、控制渡口和市场、通过礼物与婚姻维系和平为主，必要时联合周边酋邦进行有限军事应对
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving regional trade, controlling ferries and markets, and maintaining peace through gifts and marriage ties, while forming limited military coalitions with nearby chiefdoms when necessary

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期的萨赫勒河谷文明阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age Sahelian River-Valley Civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁器冶炼与锻造、洪泛平原农业适应技术、渔业与舟运工具、专业陶器生产、粮仓储藏体系以及连接萨赫勒与森林边缘的区域贸易网络
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included established iron smelting and smithing, floodplain agricultural adaptation, fishing and river transport tools, specialized pottery production, grain storage systems, and regional trade networks linking the Sahel with the forest margins

strengths.zh: 该文明的优势在于对尼日尔河流域生态的高度适应、农业渔业牧业互补、聚落网络稳定、地方工艺发达以及早期城市型中心的出现，这些因素共同支撑了长期稳定
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong adaptation to the Niger basin environment, complementary farming-fishing-pastoral economies, stable settlement networks, developed local crafts, and the emergence of early urban-style centers, all of which supported long-term stability

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分散、缺乏统一军事指挥和书写行政系统、财政汲取能力有限，并容易受到气候波动、河道变化和地方冲突影响
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, lack of unified military command and written administration, limited fiscal extraction, and vulnerability to climatic fluctuation, river changes, and local conflicts

outlook.zh: 476年前后的总体前景稳定。若区域贸易继续扩大、聚落整合增强并形成更持久的首领联盟，未来数世纪内有可能孕育更大规模的萨赫勒国家与商业文明
outlook.en: The overall outlook around 476 CE was stable. If regional trade continued to expand, settlement integration deepened, and more durable chiefly alliances emerged, the coming centuries could give rise to larger Sahelian states and commercial civilizations

description.zh: 西非萨赫勒与尼日尔河流域存在众多早期农业与贸易政体，尚未形成后世大帝国。
description.en: The West African Sahel and Niger basin contained many early agrarian and trade polities before the rise of later great empires.

name.zh: 柏柏尔—毛里塔尼亚诸王国

governmentForm.zh: 部族君主制与罗马化地方城邦并存的分散王国体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system of tribal monarchies and Romanized local polities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族联盟、定居农民、城镇居民、地方贵族、罗马化地主、宗教人士与依附民众组成；山地与高原地区以氏族纽带为主，旧罗马城镇周边则保留较强的市镇社会结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of tribal confederations, settled farmers, townspeople, local nobles, Romanized landowners, religious figures, and dependent populations; clan ties remained dominant in the mountains and uplands, while stronger municipal structures survived around former Roman towns.

rulingClass.zh: 柏柏尔部族贵族、地方国王、罗马化地主精英与高级军政首领
rulingClass.en: Berber tribal nobility, local kings, Romanized landed elites, and senior military-political chiefs

succession.zh: 王位继承多依部族习俗、家族血统与贵族拥立而定，通常并非严格长子继承；部分地区由宗族会议或武装贵族认可新王。
succession.en: Succession was shaped by tribal custom, family lineage, and noble endorsement rather than strict primogeniture; in some regions a new king required recognition by clan councils or armed aristocrats.

structure.zh: 476年的西部马格里布不存在统一中央国家，而是由若干柏柏尔王国、部族联盟与延续罗马传统的地方政权构成。阿尔塔瓦等中心由国王及其亲族、部族首领、城镇显贵和军政随从共同治理，王权在核心地区较强，在边缘地带则依赖盟约、贡赋与军事威望维系。
structure.en: In 476 the western Maghreb had no single centralized state, but rather a mosaic of Berber kingdoms, tribal confederations, and local polities preserving Roman traditions. Centers such as Altava were governed through the cooperation of kings and their kin, tribal chiefs, urban notables, and military retainers; royal authority was stronger in core territories and depended on alliances, tribute, and martial prestige in peripheral zones.

function.zh: 管理王室亲族、使节接待、贡物分配、宫廷仪式与国王近侍。
function.en: Managed the royal family, diplomatic reception, distribution of tribute, court ceremony, and the king's attendants.

function.zh: 协调各部族首领、处理盟约、征召随从、调停争端并维持边缘地区忠诚。
function.en: Coordinated tribal chiefs, handled alliances, summoned retainers, mediated disputes, and maintained loyalty in outlying districts.

function.zh: 征收地租、牲畜贡赋、市场税与道路关税，管理粮仓和实物储备。
function.en: Collected land dues, livestock tribute, market taxes, and road tolls, and managed granaries and in-kind reserves.

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、骑兵首领、边防驻点与临时征召部队。
function.en: Oversaw the royal guard, cavalry captains, frontier posts, and temporarily levied forces.

function.zh: 管理保留罗马传统的城镇、文书、土地界限、契约与部分司法事务。
function.en: Administered towns retaining Roman traditions, records, land boundaries, contracts, and parts of the judicial system.

function.zh: 与地方主教、基督教团体及传统祭祀领袖沟通，调解宗教与政治关系。
function.en: Liaised with local bishops, Christian communities, and leaders of traditional cults, mediating between religious and political authority.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政极不均一。旧罗马城镇周边保留市镇议员、地主与主教参与治理；乡村和高原地区则由部族酋长、氏族长老与王室任命的代理人共同管理。许多地区的统治更依赖人身依附、家族联盟和季节性军事巡行，而非固定文官层级。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly uneven. Around former Roman towns, civic notables, landowners, and bishops still participated in governance; in the countryside and uplands, tribal chiefs, clan elders, and royal agents ruled jointly. In many areas authority relied more on personal dependency, family alliances, and seasonal military circuits than on a fixed bureaucratic hierarchy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系具有混合性：城镇与罗马化地区延续晚期罗马法律传统、土地契约和书面仲裁；部族地区则以习惯法、赔偿、长老会议裁决和武力威慑为主。涉及婚姻、放牧、水源和复仇的争端常由部族习俗处理，而涉及地产和城镇商业的案件更可能采用罗马化文书与见证制度。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was mixed: towns and Romanized areas preserved late Roman legal traditions, land contracts, and written arbitration, while tribal zones relied more on customary law, compensation, councils of elders, and coercive enforcement. Disputes over marriage, pasturage, water, and vengeance were often handled by tribal custom, whereas property and urban commercial cases were more likely to use Romanized documents and witness procedures.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自地租、谷物与橄榄油实物征收、牲畜贡赋、牧场使用费、集市税、商路关税以及附庸部族的朝贡。税制并不统一，各王国多按地区传统征收，实物税比现金税更常见。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from land dues, in-kind collections of grain and olive oil, livestock tribute, pasture fees, market taxes, caravan tolls, and tribute from dependent tribes. Taxation was not standardized, and each kingdom collected according to local practice, with in-kind levies more common than cash taxes.

religion.zh: 宗教生活呈多元并存状态。基督教在许多城镇、罗马化居民和部分王室圈层中占重要地位，尤其是拉丁语传统地区；与此同时，柏柏尔传统信仰、祖先崇拜、地方圣地与山野祭祀仍在乡村和部族社会延续。部分地区还受到多纳图派等北非基督教内部传统影响。
religion.en: Religious life was plural. Christianity held an important place in many towns, among Romanized populations, and within some royal circles, especially in Latin-speaking regions; at the same time, Berber traditional beliefs, ancestor reverence, local sanctuaries, and rural cult practices persisted in the countryside and tribal society. Some regions were also influenced by internal North African Christian traditions such as Donatism.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化以基督教伦理、晚期罗马拉丁教育残余、部族荣誉观念与实用性政治智慧为主。正式哲学传统有限，但教会训诫、法律观念、家族名望和口述传统共同塑造社会价值。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped primarily by Christian ethics, remnants of late Roman Latin education, tribal honor values, and pragmatic political wisdom. Formal philosophical traditions were limited, but church teaching, legal concepts, family prestige, and oral tradition together shaped social values.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系为拉丁字母，用于教会、契约、墓志和行政文书；在广大柏柏尔语社会中，口述传统仍更重要，地方口语通常不系统书写。
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Latin alphabet, used in churches, contracts, epitaphs, and administration; across the wider Berber-speaking society, oral tradition remained more important, and local vernaculars were generally not written systematically.

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的文化成就体现在罗马—柏柏尔融合：边疆城镇继续使用拉丁文书、基督教教堂和墓葬铭文，乡村则保留传统服饰、口头史诗、骑马与牧养文化。建筑上多延续晚期罗马石构、堡垒化聚落和乡间庄园形式；艺术以简朴的宗教装饰、石雕和实用器物为主。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievement in this period lay in Romano-Berber synthesis: frontier towns continued to use Latin documents, Christian churches, and funerary inscriptions, while the countryside preserved traditional dress, oral epic traditions, and equestrian-pastoral culture. Architecture largely continued late Roman stone building, fortified settlements, and rural estate forms; art centered on modest religious decoration, stone carving, and practical objects.

languageFamily.zh: 主要为亚非语系柏柏尔语支，并在城镇和教会中广泛使用通俗拉丁语及其地方变体
languageFamily.en: Primarily the Berber branch of the Afroasiatic family, alongside widespread Vulgar Latin and its local varieties in towns and church life

unit.zh: 索利都斯金币每人年值

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农牧混合和区域性贸易为主，整体富裕程度低于北非沿海最繁荣的罗马行省，但在内陆西马格里布已属较稳定且具韧性的经济体系。财富分布高度不均，王室、贵族和大地主掌握较多余粮、牲畜与金银器物。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on mixed agriculture, pastoralism, and regional trade. Overall prosperity was below that of the richest coastal Roman provinces of North Africa, but within the inland western Maghreb it was a relatively stable and resilient economic system. Wealth was highly unequal, with royal households, nobles, and major landowners controlling much of the surplus grain, livestock, and precious metal goods.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧羊牧牛、马匹饲养、橄榄栽培、皮革加工、陶器、盐与地方金属工艺
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, sheep and cattle herding, horse breeding, olive cultivation, leatherworking, pottery, salt, and local metal crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、橄榄油、牲畜、马匹、皮革、羊毛、蜂蜡、盐、陶器，以及输入的精制器皿、布匹和部分奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, livestock, horses, leather, wool, beeswax, salt, pottery, and imported fine wares, textiles, and some luxury goods

name.zh: 索利都斯及地方实物交换

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以土地使用权、谷物储备、橄榄树、牲畜和家族网络体现，而非大量铸币。城镇精英和教会机构更可能积累金银器、进口陶器和书面地产契据。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in access to land, grain stores, olive trees, livestock, and kin networks rather than in large coin reserves. Urban elites and church institutions were more likely to accumulate silverware, imported pottery, and written property deeds.

unit.zh: 索利都斯金币每劳动者年值

economicSystem.zh: 以地方自给农业和牧业为基础、辅以区域市场交换和有限地中海联系的分散经济；现金流通存在，但实物征收与互惠关系更为关键。
economicSystem.en: A decentralized economy based on local subsistence farming and pastoralism, supplemented by regional market exchange and limited Mediterranean links; coin circulated, but in-kind collection and reciprocal obligations were more important.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非完全集中于单一地点，而是分散保存在王室中心、要塞粮仓、贵族保管处和教会网络中。大量财富以谷物、牲畜、布匹和贵重器物形式存在，铸币储备有限但足以支付卫队、外交礼物和紧急军费。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not concentrated in a single location, but dispersed among royal centers, fortified granaries, noble custodians, and church networks. Much wealth existed as grain, livestock, cloth, and prestige objects, while coin reserves were limited but sufficient for guards, diplomatic gifts, and emergency military needs.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重在维持王室威望、边防骑兵与地方忠诚。统治者通常避免过度现金化征收，更倾向于接受实物、牲畜和服役义务，以减少对脆弱地方经济的冲击。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, focused on maintaining royal prestige, frontier cavalry, and local loyalty. Rulers generally avoided excessive monetized extraction and preferred in-kind payments, livestock, and service obligations so as not to overstrain fragile local economies.

description.zh: 以机动性为核心的精锐力量，由王室随从、贵族战士和富裕牧民组成，适合侦察、突袭、追击与边地威慑。
description.en: The elite mobile arm, composed of royal retainers, noble warriors, and prosperous pastoralists, suited to reconnaissance, raiding, pursuit, and frontier intimidation.

description.zh: 由各部族和农村社群临时征召，装备长矛、盾牌、投枪和少量剑，适合守卫山地通道、村镇与集结地。
description.en: Levied from tribes and rural communities, armed with spears, shields, javelins, and some swords, and effective in defending passes, villages, and assembly points.

description.zh: 驻守旧罗马据点、道路节点和边境堡垒，负责警戒、通报、护送商路和控制税关。
description.en: Stationed in former Roman strongpoints, road nodes, and frontier forts, responsible for watch, signaling, escorting trade routes, and controlling toll stations.

description.zh: 常备核心兵力，负责保护国王、宫廷和主要都城，同时在战时担任战场中坚。
description.en: The permanent core force protecting the king, court, and principal centers, while serving as a battlefield nucleus in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 诸王各自统军，辅以部族首领与王室军事随从长
commanderInChief.en: Each king commanded in person, assisted by tribal chiefs and royal military retainers

technology.zh: 军事技术处于晚期古典水平，重视骑兵机动、投枪和近战武器，防御上使用石砌堡垒、瞭望点和旧罗马道路网络。装备包括长矛、标枪、圆盾、皮甲、锁甲少量流通，以及从罗马和汪达尔世界获得的头盔与金属兵装。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a late classical level, emphasizing cavalry mobility, javelins, and close-combat weapons, while defense relied on stone forts, watchpoints, and the old Roman road network. Equipment included spears, javelins, round shields, leather armor, limited mail, and helmets and metal gear obtained from the Roman and Vandal worlds.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与汪达尔北非势力的边境摩擦、邻近柏柏尔政权间竞争、草原化边缘地带的袭扰，以及沿海政治变动对贸易和忠诚关系的冲击。
threats.en: Main threats came from border friction with Vandal North Africa, rivalry among neighboring Berber polities, raiding in semi-arid frontier zones, and the disruption of trade and loyalties caused by changing coastal politics.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于西部马格里布内陆高原、河谷、山麓与旧罗马城镇周边，整体密度不高。多数居民为柏柏尔语族群，同时有相当数量罗马化拉丁语居民，特别是在城镇、教会中心和农业较发达地区。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the inland plateaus, valleys, foothills, and surroundings of former Roman towns in the western Maghreb, with generally low density. Most inhabitants were Berber-speaking, alongside a significant Romanized Latin-speaking population, especially in towns, ecclesiastical centers, and better-developed agricultural zones.

name.zh: 鲁西卡德周边内陆市镇

socialClasses.zh: 上层为地方国王、部族贵族、罗马化地主和高级教士；中层包括小地主、骑兵战士、商人、工匠和市镇精英；下层为佃农、牧民、普通农民、依附部众和奴仆。不同地区社会差异明显，部族身份与地方出身常比单纯财富更重要。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included local kings, tribal nobles, Romanized landowners, and senior clergy; the middle strata included small landholders, cavalrymen, merchants, artisans, and urban notables; the lower strata consisted of tenants, pastoralists, ordinary farmers, dependent tribesmen, and servants or slaves. Social variation between regions was strong, and tribal identity and local origin often mattered as much as wealth.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友体系；部分王国与邻近柏柏尔部族、地方罗马化城镇或反汪达尔势力保持临时合作。
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; some kingdoms maintained temporary cooperation with neighboring Berber tribes, local Romanized towns, or anti-Vandal forces.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象因地区而异，通常包括竞争性的柏柏尔王国、试图控制内陆通道的汪达尔势力，以及拒绝纳贡的边缘部族。
enemies.en: Enemies varied by region, but commonly included rival Berber kingdoms, Vandal forces seeking influence over inland routes, and peripheral tribes refusing tribute.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持地方自主、控制商路、平衡沿海强权和内陆部族关系为核心。统治者常通过婚姻、馈赠、互不侵犯协定、朝贡安排和军事援助建立短期联盟，而非追求长期统一外交体系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving local autonomy, controlling trade routes, and balancing coastal powers against inland tribal relations. Rulers commonly built short-term alliances through marriage, gift exchange, non-aggression pacts, tribute arrangements, and military aid rather than through a durable unified diplomatic system.

era.zh: 晚期古代，罗马世界解体时期
era.en: Late Antiquity, during the disintegration of the Roman world

keyInnovations.zh: 并无革命性新技术，但有效继承了晚期罗马的石构防御、蓄水设施、橄榄与谷物加工技术、马具使用、道路节点控制以及拉丁文书行政实践。
keyInnovations.en: There were no revolutionary new inventions, but the civilization effectively inherited late Roman stone fortification, water storage, olive and grain processing techniques, horse equipment usage, control of road nodes, and Latin documentary administrative practice.

strengths.zh: 优势在于地形适应力强、骑兵机动优秀、社会结构具有韧性、地方粮食与牧业基础稳固，并能灵活吸收罗马化制度遗产。分散政治结构虽限制统一，但也降低了整体崩溃风险。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong adaptation to terrain, effective mobile cavalry, resilient social structures, solid agricultural and pastoral bases, and flexible absorption of Roman institutional legacies. Though politically fragmented, this decentralization also reduced the risk of total collapse.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一中央权威、财政和文官体系有限、城镇网络规模不大、内部竞争频繁，且容易受沿海强权和跨区域贸易衰退影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the absence of unified central authority, limited fiscal and bureaucratic capacity, a modest urban network, frequent internal rivalry, and vulnerability to coastal powers and the decline of long-distance trade.

outlook.zh: 476年前后，该文明总体稳定，并将在后罗马北非秩序重组中继续发挥重要作用。短期内，内陆柏柏尔王国有望保持自治并扩大对旧罗马腹地的控制；长期则将持续在罗马、汪达尔与本地部族传统之间演化，形成更鲜明的区域王权格局。
outlook.en: Around 476, this civilization was broadly stable and would continue to play an important role in the reordering of post-Roman North Africa. In the short term, inland Berber kingdoms were likely to preserve autonomy and expand influence over former Roman hinterlands; over the longer term, they would continue evolving between Roman, Vandal, and indigenous tribal traditions into more distinct regional monarchies.

description.zh: 北非西部内陆与山地由多个柏柏尔王国和罗马化地方政权分治。
description.en: The western Maghreb interior and uplands were divided among Berber kingdoms and Romanized local polities.

governmentForm.zh: 以王族为核心、依托部族首领和商道控制的早期君主制邦国。
governmentForm.en: An early monarchical state centered on a royal lineage and sustained through tribal chiefs and control of trade routes.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、战士贵族、祭司、商人、自由农牧民与附庸群体构成。不同氏族与地方社群保留相当自主性，但在贸易、贡赋与军事保护上服从王权。城市化程度不高，聚落和市场节点比大型城市更重要。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal clan, warrior nobles, ritual specialists, merchants, free farmers and pastoralists, and dependent groups. Different clans and local communities retained considerable autonomy but submitted to royal authority in matters of trade, tribute, and military protection. Urbanization remained limited, and settlements and market nodes were more important than large cities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以瓦加杜王族及其附属部族首领为主，权力建立在黄金产地、跨撒哈拉通道和牲畜资源的控制上。宫廷祭司与商队保护者也具有较高地位。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was led by the Wagadou royal lineage and subordinate tribal chiefs, whose power rested on control of gold-producing zones, trans-Saharan routes, and livestock resources. Court ritual specialists and caravan protectors also held high status.

succession.zh: 王位多在王族内部继承，可能伴随氏族长老与贵族认可。继承规则带有口传传统色彩，王位合法性常与祭祀权威和财富再分配能力相联系。
succession.en: The throne generally passed within the royal lineage, likely with the approval of clan elders and nobles. Succession followed oral customary traditions, and legitimacy was closely tied to ritual authority and the ability to redistribute wealth.

structure.zh: 早期加纳政权结构相对简约，王廷负责贸易保护、纠纷裁决与对附属社群征贡。地方层面由氏族首领和聚落长者治理，中央更多通过盟约、馈赠和军事威慑维系统一。
structure.en: The early Ghanaian state had a relatively simple structure, with the royal court overseeing trade protection, dispute settlement, and tribute collection from subordinate communities. At the local level, clan chiefs and settlement elders governed, while the center maintained unity chiefly through alliance, gift exchange, and military deterrence.

function.zh: 负责王廷日常、贡品接收、使节接待与财富再分配。
function.en: Managed court routine, reception of tribute, envoys, and redistribution of wealth.

function.zh: 负责保护商道、征收贸易税、维持市场秩序和与商队协调。
function.en: Protected trade routes, collected trade dues, maintained market order, and coordinated with caravans.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以氏族、聚落和贸易节点为核心，由部族首领、长老和王室代理人共同运作。中央对核心商路控制较强，对边缘地带主要依靠朝贡关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on clans, settlements, and trade nodes, operated by tribal chiefs, elders, and royal agents. The center exercised stronger control over core trade corridors, while peripheral areas were tied mainly through tributary relations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传习惯法、长老裁决和王室仲裁为主。涉及商路安全、牲畜争端、婚姻和贡赋义务的案件最为重要。
legalSystem.en: Law was based primarily on oral custom, elder adjudication, and royal arbitration. Cases involving caravan security, livestock disputes, marriage, and tribute obligations were especially important.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自黄金与盐贸易税、附属社群贡品以及牲畜和农产征收。大部分税负以实物、金砂或贸易抽成形式支付。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income came mainly from taxes on gold and salt trade, tribute from subordinate communities, and levies on livestock and produce. Most obligations were paid in kind, gold dust, or commercial shares.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 早期加纳的重要成就在于形成王权—商路—贡赋相结合的萨赫勒国家模式，并发展出稳定的长距离交换网络。口述传统、祭祀制度与黄金财富展示构成其文化核心。
culturalAchievements.en: Early Ghana’s main achievement was the formation of a Sahelian state model combining kingship, trade-route control, and tribute, along with stable long-distance exchange networks. Oral traditions, ritual institutions, and the display of gold wealth formed its cultural core.

languageFamily.zh: 曼德语族与索宁克语支背景
languageFamily.en: Mande family, with a Soninke background

philosophy.zh: 以西非传统泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜和王权神圣性观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on West African animist beliefs, ancestor veneration, and the sacral character of kingship.

writingSystem.zh: 主要依靠口传传统，无本地广泛行政文字系统；跨撒哈拉交流中可能接触到柏柏尔和早期阿拉伯书写文化，但尚未制度化。
writingSystem.en: It relied mainly on oral tradition and lacked a broadly used indigenous administrative writing system; through trans-Saharan contact it may have encountered Berber and early Arabic literate cultures, but these were not yet institutionalized.

gdpDescription.zh: 早期加纳经济规模不大，但因处于金盐交换网络节点，其人均财富水平在周边萨赫勒社会中相对可观。农业、畜牧与商路税收共同构成其繁荣基础。
gdpDescription.en: Early Ghana’s economy was not large in absolute terms, but as a node in the gold-salt exchange system its per capita wealth was relatively notable for the surrounding Sahel. Agriculture, pastoralism, and trade-route taxation together underpinned its prosperity.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、粟类农业、黄金贸易、盐转运、皮革与手工业
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, millet agriculture, gold trade, salt transit, leatherwork, and crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 金砂、盐、奴隶、皮革、象牙、铜器、谷物、牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Gold dust, salt, slaves, leather, ivory, copper goods, grain, and livestock

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多体现为牲畜、谷仓储备、工具、首饰和亲族网络。商人和首领拥有更多黄金、进口盐与奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was commonly held in livestock, grain storage, tools, ornaments, and kinship ties. Merchants and chiefs possessed larger amounts of gold, imported salt, and prestige goods.

economicSystem.zh: 以农牧业自给为基础，叠加跨撒哈拉贸易、王室抽税与贡赋再分配的混合经济体系。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian-pastoral subsistence, overlaid by trans-Saharan trade, royal taxation, and tribute redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备主要以金砂、牲畜、盐块、谷物和奢侈品形式保存，而非标准化铸币。其储备能力受商道畅通与降雨周期影响显著。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves were held mainly as gold dust, livestock, salt slabs, grain, and prestige goods rather than standardized coinage. Storage capacity was heavily affected by caravan traffic and rainfall cycles.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政上强调控制商路节点、维持附庸忠诚，并通过礼物与再分配巩固王权。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized controlling trade-route nodes, maintaining subordinate loyalty, and reinforcing kingship through gifts and redistribution.

description.zh: 以矛、盾和投射武器为主，负责聚落防御、护卫商队和地区征战。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, shields, and projectile weapons, they defended settlements, escorted caravans, and fought local campaigns.

description.zh: 规模有限，主要用于巡逻、快速突袭和控制萨赫勒通道。
description.en: Limited in scale, used mainly for patrol, rapid raids, and control of Sahelian corridors.

description.zh: 负责宫廷安全、征贡监督和关键时刻的精锐作战。
description.en: Responsible for court security, oversight of tribute, and elite combat in critical situations.

technology.zh: 军事技术处于萨赫勒早期国家水平，依赖铁制兵器、皮革防护和机动巡逻，但缺乏复杂攻城与重装体系。
technology.en: Military technology stood at an early Sahelian state level, relying on iron weapons, leather protection, and mobile patrols, but lacking sophisticated siege systems or heavy armor.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边竞争性部族、商路劫掠者、气候波动导致的资源争夺，以及对黄金通道的控制竞争。
threats.en: Major threats came from rival neighboring tribes, caravan raiders, climate-driven competition for resources, and struggles over access to gold routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布以萨赫勒村落、牧营和贸易聚点为主，集中度较低。核心王都与市场聚落虽重要，但整体仍是以乡村和半流动社群为主的社会。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across Sahelian villages, pastoral camps, and trade points, with relatively low concentration. Although the royal center and market settlements were important, society remained predominantly rural and semi-mobile.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、部族首领、战士、祭司、商人、自由农牧民、附庸与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal lineage, tribal chiefs, warriors, ritual specialists, merchants, free farmers and pastoralists, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 主要通过附属氏族、商队伙伴和邻近萨赫勒社群形成松散同盟网络。
allies.en: Its allies were mainly found in loose networks of subordinate clans, caravan partners, and neighboring Sahelian communities.

enemies.zh: 并无单一固定大敌，但常与争夺贸易通道的周边部族和劫掠集团发生冲突。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent great enemy, but often clashed with neighboring tribes and raiding groups competing for trade corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重点在于确保商路安全、吸纳贡附社群并垄断金盐转运中的中介地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing trade routes, incorporating tributary communities, and monopolizing an intermediary role in the gold-salt trade.

era.zh: 西非萨赫勒早期铁器时代国家形成期
era.en: Early Iron Age state-formation phase in the West African Sahel

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器使用、长距离商路组织、王权贡赋体系、干旱带农牧结合生计模式。
keyInnovations.en: Use of iron tools and weapons, organization of long-distance trade routes, royal tribute systems, and mixed agro-pastoral lifeways adapted to the dry zone.

strengths.zh: 地处黄金与盐贸易中介位置，王权能够通过商路税收和再分配建立影响力。
strengths.en: It occupied an intermediary position in the gold-salt trade, allowing kingship to build influence through route taxation and redistribution.

weaknesses.zh: 行政整合有限，城市基础薄弱，对气候、商路波动和地方忠诚依赖很大。
weaknesses.en: Administrative integration was limited, urban foundations were weak, and the state depended heavily on climate, caravan traffic, and local loyalty.

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制跨撒哈拉贸易并扩展附属网络，早期加纳有潜力发展为更成熟的萨赫勒帝国。
outlook.en: If it could sustain control over trans-Saharan trade and broaden its subordinate networks, early Ghana had the potential to develop into a more mature Sahelian empire.

description.zh: 476年前后的早期加纳是一个以商路控制、黄金中介和部族王权为基础的萨赫勒新兴国家。
description.en: Early Ghana around 476 was a rising Sahelian state built on trade-route control, gold intermediation, and tribal kingship.

governmentForm.zh: 由切拉王室统治的世袭王国，依靠宫廷、地方首领和港口网络维持统治。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary kingdom ruled by the Chera royal house, sustained through the court, local chiefs, and port networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构由王族、婆罗门、武士贵族、商人、农民、工匠和被支配劳动力组成。种姓与地方氏族传统共同塑造社会秩序，港口地区则因海外贸易而更具流动性。宗教上以印度教为主，同时吸纳耆那教、佛教和地方神灵崇拜影响。
socialStructure.en: The social structure included the royal family, Brahmins, warrior nobles, merchants, cultivators, artisans, and subordinate laborers. Caste distinctions and local clan traditions together shaped social order, while port regions were more fluid because of overseas trade. Religion was predominantly Hindu, while also absorbing influences from Jainism, Buddhism, and local cults.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由切拉王室、婆罗门顾问、地方酋长和港口商业精英构成。王权在内陆农业区与沿海贸易区之间充当协调与仲裁中心。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Chera royal house, Brahmin advisers, regional chiefs, and port-based commercial elites. Royal power acted as a coordinating and arbitrating center between inland agrarian zones and coastal trade regions.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部传承，但具体继承常受支系力量、地方首领支持和宫廷协商影响。南印度王朝常见的旁系继承与共治现象在此时期亦可能存在。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal lineage, but actual succession was influenced by collateral branches, support from regional chiefs, and court negotiation. Collateral succession and forms of shared authority common in South Indian dynasties may also have existed in this period.

structure.zh: 切拉王国以王廷为核心，统辖内陆农业区、山地资源带与沿海港口。中央行政不如后世帝国严密，但通过婆罗门文书传统、地方首领和港口税务官实现有效统治。
structure.en: The Chera kingdom was centered on the royal court and ruled over inland agrarian zones, upland resource areas, and coastal ports. Its central administration was less rigid than that of later empires, but it governed effectively through Brahmin scribal traditions, regional chiefs, and port tax officials.

function.zh: 负责王令、文书、礼仪、对地方首领的协调与使节接待。
function.en: Handled royal orders, records, ceremony, coordination with regional chiefs, and reception of envoys.

function.zh: 负责港口管理、关税征收、仓储与海外商人监管。
function.en: Managed ports, customs collection, storage, and supervision of overseas merchants.

function.zh: 负责农业税、灌溉维护、土地登记与王室仓储。
function.en: Oversaw agrarian taxes, irrigation maintenance, land recording, and royal granaries.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖纳德、村社与地方首领，王室通过税务官、寺庙网络和军事干预保持影响力。沿海港口通常拥有较高自治性，但需向王室缴纳关税和贡品。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on nadus, village communities, and regional chiefs, while the crown maintained influence through tax agents, temple networks, and military intervention. Coastal ports often enjoyed substantial autonomy but owed customs and tribute to the crown.

legalSystem.zh: 法律混合了达摩传统、地方习惯法和王室裁断。涉及土地、商业契约、港税、婚姻与种姓秩序的纠纷最为常见。
legalSystem.en: Law blended dharmic tradition, local custom, and royal adjudication. Disputes over land, commercial contracts, port duties, marriage, and caste order were among the most common.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、港口关税、贸易抽成和附属首领贡纳为主。税收既可用粮食、香料、织物支付，也可用金属货币和贵重物折算。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on land revenue, port customs, trade shares, and tribute from subordinate chiefs. Taxes could be paid in grain, spices, and cloth, or commuted into coin and valuables.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 切拉地区延续了古典泰米尔文学传统，并在港口文化、寺庙捐赠、香料贸易和跨洋交流中展现活力。其地理位置使其成为印度洋世界中南印度文化传播的重要节点。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chera realm continued the classical Tamil literary tradition and showed vitality in port culture, temple patronage, spice trade, and transoceanic exchange. Its geography made it an important South Indian node in the wider Indian Ocean world.

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系
languageFamily.en: Dravidian

philosophy.zh: 以印度教达摩观念、泰米尔—喀拉拉地方传统和婆罗门礼仪文化为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Hindu dharmic ideas, Tamil-Kerala regional traditions, and Brahmanical ritual culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期南婆罗米及其演变形态书写泰米尔语与行政铭文，并在宗教与学术场合使用梵语。
writingSystem.en: Used early Southern Brahmi-derived scripts for Tamil and administrative inscriptions, while Sanskrit was employed in religious and scholarly contexts.

gdpDescription.zh: 切拉王国经济在南印度诸政权中相对富庶，兼具高产农业、山地资源和海上贸易收益。黑胡椒等香料及港口税收显著提高了其人均财富水平。
gdpDescription.en: The Chera kingdom was relatively prosperous among South Indian polities, combining productive agriculture, upland resources, and maritime trade revenue. Spices such as black pepper and port customs significantly raised per capita wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、香料生产、海上贸易、纺织、金属加工、林产与象牙贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, spice production, maritime trade, textiles, metalworking, forest products, and ivory trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑胡椒、豆蔻、珍珠、象牙、木材、纺织品、米、金属器、宝石
tradeGoods.en: Black pepper, cardamom, pearls, ivory, timber, textiles, rice, metal goods, and gemstones

name.zh: 本地铜币与外来金币并用
name.en: Local copper coinage and imported gold coin circulation

householdWealth.zh: 农户财富主要表现为土地、灌溉条件、谷物储备和牲畜；港口商人与寺庙则积累金银、珠宝和海外商品。贵族财富与土地控制和贸易分成密切相关。
householdWealth.en: Peasant wealth was mainly expressed through land, irrigation access, grain reserves, and livestock, while port merchants and temples accumulated gold, silver, jewels, and imported goods. Aristocratic wealth was closely tied to land control and shares in trade.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业和寺庙—王室土地体系为基础，辅以港口贸易、商人社群和税收分成的复合经济。
economicSystem.en: A composite economy based on agriculture and temple-royal land systems, supplemented by port commerce, merchant communities, and shared tax revenues.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库包括谷仓储备、香料库存、铜币、金饰及来自海外贸易的贵重物。沿海贸易收入使其现金化程度高于许多同期内陆王国。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury included grain reserves, spice stockpiles, copper coinage, gold ornaments, and valuables from overseas trade. Coastal commerce made it more monetized than many contemporary inland kingdoms.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重视港口税源、土地收益和通过寺庙赏赐巩固王权与地方合作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized port revenues, land income, and the reinforcement of royal-local cooperation through temple patronage and grants.

description.zh: 由王室军队和地方首领部众组成，擅长近战、弓箭和守卫山地通道。
description.en: Composed of royal troops and contingents from local chiefs, skilled in close combat, archery, and guarding upland passes.

description.zh: 用于冲击、威慑和王权展示，是南印度战争中的重要精锐力量。
description.en: Used for shock action, intimidation, and royal display, they were an important elite component in South Indian warfare.

description.zh: 负责保护港口、商船停泊点和沿海税关，兼具有限水上作战能力。
description.en: Protected ports, anchorages, and coastal customs stations, with limited maritime combat capability.

technology.zh: 军事技术包括铁制兵器、战象运用、木石堡垒和沿海舰船，但缺乏后世那种高度制度化常备军体系。
technology.en: Military technology included iron weaponry, war-elephant deployment, timber-and-stone fortifications, and coastal vessels, though it lacked the highly institutionalized standing armies of later periods.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自潘地亚、朱罗等南印度竞争政权，以及地方首领割据和海上贸易受扰。
threats.en: Major threats came from rival South Indian powers such as the Pandyas and Cholas, as well as from regional fragmentation and disruptions to maritime trade.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布在西海岸港口、河谷稻作区和山麓香料带之间，农村人口占主体。港口聚居地因海外贸易而较为多元，吸引商人、手工业者和宗教团体。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed among west-coast ports, riverine rice zones, and foothill spice belts, with rural inhabitants forming the majority. Port settlements were more diverse because of overseas trade, attracting merchants, artisans, and religious communities.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、婆罗门、武士贵族、商人、农民、工匠、渔民、劳役者与奴仆。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, Brahmins, warrior nobles, merchants, cultivators, artisans, fishers, laborers, and slaves/servants.

allies.zh: 与部分港口商人团体、地方首领及印度洋贸易伙伴维持互利关系。
allies.en: It maintained mutually beneficial ties with certain port merchant groups, regional chiefs, and Indian Ocean trade partners.

enemies.zh: 与潘地亚、朱罗等南印度政权长期存在竞争，并可能因港口与贸易利益发生冲突。
enemies.en: It remained in long-term rivalry with South Indian powers such as the Pandyas and Cholas, often clashing over ports and trade interests.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以控制西海岸贸易、平衡南印度诸国和维护港口繁荣为核心目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling west-coast trade, balancing against other South Indian states, and preserving the prosperity of its ports.

era.zh: 南印度古典晚期海贸王国阶段
era.en: Late Classical South Indian maritime kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 季风航海网络、香料商品化、南婆罗米书写传统、灌溉农业与战象军事组织。
keyInnovations.en: Monsoon sailing networks, commercialization of spices, Southern Brahmi writing traditions, irrigated agriculture, and military organization using war elephants.

strengths.zh: 拥有香料贸易优势、港口税收和较强文化辐射力，经济多样化程度高。
strengths.en: It possessed advantages in the spice trade, port revenues, and cultural influence, with a relatively diversified economy.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权有限，易受地方首领、王位继承争议和海贸波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was limited, and the kingdom was vulnerable to regional chiefs, succession disputes, and fluctuations in maritime trade.

outlook.zh: 若能维持港口控制并压制内部分裂，切拉王国仍可在南印度与印度洋贸易中保持重要地位。
outlook.en: If it could retain control of its ports and suppress internal fragmentation, the Chera kingdom could continue to hold an important place in South India and the Indian Ocean trade sphere.

description.zh: 476年前后的切拉王朝是一个依托香料、港口与内陆农业而繁荣的南印度海贸王国。
description.en: The Chera realm around 476 was a prosperous South Indian maritime kingdom built on spices, ports, and inland agriculture.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭王权为核心、依托地方贵族与庙宇网络治理的南印度君主制王国。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary South Indian monarchy centered on royal authority and governed through local elites and temple networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以婆罗门、武士与地方地主集团为上层，农民、手工业者与商人为主体。村社在土地占有、灌溉维护与税赋分配上具有重要作用。种姓秩序已相当稳固，但地方习惯与区域身份同样影响社会组织。
socialStructure.en: Society was led by Brahmins, warrior elites, and local landed groups, while cultivators, artisans, and merchants formed the majority. Village communities played an important role in landholding, irrigation upkeep, and tax allocation. Caste hierarchy was already significant, though local custom and regional identity also shaped social organization.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由朱罗王族、附属酋长、军事贵族和受赐地的婆罗门组成。王权在名义上居于最高地位，但对地方资源的控制依赖与各地首领的合作。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Chola royal house, subordinate chiefs, military nobles, and land-endowed Brahmins. Royal authority stood at the top in theory, but effective control of local resources depended on cooperation with regional leaders.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭传承，多由父子继承或近支宗族承继。若中央势弱，地方贵族与旁支王族可能介入继承安排。
succession.en: The throne usually passed hereditarily within the royal family, most often from father to son or to a close agnatic relative. When the center was weak, local nobles and collateral branches could influence succession.

structure.zh: 王国行政规模不大，宫廷直接掌握王室领地、贡赋与军事调动。更广大地区依靠地方酋长、村社长老与宗教机构协助治理。中央命令的执行力因地区而异，呈现分层统治特征。
structure.en: The kingdom’s administrative apparatus was modest, with the court directly controlling royal lands, tribute, and military mobilization. Wider territories were governed with the help of local chiefs, village elders, and religious institutions. Enforcement of central orders varied by region, creating a layered pattern of rule.

function.zh: 管理王室土地、贡赋、仓储与宫廷支出。
function.en: Managed royal lands, tribute, granaries, and court expenditure.

function.zh: 负责征召军队、守卫要地并协调附属首领的兵役义务。
function.en: Oversaw troop levies, guarded key points, and coordinated military obligations of subordinate chiefs.

function.zh: 记录耕地、监督水利与估定农业税基。
function.en: Recorded cultivated land, supervised irrigation, and assessed the agricultural tax base.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠村社、地方首领和受王室承认的税收中介。乌赖尤尔周边控制较强，边缘地带则更接近宗主权而非直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on village communities, regional chiefs, and crown-recognized tax intermediaries. Control was stronger around Uraiyur, while peripheral areas were governed more through suzerainty than direct rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以达摩传统、王室裁断和地方习惯法并行为主。土地、婚姻和债务纠纷多在地方层面裁决，重大案件由王廷或贵族审理。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on dharmic norms, royal judgment, and local customary practice. Disputes over land, marriage, and debt were often settled locally, while major cases were heard by the court or nobles.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税为主，辅以贸易税、贡赋和劳役。水利条件较好的稻作区承担更稳定税负，边地常以实物或部族贡纳形式缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land revenue, supplemented by trade dues, tribute, and labor obligations. Well-irrigated rice zones bore more regular burdens, while frontier areas often paid in kind or through tribal tribute.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 朱罗地区延续了古典泰米尔文化传统，诗歌、赞颂文学与宗教捐赠铭文逐渐制度化。灌溉农业与寺庙赞助共同塑造了区域性的王权文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chola region preserved the classical Tamil cultural tradition, with poetry, panegyric literature, and donative inscriptions becoming more institutionalized. Irrigated agriculture and temple patronage together shaped a regional culture of kingship.

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系，以古泰米尔语为主，精英宗教语境中使用梵语。
languageFamily.en: Dravidian language family, primarily Old Tamil, with Sanskrit used in elite religious contexts.

philosophy.zh: 婆罗门教—印度教达摩秩序、地方神祇崇拜与早期南印度王权观念并存。
philosophy.en: Brahmanical-Hindu dharmic order coexisted with local cults and early South Indian ideas of kingship.

writingSystem.zh: 泰米尔-婆罗米后裔文字与早期瓦特卢图、同时在宗教与铭文中使用梵文书写传统。
writingSystem.en: Descendant scripts of Tamil-Brahmi and early Vatteluttu, alongside Sanskrit epigraphic usage in religious and formal contexts.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业、畜力耕作和地方手工业为基础。尽管王朝处于相对衰落期，卡韦里流域的农业与沿海交换仍维持了可观产出。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture, animal-powered cultivation, and local craft production. Although the dynasty was in relative decline, the Kaveri region’s agriculture and coastal exchange still sustained notable output.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、棉织、制陶、金属加工、盐业、内河与沿海运输
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, cotton weaving, pottery, metalworking, salt production, and riverine/coastal transport

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、棉布、胡椒、珍珠、盐、铁器、象牙制品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cotton textiles, pepper, pearls, salt, iron goods, and ivory products

name.en: Gold coinage and payments in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕牛、农具、谷物储备与对村社土地的使用权。上层家庭则拥有灌溉地、首饰、牲畜和宗教捐献所带来的社会声望。
