culturalAchievements.en: They developed extraordinarily complex oral geography, seasonal ecological management, and continent-spanning songline systems. Rock art, bark painting, ceremonial dance, and sophisticated fire management reflect a highly refined cultural adaptation.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸语系，以帕马-恩永甘语系分布最广
languageFamily.en: Australian Aboriginal language families, with Pama–Nyungan the most widespread

philosophy.zh: 梦时体系将祖灵、土地、歌路、亲属秩序与现实生活结合，强调人与地域和祖先之间的持续责任。
philosophy.en: The Dreaming integrated ancestral beings, land, songlines, kinship order, and daily life, stressing continuing responsibilities between people, place, and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字书写体系，知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣、舞蹈、岩画和身体装饰传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted primarily through oral tradition, song, dance, rock art, and body decoration.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、渔猎和小规模环境管理为基础，市场化程度很低。财富更多体现在食物安全、工具、亲族网络和仪式交换能力，而非可积累货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on foraging, fishing, hunting, and small-scale environmental management, with very low marketization. Wealth was expressed more in food security, tools, kin networks, and ritual exchange capacity than in accumulable money.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、渔猎、石器制作、木器加工、贝壳与赭石交换
mainIndustries.en: Foraging, fishing and hunting, stone tool making, woodworking, and shell and ochre exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 赭石、石斧坯料、贝壳饰物、树脂、木矛、燧石与仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Ochre, stone axe blanks, shell ornaments, resin, wooden spears, flaked stone, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为工具、武器、知识权利、婚姻联系和稳定的采集地使用权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth consisted mainly of tools, weapons, rights in knowledge, marriage ties, and reliable access to foraging grounds.

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、季节迁徙和礼物交换为核心的采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy centered on kin-based sharing, seasonal mobility, and gift exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中金库，储备主要是分散保存的食物、礼品、石器原料和仪式物资。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were dispersed in food stores, gift goods, lithic materials, and ceremonial items.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持共享、再分配和群体稳定为目标，积累性征收极为有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed at maintaining sharing, redistribution, and group stability, with very limited extractive accumulation.

description.zh: 由成年男子组成，使用矛、投矛器、棍棒和盾牌进行近距离冲突与袭击。
description.en: Made up of adult men using spears, spear-throwers, clubs, and shields for local clashes and raids.

description.zh: 擅长地形辨识、追踪和远距离移动，在防御和报复性行动中尤为重要。
description.en: Skilled in terrain reading, tracking, and long-distance movement, crucial in defense and retaliatory action.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地战时首领主持
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; wartime leaders directed forces locally

technology.zh: 军事技术以木矛、石刃、投矛器、盾牌和地形知识为主，依赖伏击、追踪和小规模冲突。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden spears, stone blades, spear-throwers, shields, and terrain knowledge, relying on ambush, tracking, and small-scale fighting.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近群体间的资源竞争、复仇冲突和婚姻纠纷，而非外来帝国入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from local resource competition, vendetta conflict, and marriage disputes rather than external imperial invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布极其分散，集中在海岸、河流、湖泊和资源季节性较稳定的地区。内陆干旱区人口密度较低，但仍存在广泛的迁徙与交换网络。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely dispersed, with higher concentrations along coasts, rivers, lakes, and seasonally reliable resource zones. Arid interior regions were sparsely peopled yet still linked by wide networks of movement and exchange.

socialClasses.zh: 无严格阶级制度，主要区分为长者、成年成员、青年与儿童，以及具有特殊仪式资格者。
socialClasses.en: There was no rigid class system; distinctions lay mainly among elders, adult members, youths, children, and those with special ritual qualifications.

allies.zh: 以邻近通婚群体、交换伙伴和共同仪式网络中的氏族为主。
allies.en: Allies were chiefly neighboring marriage partners, exchange associates, and clans linked through shared ritual networks.

enemies.zh: 没有长期固定的国家级敌人，敌对关系多为地方性和周期性。
enemies.en: There were no permanent state-level enemies; hostile relations were mostly local and cyclical.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在维持交换通道、婚姻平衡和圣地边界的承认。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining exchange routes, marriage balance, and recognition of sacred territorial boundaries.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代至复杂采集社会阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age to complex foraging society stage

keyInnovations.zh: 火烧管理、复杂渔猎技术、投矛器、歌路导航、精细岩画传统。
keyInnovations.en: Fire-stick land management, complex fishing and hunting techniques, spear-throwers, songline navigation, and elaborate rock art traditions.

strengths.zh: 生态知识极深、适应多样环境、社会网络稳固且机动性强。
strengths.en: Deep ecological knowledge, adaptation to varied environments, resilient social networks, and high mobility.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、人口密度低、难以集中长期物资与兵力。
weaknesses.en: Lack of unified state power, low population density, and limited capacity to concentrate supplies and manpower for long periods.

outlook.zh: 在476年前后可长期稳定延续，但不会形成大陆级国家，发展将继续以区域文化网络为主。
outlook.en: Around 476 it could continue stably for the long term, but would not form a continent-wide state; development would remain centered on regional cultural networks.

description.zh: 476年前后的澳大利亚原住民社会是以梦时传统、亲族网络与地方长老治理为核心的复杂采集文明共同体。
description.en: Around 476, the Australian Aboriginal world was a complex foraging civilization rooted in the Dreaming, kinship networks, and local elder governance.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，各草原部族并立
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; multiple savanna tribes coexisted

governmentForm.zh: 以部族酋长、长老会和宗教权威共同运作的松散部落政治结构。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal political structure in which chiefs, elder councils, and religious authorities shared governance.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、年龄等级和家庭群体构成，生产活动通常按季节和生态带分配。牧养、采集、狩猎与初级农业在不同地区比重不同，但都依赖群体协作。威望常来自勇武、慷慨、牲畜持有量或祭仪能力。
socialStructure.en: Society was built around clans, age grades, and household groups, with production organized by season and ecological zone. Herding, foraging, hunting, and simple cultivation varied in importance by region but all depended on group cooperation. Prestige often derived from valor, generosity, livestock holdings, or ritual expertise.

rulingClass.zh: 统治核心多由部族首领、家族长者和祭司性人物组成。其权力通常受亲族共识和地方传统限制，尚未形成成熟官僚贵族。
rulingClass.en: The ruling core generally consisted of tribal chiefs, lineage elders, and priestly figures. Their authority was usually constrained by kin consensus and local custom, and had not yet developed into a mature bureaucratic nobility.

succession.zh: 首领职位多在重要家族或谱系内继承，但仍需长老和战士群体认可。若继承人能力不足，旁支或更有威望者可能取而代之。
succession.en: Chiefly office often passed within leading families or lineages, yet still required acceptance by elders and warrior groups. If an heir proved inadequate, a collateral relative or more prestigious candidate might replace him.

structure.zh: 治理以地方部族为单位，酋长负责协调迁徙、防务和对外关系，长老会负责仲裁与共识形成。跨部族联盟通常是临时性的，多为应对旱灾、战争或大型贸易集会。
structure.en: Governance operated at the tribal level, with chiefs coordinating movement, defense, and foreign relations, while elder councils handled arbitration and consensus-building. Intertribal alliances were usually temporary, formed in response to drought, war, or major trade gatherings.

function.zh: 处理土地、婚姻、牲畜和争端调解事务。
function.en: Handled land, marriage, livestock, and dispute mediation matters.

function.zh: 组织防卫、护卫牲畜、安排季节迁徙和侦察。
function.en: Organized defense, guarded herds, arranged seasonal movement, and conducted scouting.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由村落长者、放牧首领和家族代表处理。行政范围通常不超出本部族控制的水源、牧地与耕地。
localAdmin.en: Local affairs were handled by village elders, pastoral leaders, and lineage representatives. Administrative reach usually did not extend beyond the tribe's own water sources, pasture, and cultivated land.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，重视赔偿、血亲责任与公开仲裁。偷窃牲畜、婚姻违约和伤害案件往往通过赔牛、和解仪式或报复性袭击处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary, stressing compensation, kin responsibility, and public arbitration. Livestock theft, marriage breaches, and injury cases were often settled by cattle payment, reconciliation rites, or retaliatory raiding.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有规范化货币税收，首领主要通过牲畜贡献、收获分成、战利品和贸易礼物进行再分配。灾年时，义务更偏向共同储粮与护卫劳役。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized monetary taxation; chiefs drew resources mainly through livestock contributions, crop shares, war booty, and trade gifts for redistribution. In bad years, obligations leaned more toward communal grain storage and guard labor.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了与季风、草原和牧养生活相适应的口述史诗、舞蹈和年龄组仪式。铁器使用初见于部分地带，增强了工具制作和区域交换能力。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed oral epics, dances, and age-set rituals adapted to monsoon, savanna, and pastoral life. Iron use appeared in some zones, improving tool production and regional exchange.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系、尼罗-撒哈拉语系及邻近草原语言群
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Nilo-Saharan, and neighboring savanna language groups

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵和祖先崇拜为核心，强调人与土地、雨季、牲畜和祖灵之间的平衡。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism and ancestor veneration, it emphasized balance among people, land, the rains, livestock, and ancestral spirits.

writingSystem.zh: 大多数地区无本土文字，历史、谱系和法律靠口传、鼓乐、赞歌和记忆传统保存。
writingSystem.en: Most groups lacked indigenous writing; history, genealogy, and law were preserved through oral transmission, drums, praise songs, and memory traditions.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济由牧养、采集、狩猎与局部农业共同构成，地区差异明显。牲畜和粮食是财富核心，跨区域贸易规模有限但对盐、铁和装饰品流通很重要。
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined herding, foraging, hunting, and localized farming, with strong regional variation. Livestock and grain were the core of wealth, while long-distance trade remained modest but important for salt, iron, and ornaments.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧养、狩猎、采集、黍稷种植、制陶、初级冶铁
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, hunting, gathering, millet cultivation, pottery, and early ironworking

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、象牙、蜂蜜、谷物、盐、铁器和珠饰
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, ivory, honey, grain, salt, iron tools, and beads

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为牲畜数量、粮仓储备、铁器、婚姻交换资源和可动员亲族劳力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in herd size, grain stores, iron tools, marriage exchange resources, and kin labor that could be mobilized.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、牲畜财富和部族再分配为核心的混合生计经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed subsistence economy centered on household production, livestock wealth, and tribal redistribution.

category.zh: 首领再分配与盟友赏赐

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要不是金属货币，而是牲畜、谷物、盐块和可再分配的礼品物资。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were held mainly not as metal money but as livestock, grain, salt blocks, and redistributable gift goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政目标是维持首领威望、灾年赈济与战时动员，征收依赖传统义务而非固定税率。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to sustain chiefly prestige, famine relief, and wartime mobilization, with extraction based on traditional obligations rather than fixed tax rates.

description.zh: 主要由矛兵和盾兵组成，承担护卫、水源争夺和袭击任务。
description.en: Mainly spear and shield fighters responsible for guarding, water-source contests, and raids.

description.zh: 熟悉草原与灌木地带地形，用于追踪、侦察和伏击。
description.en: Familiar with savanna and bush terrain, they were used for tracking, scouting, and ambush.

description.zh: 在拥有骑乘传统或快速放牧群体的地区承担机动护卫职能，规模有限。
description.en: In regions with riding traditions or fast-moving pastoral groups, these provided mobile herd escort on a limited scale.

commanderInChief.zh: 部族联军首领或最强酋长
commanderInChief.en: Confederate war leader or the strongest chief

technology.zh: 武器以木矛、铁矛头、弓箭、皮盾和棍棒为主，部分地区开始使用铁匠打造的刀具与农具。
technology.en: Weapons were mainly wooden spears, iron spearheads, bows, hide shields, and clubs, with some regions beginning to use smith-made blades and tools.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为部族间牲畜掠夺、旱灾引发的迁徙冲突，以及来自邻近更强酋邦的扩张压力。
threats.en: The principal threats were cattle raiding between tribes, migration conflicts caused by drought, and expansion pressure from stronger neighboring chiefdoms.

populationDescription.zh: 人口多分布于河谷、湖区、季节性水源和较肥沃草原边缘。大部分居民居于村落或半流动营地，完全城市化尚未出现。
populationDescription.en: Population clustered along river valleys, lake zones, seasonal water sources, and the margins of more fertile savannas. Most people lived in villages or semi-mobile camps, with true urbanization not yet present.

socialClasses.zh: 主要分为首领家族、自由家户、战士青年、工匠与依附者，阶层差异存在但尚不固定为国家等级制度。
socialClasses.en: The main strata were chiefly families, free households, warrior youths, artisans, and dependents, with social differences present but not yet fixed into a state class hierarchy.

allies.zh: 盟友通常是通婚部族、共同放牧联盟和贸易伙伴村落。
allies.en: Allies were usually intermarrying tribes, shared pastoral confederates, and trade partner villages.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺水源、牧场和牲畜的邻近部族。
enemies.en: Enemies were generally neighboring tribes contesting water, pasture, and livestock.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在平衡贸易与袭击、维持迁徙通道，并在旱灾时争取临时联盟。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on balancing trade and raiding, preserving migration corridors, and seeking temporary alliances in times of drought.

era.zh: 早期铁器时代草原部族阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age savanna tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 初级冶铁、谷物储藏、皮革加工、牧群管理和季节性迁徙组织。
keyInnovations.en: Early ironworking, grain storage, leatherworking, herd management, and organization of seasonal mobility.

strengths.zh: 机动性强、适应草原气候、牲畜财富可快速动员、地方联盟弹性高。
strengths.en: High mobility, strong adaptation to savanna climate, herd wealth that could be rapidly mobilized, and flexible local alliances.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂、抗旱能力有限、储备薄弱且容易陷入报复性冲突循环。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, limited resilience to severe drought, shallow reserves, and vulnerability to cycles of retaliatory conflict.

outlook.zh: 在476年前后将维持部族并立格局，部分地区可能逐步向更稳定的酋邦或早期王国演变。
outlook.en: Around 476 the region would remain tribally divided, though some areas might gradually evolve into more stable chiefdoms or early kingdoms.

description.zh: 476年前后的热带草原诸族是以牧养、采集和部族共识治理为基础的分散社会共同体。
description.en: Around 476, the savanna peoples were a dispersed social world based on herding, foraging, and tribal consensus governance.

governmentForm.zh: 以神圣君主为核心、由酋长网络和朝贡体系支撑的早期海洋王国。
governmentForm.en: An early maritime kingdom centered on a sacred monarch and supported by a network of chiefs and tributary relationships.

socialStructure.zh: 社会层级较明确，王室与高阶贵族居于顶层，其下为地方酋长、自由民和从属劳作者。血缘、婚姻与仪式地位决定政治等级，妇女在高贵血统传承和联盟联姻中具有重要作用。岛际关系通过贡品、婚姻和宗教威望维系。
socialStructure.en: Society was clearly stratified, with the royal house and high nobles at the top, followed by local chiefs, commoners, and subordinate laborers. Political rank was shaped by descent, marriage, and ritual status, and elite women played an important role in transmitting noble blood and forging alliances. Inter-island relations were maintained through tribute, marriage, and religious prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由图伊·汤加王室、近支贵族和掌控重要岛屿的酋长组成。其统治合法性来自祖先神圣性、礼仪中心地位和对长距离海上关系的控制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Tu'i Tonga royal house, close nobles, and chiefs controlling major islands. Their legitimacy derived from ancestral sacredness, ritual centrality, and control of long-distance maritime relations.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，强调高贵血统与仪式正统。继位虽多具世袭性质，但仍需主要酋长和王室支系认可。
succession.en: The throne generally passed within the royal family, emphasizing noble blood and ritual orthodoxy. Succession was largely hereditary, though still dependent on recognition by principal chiefs and royal branches.

structure.zh: 王国以穆阿为核心礼仪与政治中心，通过各岛酋长实行间接统治。中央规模不大，但对贡赋、劳役、海上航行和王室仪式拥有较强号召力。
structure.en: The kingdom was centered on Mu'a as its ritual and political core, ruling indirectly through island chiefs. The central apparatus was small, but it exercised strong authority over tribute, labor service, seafaring, and royal ceremony.

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、祖先仪式和加冕礼，维护神圣王权。
function.en: Managed royal worship, ancestral rites, and enthronement ceremonies, sustaining sacred kingship.

function.zh: 征集各岛贡品、组织公共工程并协调王室仓储。
function.en: Collected tribute from the islands, organized public works, and coordinated royal storage.

function.zh: 安排远航、联络附属岛群并接待来访酋长与使者。
function.en: Arranged long-distance voyages, maintained ties with subordinate island groups, and received visiting chiefs and envoys.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由岛屿酋长和村社首领负责，向王室输送贡品、劳力和战士。中央对边远岛屿的控制多依赖宗教威望和婚姻联盟，而非密集常设官员。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by island chiefs and village leaders, who supplied tribute, labor, and warriors to the crown. Central control over outlying islands depended more on religious prestige and marriage alliances than on dense permanent officialdom.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、酋长裁决和宗教禁忌为主，尤其重视等级秩序、土地分配和对王室的义务。严重违法行为可能受到放逐、财物没收或仪式惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, chiefly judgment, and sacred taboo, especially regarding hierarchy, land allocation, and duties to the royal house. Serious violations could bring exile, confiscation, or ritual punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要以贡品和劳役形式征收，包括芋头、山药、椰子制品、细席、羽饰和独木舟服务。王室在大型仪式和建筑工程期间征发能力最强。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute and labor service, including taro, yams, coconut products, fine mats, feather goods, and canoe service. Royal extraction was strongest during major ceremonies and construction projects.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 汤加已形成高度发展的远洋航海、独木舟建造、巨石墓丘与礼仪建筑传统。其王权仪式和岛际交换网络使之成为南太平洋最具组织性的早期政体之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Tonga had already developed advanced blue-water navigation, canoe construction, and traditions of monumental tomb mounds and ceremonial architecture. Its royal ritual system and inter-island exchange networks made it one of the most organized early polities in the South Pacific.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，波利尼西亚语支
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 波利尼西亚多神信仰强调祖先、神灵、禁忌与首领神圣性，政治与宗教密不可分。
philosophy.en: Polynesian polytheism emphasized ancestors, deities, taboo, and the sacredness of chiefs, with politics and religion inseparably linked.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，历史与谱系通过颂歌、口述传统和仪式记忆保存。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; history and genealogy were preserved through chant, oral tradition, and ceremonial memory.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以园圃农业、渔业、手工业和岛际交换为核心，虽然缺乏货币市场，但再分配效率较高。王室通过贡赋调动剩余产出，用于礼仪、建筑和航海活动。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on horticulture, fishing, craft production, and inter-island exchange; though lacking a coin market, it redistributed resources efficiently. The royal house mobilized surplus through tribute for ritual, construction, and maritime activity.

mainIndustries.zh: 芋头与山药种植、渔业、独木舟制造、席编织、木石工艺
mainIndustries.en: Taro and yam cultivation, fishing, canoe building, mat weaving, and wood and stone craftsmanship

tradeGoods.zh: 细席、树皮布、羽饰、贝壳、渔产、木材、黑曜石与礼仪制品
tradeGoods.en: Fine mats, barkcloth, feather ornaments, shells, fish products, timber, obsidian, and ceremonial goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现为园地面积、船只、席布储备、劳动力和与高阶酋长的联系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed in garden land, boats, mat and barkcloth reserves, labor capacity, and ties to high chiefs.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室再分配、村社生产和岛际朝贡交换为核心的海洋园圃经济。
economicSystem.en: A maritime horticultural economy centered on royal redistribution, village production, and inter-island tributary exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富主要储存为贡品仓储、细席、树皮布、食物储备、礼仪器物和船只，而非金属货币。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth was stored chiefly in tribute depots, fine mats, barkcloth, food reserves, ceremonial objects, and vessels rather than in metal coin.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在通过贡赋再分配巩固王权、支持礼仪工程并维持岛际联系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on reinforcing royal authority through tributary redistribution, supporting ceremonial works, and sustaining inter-island connections.

description.zh: 负责王庭护卫和礼仪性武装展示，也是核心作战力量。
description.en: They guarded the court and provided ceremonial displays of force while serving as the core combat element.

description.zh: 由各岛酋长按需征发，用于登陆作战、部族冲突和王室惩戒远征。
description.en: Raised by island chiefs as needed for landings, intergroup conflict, and royal punitive expeditions.

description.zh: 以大型航海独木舟执行运输、护航和跨岛投送任务，是王国力量的重要基础。
description.en: Using large seagoing canoes, they carried out transport, escort, and cross-island projection, forming a vital basis of royal power.

technology.zh: 军事技术以木矛、棍棒、石制或贝制武器、防护垫甲和大型远航独木舟为主，海上机动能力强于陆战持久力。
technology.en: Military technology relied on wooden spears, clubs, stone or shell weapons, protective fiber armor, and large oceangoing canoes; maritime mobility exceeded staying power in land campaigns.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近波利尼西亚岛群的竞争、附属岛屿离心以及海难和飓风对舰队的破坏。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry with neighboring Polynesian island groups, centrifugal tendencies in subordinate islands, and storm or cyclone damage to the fleet.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于汤加塔布等核心岛屿的沿海村社与园圃区，穆阿是最重要的礼仪与政治中心。整体人口不多，但在南太平洋诸岛中组织程度较高。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in coastal villages and garden zones on core islands such as Tongatapu, with Mu'a serving as the foremost ritual and political center. Total numbers were modest, but organization was high by South Pacific standards.

socialClasses.zh: 社会大致分为王室、高级酋长、地方酋长、自由民与从属劳作者，等级秩序受血统与禁忌严格约束。
socialClasses.en: Society was broadly divided into the royal house, high chiefs, local chiefs, commoners, and subordinate laborers, with rank tightly regulated by descent and taboo.

allies.zh: 盟友包括与王室通婚或承认其宗教威望的邻近波利尼西亚岛屿酋长。
allies.en: Allies included neighboring Polynesian chiefs tied to the royal house by marriage or acknowledging its religious prestige.

enemies.zh: 敌手主要是拒绝朝贡的岛群、竞争性酋邦和偶发性的岛际反叛力量。
enemies.en: Opponents were mainly island groups refusing tribute, rival chiefdoms, and occasional inter-island rebel forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策强调以航海、婚姻、礼仪影响和有限武力维护汤加在区域海域的宗主地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized preserving Tongan predominance in regional seas through navigation, marriage, ritual influence, and limited force.

era.zh: 波利尼西亚早期王国与远洋航海时代
era.en: Polynesian early kingdom and blue-water navigation era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型双体或远洋独木舟、精密航海知识、巨石墓丘、细席与树皮布专业制作。
keyInnovations.en: Large double-hulled or oceangoing canoes, sophisticated navigation knowledge, monumental tomb mounds, and specialized production of fine mats and barkcloth.

strengths.zh: 王权神圣性强、岛际航海能力突出、朝贡网络稳定、社会组织度高。
strengths.en: Strong sacred kingship, excellent inter-island seafaring, a stable tributary network, and a high degree of social organization.

weaknesses.zh: 资源基础有限、人口规模小、对天气和海上交通高度敏感。
weaknesses.en: A limited resource base, small population, and high sensitivity to weather and maritime communications.

outlook.zh: 476年前后汤加具有继续扩展区域影响的潜力，但其长期稳定仍取决于王室对岛际贡赋和航海网络的持续掌控。
outlook.en: Around 476, Tonga had the potential to expand its regional influence further, but its long-term stability depended on continued royal control over tribute and maritime networks.

description.zh: 476年前后的图伊·汤加王国是南太平洋一个以神圣王权、贡赋网络和远洋航海能力著称的早期海洋国家。
description.en: Around 476, the Tu'i Tonga realm was an early maritime state in the South Pacific distinguished by sacred kingship, tributary networks, and blue-water seafaring.

governmentForm.zh: 以若干亲族群体和季节性营地为基础，由部族长老协商决策的松散部落治理。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal order based on kin groups and seasonal camps, governed through consensus among tribal elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家庭与扩展亲族为核心，按季节在海岸与内陆猎场之间迁移。经验丰富的猎手、萨满与长老拥有较高威望，但财富积累有限，整体分层较弱。劳作按性别与年龄分工，狩猎、制皮、骨角器加工与营地维护彼此依赖。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on households and extended kin groups that moved seasonally between coasts and inland hunting grounds. Skilled hunters, shamans, and elders held prestige, but wealth accumulation was limited and social stratification remained weak. Labor was divided by age and gender, with hunting, hide working, bone and antler craft, and camp maintenance all interdependent.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族，领导权主要掌握在长老、杰出猎手与宗教权威手中。其权威来自资历、技艺、慷慨分配与精神威望。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal hereditary nobility; leadership rested mainly with elders, accomplished hunters, and ritual authorities. Their authority came from seniority, skill, generous redistribution, and spiritual prestige.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常并非固定王位，而是在长老会与家族共识中形成。若某位领袖失去声望，社群可以转而追随其他长者或分裂为新营地。
succession.en: Leadership was not a fixed throne but emerged through the consensus of elders and kin groups. If a leader lost prestige, the community could shift allegiance to another elder or divide into a new camp.

structure.zh: 多塞特社群没有常设官僚机构，公共事务主要在营地会议中处理。狩猎组织、迁徙时机、资源分配与纠纷调停由长老与萨满共同协调。不同聚落之间保持松散联系，在大型猎猎季或危机时期临时合作。
structure.en: Dorset communities had no permanent bureaucracy, and public affairs were handled mainly in camp councils. Elders and shamans coordinated hunting organization, migration timing, resource sharing, and dispute mediation. Different settlements maintained loose ties and cooperated temporarily during major hunting seasons or times of stress.

function.zh: 协商迁徙、狩猎分配、纠纷调停与对外关系。
function.en: Deliberated migration, hunting allocation, dispute mediation, and external relations.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、照料共用物资，并在困难季节安排再分配。
function.en: Conducted rituals, watched communal stores, and arranged redistribution in difficult seasons.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即各个季节性营地的自我管理，通常由最有威望的家庭长者主持。不存在固定分区行政，营地间关系依赖婚姻、互助与共同猎场传统。
localAdmin.en: Local administration consisted of self-management by each seasonal camp, usually led by the most respected household elders. There were no fixed territorial districts; ties between camps depended on marriage, mutual aid, and customary hunting grounds.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以口头传统维系。伤害、盗窃或分配不公多通过赔偿、公开劝诫、祭仪净化或分离营地解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary and preserved through oral tradition. Injury, theft, or unfair distribution was usually resolved through compensation, public admonition, ritual purification, or separation of camps.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正式税制，资源以互惠、贡献与共享形式流动。优秀猎手在社会压力下需向弱者和长老分配猎获，相当于非正式再分配机制。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources moved through reciprocity, contributions, and shared use. Successful hunters were under social pressure to distribute catches to the weak and to elders, functioning as an informal redistributive mechanism.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 多塞特文化以适应极寒海洋环境的狩猎技术、骨角器制作和小型雕刻见长。其灯具、雪屋或半地下居所传统，以及对海豹与海象生态的深刻理解，是其最重要的文化成就之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Dorset culture was notable for hunting techniques adapted to severe Arctic marine conditions, bone and antler craftsmanship, and small carvings. Their lamp technology, snow or semi-subterranean dwelling traditions, and deep knowledge of seal and walrus ecology were among their key cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 未知，可能属于已消失的古北极人群语言。
languageFamily.en: Unknown, possibly belonging to an extinct Paleo-Arctic linguistic tradition.

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、萨满实践与人与海兽环境的平衡观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic belief, shamanic practice, and the idea of balance between people and the marine-animal environment.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依靠口传、仪式与器物纹饰传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, ritual, and artifact decoration.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性海猎和采集为主，银当量估算仅用于横向比较。生产剩余很低，但在海豹、鱼类、骨角与皮革等资源上具有地方性稳定性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence marine hunting and gathering; silver-equivalent estimates are used only for comparison. Surplus production was low, but the society maintained local stability through seals, fish, bone, antler, and hides.

mainIndustries.zh: 海豹猎取、渔业、骨角器制作、皮革加工、季节性采集
mainIndustries.en: Seal hunting, fishing, bone and antler craft, hide processing, seasonal gathering

tradeGoods.zh: 皮革、骨角器、象牙、石料、鱼干与海兽制品
tradeGoods.en: Hides, bone and antler tools, ivory, stone materials, dried fish, and marine-animal products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在皮舟、狩猎工具、油脂储备、皮衣与社会互助关系上。可移动财产不多，但生存物资价值极高。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in boats, hunting tools, stored oil, warm clothing, and social reciprocity networks. Movable property was limited, but survival goods carried very high practical value.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、共有资源利用和互惠交换为基础的部落生计经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal subsistence economy based on household production, shared access to resources, and reciprocal exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要表现为分散储存在各营地的油脂、皮革、工具和应急食物，而非集中金属财富。其价值在灾年与猎季失利时尤为关键。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of dispersed stores of oil, hides, tools, and emergency food rather than concentrated metal wealth. Its value was especially crucial in bad seasons and failed hunts.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践强调维持最低生存保障和共享储备，避免任何家庭因灾荒而被完全抛弃。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized maintaining minimum subsistence security and communal reserves so that no household would be entirely abandoned in hardship.

description.zh: 由成年猎手兼任，负责营地防卫、巡查与短程袭扰。
description.en: Adult hunters serving part-time in camp defense, patrols, and short-range raids.

description.zh: 以轻型舟艇活动于近海，兼具狩猎、侦察与运输功能。
description.en: Light boat crews operating along the coast for hunting, scouting, and transport.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定总司令，由资深长老与首席猎手临时统率
commanderInChief.en: No fixed commander-in-chief; senior elders and leading hunters directed forces as needed

technology.zh: 军事技术与狩猎技术高度重合，主要依赖骨角矛头、石刃、弓矢、皮舟与寒地生存技能。缺乏金属武备与复杂攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology overlapped heavily with hunting technology, relying on bone and antler spearheads, stone blades, bows, skin boats, and cold-environment survival skills. Metal armament and siege capability were lacking.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自气候波动、资源短缺、邻近群体竞争以及远距离迁徙带来的生存风险，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from climatic fluctuation, resource scarcity, competition with neighboring groups, and the risks of long-distance movement rather than large state warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口稀少且分散，聚居点规模很小，随季节显著波动。婴幼儿死亡率高，人口增长缓慢，对环境变化极为敏感。
populationDescription.en: The total population was sparse and dispersed, with very small settlements that fluctuated strongly by season. Infant mortality was high, population growth was slow, and communities were highly sensitive to environmental change.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与萨满、优秀猎手、普通家庭成员、依附弱者与孤寡者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and shamans, successful hunters, ordinary household members, and dependents such as the weak or isolated.

allies.zh: 无正式盟邦，主要依赖邻近营地和亲族网络的互助。
allies.en: No formal allies; support came mainly from nearby camps and kinship networks.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌人，但可能与邻近北极族群在猎场与迁徙路线方面存在摩擦。
enemies.en: No fixed state enemies, though friction with neighboring Arctic groups likely occurred over hunting grounds and migration routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以避免冲突、维持交换和保全生存空间为优先。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations prioritized avoiding conflict, maintaining exchange, and preserving space for survival.

era.zh: 北极晚期石器时代海猎文化
era.en: Late Arctic Stone Age marine-hunting culture

keyInnovations.zh: 脂灯、骨角复合工具、寒地服饰技术、皮舟与海兽追踪经验。
keyInnovations.en: Oil lamps, composite bone and antler tools, cold-weather clothing techniques, skin boats, and expertise in tracking marine animals.

strengths.zh: 对极端环境适应力强，社群合作紧密，海猎知识深厚。
strengths.en: Strong adaptation to extreme environments, close communal cooperation, and deep marine-hunting knowledge.

weaknesses.zh: 人口过少、技术基础有限、缺乏冗余资源，极易受气候与食物链变化冲击。
weaknesses.en: Very small population, limited technical base, and little surplus, making the society vulnerable to climate and food-chain disruption.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可维持稳定生计，但长期前景取决于生态条件与外来北极人群压力。
outlook.en: The society could maintain short-term stability, but its long-term outlook depended on ecological conditions and pressure from incoming Arctic peoples.

description.zh: 多塞特文化是在极寒北极环境中依靠海猎、互惠与长老协商维系生存的分散部落社会。
description.en: Dorset Culture was a dispersed tribal society that survived in the high Arctic through marine hunting, reciprocity, and elder-led consensus.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、依托婆罗门支持与地方封授关系运作的早期南印度君主制王国。
governmentForm.en: An early South Indian monarchy centered on royal authority and supported by Brahmanical legitimacy and local grant-based relationships.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构以种姓秩序、农业村社与地方豪族为基础。婆罗门在祭祀、教育与土地受赠中地位显著，武士性统治集团控制军政资源，农民和手工业者承担主要生产。商人群体在内陆与沿海交换中日益重要，但总体仍从属于王权与地方精英。
socialStructure.en: Social organization rested on caste hierarchy, agrarian villages, and local landed elites. Brahmins held significant status through ritual, learning, and land grants, while the warrior-oriented ruling stratum controlled military and political resources, and peasants and artisans carried most production. Merchant groups were increasingly important in inland and coastal exchange, though they remained subordinate to royal and regional elites.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、王族、军事贵族、受封地方首领和婆罗门受赠者构成。王朝通过赏赐土地、婚姻联盟与宗教 patronage 巩固统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal kin, military nobles, subordinate local chiefs, and Brahmin donees. The dynasty consolidated rule through land grants, marriage alliances, and religious patronage.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，优先由直系男性继承，但在权力竞争中也可能由兄弟支系或强势宗室取得。继承的合法性常通过铭文、祭仪与军事胜利加以确认。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal family, usually favoring direct male succession, though brothers or powerful collateral branches could prevail in struggles for power. Legitimacy was commonly confirmed through inscriptions, rituals, and military success.

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕国王、宫廷与书记系统运作，依靠地方总督、属邦首领和受封官员管理地区。王命通过铭文、土地赐予和税役安排落实到村社层面。尽管官僚体系尚不完全定型，但已经具备较明显的层级统治结构。
structure.en: The central government operated around the king, court, and scribal apparatus, relying on provincial governors, subordinate chiefs, and granted officials to manage regions. Royal authority reached village society through inscriptions, land grants, and tax-labor arrangements. Though not yet a fully regularized bureaucracy, it displayed a clear hierarchical order.

function.zh: 起草铭文、管理土地赐予、记录王命与外交文书。
function.en: Drafted inscriptions, managed land grants, and recorded royal orders and diplomatic documents.

function.zh: 征收地税、贸易税与附属贡赋，核定村社义务。
function.en: Collected land tax, trade dues, and subordinate tribute, and assessed village obligations.

function.zh: 统筹常备军、征召附属兵力、保障要塞与边境通道。
function.en: Coordinated the standing forces, summoned subordinate troops, and secured forts and frontier routes.

function.zh: 管理寺庙、婆罗门聚落受赠地与相关司法豁免。
function.en: Managed temples, Brahmin settlements, donated lands, and associated judicial exemptions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托省级官员、地方豪族和村社长老共同运作。王朝通过授地、确认地方习惯与征集兵役，换取地方精英对国王的承认。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through provincial officers, regional landed elites, and village elders. The dynasty secured recognition from local elites through grants, confirmation of local custom, and the mobilization of service and troops.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王命、婆罗门法学传统和地方习惯。土地边界、赋役、宗教受赠权与继承争议通常通过铭文、见证人和地方议决处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal commands, Brahmanical jurisprudence, and local custom. Disputes over land boundaries, dues, religious grants, and inheritance were commonly settled through inscriptions, witnesses, and local adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖农业地税、附属贡赋、市场与通行税，以及在部分地区征收劳役。王室常将部分税源转赐寺庙或婆罗门，从而换取宗教合法性与地方整合。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended mainly on agrarian land tax, subordinate tribute, market and transit dues, and labor obligations in some areas. The crown often reassigned parts of these revenues to temples or Brahmins in exchange for religious legitimacy and local integration.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 毗湿奴昆丁时期以土地赐予铭文、婆罗门聚落扩展和印度教王权表达见长。其政治整合促进了安得拉内陆的寺庙文化、梵文铭刻与区域国家形成。
culturalAchievements.en: The Vishnu-Kundin period was marked by land-grant inscriptions, the expansion of Brahmin settlements, and the articulation of Hindu kingship. Its political consolidation advanced temple culture, Sanskrit epigraphy, and regional state formation in inland Andhra.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言环境属德拉威语系（如早期泰卢固语）并受梵语强烈影响
languageFamily.en: The main linguistic environment belonged to the Dravidian family, including early Telugu, under strong Sanskrit influence

philosophy.zh: 以印度教婆罗门传统、王权达摩观念与地方宗教融合为主。
philosophy.en: Dominated by Brahmanical Hindu tradition, the idea of royal dharma, and interaction with local religious practices.

writingSystem.zh: 婆罗米系南印度文字，行政与铭文中常用梵语并夹用俗语成分。
writingSystem.en: A southern Brahmi-derived script, with Sanskrit common in inscriptions and administrative usage alongside vernacular elements.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是稻作、旱地农业、畜牧与区域贸易，王朝处于德干东部的重要内陆交通节点。银当量估算反映的是农业剩余、税收能力与手工业产出的综合规模，而非货币化程度本身。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice cultivation, dry farming, herding, and regional trade, with the dynasty situated on an important inland corridor in the eastern Deccan. The silver-equivalent estimate reflects the combined scale of agrarian surplus, fiscal capacity, and artisanal output rather than a fully monetized economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、灌溉、棉纺织、陶器、金属加工、林产与牲畜养殖
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, irrigation, cotton textiles, pottery, metalworking, forest products, and livestock raising

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、棉布、胡椒与香料转运品、象牙、铁器、珠饰、食盐
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cotton cloth, transit pepper and spices, ivory, iron goods, beads, and salt

name.zh: 金币与铜币并行，兼用实物结算
name.en: Gold and copper coinage alongside payments in kind

unitName.zh: 第纳罗、钱币与谷物折算
unitName.en: dinara-type gold, copper cash, and grain reckoning

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要取决于耕地、牲畜、谷物储备与农具。地方豪族和婆罗门受赠者则以土地控制、佃户依附和宗教特权积累更大财富。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth depended mainly on cultivated land, livestock, grain reserves, and tools. Local magnates and Brahmin donees accumulated greater wealth through land control, dependent cultivators, and religious privileges.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和王权再分配为核心、并辅以手工业与区域贸易的早期王国经济。
economicSystem.en: An early kingdom economy centered on agrarian rent and royal redistribution, supplemented by crafts and regional trade.

source.en: Mines, forest products, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财政既包括金银与铜币储备，也包括谷物、牲畜和应急军需。由于赐地制度发达，部分财富实际上分散在地方精英和宗教机构手中。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury included reserves of precious metals and copper coin as well as grain, livestock, and emergency military supplies. Because the grant system was extensive, a portion of effective wealth was dispersed among local elites and religious institutions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持军政控制、支持宗教合法性并扩大农业开发为中心，倾向通过赐地换取忠诚而非完全官僚化征收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining military-political control, supporting religious legitimacy, and expanding agrarian development, relying more on grants to secure loyalty than on fully bureaucratic extraction.

description.zh: 军队主体，使用长矛、盾牌、弓箭与刀剑，含王室部队和地方征召兵。
description.en: The main body of the army, using spears, shields, bows, and swords, including royal troops and locally levied soldiers.

description.zh: 机动力较强，多用于追击、侦察与快速镇压地方叛乱。
description.en: A more mobile arm used for pursuit, scouting, and rapid suppression of local revolts.

description.zh: 象兵数量有限但象征王权威势，适合冲击敌阵和震慑属邦。
description.en: Limited in number but symbolically powerful, elephants were suited to breaking enemy lines and intimidating subordinate rulers.

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代成熟阶段，装备包括铁制兵刃、复合弓、战象和要塞守备工事。虽不及大型帝国军制复杂，但足以在安得拉内陆进行区域争霸。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a mature Iron Age level, including iron weapons, bows, war elephants, and fortified defenses. It was less complex than the systems of major empires, but sufficient for regional power struggles in inland Andhra.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自德干及安得拉邻近王朝、地方豪族离心倾向，以及王朝扩张中对边缘地区控制不足。
threats.en: Major threats came from neighboring Deccan and Andhra polities, centrifugal tendencies among local magnates, and incomplete control over frontier zones during expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以农村农民为绝对多数，分布在河谷、灌溉区与村社网络中。城市人口有限，但宗教中心、行政据点和市场聚落正在增长。
populationDescription.en: The population was overwhelmingly rural, distributed through river valleys, irrigated zones, and village networks. Urban population was limited, but religious centers, administrative seats, and market settlements were growing.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与军事贵族、婆罗门、地方豪族与官吏、商人和手工业者、自由农民、佃户与劳役者。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and military nobility, Brahmins, local landed elites and officials, merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, and laboring dependents.

allies.zh: 与部分地方首领、婆罗门受赠集团及婚姻联姻对象维持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative relations with certain local chiefs, Brahmin donee networks, and marriage allies.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括邻近德干王朝、东海岸竞争势力及不服从的属邦首领。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included neighboring Deccan dynasties, rival powers on the eastern coast, and recalcitrant subordinate chiefs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以扩展区域霸权、确保内陆通道和通过宗教与婚姻手段整合地方为目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at expanding regional hegemony, securing inland routes, and integrating local powers through religion and marriage.

era.zh: 南亚古典后期铁器王国阶段
era.en: Late classical South Asian Iron Age kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 灌溉农业扩张、土地赐予制度、梵文铭文行政、铁制兵器与区域寺庙网络。
keyInnovations.en: Expanded irrigation agriculture, land-grant institutions, Sanskrit inscriptional administration, iron weaponry, and regional temple networks.

strengths.zh: 农业基础较稳，宗教合法性强，具备区域性军力和政治整合能力。
strengths.en: It had a stable agrarian base, strong religious legitimacy, and meaningful regional military and political integrative capacity.

weaknesses.zh: 对地方豪族依赖较深，行政集中度有限，财政部分被赐地制度分流。
weaknesses.en: It depended heavily on local magnates, had limited administrative centralization, and saw part of its fiscal base diverted through land grants.

outlook.zh: 短中期内仍有扩张和巩固空间，但王朝长期命运将取决于能否压制地方离心并在竞争王朝间维持优势。
outlook.en: In the short to medium term it retained room for expansion and consolidation, but its long-term fate depended on containing local centrifugal forces and maintaining advantage over rival dynasties.

description.zh: 毗湿奴昆丁王朝是476年前后安得拉内陆一支依托农业、婆罗门合法性与区域军力崛起的印度教王国。
description.en: Around 476, the Vishnu-Kundins were a Hindu kingdom rising in inland Andhra through agrarian strength, Brahmanical legitimacy, and regional military power.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个地方部族、城镇精英与不列颠移民社群共同构成的松散基督教化部落联盟。
governmentForm.en: A loose Christianized tribal confederation composed of local tribes, town elites, and migrant British communities.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构混合了晚期罗马高卢旧有地主、城市教士、乡村自由民与部族武装首领。随着不列颠移民增加，沿海与半岛地区形成新的族群网络。社会分层较多于北方纯部落社会，但中央集权仍然薄弱。
socialStructure.en: Social structure blended surviving late Roman Gallic landholders, urban clergy, rural freemen, and tribal war leaders. As migration from Britain increased, new ethnic networks formed in coastal and peninsular zones. Society was more stratified than in purely northern tribal settings, yet central authority remained weak.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层包括地方部族首领、拥有土地的高卢-罗马精英以及主教等教会人物。其权威来自武力、土地、罗马行政遗产与宗教正统性。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum included tribal chiefs, landholding Gallo-Roman elites, and church figures such as bishops. Their authority derived from force, land, Roman administrative legacy, and religious orthodoxy.

succession.zh: 部族首领职位多在家族内部传承，但需获得战士与地方显贵认可。若联盟层面形成共同领袖，其地位通常依赖个人军功与协商，而非稳固的法定继承。
succession.en: Chiefly offices often passed within families but required recognition from warriors and local notables. When a broader confederate leader emerged, his position usually depended on personal military success and negotiation rather than fixed legal succession.

structure.zh: 阿莫里卡缺乏强有力统一王廷，实际治理由地方城镇、主教区与部族领袖分掌。部分地区仍沿用罗马晚期的税务、地籍和教会组织框架，但整体上政治权力高度地方化。对外防务和集体行动往往以临时联盟形式展开。
structure.en: Armorica lacked a strong unified royal court, and governance was divided among local towns, bishoprics, and tribal leaders. Some areas still used late Roman frameworks of taxation, landholding, and ecclesiastical organization, but political power was highly localized overall. External defense and collective action were often organized through temporary coalitions.

function.zh: 协调首领间事务、征召战士并处理联盟层面的争端。
function.en: Coordinated affairs among chiefs, summoned warriors, and handled disputes at the confederate level.

function.zh: 管理教区文书、教会财产、调解纠纷并维系罗马式记录传统。
function.en: Managed diocesan records, church property, dispute mediation, and the survival of Roman-style record keeping.

function.zh: 在需要时征集粮食、运输与守备物资，维持地方武装。
function.en: Gathered grain, transport, and defensive supplies when needed and sustained local armed forces.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由乡村社群、残存市镇机构、主教与首领共同实施。沿海地区和新移民聚落常形成较强自治传统，与内陆旧罗马庄园区并存。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out jointly by village communities, surviving town institutions, bishops, and chiefs. Coastal zones and migrant settlements often developed strong traditions of autonomy alongside inland former Roman estate districts.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具罗马习惯法、教会裁决和部族习俗。土地、继承和伤害赔偿可由主教、地方显贵或首领会议共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman customary law, ecclesiastical judgment, and tribal usage. Land, inheritance, and injury compensation might be decided by bishops, local notables, or assemblies of chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 正式税收能力较晚期罗马时期明显下降，主要依靠地方征收、教会什一式供奉前身、港口税和战时临时摊派。许多义务以实物、劳役和防务服务形式履行。
taxationSystem.en: Formal tax capacity was markedly weaker than under the late Roman regime, relying mainly on local collections, proto-tithe church support, port dues, and wartime assessments. Many obligations were fulfilled in kind, through labor, or through defense service.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿莫里卡在476年前后最显著的文化特点是罗马高卢传统、不列颠移民文化与基督教组织的融合。修道和主教网络为后来布列塔尼地区的圣徒传说、地方身份和拉丁-凯尔特混合文化奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Around 476, Armorica's most notable cultural feature was the fusion of Roman Gallic tradition, British migrant culture, and Christian organization. Monastic and episcopal networks laid the basis for later Breton saint traditions, regional identity, and a mixed Latin-Celtic culture.

languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族与通俗拉丁语并存
languageFamily.en: Celtic languages alongside Vulgar Latin

philosophy.zh: 以拉丁基督教世界观为主，并保留部分凯尔特地方传统与圣徒崇敬。
philosophy.en: Predominantly shaped by a Latin Christian worldview while retaining elements of local Celtic tradition and saintly devotion.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母，教会和少数行政文书继续使用拉丁文。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet, with Latin continuing in church and limited administrative documents.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、沿海渔业与地方贸易为基础，货币化程度低于帝国全盛时期。尽管政治分裂，但阿莫里卡凭借海岸线、港口和相对独立的地方社群维持了中等规模的区域经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, herding, coastal fishing, and local trade, with a lower degree of monetization than in the imperial high period. Despite political fragmentation, Armorica maintained a moderate regional economy through its coastline, ports, and relatively autonomous local communities.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、渔业、盐业、木材加工、小型金属作坊
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock raising, fishing, salt production, timber working, and small-scale metal craft

tradeGoods.zh: 食盐、鱼类、皮革、木材、谷物、葡萄酒转运品、金属器
tradeGoods.en: Salt, fish, leather, timber, grain, re-exported wine, and metal goods

name.zh: 罗马残存铜币与实物交换并用

unitName.en: solidi fractions and goods in kind

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、牲畜、粮仓、渔具与木石住宅。沿海首领和旧罗马地主拥有更大庄园、仓储和进口奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to land, livestock, granaries, fishing equipment, and timber or stone housing. Coastal chiefs and former Roman landholders possessed larger estates, storage capacity, and imported luxuries.

economicSystem.zh: 以地方农业和实物再分配为主、保留部分罗马港市交换网络的混合型后罗马经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed post-Roman economy based on local agriculture and redistribution in kind, while retaining fragments of Roman port and market exchange networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备零散分布在首领宅邸、教会库藏和港口仓储中，不具备帝国时代那种统一国库。实物储备如粮食、盐与武器的重要性高于现钱。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were scattered among chiefly residences, church stores, and port granaries, lacking the unified treasury of imperial times. Material reserves such as grain, salt, and weapons mattered more than cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向应急与地方自筹，优先维持防务、教会秩序和基本运输设施。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was oriented toward emergency needs and local self-provisioning, prioritizing defense, church order, and basic transport infrastructure.

description.zh: 由自由民和首领随从组成，装备矛、盾、短剑，适合地方防御与伏击。
description.en: Composed of freemen and chiefly retainers, armed with spears, shields, and short blades, suited for local defense and ambush.

description.zh: 数量不多，但由精英战士组成，用于通信、追击与威慑。
description.en: Not numerous, but made up of elite warriors used for communication, pursuit, and intimidation.

description.zh: 负责港口巡防、短程海运与应对沿海袭扰。
description.en: Responsible for port patrols, short-range sea transport, and countering coastal raiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各部族首领与地方战团首脑协同指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; tribal chiefs and local warband leaders coordinated command

technology.zh: 军备继承了部分晚期罗马器械传统，但总体以步兵武装和地方筑垒为主。铁制兵器常见，海岸运输和小型船只具有一定战略价值，但缺少统一重装野战军。
technology.en: Armament retained some late Roman material traditions, but was based chiefly on infantry equipment and local fortification. Iron weapons were common, and coastal transport and small craft had strategic value, but there was no unified heavy field army.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自法兰克势力扩张、地方割据冲突、海上袭扰以及后罗马秩序崩解带来的安全真空。
threats.en: The main threats came from Frankish expansion, local factional conflict, maritime raiding, and the security vacuum created by the breakdown of Roman order.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于乡村、海岸聚落和少量衰落中的罗马旧城。随着不列颠移民进入，部分地区人口结构和语言面貌正在变化。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the countryside, coastal settlements, and a few declining former Roman towns. With migration from Britain, some areas were undergoing change in population structure and language.

socialClasses.zh: 部族首领与战士随从、教士、旧罗马地主与市镇精英、自由农民、佃户与手工业者。
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and warrior retainers, clergy, former Roman landholders and town elites, free peasants, tenants, and artisans.

allies.zh: 可能与部分不列颠移民社群、地方主教网络及反法兰克势力保持合作。
allies.en: Likely cooperated with some British migrant communities, regional bishopric networks, and anti-Frankish forces.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌人包括法兰克扩张者、地方竞争首领以及海上袭扰者。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included expanding Franks, rival local chiefs, and maritime raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护地方自治、利用海峡联系和避免被单一强权彻底吞并为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving local autonomy, using Channel connections, and avoiding complete absorption by any single stronger power.

era.zh: 后罗马西欧早期中世纪过渡阶段
era.en: Post-Roman Western European early medieval transition

keyInnovations.zh: 保留拉丁文书传统、教会组织网络、海岸航运经验和地方筑垒防御。
keyInnovations.en: Retained Latin documentary practice, church organizational networks, coastal shipping experience, and local fortification defense.

strengths.zh: 海岸地理有利防御，地方自治韧性强，保留部分罗马制度与基督教组织。
strengths.en: Its coastline favored defense, local autonomy was resilient, and parts of Roman institutions and Christian organization survived.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，财政薄弱，难以长期维持统一军力。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation was severe, finances were weak, and it struggled to sustain a unified military force over time.

outlook.zh: 短期内可凭地方联盟维持稳定，但中长期将受到法兰克王权增长和内部区域化发展的双重塑形。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain stable through local alliances, but in the medium to long term it would be shaped by both Frankish royal growth and internal regionalization.

description.zh: 阿莫里卡在476年前后是一个融合罗马遗产、凯尔特传统与基督教网络的松散后罗马部落联盟地区。
description.en: Around 476, Armorica was a loose post-Roman confederated region blending Roman legacy, Celtic tradition, and Christian networks.

dynasty.zh: 狄奥多西王朝末期与西罗马宫廷残余
dynasty.en: Late Theodosian line and the residual Western Roman court

governmentForm.zh: 名义上为基督教罗马皇帝统治的君主制帝国，但到476年实际已被军阀与蛮族将领主导。
governmentForm.en: Nominally a Christian imperial monarchy, but by 476 it was in practice dominated by military strongmen and barbarian generals.

socialStructure.zh: 社会仍保留晚期罗马的等级秩序，包括元老贵族、教会神职、城市平民、佃农与奴隶。地方上大地产拥有者势力增强，许多自由农民逐渐依附庄园。基督教会在社会救济、教育与地方秩序中扮演越来越重要的角色。
socialStructure.en: Society retained the late Roman hierarchical order of senatorial aristocrats, clergy, urban commoners, coloni, and slaves. Provincial great landowners had grown in power, and many free peasants were increasingly tied to estates. The Christian Church played an ever larger role in charity, education, and local order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由宫廷官僚、意大利元老贵族、主教集团及日耳曼裔军政强人组成。到罗慕路斯·奥古斯都时期，真正的权力主要掌握在其父奥瑞斯特斯及其他军队首领手中。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of court bureaucrats, the Italian senatorial aristocracy, bishops, and military strongmen of Germanic background. By the time of Romulus Augustulus, real power lay chiefly in the hands of his father Orestes and other army leaders.

succession.zh: 皇位继承在法律上仍属皇帝任命、军队支持与朝廷承认的结合，并非严格世袭。5世纪后期频繁出现军队拥立、废立皇帝的局面，继承秩序极不稳定。
succession.en: Imperial succession remained legally a combination of appointment, military backing, and court recognition rather than strict heredity. In the late 5th century, emperors were frequently elevated and deposed by the army, making succession highly unstable.

structure.zh: 西罗马中央政府沿用晚期帝国官僚体系，以皇帝与宫廷为核心，设有文官、财政与军事职官。然至476年，其有效统治基本限于意大利部分地区，许多行省已由蛮族王国或地方势力控制。朝廷虽然保留罗马法与官衔体系，但行政能力大幅萎缩。
structure.en: The Western Roman central government preserved the late imperial bureaucratic framework, centered on the emperor and court with civil, fiscal, and military offices. By 476, however, effective rule was largely confined to parts of Italy, while many provinces had passed to barbarian kingdoms or local powers. The court retained Roman law and titulature, but its administrative capacity had drastically contracted.

function.zh: 负责民政协调、税收征解、粮食供给与地方行政监督，是名义上的最高文官机构。
function.en: Handled civil coordination, tax collection, grain supply, and oversight of provincial administration, serving as the nominal highest civilian office.

function.zh: 管理铸币、国库现银、宫廷支付与部分商业税收。
function.en: Managed minting, treasury bullion, court payments, and parts of commercial taxation.

function.zh: 管理皇室地产、租赋与附属收入，以补充衰弱的国家财政。
function.en: Administered imperial estates, rents, and dependent revenues to supplement the weakened state finances.

function.zh: 统筹野战军、蛮盟军与边防残部的调动，是晚期西罗马最有实权的机构之一。
function.en: Directed field armies, federate contingents, and surviving frontier troops, making it one of the most powerful offices in the late Western Empire.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政名义上仍分行省、城市与市议会层级，但许多地区的市政制度已衰落。意大利境内仍有主教、地主与地方官共同维持治安、征税与司法。
localAdmin.en: Local administration nominally still operated through provinces, cities, and municipal councils, but civic institutions had decayed in many areas. Within Italy, bishops, landlords, and local officials jointly maintained order, taxation, and justice.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以罗马法为基础，受《狄奥多西法典》及后续敕令规范。实际执行 increasingly 依赖地方官、主教裁断与军事实力，法律统一性已受削弱。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Roman law, especially the Theodosian Code and subsequent enactments. In practice, enforcement increasingly depended on local officials, episcopal judgment, and military power, weakening legal uniformity.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、人头附加、实物征收与关税为主，仍保留晚期帝国按田亩与人口摊派的传统。因领土丧失与逃税严重，税基大幅缩水，负担更多压在意大利与幸存大庄园上。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land tax, poll-related assessments, payments in kind, and customs dues, continuing the late imperial practice of assessments by land and population. With territorial losses and widespread evasion, the tax base shrank sharply, leaving Italy and surviving great estates to bear much of the burden.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管政权濒于瓦解，西罗马仍保存了拉丁法政传统、基督教主教网络与古典教育残脉。修道、抄写与教会文学为后世中世纪西欧文化延续提供了关键桥梁。
culturalAchievements.en: Even as the regime neared collapse, the Western Empire still preserved Latin legal-political traditions, episcopal networks, and remnants of classical education. Monasticism, manuscript copying, and church literature provided a crucial bridge to medieval Western Europe.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系意大利语族，以拉丁语为官方与高文化语言。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Italic branch, with Latin as the official and high cultural language.

philosophy.zh: 以尼西亚基督教神学为主导，同时延续部分古典拉丁修辞学、法学与斯多亚传统。
philosophy.en: Dominated by Nicene Christian theology while still preserving elements of classical Latin rhetoric, jurisprudence, and Stoic tradition.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母书写体系，行政与法律文书主要使用拉丁文。
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet writing system, with Latin as the principal language of administration and law.

gdpDescription.zh: 476年的西罗马经济规模已远逊于帝国盛期，主要依靠意大利农业、地产租赋与有限的地中海贸易维持。高税负、战争破坏与行省丧失使货币流通和城市经济明显萎缩。
gdpDescription.en: By 476, the Western Roman economy was far smaller than at the empire’s height, relying mainly on Italian agriculture, estate rents, and limited Mediterranean trade. Heavy taxation, war damage, and the loss of provinces severely reduced monetization and urban economic life.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、陶器、金属加工、纺织、军需补给
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, wine and olive oil production, pottery, metalworking, textiles, and military supply

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、葡萄酒、橄榄油、盐、陶器、纺织品、奴隶、金属器、奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, salt, pottery, textiles, slaves, metal goods, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 财富分布高度不均，元老贵族和大地主仍掌握大量土地与储藏金属货币，多数农户则接近温饱。城市手工业者与小市民因税负和治安恶化而普遍贫困化。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was highly unequal, with senatorial aristocrats and great landlords still controlling extensive lands and stored precious metal, while most rural households lived near subsistence. Urban artisans and petty townspeople were widely impoverished by taxation and insecurity.

economicSystem.zh: 以大地产农业和国家税收征发为核心的晚期古代混合经济，兼具货币交换、实物地租与强制征购特征。
economicSystem.en: A late antique mixed economy centered on large-estate agriculture and state extraction, combining monetary exchange, rents in kind, and compulsory requisition.

category.en: Relief, diplomatic gifts, and debt obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库已严重枯竭，依赖临时征敛、贵族借贷与对皇室私产的挪用维系。476年前夕的财政储备不足以长期支付军队与边防开支。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was badly depleted, surviving through extraordinary levies, aristocratic loans, and diversion of imperial estate income. On the eve of 476, reserves were insufficient to sustain army pay and frontier expenses for long.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持军费和宫廷运转为优先，表现为加重意大利税收、增加实物征发并频繁依赖非常性收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military upkeep and court survival, marked by heavier taxation in Italy, increased requisitions in kind, and repeated reliance on extraordinary revenues.

description.zh: 由中央机动部队和将领直属部众组成，负责应对意大利境内的主要军事威胁。
description.en: Comprised central mobile forces and commanders’ personal contingents, tasked with responding to major threats within Italy.

description.zh: 原属边境驻军的残余单位，战斗力与编制完整性普遍下降。
description.en: Remnants of former frontier garrisons, generally reduced in strength and organizational integrity.

description.zh: 由日耳曼部族武士和受雇盟军构成，是西罗马后期不可或缺但政治风险极高的军事力量。
description.en: Made up of Germanic warriors and hired allied contingents, indispensable to the late Western Empire but politically highly dangerous.

commanderInChief.zh: 皇帝名义统帅，实权多在军务长官与拥兵权臣手中
commanderInChief.en: The emperor was nominal commander-in-chief, but real control usually lay with the master of soldiers and armed strongmen

technology.zh: 军备沿用晚期罗马传统，装备包括长矛、剑、复合弓、椭圆盾、锁子甲与攻城器械，但生产、训练和后勤体系均已退化。
technology.en: Military equipment followed late Roman practice, including spears, swords, composite bows, oval shields, mail armor, and siege machinery, but production, training, and logistics had all deteriorated.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自驻意大利的蛮盟军兵变、奥多亚塞尔等日耳曼军事集团、汪达尔人的海上袭扰，以及高卢、西班牙和非洲行省的永久丧失。
threats.en: Major threats came from mutiny among federate troops in Italy, Germanic military groups such as Odoacer’s followers, Vandal maritime raids, and the permanent loss of Gaul, Spain, and Africa.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约500万，主要集中于意大利及少数仍存控制区，较帝国盛期已显著下降。农村人口占绝大多数，许多旧行省的城市网络衰退，人口向乡村庄园与防御性聚落回流。
populationDescription.en: The population of about 5 million was concentrated chiefly in Italy and a few remaining controlled areas, far below imperial peak levels. Rural inhabitants formed the vast majority, and many urban networks in former provinces had declined as people shifted toward estates and defensive settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 元老贵族、骑士与官僚、神职人员、城市平民、佃农、自由农、小手工业者、奴隶与蛮盟军社群。
socialClasses.en: Senatorial aristocrats, equestrians and officials, clergy, urban commoners, coloni, free peasants, small artisans, slaves, and federate communities.

allies.zh: 形式上仍与东罗马帝国保持罗马共同体关系，并在局部依赖某些蛮族盟军与意大利地方贵族支持。
allies.en: Formally it still maintained a Roman commonwealth relationship with the Eastern Empire and locally depended on certain barbarian federates and Italian aristocratic support.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量包括汪达尔王国、未受控的日耳曼雇佣军集团，以及已事实上脱离西罗马的各蛮族王国。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries included the Vandal Kingdom, uncontrolled Germanic mercenary groups, and the various barbarian kingdoms that had effectively broken away from Western rule.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以求存为主，依赖承认、联姻、授衔、贡赐与招抚蛮族首领来维持名义权威，而非恢复全面帝国控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was fundamentally one of survival, relying on recognition, marriage ties, titles, gifts, and accommodation of barbarian leaders rather than restoration of full imperial control.

era.zh: 晚期古典时代末期
era.en: Late Antiquity, terminal phase

keyInnovations.zh: 主要承继而非创新：成熟的罗马法行政文书体系、道路与工程传统、晚期堡垒化城市、基督教书籍抄写文化与金币本位财政惯例。
keyInnovations.en: Characterized more by inheritance than innovation: mature Roman legal-administrative documentation, road and engineering traditions, fortified late antique cities, Christian manuscript culture, and gold-based fiscal practice.

strengths.zh: 仍保有罗马帝国的法统、拉丁行政传统、意大利核心区、主教网络与一定城市基础。贵族财富与教会组织在危机中仍能提供部分社会整合能力。
strengths.en: It still possessed Roman imperial legitimacy, Latin administrative traditions, the Italian core, episcopal networks, and a residual urban base. Aristocratic wealth and church organization could still provide some social cohesion in crisis.

weaknesses.zh: 财政破产、军队依赖蛮盟军、领土严重缩小、税基崩溃与继承失序是根本弱点。中央朝廷缺乏稳定军事实力，难以压制地方豪强和拥兵将领。
weaknesses.en: Its fundamental weaknesses were fiscal insolvency, reliance on federate troops, severe territorial contraction, a collapsing tax base, and chaotic succession. The central court lacked the stable military power needed to control magnates and warlords.

outlook.zh: 476年时西罗马帝国实际上已无独立复兴能力，皇位被废只是长期瓦解的制度性终点。其行政、法律与宗教结构将转化并延续于后继的日耳曼王国和中世纪拉丁欧洲。
outlook.en: By 476, the Western Roman Empire had virtually no independent capacity for recovery, and the deposition of its emperor was merely the institutional endpoint of a long disintegration. Its administrative, legal, and religious structures would survive in transformed form under successor Germanic kingdoms and medieval Latin Europe.

description.zh: 476年的西罗马帝国是一个保有罗马名义与制度外壳、但已在财政与军事上濒临终结的残存帝国。
description.en: In 476, the Western Roman Empire was a remnant state that retained the Roman name and institutional shell but was near terminal collapse in fiscal and military terms.

capital.zh: 无固定都城（潘诺尼亚境内流动驻地）
capital.en: No fixed capital (migrant settlements in Pannonia)

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭王权为核心、部落会议与军事贵族参与决策的日耳曼王国体制
governmentForm.en: A Germanic kingship system centered on hereditary royal power, with tribal assemblies and military nobility participating in decision-making

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与军事贵族、自由战士、自由农民、半自由依附者和奴隶构成，忠诚和战功是社会晋升的关键
socialStructure.en: Society comprised the royal family and military nobility, free warriors, free peasants, semi-free dependents, and slaves, with loyalty and martial valor as keys to social advancement

rulingClass.zh: 莱辛格王族、部落贵族与亲随战士首领
rulingClass.en: The Lething royal family, tribal nobles, and retinue warrior leaders

succession.zh: 王位在王族内世袭传承，但需获得武士大会的认可与拥戴
succession.en: The throne passed hereditarily within the royal family, but required acclamation and approval by the warrior assembly

structure.zh: 伦巴第人在476年尚处于潘诺尼亚的半迁徙阶段，政府为王权与部落会议并行的简单体制。国王统率军事行动与对外关系，但内部事务仍依赖氏族长老和部落集会协商。
structure.en: In 476 the Lombards were still in a semi-migratory phase in Pannonia. Government was a simple system of parallel royal authority and tribal assembly. The king led military operations and foreign affairs, while internal matters still relied on clan elders and tribal council deliberation.

function.zh: 管理国王亲随、贡赋收集、宴飨与对外使节接待
function.en: Managed the king's retinue, tribute collection, feasting, and reception of foreign envoys

function.zh: 由自由民和贵族共同参与，审议迁徙、战争、联盟和法律事务
function.en: Attended by freemen and nobles, deliberating on migration, war, alliances, and legal affairs

function.zh: 国王核心武装力量，负责征战、护卫与战利品分配
function.en: The king's core armed force, responsible for warfare, guarding, and distribution of booty

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由氏族首领和村社长老负责，无书面行政体系，以习惯法和口头裁决为主。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was handled by clan leaders and village elders, with no written administrative system, relying on customary law and oral rulings.

legalSystem.zh: 以日耳曼习惯法为基础，强调赔命金、宣誓和亲族连带责任，纠纷由部落会议或长老裁定。
legalSystem.en: Based on Germanic customary law, emphasizing wergild, oaths, and kin-group liability, with disputes adjudicated by tribal assemblies or elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，国王收入依赖战利品、附庸贡纳、王室牧场与牲畜收益以及司法罚金。
taxationSystem.en: No formal tax system; royal income depended on war booty, vassal tribute, royal pasture and livestock revenues, and judicial fines.

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神信仰为主，崇拜沃丹、弗蕾亚等神灵，兼有泛灵崇拜与祖先祭祀
religion.en: Predominantly Germanic polytheism, worshipping Wodan, Freyja, and other deities, combined with animist and ancestor worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精湛的金属工艺著称，尤其是武器锻造、金银饰品和动物风格装饰艺术，此外还有口头英雄史诗传统
culturalAchievements.en: Renowned for superb metalwork, especially weapon forging, gold and silver jewelry, and animal-style decorative art, alongside an oral heroic epic tradition

languageFamily.zh: 西日耳曼语支，使用早期伦巴第方言
languageFamily.en: West Germanic, speaking an early Lombard dialect

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学传统，价值体系强调勇武、荣誉、忠诚和命运
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophical tradition; the value system emphasized martial valor, honor, loyalty, and fate

writingSystem.zh: 主要为口述文化，偶尔使用如尼文字铭刻武器或纪念物
writingSystem.en: Primarily oral culture, with occasional use of runic inscriptions on weapons or commemorative objects

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 伦巴第经济以自给农牧和战争掠夺为基础，货币化程度很低，财富多以牲畜、武器和金属器形式体现。
gdpDescription.en: The Lombard economy was based on subsistence agro-pastoralism and war plunder, with very low monetization; wealth was largely expressed in livestock, weapons, and metalwork.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧业、农业、铁器锻造、武器制造、畜产品加工
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, agriculture, iron smithing, weapon manufacture, livestock product processing

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、毛皮、武器、金属饰品、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, leather, furs, weapons, metal ornaments, slaves

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以耕地使用权、牛马、农具和房屋为财富核心；贵族家庭积累武器、金银饰品和奴隶
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households based wealth on land-use rights, cattle, horses, tools, and houses; noble families accumulated weapons, gold and silver ornaments, and slaves

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、部落贡纳与战利品再分配为核心的部落经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on village subsistence, tribal tribute, and redistribution of war booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 伦巴第人无统一国库，储备分散于国王私藏、贵族宝物和神庙献品中。
treasuryDescription.en: The Lombards had no unified state treasury; 'reserves' were dispersed among the king's personal hoard, noble treasures, and temple offerings.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 无正式财政政策，以战争获利和部落义务为收入来源，军事支出优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: No formal fiscal policy; income derived from warfare gains and tribal obligations, with military spending prioritized.

description.zh: 以长矛、盾牌和短剑武装的部落步兵，构成军队主力
description.en: Tribal foot soldiers armed with spears, shields, and short swords, forming the bulk of the army

description.zh: 贵族与国王亲卫骑兵，装备较精良，是突击力量核心
description.en: Noble and royal guard cavalry, better equipped and serving as the core shock force

technology.zh: 日耳曼铁制武器与盾牌、骑术与马上作战技术，部分受罗马和草原民族军事影响
technology.en: Germanic iron weaponry and shields, horsemanship and mounted combat techniques, partially influenced by Roman and steppe military practices

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自匈人残余势力、格皮德人、赫鲁利人以及潘诺尼亚地区的其他日耳曼竞争部族
threats.en: Main threats came from Hunnic remnants, Gepids, Heruli, and other competing Germanic peoples in the Pannonian region

populationDescription.zh: 约10万人，分散在潘诺尼亚中部和北部的半定居村落和流动营地中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 100,000 people, dispersed across semi-settled villages and mobile camps in central and northern Pannonia.

name.zh: 无固定中心（国王驻地随季节迁移）
name.en: No fixed center (royal seat migrated seasonally)

socialClasses.zh: 王族、军事贵族、自由战士、自由农民、半自由依附者、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, military nobility, free warriors, free peasants, semi-free dependents, slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分潘诺尼亚日耳曼部落保持松散联盟，偶尔与东罗马帝国有接触。
allies.en: Maintained loose alliances with some Pannonian Germanic tribes, with occasional contact with the Eastern Roman Empire.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为格皮德人和赫鲁利人，以及匈人残余势力。
enemies.en: Chief adversaries were the Gepids and Heruli, as well as Hunnic remnant forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以生存和领土扩张为导向，通过联盟、迁徙与战争在多民族竞争的潘诺尼亚地区寻求立足之地。
foreignPolicy.en: Oriented toward survival and territorial expansion, seeking a foothold in the multi-ethnic competitive Pannonian region through alliances, migration, and warfare.

era.zh: 民族大迁徙时代
era.en: Migration Period

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造、骑兵战术、日耳曼式木构建筑与部落防御工事
keyInnovations.en: Iron forging, cavalry tactics, Germanic timber construction, and tribal defensive earthworks

strengths.zh: 王权凝聚力较强，战士忠诚度高，机动性好，善于利用权力真空扩张领地。
strengths.en: Relatively strong royal cohesion, high warrior loyalty, good mobility, and skill at exploiting power vacuums for territorial expansion.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、经济基础薄弱、无稳固领土、面对多方竞争对手易陷入孤立。
weaknesses.en: Small population, weak economic base, no secure territory, and vulnerability to isolation when facing multiple competitors.

outlook.zh: 伦巴第人正处于积蓄力量阶段，未来将南迁意大利并建立持久的伦巴第王国。
outlook.en: The Lombards were in a phase of building strength, and would eventually migrate south into Italy and establish the enduring Lombard Kingdom.

description.zh: 476年的伦巴第人是一支驻留于潘诺尼亚的日耳曼部族，拥有世袭王权，以农牧与征战为生，尚处于民族大迁徙的积蓄阶段。
description.en: In 476, the Lombards were a Germanic tribe residing in Pannonia with hereditary kingship, living by agro-pastoralism and warfare, still in the accumulative phase of the Migration Period.

ruler.zh: 多位地区国王与酋长（无统一君主）
ruler.en: Multiple regional kings and chieftains (no unified monarch)

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝（多个竞争王族）
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty (multiple competing royal families)

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各部各有中心聚落）
capital.en: No unified capital (each sub-tribe had its own central settlement)

governmentForm.zh: 松散的日耳曼部落联盟体制，由多位国王、酋长和部落会议共治
governmentForm.en: A loose Germanic tribal confederacy governed jointly by multiple kings, chieftains, and tribal assemblies

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、军事贵族、自由民农战士、半自由佃户和奴隶组成，以战功和世系决定地位
socialStructure.en: Society comprised royal lineages, military aristocrats, free farmer-warriors, semi-free tenants, and slaves, with status determined by martial achievement and lineage

rulingClass.zh: 多个竞争王族、军事贵族和氏族长老
rulingClass.en: Multiple competing royal families, military aristocrats, and clan elders

succession.zh: 各部落国王位由王族内传承，但须经武士大会拥戴，导致频繁的内部竞争
succession.en: Each sub-tribal kingship passed within the royal family, but required warrior assembly acclamation, leading to frequent internal competition

structure.zh: 阿拉曼尼联盟由多个半独立部落王国和酋邦组成，无中央政府。各部国王在本部独立施政，联合行动仅在共同威胁时临时协调。
structure.en: The Alamannic confederation consisted of multiple semi-independent tribal kingdoms and chieftaincies, with no central government. Each sub-king governed independently within his own polity, with joint action only temporarily coordinated against common threats.

function.zh: 管理国王或酋长的亲随、宴飨、贡赋收缴与外事接待
function.en: Managed the king's or chieftain's retinue, feasting, tribute collection, and foreign receptions

function.zh: 自由民大会，审议战争、法律裁决、联盟和迁徙等重大事务
function.en: Assembly of freemen deliberating on war, legal judgments, alliances, and migration

function.zh: 各部战团执行征战、边境防御和内部秩序维护
function.en: War-bands of each sub-tribe conducted warfare, frontier defense, and internal order maintenance

name.zh: 地方百户长与村社长老

function.zh: 在村社层面主持司法裁决、土地分配与日常事务管理
function.en: Presided over judicial rulings, land allocation, and daily affairs at the village level

localAdmin.zh: 地方由百户区和村社组织治理，长老和百户长依据习惯法处理日常纠纷和土地事务。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were governed by hundredal districts and village organizations, with elders and hundredmen handling disputes and land matters according to customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 日耳曼习惯法传统，强调赔命金、宣誓裁判、亲族连带和荣誉决斗，后世编纂为《阿拉曼尼法典》。
legalSystem.en: Germanic customary law tradition, emphasizing wergild, oath-based adjudication, kin-group liability, and honor combat, later codified as the Lex Alamannorum.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，各部国王收入来源包括王室地产、贡纳、司法罚金和战利品分成。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system; each sub-king's income derived from royal estates, tribute, judicial fines, and shares of war booty.

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神信仰为主，崇拜提瓦兹、沃丹等神灵，兼有自然精灵崇拜；基督教尚未渗入
religion.en: Predominantly Germanic polytheism, worshipping Tiwaz, Wodan, and other deities, with nature-spirit veneration; Christianity had not yet penetrated

culturalAchievements.zh: 精美的金属工艺和动物风格装饰、木构建筑技术、口头英雄传说和如尼铭文传统
culturalAchievements.en: Fine metalwork and animal-style ornamentation, timber construction techniques, oral heroic legends, and runic inscription traditions

languageFamily.zh: 西日耳曼语支，使用早期阿拉曼方言（古高德语先驱）
languageFamily.en: West Germanic, speaking an early Alamannic dialect (precursor of Old High German)

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学，价值体系以勇气、忠诚、荣誉和命运为核心
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy; the value system centered on courage, loyalty, honor, and fate

writingSystem.zh: 口述文化为主，如尼文字用于铭刻器物和纪念碑
writingSystem.en: Predominantly oral culture, with runic script used for inscribing objects and monuments

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 阿拉曼尼经济以农牧为基础，兼有林业和铁器手工业，受惠于莱茵河流域的贸易通道。
gdpDescription.en: The Alamannic economy was based on agro-pastoralism, supplemented by forestry and iron handicrafts, and benefited from Rhine valley trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、林业、铁器锻造、纺织、制陶
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, forestry, iron forging, textile weaving, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、木材、铁器、皮革、琥珀、毛皮、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, timber, iron goods, leather, amber, furs, slaves

name.zh: 以物易物与罗马旧币并行

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以耕地、牛羊、农具和房屋为主要财产；贵族家庭积累武器、金饰、马匹和奴隶
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held arable land, cattle, sheep, tools, and houses as principal assets; noble families accumulated weapons, gold ornaments, horses, and slaves

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、贵族地产和战利品再分配为核心的混合部落经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal economy centered on village subsistence, aristocratic estates, and redistribution of war booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 各部国王各有私藏，无联盟统一国库，储备以金银器、武器和牲畜分散保管。
treasuryDescription.en: Each sub-king maintained a private hoard; there was no confederate treasury, with reserves dispersed as gold and silver vessels, weapons, and livestock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策因部而异，总体以保障军事开支和王室供养为首要目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy varied by sub-tribe, with the general priority of sustaining military expenditure and royal household provision.

description.zh: 自由民农战士，战时应召集结，以长矛、斧头和盾牌为主武器
description.en: Free farmer-warriors called up in wartime, armed primarily with spears, axes, and shields

description.zh: 国王和贵族的骑马随从，装备精良，承担冲击与追击任务
description.en: Mounted retainers of kings and nobles, well-equipped, performing shock charges and pursuit

description.zh: 驻守莱茵河和多瑙河沿线前哨的轻装巡逻部队
description.en: Lightly armed patrol units stationed at outposts along the Rhine and Danube frontiers

commanderInChief.zh: 各部国王独立指挥，联合作战时由声望最高者临时统领
commanderInChief.en: Each sub-king commanded independently; in joint operations, the most prestigious leader served as temporary supreme commander

technology.zh: 精良的铁制武器和盔甲、骑兵战术、借鉴罗马边防工事技术
technology.en: Excellent iron weaponry and armor, cavalry tactics, and borrowings from Roman frontier fortification techniques

threats.zh: 法兰克人自北面扩张的压力、勃艮第人从西面的竞争、以及东罗马帝国的潜在干预
threats.en: Frankish expansion pressure from the north, Burgundian competition from the west, and potential Eastern Roman intervention

populationDescription.zh: 约30万人，分布于上莱茵河、博登湖、施瓦本高地及多瑙河上游地区的定居村落中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 300,000 people, spread across settled villages in the upper Rhine, Lake Constance, Swabian highlands, and upper Danube regions.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、军事贵族、自由民农战士、半自由佃户、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal families, military aristocrats, free farmer-warriors, semi-free tenants, slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分莱茵河流域日耳曼部落有松散往来，偶与东罗马帝国有外交接触。
allies.en: Maintained loose contacts with some Rhine-region Germanic tribes, with occasional diplomatic engagement with the Eastern Roman Empire.

enemies.zh: 法兰克人和勃艮第人是主要竞争者；与高卢-罗马地方政权也存在摩擦。
enemies.en: The Franks and Burgundians were main competitors; friction also existed with Gallo-Roman local authorities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持莱茵河-施瓦本地区的控制为核心，在多方势力间以联姻和战争交替周旋。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining control of the Rhine-Swabian region, alternating between marriage alliances and warfare among multiple powers.

era.zh: 民族大迁徙时代
era.en: Migration Period

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造与武器制造、农业三田轮作雏形、利用前罗马基础设施（道路、要塞）
keyInnovations.en: Iron forging and weapon manufacture, embryonic three-field crop rotation, and utilization of former Roman infrastructure (roads, forts)

strengths.zh: 占据肥沃的莱茵-施瓦本地区，人口较多，武士传统深厚，善于利用罗马遗留基础设施。
strengths.en: Held fertile Rhine-Swabian lands, had a relatively large population, deep warrior tradition, and skill in utilizing Roman-era infrastructure.

weaknesses.zh: 联盟内部缺乏统一政权，多王并立易致内耗；面临法兰克和勃艮第双面夹击。
weaknesses.en: The confederation lacked unified governance; multiple competing kings led to internal friction; faced pressure from both Franks and Burgundians.

outlook.zh: 阿拉曼尼人控制着重要的莱茵河地区，但联盟松散将使其在未来难以抵御法兰克人的统一攻势。
outlook.en: The Alamanni controlled the important Rhine region, but their loose confederation would make it difficult to withstand the Franks' unified offensive in the future.

description.zh: 476年的阿拉曼尼人是占据上莱茵河与施瓦本地区的日耳曼部落联盟，由多位国王和酋长共治，以农牧和战争为经济基础。
description.en: In 476, the Alamanni were a Germanic tribal confederation occupying the upper Rhine and Swabian region, jointly ruled by multiple kings and chieftains, with an economy based on agro-pastoralism and warfare.

capital.zh: 无确定都城（可能在图林根中部地区）
capital.en: No certain capital (likely in central Thuringia)

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭王权为核心、地方酋长和自由民大会为辅的日耳曼王国体制
governmentForm.en: A Germanic kingdom with hereditary kingship at its core, assisted by local chieftains and free assembly

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、军事贵族（阿德尔）、自由民、半自由佃户和奴隶构成
socialStructure.en: Society comprised the royal family, military aristocrats (adel), freemen, semi-free tenants, and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 王族和军事贵族阶层
rulingClass.en: The royal family and military aristocratic class

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，但常受贵族会议影响和内部竞争制约
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, but was often influenced by noble councils and constrained by internal competition

structure.zh: 图林根王国在476年由国王比辛统治，虽建立了相对集中的王权，但地方仍由氏族酋长和区域贵族管理。国王是军事领袖兼最高裁判者。
structure.en: The Thuringian kingdom in 476 was ruled by King Bisinus, who had established relatively centralized royal authority, though local areas were still managed by clan chieftains and regional nobles. The king was the military leader and supreme judge.

function.zh: 管理国王家事、亲随供养、贡赋征集与外交事务
function.en: Managed royal domestic affairs, retinue provision, tribute collection, and diplomatic matters

function.zh: 国王核心武装力量，负责作战、护卫和秩序维护
function.en: The king's core armed force, responsible for combat, guarding, and maintaining order

function.zh: 在各区域代行行政与司法职能
function.en: Exercised administrative and judicial functions at the regional level

localAdmin.zh: 地方由区域酋长和村社长老治理，依据日耳曼习惯法处理事务。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were governed by regional chieftains and village elders, handling affairs according to Germanic customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 日耳曼习惯法体系，以赔命金、宣誓和亲族集体责任为核心机制。
legalSystem.en: A Germanic customary law system, with wergild, oaths, and collective kin responsibility as its core mechanisms.

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡纳、王室地产收入和司法罚金为主要财政来源，无货币化税收体制。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue primarily from tribute in kind, royal estate income, and judicial fines; no monetized taxation system.

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神信仰为主，崇拜沃丹、杜纳尔等战神与丰产神
religion.en: Predominantly Germanic polytheism, worshipping war and fertility deities such as Wodan and Thunar
