culturalAchievements.zh: 精良的武器和金属饰品制造、口头英雄诗歌与传说、木构建筑与山丘堡垒
culturalAchievements.en: Excellent weapon and metal ornament crafting, oral heroic poetry and legends, timber construction, and hilltop fortifications

languageFamily.zh: 西日耳曼语支，使用早期图林根方言
languageFamily.en: West Germanic, speaking an early Thuringian dialect

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学传统，价值观以勇武、忠诚、荣誉为核心
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophical tradition; values centered on martial valor, loyalty, and honor

writingSystem.zh: 主要为口述文化，偶用如尼文字铭刻
writingSystem.en: Primarily oral culture, with occasional runic inscriptions

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农牧业为基础，手工业集中于铁器和纺织，对外贸易有限但受益于中欧陆路通道。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agro-pastoralism, with handicrafts focused on ironwork and textiles; foreign trade was limited but benefited from Central European overland routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、铁器锻造、盐业、纺织
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, iron smithing, salt production, textile weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、铁器、盐、毛皮、琥珀、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, iron goods, salt, furs, amber, slaves

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以耕地、牲畜和房屋为主要财产；贵族积累武器、马匹和金属饰品
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held arable land, livestock, and houses as main property; nobles accumulated weapons, horses, and metal ornaments

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自给农牧和实物贡纳为核心的王国经济
economicSystem.en: A kingdom economy centered on subsistence agro-pastoralism and tribute in kind

treasuryDescription.zh: 国王私藏与贵族宝物构成储备，以金银器皿、武器和牲畜为主要形式。
treasuryDescription.en: The king's personal hoard and noble treasures constituted reserves, mainly in the form of gold and silver vessels, weapons, and livestock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以保障国王军事力量和维持贵族忠诚为财政优先方向。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priority was to sustain the king's military force and maintain noble loyalty.

description.zh: 自由民征召步兵，装备长矛、斧头和盾牌
description.en: Levied freemen infantry armed with spears, axes, and shields

description.zh: 国王与贵族的骑马武士，装备较精良
description.en: Mounted warriors of the king and nobility, with superior equipment

description.zh: 驻守山丘堡垒和关键通道的防御部队
description.en: Defensive troops garrisoning hilltop forts and key passes

technology.zh: 日耳曼铁制武器、盾牌方阵战术、山丘堡垒防御工事
technology.en: Germanic iron weaponry, shield-wall tactics, and hilltop fortress defenses

threats.zh: 法兰克人从西北方向的扩张威胁，以及撒克逊人和其他日耳曼部族的竞争
threats.en: Frankish expansion from the northwest, and competition from Saxons and other Germanic tribes

populationDescription.zh: 约20万人，分布于德国中部的图林根盆地和丘陵地带。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 200,000 people, distributed across the Thuringian Basin and hill country of central Germany.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族、自由民农战士、半自由佃户、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, nobility, free farmer-warriors, semi-free tenants, slaves.

allies.zh: 与法兰克王族有联姻关系（传说希尔德里克一世曾流亡至图林根），并与邻近日耳曼部族保持松散联系。
allies.en: Had marriage ties with the Frankish royal house (Childeric I was said to have taken refuge in Thuringia), and maintained loose contacts with neighboring Germanic tribes.

enemies.zh: 撒克逊人和其他北方日耳曼部族是潜在竞争对手。
enemies.en: Saxons and other northern Germanic tribes were potential competitors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持中部德国的独立地位为核心，通过联姻和选择性结盟在各势力间周旋。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining an independent position in central Germany, maneuvering among powers through marriage alliances and selective coalitions.

era.zh: 民族大迁徙时代
era.en: Migration Period

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造、山丘要塞建筑、农业耕作改良
keyInnovations.en: Iron forging, hilltop fortress construction, and agricultural cultivation improvements

strengths.zh: 拥有相对统一的王权、地处中欧战略要地、农业基础稳固。
strengths.en: Possessed relatively unified royal authority, strategically located in Central Europe, with a stable agricultural base.

weaknesses.zh: 人口有限、经济规模小、处于法兰克扩张的潜在攻击方向上。
weaknesses.en: Limited population, small economic scale, and located in the potential path of Frankish expansion.

outlook.zh: 图林根王国在5世纪末处于鼎盛期，但其领土将在6世纪被法兰克人征服。
outlook.en: The Thuringian kingdom was at its peak in the late 5th century, but its territory would be conquered by the Franks in the 6th century.

description.zh: 476年的图林根王国是一个由国王比辛统治的中等规模日耳曼王国，位于德国中部，以农牧和军事为经济基础，正处于鼎盛时期。
description.en: In 476, the Thuringian kingdom was a mid-sized Germanic kingdom ruled by King Bisinus in central Germany, based on agro-pastoralism and military activity, at the height of its power.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的沿海部落酋邦体制，以氏族自治和民众集会为基础
governmentForm.en: A dispersed coastal tribal chieftaincy system based on clan autonomy and popular assemblies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以自由农民和渔民为主体，上有酋长和显贵家族，下有依附者和少量奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society centered on free farmers and fishermen, with chieftains and notable families above, and dependents and a small number of slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长和显贵家族
rulingClass.en: Local chieftains and notable families

succession.zh: 酋长地位在显贵家族中传承，但需获得自由民集会的认可
succession.en: Chieftainly status passed within notable families, but required recognition by the assembly of freemen

structure.zh: 弗里斯兰人在476年无统一政权，由沿北海海岸分布的多个部落共同体独立治理。各部事务由酋长与自由民集会协商决定。
structure.en: In 476, the Frisians had no unified polity but were governed independently by multiple tribal communities distributed along the North Sea coast. Affairs were decided through consultation between chieftains and assemblies of freemen.

function.zh: 管理酋长家事、贡赋收集和对外联络
function.en: Managed chieftainly domestic affairs, tribute collection, and external contacts

function.zh: 审议战争、法律纠纷和公共事务的民众大会
function.en: Popular assembly deliberating on war, legal disputes, and public affairs

function.zh: 组织和维护海岸防洪堤坝、围垦造田的协调工作
function.en: Organized and maintained coastal flood dykes and coordinated land-reclamation efforts

localAdmin.zh: 各村落和特尔普（人工丘）聚落由长老和酋长自治管理。
localAdmin.en: Individual villages and terp (artificial mound) settlements were self-governed by elders and chieftains.

legalSystem.zh: 日耳曼习惯法，强调自由民权利、赔命金和集会裁决，后来发展为《弗里斯兰法》。
legalSystem.en: Germanic customary law, emphasizing freemen's rights, wergild, and assembly-based adjudication, later developing into the Lex Frisionum.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共收入来自贸易过境费、司法罚金和自愿贡纳。
taxationSystem.en: No formal tax system; public income derived from trade transit fees, judicial fines, and voluntary tribute.

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神信仰，尤重海洋神灵和丰产祭祀，以及沼泽与泉水崇拜
religion.en: Germanic polytheism, with particular emphasis on sea-related deities and fertility rites, along with veneration of marshes and springs

culturalAchievements.zh: 卓越的海岸工程技术（特尔普人工丘与堤坝）、造船术、精美的金属饰品和贸易网络
culturalAchievements.en: Outstanding coastal engineering (terp mounds and dykes), shipbuilding, fine metalwork ornaments, and trade networks

languageFamily.zh: 西日耳曼语支，使用早期弗里斯兰方言
languageFamily.en: West Germanic, speaking an early Frisian dialect

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学，重视自由、独立、集体协作和与海洋共存的实用智慧
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy; valued freedom, independence, collective cooperation, and practical wisdom of coexistence with the sea

writingSystem.zh: 口述文化为主，少量如尼铭文
writingSystem.en: Predominantly oral culture, with sparse runic inscriptions

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 弗里斯兰经济以渔业、畜牧和沿海贸易为特色，因地处北海贸易通道而受益，货币化程度低但贸易活跃。
gdpDescription.en: The Frisian economy was characterized by fishing, pastoralism, and coastal trade, benefiting from its location on North Sea trade routes; low monetization but active commerce.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、畜牧、制盐、造船、纺织、沿海贸易
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, pastoralism, salt production, shipbuilding, textile weaving, coastal trade

tradeGoods.zh: 鱼类、盐、牲畜、羊毛制品、皮革、琥珀、毛皮
tradeGoods.en: Fish, salt, livestock, wool products, leather, amber, furs

name.zh: 以物易物为主，少量罗马旧币流通
name.en: Predominantly barter, with some circulating Roman coins

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以牲畜、渔船、盐田和特尔普住宅为主要财产
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households based wealth on livestock, fishing boats, salt pans, and terp dwellings

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给与沿海贸易并重的海洋部落经济
economicSystem.en: A maritime tribal economy balancing village subsistence with coastal trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 无统一国库，各酋长和显贵家族各有储备，以贵金属、贸易品和牲畜为主。
treasuryDescription.en: No unified treasury; each chieftain and notable family held reserves, mainly in precious metals, trade goods, and livestock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 公共支出优先保障堤坝维护和海岸防御，由集会协商分摊。
fiscalPolicy.en: Public expenditure prioritized dyke maintenance and coastal defense, apportioned through assembly consultation.

description.zh: 自由民征召步兵，负责保卫聚落和海岸线
description.en: Levied freemen infantry, responsible for defending settlements and the coastline

description.zh: 精通航海的战士，驾船巡防海域并参与近海作战
description.en: Seafaring warriors who patrolled waters by boat and engaged in coastal combat

technology.zh: 沿海防御工事、精良造船术、水战技巧
technology.en: Coastal defensive works, expert shipbuilding, and naval combat skills

threats.zh: 撒克逊人和法兰克人的领土扩张压力，以及北海海盗威胁
threats.en: Territorial expansion pressure from Saxons and Franks, and North Sea pirate threats

populationDescription.zh: 约10万人，散居于北海沿岸的特尔普人工丘聚落和低地村庄中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 100,000 people, scattered across terp mound settlements and lowland villages along the North Sea coast.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长、显贵家族、自由农渔民、依附者、少量奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Chieftains, notable families, free farmers and fishermen, dependents, small number of slaves.

allies.zh: 与撒克逊诸部和盎格鲁人有文化亲缘关系和松散往来。
allies.en: Shared cultural affinity and loose contacts with the Saxon tribes and Angles.

enemies.zh: 法兰克人是主要潜在威胁，与南方势力存在领土摩擦。
enemies.en: The Franks were the main potential threat, with territorial friction with southern powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以保持沿海独立和自由贸易为核心，避免与强邻正面冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining coastal independence and free trade, avoiding direct confrontation with powerful neighbors.

era.zh: 民族大迁徙时代
era.en: Migration Period

keyInnovations.zh: 特尔普人工丘建造技术、海岸堤坝工程、近海造船与航海术
keyInnovations.en: Terp mound construction, coastal dyke engineering, and coastal shipbuilding and navigation

strengths.zh: 独特的沿海适应技术、强大的自由民传统、有利的贸易位置。
strengths.en: Unique coastal adaptation technologies, strong freemen tradition, and advantageous trade location.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、缺乏统一政权、易受大国挤压、依赖脆弱的海岸生态。
weaknesses.en: Small population, lack of unified governance, vulnerability to great-power pressure, and dependence on fragile coastal ecology.

outlook.zh: 弗里斯兰人将凭借贸易优势在中世纪早期繁荣一时，但最终被法兰克王国吞并。
outlook.en: The Frisians would enjoy a period of prosperity in the early Middle Ages through trade, but would eventually be absorbed by the Frankish kingdom.

description.zh: 476年的弗里斯兰人是北海沿岸的日耳曼部落群体，以渔业、畜牧和沿海贸易为生，居住在特尔普人工丘上，自由民传统深厚。
description.en: In 476, the Frisians were a Germanic tribal group along the North Sea coast, living by fishing, pastoralism, and coastal trade, dwelling on terp mounds, with a deep tradition of freemen's rights.

ruler.zh: 众里（国王），名义上的最高王驻塔拉
ruler.en: Multiple rí (kings), with a nominal High King at Tara

dynasty.zh: 尼尔王族等多个竞争王朝
dynasty.en: The Uí Néill and several other competing dynasties

governmentForm.zh: 多级世袭王权体制，由最高王、省王（rí cóicid）和地方王（rí túaithe）构成分层统治
governmentForm.en: A multi-tiered hereditary kingship system, with layered rule by a High King, provincial kings (rí cóicid), and local kings (rí túaithe)

socialStructure.zh: 严格的等级社会，由王族、贵族（flaith）、德鲁伊/基督教教士、法学家（brehon）、自由民（bó-aire）、佃农和奴隶构成
socialStructure.en: A strictly hierarchical society comprising royal families, nobility (flaith), druids/Christian clergy, brehon jurists, freemen (bó-aire), tenant farmers, and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 王族、上层贵族与教会领袖
rulingClass.en: Royal families, upper nobility, and church leaders

succession.zh: 王位通过“指定继承人”（tánaiste）制度在王族内传承，由宗族推选最有能力的候选人
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family via the 'designated heir' (tánaiste) system, with the most capable candidate chosen by the kindred

structure.zh: 爱尔兰在476年由约150个小王国（túath）组成，分属五大省区（康诺特、阿尔斯特、伦斯特、芒斯特和米斯），名义上尊奉塔拉最高王。实际权力高度分散于各级国王之间。
structure.en: In 476, Ireland comprised roughly 150 petty kingdoms (túatha), grouped into five major provinces (Connacht, Ulster, Leinster, Munster, and Meath), nominally recognizing the High King at Tara. Real power was highly dispersed among kings at various levels.

function.zh: 管理国王宴飨、客征税（食宿征发）、亲随与使节接待
function.en: Managed royal feasting, coshering (food-render exaction), retinue, and reception of envoys

function.zh: 依据传统布雷洪法裁决纠纷、确定赔偿和维护法律秩序
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, determined compensation, and maintained legal order according to traditional Brehon law

function.zh: 提供宗教、教育、文字记录和外交调停服务
function.en: Provided religious, educational, record-keeping, and diplomatic mediation services

function.zh: 各小王国的实际统治者，负责本地治安、司法和征兵
function.en: The actual rulers of each petty kingdom, responsible for local order, justice, and military levies

localAdmin.zh: 各小王国由地方国王独立治理，布雷洪法官和教会在跨区域事务中发挥调停作用。
localAdmin.en: Each petty kingdom was governed independently by its local king, with brehon jurists and the church mediating in cross-regional matters.

legalSystem.zh: 布雷洪法（Brehon Law），一套复杂的习惯法体系，涵盖赔偿、荣誉价格、婚姻、财产和契约关系。
legalSystem.en: Brehon Law, a complex customary legal system covering compensation, honor price, marriage, property, and contractual relations.

taxationSystem.zh: 无货币税制，以客征税（食宿征发）、牲畜贡纳、劳役义务和战时征召为主要方式。
taxationSystem.en: No monetary taxation; revenue came mainly from coshering (food-render exaction), livestock tribute, labor obligations, and wartime levies.

religion.zh: 基督教迅速扩张中（圣帕特里克传教后约三十年），与残余的德鲁伊信仰并存；修院制度正在兴起
religion.en: Christianity was rapidly expanding (about thirty years after St. Patrick's mission), coexisting with residual druidic beliefs; the monastic system was rising

culturalAchievements.zh: 欧甘文字与拉丁文并用、早期修院手稿文化萌芽、金属工艺（胸针、圣杯）、吟唱诗人传统和传奇文学
culturalAchievements.en: Ogham and Latin scripts in parallel use, early monastic manuscript culture emerging, metalwork (brooches, chalices), bardic tradition, and legendary literature

languageFamily.zh: 盖尔语（原始爱尔兰语向古爱尔兰语过渡期）
languageFamily.en: Goidelic (transitional phase from Primitive Irish to Old Irish)

philosophy.zh: 德鲁伊知识传统与基督教经院学问交融，重视诗歌、法律和记忆术
philosophy.en: A blend of druidic knowledge tradition and Christian scholastic learning, valuing poetry, law, and the art of memory

writingSystem.zh: 欧甘文字（用于石刻铭文）与初传的拉丁文字并行
writingSystem.en: Ogham script (for stone inscriptions) alongside the newly introduced Latin script

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 爱尔兰经济以畜牧业为核心（牛是主要财富度量），辅以农耕、渔猎和修院手工业，几乎无货币流通。
gdpDescription.en: The Irish economy was centered on pastoralism (cattle as the primary measure of wealth), supplemented by arable farming, fishing, hunting, and monastic handicrafts, with virtually no monetary circulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧（以牛为主）、农业、渔业、金属工艺、修院手抄与纺织
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism (cattle-based), agriculture, fishing, metalworking, monastic scribing, and textile weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 牛、皮革、羊毛、鱼、蜂蜜、金属饰品、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, leather, wool, fish, honey, metal ornaments, slaves

name.en: Cattle as the unit of value

householdWealth.zh: 财富以牛群规模衡量，自由民拥有耕地、牲畜和房屋；贵族还持有客户（附庸佃农）和金属器
householdWealth.en: Wealth was measured by herd size; freemen held arable land, livestock, and houses; nobles also held clients (dependent tenants) and metalwork

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以牛为核心的牧业经济，以客户制和食宿征发为财富再分配机制
economicSystem.en: A cattle-centered pastoral economy, with the clientage system and food-render as wealth redistribution mechanisms

category.zh: 法律官与吟游诗人供养

treasuryDescription.zh: 各级国王各自持有“国库”，以牛群、金属器物、武器和教会捐赠品为主要形式。
treasuryDescription.en: Each level of king maintained a 'treasury,' mainly in the form of cattle herds, metalwork, weapons, and church donations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持王室宴飨能力（象征权力的核心）和军事征召能力为首要目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining the king's feasting capacity (the core symbol of power) and military levy capability.

description.zh: 各小王国的自由民征召步兵，装备长矛、剑和盾牌
description.en: Freemen levy infantry from each petty kingdom, armed with spears, swords, and shields

description.zh: 贵族精英的骑马或乘车武士
description.en: Noble elite mounted or chariot-borne warriors

description.zh: 对不列颠和高卢海岸进行袭击的水手战士
description.en: Sailor-warriors raiding the coasts of Britain and Gaul

commanderInChief.zh: 各级国王独立指挥，名义上最高王可协调联合行动
commanderInChief.en: Each king commanded independently; nominally the High King could coordinate joint operations

technology.zh: 铁制武器和盾牌、战车传统（渐渐衰退）、以及跨海袭击的造船与航海术
technology.en: Iron weaponry and shields, chariot tradition (gradually declining), and shipbuilding and navigation for cross-sea raiding

threats.zh: 内部诸王国间的持续争斗是最大军事消耗，外部尚无重大威胁
threats.en: Continuous strife among the internal kingdoms was the greatest military drain; no major external threat existed yet

populationDescription.zh: 约50万人，分布于爱尔兰全岛的散居环形堡（ráth）和村庄中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 500,000 people, spread across the entire island of Ireland in scattered ringforts (ráth) and villages.

name.zh: 克朗马克诺伊斯（初创期）

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族（flaith）、教士与法学家、自由民（bó-aire）、佃农、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal families, nobility (flaith), clergy and jurists, freemen (bó-aire), tenant farmers, slaves.

allies.zh: 与不列颠北部的盖尔殖民区（达尔里阿达）有密切联系，部分王国与威尔士和康沃尔有海上往来。
allies.en: Close ties with the Gaelic colony in northern Britain (Dál Riata), and some kingdoms had maritime contacts with Wales and Cornwall.

enemies.zh: 主要是内部各王国间的竞争和领土争夺。
enemies.en: Mainly internal competition and territorial rivalry among the various kingdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外以跨海掠夺、殖民和与不列颠盖尔人联络为主，无统一外交政策。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy consisted mainly of cross-sea raiding, colonization, and contact with British Gaels; no unified diplomatic policy existed.

era.zh: 后罗马与早期基督教时代
era.en: Post-Roman and early Christian era

keyInnovations.zh: 环形堡防御建筑、金属工艺（镶嵌与珐琅技术）、欧甘文字与早期拉丁文抄本、修院农耕改良
keyInnovations.en: Ringfort defensive architecture, metalwork (inlay and enamel techniques), Ogham script and early Latin manuscripts, monastic agricultural improvements

strengths.zh: 独特的盖尔法律与文化传统、强大的修院学术网络、远离大陆入侵的地理优势、丰富的畜牧资源。
strengths.en: Unique Gaelic legal and cultural traditions, strong monastic scholarly network, geographic advantage of distance from continental invasions, and rich pastoral resources.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分裂、无统一军事力量、城市化极低、缺乏货币经济。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, no unified military force, extremely low urbanization, and lack of a monetary economy.

outlook.zh: 爱尔兰将在接下来的几个世纪进入修院文明的黄金时代，成为西欧的学术中心，但政治分裂将持续直到维京入侵带来根本性挑战。
outlook.en: Ireland would enter a golden age of monastic civilization over the following centuries, becoming a center of learning in Western Europe, though political fragmentation would persist until the Viking invasions posed a fundamental challenge.

description.zh: 476年的爱尔兰是由约150个小王国组成的盖尔世界，以牧牛经济和布雷洪法为基础，基督教与修院文化正迅速兴起。
description.en: In 476, Ireland was a Gaelic world of roughly 150 petty kingdoms, based on a cattle economy and Brehon law, with Christianity and monastic culture rapidly rising.

ruler.zh: 多位布立吞王（格温内德、波伊斯等王国之王）
ruler.en: Multiple British kings (kings of Gwynedd, Powys, and other kingdoms)

dynasty.zh: 库涅达王族（格温内德）等多个王朝
dynasty.en: The house of Cunedda (Gwynedd) and other dynasties

capital.zh: 迪加恩维（格温内德王庭）

governmentForm.zh: 后罗马不列颠式世袭王权，融合罗马行政残余和凯尔特部落传统
governmentForm.en: Post-Roman British hereditary kingship, blending Roman administrative remnants with Celtic tribal traditions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、世俗贵族（uchelwyr）、教士、自由民（bonheddig）、佃农和奴隶组成
socialStructure.en: Society comprised royal families, secular nobility (uchelwyr), clergy, freemen (bonheddig), tenant farmers, and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 王族与世俗贵族阶层
rulingClass.en: Royal families and the secular nobility

succession.zh: 王位在广义王族内传承，候选人通常从王子中择优选出
succession.en: The throne passed within the broader royal kin, with candidates usually chosen from among the princes on merit

structure.zh: 威尔士地区在476年由多个后罗马不列颠王国分治，其中格温内德和波伊斯最为强大。各王独立施政，保留部分罗马地方行政术语和基督教教会组织。
structure.en: In 476, the Welsh region was divided among several post-Roman British kingdoms, of which Gwynedd and Powys were the most powerful. Each king governed independently, retaining some Roman local administrative terminology and Christian church organization.

function.zh: 管理国王巡回法庭、宴飨、客征税和使节接待
function.en: Managed the king's circuit court, feasting, food-render, and envoy reception

function.zh: 提供宗教服务、教育、文字记录与跨区域外交调停
function.en: Provided religious services, education, record-keeping, and cross-regional diplomatic mediation

function.zh: 国王核心武装力量，负责防御、征战和内部秩序
function.en: The king's core armed force, responsible for defense, warfare, and internal order

localAdmin.zh: 地方由氏族长老和教区教士共同管理，依据威尔士习惯法处理纠纷和土地事务。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were jointly managed by clan elders and parish clergy, handling disputes and land matters according to Welsh customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 凯尔特-不列颠习惯法体系，以荣誉价格、亲族责任和补偿为核心（后来编纂为豪厄尔法典）。
legalSystem.en: A Celtic-British customary law system centered on honor price, kin responsibility, and compensation (later codified as the Laws of Hywel).

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡纳（食宿征发）、牲畜贡献和劳役义务为主，无货币税制。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue primarily from tribute in kind (food-render), livestock contributions, and labor obligations; no monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 基督教（不列颠式教会传统，独立于罗马教廷），修院与隐修制度兴盛
religion.en: Christianity (British church tradition, independent of the Roman papacy), with flourishing monastic and eremitic institutions

culturalAchievements.zh: 吟唱诗人（bardd）传统、拉丁与布立吞双语文学、金属工艺、石刻十字架与早期修院手抄本
culturalAchievements.en: Bardic (bardd) tradition, bilingual Latin and Brittonic literature, metalwork, stone-carved crosses, and early monastic manuscripts

languageFamily.zh: 布立吞语支（原始威尔士语），兼用拉丁语（教会与行政）
languageFamily.en: Brythonic (Primitive Welsh), with Latin also used (church and administration)

philosophy.zh: 融合基督教神学和凯尔特诗歌智慧传统，重视记忆、族谱和预言文学
philosophy.en: A blend of Christian theology and Celtic poetic wisdom tradition, valuing memory, genealogy, and prophetic literature

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁文字为主要书面媒介，欧甘文字用于部分石刻铭文
writingSystem.en: Latin script as the primary written medium, with Ogham used for some stone inscriptions

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧业（牛羊）和山地农业为基础，罗马时代城镇经济已基本消亡，修院是少数的手工业和贸易节点。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism (cattle and sheep) and upland agriculture; Roman-era town economies had largely disappeared, with monasteries as the few remaining handicraft and trade nodes.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、山地农业、渔业、修院手工业（金属工艺、手抄）、羊毛纺织
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, upland agriculture, fishing, monastic handicrafts (metalwork, scribing), wool textiles

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、羊毛、皮革、鱼、蜂蜜、金属饰品
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, wool, leather, fish, honey, metal ornaments

name.zh: 以牛为价值单位，少量罗马旧币
name.en: Cattle as the unit of value, with some Roman-era coins

householdWealth.zh: 以牲畜、耕地和环形堡住宅为普通家庭的主要财富
householdWealth.en: Livestock, arable land, and ringfort dwellings constituted the main wealth of ordinary households

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以牧业和食宿征发为核心的后罗马地方经济
economicSystem.en: A post-Roman local economy centered on pastoralism and food-render

treasuryDescription.zh: 各王国国王各有私藏，以金属器、牲畜和教会捐赠品为主要储备形式。
treasuryDescription.en: Each kingdom's king held private reserves, mainly in the form of metalwork, livestock, and church donations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政优先保障军事亲随的供养和王室巡回宴飨的维持。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priority was to sustain the royal war-band and maintain the king's circuit feasting.

description.zh: 国王的精锐职业武士，全年服役
description.en: The king's elite professional warriors, serving year-round

description.zh: 战时征召的自由民步兵，装备长矛和盾牌
description.en: Freemen infantry levied in wartime, armed with spears and shields

description.zh: 贵族骑兵，可能延续罗马骑兵传统
description.en: Noble cavalry, possibly continuing Roman cavalry traditions

technology.zh: 罗马式与凯尔特式混合武器、山地防御战术、利用罗马遗留堡垒
technology.en: Hybrid Roman-Celtic weaponry, mountain defense tactics, and use of Roman-era fortifications

threats.zh: 盎格鲁-撒克逊人从东方的持续扩张是最大外部威胁，爱尔兰海盗的袭扰也时有发生
threats.en: Continuous Anglo-Saxon expansion from the east was the greatest external threat, with Irish pirate raids also occurring periodically

populationDescription.zh: 约20万人，分散在威尔士山区和河谷的环形堡、修院与小型聚落中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 200,000 people, scattered across ringforts, monasteries, and small settlements in Welsh mountains and river valleys.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族（uchelwyr）、教士、自由民（bonheddig）、佃农、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal families, nobility (uchelwyr), clergy, freemen (bonheddig), tenant farmers, slaves.

allies.zh: 与其他后罗马不列颠王国（如杜姆诺尼亚）有松散联系，部分与爱尔兰盖尔殖民者达成共处。
allies.en: Maintained loose contacts with other post-Roman British kingdoms (such as Dumnonia), and some reached accommodation with Irish Gaelic settlers.

enemies.zh: 盎格鲁-撒克逊入侵者是主要威胁；部分王国之间也存在竞争。
enemies.en: Anglo-Saxon invaders were the main threat; some competition also existed among the Welsh kingdoms themselves.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以抵御盎格鲁-撒克逊扩张为首要目标，同时维持不列颠人诸王国间的松散合作。
foreignPolicy.en: Resisting Anglo-Saxon expansion was the primary goal, while maintaining loose cooperation among the British kingdoms.

era.zh: 后罗马时代
era.en: Post-Roman period

keyInnovations.zh: 罗马工程遗产的部分保留（道路、堡垒）、修院手抄本制作、凯尔特金属工艺
keyInnovations.en: Partial preservation of Roman engineering heritage (roads, forts), monastic manuscript production, and Celtic metalwork

strengths.zh: 山地地形提供天然防御、罗马文化遗产和基督教教会提供文化凝聚力、强悍的战士传统。
strengths.en: Mountainous terrain provided natural defense; Roman cultural heritage and the Christian church provided cultural cohesion; a hardy warrior tradition.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、政治分裂、经济基础薄弱、面对盎格鲁-撒克逊人的持续压力日益严重。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, weak economic base, and increasingly severe pressure from the Anglo-Saxons.

outlook.zh: 威尔士诸王国将在未来几个世纪逐步退缩至威尔士山区，但维持独立文化和政治实体直到中世纪晚期。
outlook.en: The Welsh kingdoms would gradually retreat into the Welsh mountains over the coming centuries, but would maintain independent cultural and political entities into the late Middle Ages.

description.zh: 476年的威尔士诸王国是后罗马不列颠人在西部山区建立的多个小型王国，保留了罗马文化遗产和基督教信仰，正面对盎格鲁-撒克逊人的东进压力。
description.en: In 476, the Welsh kingdoms were multiple small post-Roman British kingdoms established in the western mountains, preserving Roman cultural heritage and Christian faith, facing pressure from the Anglo-Saxon advance from the east.

ruler.zh: 加拉曼特国王（具体姓名不可考）
ruler.en: King of the Garamantes (specific name not attested)

governmentForm.zh: 撒哈拉绿洲王国体制，国王统领部落联盟，依赖灌溉系统维系定居聚落
governmentForm.en: A Saharan oasis kingdom, with the king leading a tribal confederation and relying on irrigation systems to sustain settled communities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、贵族、自由定居农民、游牧附庸和奴隶构成
socialStructure.en: Society comprised the royal family, aristocracy, free settled farmers, nomadic dependents, and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 王族和绿洲贵族
rulingClass.en: The royal family and oasis aristocracy

succession.zh: 王位在王族内世袭传承
succession.en: The throne passed hereditarily within the royal family

structure.zh: 加拉曼特国家以盖拉玛为中心，国王统领各绿洲定居点和游牧附庸部落。到476年国家已经严重衰退，灌溉系统退化，中央权威削弱。
structure.en: The Garamantian state was centered on Garama, with the king overseeing various oasis settlements and nomadic vassal tribes. By 476, the state was in severe decline, with irrigation systems degrading and central authority weakening.

function.zh: 管理王室事务、贡赋收集和外交接待
function.en: Managed royal affairs, tribute collection, and diplomatic reception

function.zh: 维护和管理雾盖拉（foggara）地下灌溉渠系统
function.en: Maintained and managed the foggara underground irrigation channel system

function.zh: 组织跨撒哈拉商队贸易和收取通行费
function.en: Organized trans-Saharan caravan trade and collected transit fees

localAdmin.zh: 各绿洲由地方长老和部落首领管理，中央对偏远聚落的控制力已很弱。
localAdmin.en: Each oasis was managed by local elders and tribal leaders; central control over remote settlements was already very weak.

legalSystem.zh: 撒哈拉习惯法传统，结合柏柏尔部落惯例和绿洲定居社会规范。
legalSystem.en: Saharan customary law tradition, combining Berber tribal practices with settled oasis community norms.

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡纳和商队通行费为主要收入，货币使用极少。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue mainly from tribute in kind and caravan transit fees; monetary use was minimal.

religion.zh: 本土撒哈拉信仰为主，融合部分罗马和利比亚宗教元素，基督教可能有少量渗入
religion.en: Predominantly indigenous Saharan beliefs, with some Roman and Libyan religious elements blended in; Christianity may have slightly penetrated

culturalAchievements.zh: 卓越的雾盖拉地下灌溉工程技术、撒哈拉岩画传统、独特的建筑与墓葬文化
culturalAchievements.en: Outstanding foggara underground irrigation engineering, Saharan rock art tradition, and unique architectural and funerary culture

languageFamily.zh: 柏柏尔语族（加拉曼特方言）
languageFamily.en: Berber (Garamantian dialect)

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学传统，文化重视水源管理智慧和沙漠生存知识
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophical tradition; culture valued water management wisdom and desert survival knowledge

writingSystem.zh: 古利比亚文字（提菲纳格前身），用于简短铭文
writingSystem.en: Old Libyan script (precursor of Tifinagh), used for short inscriptions

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 加拉曼特经济以绿洲灌溉农业和跨撒哈拉中转贸易为支柱，但到476年灌溉系统退化和贸易路线转移导致严重衰退。
gdpDescription.en: The Garamantian economy was supported by oasis irrigation agriculture and trans-Saharan transit trade, but by 476, degradation of irrigation systems and shifting trade routes had caused severe decline.

mainIndustries.zh: 绿洲农业（椰枣、谷物）、跨撒哈拉商队贸易、畜牧、盐业
mainIndustries.en: Oasis agriculture (dates, grain), trans-Saharan caravan trade, pastoralism, salt production

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、椰枣、奴隶、宝石（石榴石）、牲畜、皮革
tradeGoods.en: Salt, dates, slaves, gemstones (garnets), livestock, leather

householdWealth.zh: 以灌溉田地、椰枣树、牲畜和泥砖住宅为主要家庭财富
householdWealth.en: Irrigated fields, date palms, livestock, and mudbrick dwellings constituted the main household wealth

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以绿洲灌溉农业和跨撒哈拉贸易中转为核心的衰退型绿洲经济
economicSystem.en: A declining oasis economy centered on irrigated agriculture and trans-Saharan trade transit

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库已接近枯竭，灌溉系统退化导致农业减产，贸易收入也因路线转移而萎缩。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was nearly exhausted; irrigation system degradation had reduced agricultural output, and trade income had also shrunk due to shifting routes.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 试图优先维护灌溉系统以保障基本生存，但资源日益不足。
fiscalPolicy.en: Attempted to prioritize irrigation maintenance to secure basic survival, but resources were increasingly insufficient.

description.zh: 骑乘骆驼的沙漠战士，是加拉曼特军事传统的核心力量
description.en: Camel-mounted desert warriors, the core of Garamantian military tradition

description.zh: 守卫绿洲聚落和灌溉设施的步兵
description.en: Infantry guarding oasis settlements and irrigation infrastructure

technology.zh: 骆驼骑兵战术、沙漠游击作战、绿洲防御工事
technology.en: Camel cavalry tactics, desert guerrilla warfare, and oasis defensive fortifications

threats.zh: 周边游牧部落的劫掠、灌溉系统崩溃导致的内部动荡，以及汪达尔王国对北非贸易的干扰
threats.en: Raids by surrounding nomadic tribes, internal instability from irrigation system collapse, and Vandal Kingdom disruption of North African trade

populationDescription.zh: 约5万人，集中在费赞地区（利比亚西南部）的绿洲聚落中，较鼎盛期已大幅衰减。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 50,000 people, concentrated in oasis settlements of the Fezzan region (southwestern Libya), greatly diminished from peak population.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族、自由定居农民、游牧附庸、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, aristocracy, free settled farmers, nomadic dependents, slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分撒哈拉游牧部落维持附庸关系，但影响力大幅缩减。
allies.en: Maintained vassal relations with some Saharan nomadic tribes, but influence had greatly diminished.

enemies.zh: 周边掠夺性游牧群体和汪达尔王国对贸易路线的干扰。
enemies.en: Predatory nomadic groups in the vicinity and Vandal Kingdom disruption of trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持跨撒哈拉贸易中转地位和保护绿洲为最后目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Final foreign policy goal was to maintain the trans-Saharan trade transit position and protect the oases.

era.zh: 晚期古代撒哈拉
era.en: Late antique Sahara

keyInnovations.zh: 雾盖拉地下灌溉系统（已建但退化中）、骆驼驯化与运输、绿洲建筑技术
keyInnovations.en: Foggara underground irrigation system (built but degrading), camel domestication and transport, and oasis construction techniques

strengths.zh: 独特的沙漠灌溉技术遗产、跨撒哈拉贸易网络的历史积累、骆驼骑兵机动能力。
strengths.en: Unique heritage of desert irrigation technology, historical accumulation of trans-Saharan trade networks, and camel cavalry mobility.

weaknesses.zh: 灌溉系统严重退化、人口大幅减少、贸易路线转移、财政枯竭。
weaknesses.en: Severe degradation of irrigation systems, sharp population decline, shifting trade routes, and fiscal exhaustion.

outlook.zh: 加拉曼特文明正走向终结，其绿洲聚落将在未来几个世纪被撒哈拉游牧民族取代。
outlook.en: Garamantian civilization was approaching its end; its oasis settlements would be replaced by Saharan nomadic peoples over the following centuries.

description.zh: 476年的加拉曼特人是撒哈拉费赞地区的衰落绿洲文明，其标志性的地下灌溉系统正在退化，贸易和人口均大幅萎缩。
description.en: In 476, the Garamantes were a declining oasis civilization in the Saharan Fezzan, whose iconic underground irrigation systems were degrading, with trade and population both sharply diminished.

name.zh: 西非班图-沃尔特铁器文明

ruler.zh: 众多地方酋长与氏族长老
ruler.en: Numerous local chiefs and clan elders

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各地方聚落自治）

governmentForm.zh: 分散的氏族-村社自治体制，由长老会议和酋长共同治理
governmentForm.en: A dispersed clan-village self-governing system, jointly administered by elder councils and chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩展家庭和氏族为基本单位，铁匠享有特殊地位，上有酋长和长老，下有年轻自由民和少量奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around extended families and clans, with blacksmiths holding special status; chiefs and elders were above, with young freemen and a small number of slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 氏族长老、酋长和铁匠-祭司阶层
rulingClass.en: Clan elders, chiefs, and the blacksmith-priest class

succession.zh: 酋长在氏族内传承，通常由长老会议从合格候选人中推选
succession.en: Chieftainship passed within the clan, usually selected from qualified candidates by the elder council

structure.zh: 西非铁器农耕民族在476年无统一国家组织，由无数分散的村社和氏族共同体组成。各聚落由长老会议和酋长治理，以血缘和地缘纽带维系社会秩序。
structure.en: In 476, the West African iron-working peoples had no unified state organization, consisting of innumerable dispersed village and clan communities. Each settlement was governed by elder councils and chiefs, with kinship and locality bonds maintaining social order.

function.zh: 审议村社公共事务、调解纠纷和制定季节性农牧计划
function.en: Deliberated on village public affairs, mediated disputes, and determined seasonal farming and herding plans

function.zh: 管理酋长事务、贡赋收集与跨村联络
function.en: Managed chiefly affairs, tribute collection, and inter-village communication

function.zh: 掌握冶铁技术和宗教仪式，在社会中享有特殊权威
function.en: Mastered iron-smelting technology and religious ceremonies, holding special authority in society

localAdmin.zh: 完全依赖村社自治和氏族长老裁决，无跨区域行政体系。
localAdmin.en: Entirely dependent on village self-governance and clan elder adjudication; no cross-regional administrative system.

legalSystem.zh: 口头习惯法，以长老裁决、赔偿、宣誓和社区共识为基础。
legalSystem.en: Oral customary law, based on elder rulings, compensation, oaths, and community consensus.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，以实物贡纳、劳役义务和酋长份额为主要公共资源来源。
taxationSystem.en: No formal tax system; tribute in kind, labor obligations, and chiefly shares constituted the main public resource base.

religion.zh: 多种本土信仰体系，崇拜祖先、自然灵力和创世神灵，铁匠常兼具祭司角色
religion.en: Multiple indigenous belief systems, worshipping ancestors, natural spirits, and creator deities; blacksmiths often served dual roles as priests

culturalAchievements.zh: 卓越的冶铁与铸造技术、雕塑传统（诺克文化的后继者）、鼓乐和口述史诗、纺织与陶器制作
culturalAchievements.en: Outstanding iron-smelting and casting techniques, sculptural traditions (successors of the Nok culture), drumming and oral epics, textile weaving and pottery

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系（班图语支、沃尔特语支等多种语言）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo family (multiple languages including Bantu, Volta-Niger, and others)

philosophy.zh: 无文字哲学传统，智慧通过谚语、故事、歌谣和仪式代代传承
philosophy.en: No literate philosophical tradition; wisdom was transmitted across generations through proverbs, stories, songs, and rituals

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，完全依赖口述传统
writingSystem.en: No writing system; entirely dependent on oral tradition

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以刀耕火种农业和铁器手工业为基础，辅以狩猎、采集和地方贸易。铁器生产赋予这些民族对周边民族的技术优势。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on slash-and-burn agriculture and iron handicrafts, supplemented by hunting, gathering, and local trade. Iron production gave these peoples a technological advantage over surrounding groups.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（薯蓣、谷子、高粱）、冶铁与铁器制造、狩猎、采集、纺织、制陶
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (yams, millet, sorghum), iron smelting and tool manufacture, hunting, gathering, textile weaving, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、农产品、棕榈油、盐、陶器、纺织品、象牙
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, agricultural products, palm oil, salt, pottery, textiles, ivory

name.zh: 以物易物，以铁条和贝壳为交换媒介
name.en: Barter, with iron bars and cowrie shells as exchange media

householdWealth.zh: 以耕地、铁器、牲畜（少量）和粮食储备为主要家庭财产
householdWealth.en: Arable land, iron tools, limited livestock, and grain reserves constituted main household wealth

unit.en: silver denarius equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给和铁器生产为核心的扩张型农耕经济
economicSystem.en: An expansive agrarian economy centered on village subsistence and iron-tool production

category.zh: 农业生产（种子、工具、劳力）

treasuryDescription.zh: 无集中国库，财富分散于各氏族和酋长储备中，以铁器、粮食储备和贝壳为主要形式。
treasuryDescription.en: No centralized treasury; wealth was dispersed among clan and chiefly reserves, mainly in the form of iron goods, grain stores, and cowrie shells.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以保障农业生产和冶铁能力为核心，通过社区协作和互惠义务实现资源分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on sustaining agricultural production and iron-smelting capability, with resource allocation through community cooperation and reciprocal obligations.

description.zh: 各村社的成年男性战士，装备铁矛、铁刀和盾牌，战时动员
description.en: Adult male warriors from each village, armed with iron spears, iron knives, and shields, mobilized in wartime

description.zh: 酋长和显贵家族的亲卫战士，装备更精良
description.en: Guard warriors of chiefs and notable families, with superior equipment

commanderInChief.zh: 各酋长在本地独立指挥
commanderInChief.en: Each chief commanded independently within their locality

technology.zh: 铁制武器（矛、刀、箭头）、木盾与皮甲、丛林伏击与村社防御战术
technology.en: Iron weapons (spears, knives, arrowheads), wooden shields and leather armor, jungle ambush and village defense tactics

threats.zh: 邻近部族的竞争与冲突、疾病（疟疾等热带病）以及自然灾害
threats.en: Competition and conflict with neighboring tribes, diseases (malaria and other tropical illnesses), and natural disasters

populationDescription.zh: 约200万人，广泛分布于西非热带森林和萨赫勒草原过渡带的散居村落中。
populationDescription.en: Approximately 2 million people, widely distributed in scattered villages across West African tropical forests and the Sahel savanna transition zone.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与长老、铁匠-祭司、自由农民、年轻战士、依附者、少量奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Chiefs and elders, blacksmith-priests, free farmers, young warriors, dependents, small number of slaves.

allies.zh: 各村社间通过联姻和贸易维持和平关系，部分地区形成松散的部落联盟。
allies.en: Villages maintained peaceful relations through intermarriage and trade; in some areas, loose tribal alliances formed.

enemies.zh: 邻近的狩猎-采集民族和竞争性农耕部族是潜在冲突来源。
enemies.en: Neighboring hunter-gatherer peoples and competing farming tribes were potential sources of conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无统一外交政策，各村社和酋邦以自给自足和地方性贸易为主要对外方式。
foreignPolicy.en: No unified foreign policy; each village and chieftaincy focused on self-sufficiency and local trade as their main external engagement.

era.zh: 非洲铁器时代
era.en: African Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 独立发展的冶铁与铸造技术、刀耕火种农业系统、薯蓣和谷子栽培、陶器制造
keyInnovations.en: Independently developed iron smelting and casting, slash-and-burn agriculture, yam and millet cultivation, pottery production

strengths.zh: 掌握先进冶铁技术、人口庞大且持续增长、适应多种热带生态环境、农业生产力不断提升。
strengths.en: Commanded advanced iron technology, had a large and continuously growing population, adapted to diverse tropical ecologies, and enjoyed rising agricultural productivity.

weaknesses.zh: 极度政治分散、无文字记录系统、城市化极低、热带疾病压力大。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically dispersed, no written record system, extremely low urbanization, and severe tropical disease pressure.

outlook.zh: 西非铁器民族正处于持续扩张阶段，其冶铁技术和农业能力将支撑未来几个世纪中更复杂的国家组织（如加纳帝国）的形成。
outlook.en: The West African iron-working peoples were in a phase of continuous expansion; their iron technology and agricultural capabilities would support the formation of more complex state organizations (such as the Ghana Empire) over the coming centuries.

description.zh: 476年的西非铁器农耕民族是分布广泛的班图和沃尔特语族群体，以先进的冶铁技术和刀耕火种农业为基础，正处于持续扩张阶段。
description.en: In 476, the West African iron-working peoples were widely distributed Bantu and Volta-Niger groups, based on advanced iron-smelting technology and slash-and-burn agriculture, in a phase of continuous expansion.

capital.zh: 无统一首都（库克沼泽地区为农业核心区）
capital.en: No unified capital (Kuk Swamp area was the agricultural core)

governmentForm.zh: 以「大人物」制度为核心的平等主义部落社会，领导力基于个人声望、财富积累和馈赠网络。
governmentForm.en: An egalitarian tribal society centered on the 'Big Man' system, where leadership was based on personal prestige, wealth accumulation, and gift-exchange networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族为基本单位，无世袭等级。大人物通过组织猪只宰杀仪式、调解纠纷和领导园圃活动来获得影响力，但权力不可继承。性别分工明确，男女在仪式生活中各有角色。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans with no hereditary stratification. Big Men gained influence by organizing pig-kill feasts, mediating disputes, and leading horticultural activities, but their power was not heritable. Gender roles were distinct, with men and women holding separate ceremonial roles.

rulingClass.zh: 无固定统治阶层，大人物和资深长老因个人成就而获得暂时性权威。
rulingClass.en: No fixed ruling class; Big Men and senior elders gained temporary authority through personal achievement.

succession.zh: 无世袭继承制度，新一代大人物通过竞争性馈赠和声望积累自行崛起。
succession.en: No hereditary succession; new Big Men rose through competitive gift-giving and prestige accumulation.

structure.zh: 无中央政府，各谷地氏族独立运作。大人物在本氏族内行使协调职能，氏族间关系通过贸易、联姻和仪式交换维持。
structure.en: No central government; valley clans operated independently. Big Men coordinated within their own clans, while inter-clan relations were maintained through trade, marriage, and ritual exchange.

function.zh: 组织湿地排水、芋头与山药种植及猪只饲养。
function.en: Organized wetland drainage, taro and yam cultivation, and pig husbandry.

localAdmin.zh: 各氏族长老和大人物在谷地层面自主管理，无上级行政体系。
localAdmin.en: Clan elders and Big Men managed autonomously at the valley level with no higher administrative system.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法和氏族内部调解为主，严重冲突可能引发氏族间报复性战争。
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law and intra-clan mediation; serious conflicts could trigger retaliatory warfare between clans.

taxationSystem.zh: 无税收制度，资源通过互惠馈赠和大人物主导的再分配仪式流通。
taxationSystem.en: No taxation; resources circulated through reciprocal gift-giving and Big Man–led redistribution feasts.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰与祖先崇拜
religion.en: Animism and ancestor veneration

culturalAchievements.zh: 全球最早的独立农业起源之一（库克沼泽约公元前7000年），发展出复杂的湿地排水和梯田灌溉农业系统。猪只宰杀仪式（莫卡）是社会生活的核心。
culturalAchievements.en: One of the world's earliest independent origins of agriculture (Kuk Swamp, c. 7000 BCE), with complex wetland drainage and terraced irrigation systems. Pig-kill ceremonies (moka) were central to social life.

languageFamily.zh: 跨新几内亚语系及其他巴布亚语系（极度多样化，超过800种语言）
languageFamily.en: Trans–New Guinea and other Papuan language phyla (extremely diverse, over 800 languages)

philosophy.zh: 强调互惠、声望竞争和人与自然界精灵的和谐关系。
philosophy.en: Emphasized reciprocity, prestige competition, and harmonious relations between humans and nature spirits.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字
writingSystem.en: No writing system

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地湿地园圃农业为基础，种植芋头、山药、甘蔗和香蕉，辅以猪只饲养和小规模狩猎采集。谷地间存在贝壳、石斧和鸟羽的远距离贸易网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on highland wetland horticulture, growing taro, yams, sugarcane, and bananas, supplemented by pig husbandry and small-scale hunting-gathering. Long-distance trade networks for shells, stone axes, and bird plumes connected valleys.

mainIndustries.zh: 湿地农业（芋头、山药）、猪只饲养、石器制造、贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Wetland agriculture (taro, yams), pig husbandry, stone tool manufacture, shell processing

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳饰品、石斧、鸟羽、盐、猪只、树油
tradeGoods.en: Shell ornaments, stone axes, bird plumes, salt, pigs, tree oil

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以猪只数量、园地面积和贝壳储备衡量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by pig herds, garden area, and shell reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 以互惠馈赠和大人物主导的再分配仪式为核心的自给园圃经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence horticultural economy centered on reciprocal gift-giving and Big Man–led redistribution feasts.

treasuryDescription.zh: 无中央国库，财富分散于各氏族大人物手中，以猪只和贝壳为主要储藏形式。
treasuryDescription.en: No central treasury; wealth was dispersed among clan Big Men, with pigs and shells as the main stores of value.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 无正式财政政策，资源通过竞争性馈赠和仪式交换流通。
fiscalPolicy.en: No formal fiscal policy; resources circulated through competitive gift-giving and ceremonial exchange.

description.zh: 各氏族成年男性在冲突时充当战士，装备弓箭、石斧和木盾。
description.en: Adult males from each clan served as warriors during conflicts, armed with bows, stone axes, and wooden shields.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一指挥，各氏族大人物分别领导
commanderInChief.en: No unified command; each clan's Big Man led independently

technology.zh: 弓箭、石斧、木矛、木盾；善于山地伏击和谷地防御。
technology.en: Bows, stone axes, wooden spears, wooden shields; skilled in highland ambush and valley defense.

threats.zh: 邻近氏族的领土争端和报复性战争，以及低地沿海人群的偶尔冲突。
threats.en: Territorial disputes and retaliatory warfare with neighboring clans, and occasional conflicts with lowland coastal peoples.

populationDescription.zh: 约40万人分布于新几内亚中部高地的各个山谷中，人口密度因湿地农业而较高。
populationDescription.en: About 400,000 people distributed across the central highland valleys of New Guinea, with relatively high density due to wetland agriculture.

socialClasses.zh: 大人物、普通氏族成员、妇女（独立园圃角色）、被俘者
socialClasses.en: Big Men, ordinary clan members, women (independent horticultural roles), captives

allies.zh: 氏族间通过联姻、贸易和仪式交换维持和平关系。
allies.en: Inter-clan peace was maintained through marriage, trade, and ceremonial exchange.

enemies.zh: 竞争性氏族因土地、猪只和声望引发的冲突是常态。
enemies.en: Competing clans frequently clashed over land, pigs, and prestige.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无统一外交政策，各氏族独立处理对外关系。
foreignPolicy.en: No unified foreign policy; each clan handled external relations independently.

era.zh: 新石器时代晚期（高地农业复合体）
era.en: Late Neolithic (Highland Agricultural Complex)

keyInnovations.zh: 湿地排水系统（库克沼泽式梯田）、芋头和山药密集栽培、猪只驯化、石斧精制
keyInnovations.en: Wetland drainage systems (Kuk Swamp–style terracing), intensive taro and yam cultivation, pig domestication, refined stone axes

strengths.zh: 全球最早的独立农业传统之一，复杂的湿地管理系统，高人口密度与高度适应的山地生态。
strengths.en: One of the world's earliest independent agricultural traditions, complex wetland management systems, high population density, and highly adapted highland ecology.

weaknesses.zh: 极度政治分散，无文字，无金属冶炼技术，语言极度碎片化阻碍大规模合作。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, no writing, no metal smelting, and extreme linguistic fragmentation hindering large-scale cooperation.

outlook.zh: 巴布亚高地农业社群将在数千年中保持稳定的自给农业生活方式，直到16世纪欧洲人到来。
outlook.en: Papuan highland agricultural communities would maintain their stable subsistence farming lifestyle for millennia, until the arrival of Europeans in the 16th century.

description.zh: 476年的巴布亚高地社群以库克沼泽式湿地农业为基础，拥有全球最古老的独立农业传统之一，社会组织以大人物制度为特征。
description.en: In 476, the Papuan highland communities were based on Kuk Swamp–style wetland agriculture, possessing one of the world's oldest independent agricultural traditions, with social organization characterized by the Big Man system.

dynasty.en: Independent chiefly lineages on each island

capital.zh: 无统一首都（马克萨斯群岛和社会群岛为主要定居区）
capital.en: No unified capital (Marquesas and Society Islands were the main settlement areas)

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭酋长制为核心的分散海洋社会，各岛群由独立的阿里基统治。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed maritime society centered on hereditary chieftainships, with each island group ruled by independent ariki.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为贵族（阿里基及其家族）、自由民（图阿）和依附者/奴隶（图图）。血统和祖先决定社会地位，长子继承制为主。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into nobility (ariki and their families), commoners (tua), and dependents/slaves (tutu). Ancestry and descent determined social rank, with primogeniture as the primary succession rule.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭贵族阶层，权威来自祖先的神圣性和对马纳（超自然力量）的掌控。
rulingClass.en: Hereditary noble class, with authority derived from ancestral sanctity and control of mana (supernatural power).

succession.zh: 长子世袭为主，但实际继承可能因竞争和军事实力而调整。
succession.en: Primarily by primogeniture, though actual succession could be adjusted by competition and military strength.

structure.zh: 各岛群独立治理，酋长通过宗教权威和再分配机制管理社区。
structure.en: Each island group was governed independently, with chiefs managing communities through religious authority and redistribution.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、天文观测和远洋航海指导。
function.en: Conducted religious ceremonies, astronomical observations, and long-distance navigation guidance.

localAdmin.zh: 各岛由酋长和长老会管理，村社层面实行自治。
localAdmin.en: Each island was managed by chiefs and elder councils, with village-level self-governance.

legalSystem.zh: 以塔布（禁忌）制度和酋长裁决为核心的习惯法。
legalSystem.en: Customary law centered on the tapu (taboo) system and chiefly adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 以贡品和劳役为主，包括食物、工艺品和独木舟服务。
taxationSystem.en: Based on tribute and labor service, including food, craft goods, and canoe service.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出人类历史上最伟大的远洋航海技术，凭借星象导航、洋流知识和双体独木舟横渡数千公里的开阔太平洋。正在开拓马克萨斯群岛和社会群岛。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed some of the greatest open-ocean navigation skills in human history, using stellar navigation, ocean current knowledge, and double-hulled canoes to cross thousands of kilometers of open Pacific. Currently pioneering settlement of the Marquesas and Society Islands.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，波利尼西亚语支
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 强调马纳（超自然力量）、塔布（神圣禁忌）和祖先崇拜，海洋被视为连接而非分割各社群的纽带。
philosophy.en: Emphasized mana (supernatural power), tapu (sacred prohibitions), and ancestor worship; the ocean was seen as connecting rather than dividing communities.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，通过口述传统、吟唱和谱系记忆保存知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral tradition, chanting, and genealogical memory.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以园圃农业（芋头、面包果、山药、椰子）、渔业和猪鸡饲养为基础，岛际贸易提供贝壳、黑曜石和羽饰等。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on horticulture (taro, breadfruit, yams, coconuts), fishing, and pig and chicken husbandry, with inter-island trade providing shells, obsidian, and feather ornaments.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔业、独木舟建造、树皮布制作
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing, canoe building, barkcloth production

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳、黑曜石、羽饰、渔产、树皮布、独木舟
tradeGoods.en: Shells, obsidian, feather ornaments, fish products, barkcloth, canoes

householdWealth.zh: 以园地、猪只、独木舟和与酋长的关系衡量。
householdWealth.en: Measured by garden plots, pig herds, canoes, and ties to chiefs.

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配和村社生产为核心的海洋园圃经济。
economicSystem.en: A maritime horticultural economy centered on chiefly redistribution and village production.

treasuryDescription.zh: 无中央国库，酋长储备以贡品、食物和礼仪器物为主要形式。
treasuryDescription.en: No central treasury; chiefly reserves consisted mainly of tribute, food stores, and ceremonial objects.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 通过贡赋再分配巩固酋长权威，支持航海探索和仪式活动。
fiscalPolicy.en: Reinforced chiefly authority through tributary redistribution, supporting voyaging exploration and ceremonial activities.

description.zh: 各岛酋长征召的成年男性战士，装备木矛、棍棒和石制武器。
description.en: Adult male warriors levied by island chiefs, armed with wooden spears, clubs, and stone weapons.

description.zh: 利用双体独木舟进行海上机动、岛间投送和海战。
description.en: Used double-hulled canoes for maritime mobility, inter-island deployment, and naval combat.

technology.zh: 木矛、棍棒、石制和贝制武器、纤维甲、双体远航独木舟。
technology.en: Wooden spears, clubs, stone and shell weapons, fiber armor, double-hulled oceangoing canoes.

threats.zh: 岛际争端和自然灾害（风暴、海啸、火山活动）。
threats.en: Inter-island disputes and natural hazards (storms, tsunamis, volcanic activity).

populationDescription.zh: 约8万人散布于东波利尼西亚各岛群，包括马克萨斯群岛、社会群岛等新近定居区。
populationDescription.en: About 80,000 people scattered across eastern Polynesian island groups, including recently settled Marquesas and Society Islands.

socialClasses.zh: 阿里基（酋长贵族）、图阿（自由民）、图图（依附者/奴隶）
socialClasses.en: Ariki (chiefly nobility), tua (commoners), tutu (dependents/slaves)

allies.zh: 各岛群通过联姻和贸易维持关系。
allies.en: Island groups maintained relations through intermarriage and trade.

enemies.zh: 岛际领土和资源争端时有发生。
enemies.en: Inter-island territorial and resource disputes occurred periodically.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无统一外交政策，各岛群独立探索和定居新岛屿。
foreignPolicy.en: No unified foreign policy; each island group independently explored and settled new islands.

era.zh: 波利尼西亚远洋航海时代
era.en: Polynesian Open-Ocean Voyaging Era

keyInnovations.zh: 星象导航术、双体远航独木舟、园圃农业移植技术、鱼钩与渔网技术
keyInnovations.en: Stellar navigation, double-hulled oceangoing canoes, horticultural transplantation techniques, fishhook and net technology

strengths.zh: 拥有全球最先进的远洋航海技术，成功定居数千公里外的孤立岛屿，社会组织适应海洋环境。
strengths.en: Possessed the world's most advanced open-ocean navigation technology, successfully settled isolated islands thousands of kilometers away, with social organization adapted to the maritime environment.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少而分散，缺乏金属和文字，资源基础有限。
weaknesses.en: Small and dispersed population, lacking metals and writing, with limited resource base.

outlook.zh: 波利尼西亚航海者正处于东太平洋大扩张阶段，未来数百年将陆续抵达夏威夷、复活节岛和新西兰。
outlook.en: Polynesian voyagers were in the midst of the great eastern Pacific expansion, and in the coming centuries would reach Hawaii, Easter Island, and New Zealand.

description.zh: 476年的波利尼西亚航海者正处于东太平洋大扩张阶段，凭借无与伦比的远洋航海技术定居马克萨斯群岛和社会群岛。
description.en: In 476, the Polynesian voyagers were in the midst of the great eastern Pacific expansion, settling the Marquesas and Society Islands with unmatched open-ocean navigation skills.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎营地。
governmentForm.en: Mobile camps.

socialStructure.zh: 平等。
socialStructure.en: Egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 森林生存。
culturalAchievements.en: Forest survival.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡/阿尔冈昆
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan/Algonquian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮
tradeGoods.en: Furs

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟。
householdWealth.en: Canoes.

fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 饥荒。

populationDescription.zh: 亚寒带森林。

socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

strengths.zh: 适应力。

weaknesses.zh: 低密度。

outlook.zh: 持续。
outlook.en: Persist.

description.zh: 亚北极森林猎人。

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。

threats.zh: 突袭。
threats.en: Raids.

populationDescription.zh: 亚马逊。
populationDescription.en: Amazon.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 去毒。
keyInnovations.en: Detox.

strengths.zh: 稳定。
strengths.en: Stable.

weaknesses.zh: 分散。
weaknesses.en: Fragmented.

outlook.zh: 扩展。
outlook.en: Expanding.

description.zh: 亚马逊木薯农民。
description.en: Amazonian Manioc Farmers.

governmentForm.zh: 氏族和村社联合体。
governmentForm.en: Clan and village confederacies.

socialStructure.zh: 氏族组织，玉米农业传入中。
socialStructure.en: Clan organization, maize adoption underway.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 早期密西西比文化萌芽。
culturalAchievements.en: Early Mississippian culture stirrings.

languageFamily.zh: 阿尔冈昆/易洛魁/苏语
languageFamily.en: Algonquian/Iroquoian/Siouan

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。

writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

dynasty.zh: 无王朝传统，世系与部族认同并行
dynasty.en: No dynastic tradition; lineage and band identity coexisted

capital.zh: 无固定都城，随季节迁移

governmentForm.zh: 分散的猎采营地与半定居冬营，由长老合议决策
governmentForm.en: Dispersed hunting camps and semi-sedentary winter settlements governed by elder councils

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以血缘纽带和互惠交换维系群体凝聚。各群组规模小，季节性聚散，依据猎物迁徙和河流冰冻周期调整活动范围。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian society held together by kinship bonds and reciprocal exchange. Groups were small, seasonally aggregating and dispersing according to animal migration and river freeze cycles.

rulingClass.zh: 长老、杰出猎手与萨满
rulingClass.en: Elders, distinguished hunters, and shamans

succession.zh: 领导地位依据个人狩猎才能、年龄经验和群体推举确定，不存在世袭制度。
succession.en: Leadership was determined by hunting skill, age-based experience, and group consensus, with no hereditary succession.

structure.zh: 无中央政府。各群组以长老议事、集体协商形式运作，萨满在精神事务上具有权威。
structure.en: No central government. Each band operated through elder consultation and collective decision-making, with shamans holding authority in spiritual matters.

function.zh: 协调迁移路线、猎场分配、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated migration routes, hunting ground allocation, dispute resolution, and ritual organization.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、治病与沟通灵界，维系群体精神凝聚。
function.en: Conducted sacrifices, divination, healing, and spirit communication, maintaining the spiritual cohesion of the group.

localAdmin.zh: 无正式地方行政；各营地由家族长者自主管理，不同群组间通过婚姻与交换维持松散联系。
localAdmin.en: No formal local administration; each camp was managed autonomously by family elders, with loose ties between groups maintained through marriage and exchange.

legalSystem.zh: 依据习惯法和口头传统处理纠纷，严重冲突通过调解、赔偿或流放解决。
legalSystem.en: Disputes were handled through customary law and oral tradition; serious conflicts were resolved by mediation, compensation, or banishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 无税制。猎获物和采集品在群体内依互惠原则分配。
taxationSystem.en: No taxation system. Game and gathered resources were distributed within the group on a reciprocal basis.

religion.zh: 萨满教与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Shamanism and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 适应极寒环境的生存技术、驯鹿与河流冰上狩猎传统、精细的骨雕与皮革加工工艺、以歌谣和萨满仪式传承的口述历史。
culturalAchievements.en: Survival technologies adapted to extreme cold, reindeer and river-ice hunting traditions, fine bone carving and leatherworking, and oral histories transmitted through songs and shamanic ritual.

languageFamily.zh: 古西伯利亚诸语（楚科奇-堪察加语系、尤卡吉尔语系等）
languageFamily.en: Paleo-Siberian languages (Chukotko-Kamchatkan, Yukaghir, and related isolates)

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵的世界观构成精神核心，认为动物、河流、山林各有灵魂，人必须通过仪式维持与自然灵界的平衡。
philosophy.en: An animistic worldview formed the spiritual core, holding that animals, rivers, and forests all possessed spirits, and that humans must maintain balance with the spirit world through ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统；知识通过口传、歌谣和萨满仪式代代相传。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted orally through storytelling, songs, and shamanic ceremony across generations.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济完全依赖狩猎采集，以驯鹿、麋鹿、鱼类和野生植物为主要生计来源。群组间存在季节性聚会时的物品交换。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended entirely on hunting and gathering, with reindeer, elk, fish, and wild plants as the main subsistence sources. Seasonal gatherings between groups enabled barter exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、渔捞、采集、皮革加工、骨器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, gathering, leatherworking, bone-tool production

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、骨角器、河鱼干、石器原料
tradeGoods.en: Furs, bone and antler tools, dried river fish, raw stone materials

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为帐篷、雪橇、毛皮储备、猎具和驯化动物。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied in tents, sleds, fur stores, hunting equipment, and domesticated animals.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭和群组为单位的自给型狩猎采集经济，辅以群组间互惠交换。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence hunting-gathering economy organized at the household and band level, supplemented by reciprocal exchange between groups.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 「国库」实为首领控制的毛皮、干肉和工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" was effectively the chief's controlled reserves of furs, dried meat, and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持越冬储备和群组间互惠义务为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on maintaining winter reserves and meeting reciprocal obligations between groups.

description.zh: 所有成年男性在必要时均可充当战士，使用弓箭、石矛和骨刀，擅长伏击和游击战术。
description.en: All adult males could serve as warriors when needed, using bows, stone-tipped spears, and bone knives, with expertise in ambush and guerrilla tactics.

technology.zh: 以弓箭、石器和骨制武器为主，无金属冶炼。冬季利用雪地和严寒作为天然防御。
technology.en: Based on bows, stone tools, and bone weapons, with no metal smelting. Winter conditions and snow terrain served as natural defenses.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 来自草原游牧民族（柔然等）的零星袭扰，以及群组间的领地纠纷。严寒与饥荒是更大的生存威胁。
threats.en: Sporadic raids from steppe nomads (such as the Rouran) and inter-band territorial disputes. Extreme cold and famine posed greater existential threats.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极度分散于广袤的西伯利亚内陆，从叶尼塞河流域到勒拿河、科利马河，密度极低，以数十人规模的群组沿河流和猎场分布。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely dispersed across the vast Siberian interior, from the Yenisei basin to the Lena and Kolyma rivers, at very low density, with bands of several dozen people distributed along rivers and hunting grounds.

socialClasses.zh: 长老、杰出猎手、萨满、普通成员、依附者。社会分层极弱。
socialClasses.en: Elders, distinguished hunters, shamans, ordinary members, dependents. Social stratification was minimal.

allies.zh: 无正式盟国。亲缘群组间存在互助关系，季节性聚会时缔结临时合作。
allies.en: No formal allies. Mutual-aid relationships existed between kindred groups, with temporary cooperation forged at seasonal gatherings.

enemies.zh: 无固定敌国。与南方草原民族和邻近通古斯群体偶有冲突。
enemies.en: No fixed enemies. Occasional conflicts with southern steppe peoples and neighboring Tungusic groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以避免冲突、维持猎场边界和与邻近群体的交换关系为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy focused on avoiding conflict, maintaining hunting-ground boundaries, and sustaining exchange relations with neighboring groups.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 雪地行走工具（雪鞋与雪橇）、弓箭复合技术、骨角雕刻、兽皮帐篷、河冰捕鱼术。
keyInnovations.en: Snow-travel equipment (snowshoes and sleds), composite bow technology, bone and antler carving, hide tents, and under-ice fishing techniques.

strengths.zh: 对极寒环境的卓越适应能力、广阔而难以征服的领土、丰富的毛皮资源。
strengths.en: Outstanding adaptation to extreme cold, vast and nearly unconquerable territory, rich fur resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极度稀疏、无金属技术、缺乏政治统一、对外交流有限。
weaknesses.en: Extremely sparse population, no metal technology, lack of political unity, limited external contact.

outlook.zh: 将长期延续分散的猎采生活方式，直至数百年后通古斯和突厥系民族的扩张逐步改变区域格局。
outlook.en: Dispersed hunter-gatherer lifestyles would persist for centuries, until Tungusic and Turkic expansion gradually reshaped the regional landscape.

description.zh: 古西伯利亚猎采群落散布于叶尼塞河至科利马河的广袤冻土与针叶林中，以驯鹿追猎、河冰捕鱼和采集为生。各群组规模通常仅数十人，随季节在河谷冬营与高地夏猎场间迁移。萨满教信仰贯穿生活，长老合议决定迁徙与狩猎事宜。5世纪时，这些群体尚未受到草原帝国的直接控制，保持着数千年来几乎未变的生存方式。
description.en: Paleo-Siberian hunter-gatherer bands were scattered across the vast permafrost and taiga from the Yenisei to the Kolyma rivers, subsisting on reindeer hunting, under-ice fishing, and gathering. Groups typically numbered only a few dozen, migrating seasonally between river-valley winter camps and upland summer hunting grounds. Shamanic belief pervaded daily life, and elder councils decided matters of migration and hunting. In the 5th century, these peoples remained beyond the direct control of steppe empires, maintaining a way of life virtually unchanged for millennia.

name.zh: 早期通古斯部落联合体

dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; each tribe was autonomous

governmentForm.zh: 松散部落联合，各部酋长与长老主导决策
governmentForm.en: Loose tribal confederation with chiefs and elders leading decision-making

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族为基本单元，各氏族拥有特定的驯鹿牧场和渔猎领地。氏族间通过婚姻联盟和贡物交换维持联系。狩猎能力和驯鹿管理技术决定个人地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, each controlling specific reindeer pastures and hunting-fishing territories. Inter-clan ties were maintained through marriage alliances and tribute exchange. Hunting prowess and reindeer management skills determined individual status.

rulingClass.zh: 氏族长老、杰出猎手与萨满
rulingClass.en: Clan elders, prominent hunters, and shamans

succession.zh: 氏族首领通常在家族内传承，但须得到族人认可；卓越的狩猎和作战能力可使外氏族成员获得领导权。
succession.en: Clan leadership usually passed within the family but required community approval; outstanding hunting and combat ability could bring outsiders to leadership.

structure.zh: 各氏族自治，遇重大事务（战争、迁徙、与外族交涉）时由多部酋长会议协商。无常设官僚机构。
structure.en: Each clan was self-governing, with multi-tribal councils of chiefs convened for major matters such as war, migration, or negotiations with outsiders. No standing bureaucracy existed.

function.zh: 协调牧场与猎场分配、处理氏族间纠纷、决定迁移路线。
function.en: Coordinated pasture and hunting-ground allocation, settled inter-clan disputes, and determined migration routes.

function.zh: 主持祭天、占卜、治病和驯鹿祈福仪式。
function.en: Conducted sky worship, divination, healing, and reindeer blessing ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 各营地由氏族长自主管理，日常事务依据习惯处理。
localAdmin.en: Each camp was managed autonomously by the clan head, with daily affairs handled according to custom.

legalSystem.zh: 法律依据口头习惯法，纠纷通过调解、赔偿或氏族间仲裁解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral customary rules, with disputes resolved through mediation, compensation, or inter-clan arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制。驯鹿产品和猎获物在氏族内共享，向首领提供贡品作为象征性支持。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation. Reindeer products and game were shared within the clan, with symbolic tribute offered to the chief.

religion.zh: 萨满教信仰为核心，崇拜天神、动物灵魂与祖灵，驯鹿在信仰中占有特殊地位
religion.en: Shamanism formed the core belief system, with worship of sky deities, animal spirits, and ancestors; reindeer held a special place in the faith

culturalAchievements.zh: 驯鹿驯化与管理技术、精湛的皮革和桦树皮手工艺、独特的萨满鼓乐传统、适应极端环境的狩猎技术体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Reindeer domestication and management techniques, masterful leather and birch-bark craftsmanship, distinctive shamanic drum traditions, and a hunting technology system adapted to extreme environments.

languageFamily.zh: 通古斯语族（阿尔泰语系）
languageFamily.en: Tungusic languages (Altaic family)

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与天崇拜构成思想根基，强调人与驯鹿、森林、河流之间的灵性纽带，萨满作为人界与灵界的中介。
philosophy.en: Animism and sky worship formed the intellectual foundation, emphasizing spiritual bonds between humans, reindeer, forests, and rivers, with shamans serving as intermediaries between the human and spirit realms.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字；知识通过口述传统、萨满歌谣和氏族叙事代代相传。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, shamanic songs, and clan narratives across generations.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以驯鹿畜牧和狩猎为主，辅以河流捕鱼和林产品采集。与草原民族的毛皮贸易为重要补充收入来源。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on reindeer herding and hunting, supplemented by river fishing and forest-product gathering. Fur trade with steppe peoples was an important supplementary income source.

mainIndustries.zh: 驯鹿畜牧、狩猎、渔捞、皮革加工、桦树皮器制作
mainIndustries.en: Reindeer herding, hunting, fishing, leatherworking, birch-bark craft

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮（貂皮尤贵）、驯鹿产品、鱼干、桦树皮器、骨角器
tradeGoods.en: Furs (sable especially prized), reindeer products, dried fish, birch-bark wares, bone and antler tools

name.zh: 无铸币，以毛皮为主要交换媒介
name.en: No coinage; furs served as the primary medium of exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以驯鹿数量、毛皮储备、帐篷和猎具为主要衡量标准。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured primarily by reindeer herd size, fur reserves, tents, and hunting equipment.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族为单位的驯鹿畜牧-狩猎混合经济，群组内互惠分配，群组间以毛皮为媒介进行交换。
economicSystem.en: A clan-based mixed reindeer-herding and hunting economy, with reciprocal distribution within groups and fur-mediated exchange between them.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
