grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 「国库」为酋长控制的毛皮、干肉和驯鹿储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted of the chief's controlled reserves of furs, dried meat, and reindeer.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持驯鹿群规模、越冬储备和对外交换关系为财政核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on maintaining herd sizes, winter reserves, and external exchange relationships.

description.zh: 成年男性在需要时充当战士，使用弓箭、铁刃矛和骨刀。擅长林地伏击和骑驯鹿机动，冬季战斗力尤强。
description.en: Adult males served as warriors when needed, using bows, iron-tipped spears, and bone knives. Skilled in forest ambush and reindeer-mounted mobility, they were especially effective in winter combat.

technology.zh: 弓箭为主要远程武器，部分铁器通过草原贸易获得。驯鹿作为运输工具增强机动性。
technology.en: Bows were the main ranged weapon, with some iron implements obtained through steppe trade. Reindeer served as transport animals enhancing mobility.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 草原游牧民族（柔然等）的袭扰、古西伯利亚群体间的领地冲突、北魏帝国边境影响的潜在渗透。
threats.en: Raids from steppe nomads (such as the Rouran), territorial conflicts with Paleo-Siberian groups, and potential encroachment from the Northern Wei frontier.

populationDescription.zh: 通古斯诸部分布于东西伯利亚至满洲北部的森林与河谷中，以贝加尔湖以东至黑龙江上游为核心区域。人口密度极低，各氏族分散于广阔的针叶林和山地之中。
populationDescription.en: Tungusic peoples were spread across the forests and river valleys from eastern Siberia to northern Manchuria, with the core area east of Lake Baikal to the upper Amur. Population density was very low, with clans dispersed across vast taiga and mountain terrain.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族长、长老、萨满、自由牧猎民、依附者。分层较弱。
socialClasses.en: Clan heads, elders, shamans, free herder-hunters, dependents. Social stratification was weak.

allies.zh: 无正式盟国。与邻近通古斯氏族间存在婚姻联盟和互助关系。
allies.en: No formal allies. Marriage alliances and mutual aid existed between neighboring Tungusic clans.

enemies.zh: 无固定敌国。与柔然、古西伯利亚群体和北魏边境势力偶有摩擦。
enemies.en: No fixed enemies. Occasional friction with the Rouran, Paleo-Siberian groups, and Northern Wei frontier forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护牧猎领地、保持与草原商路的贸易通道和避免大国卷入为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy focused on protecting herding-hunting territories, maintaining trade access to steppe routes, and avoiding entanglement with major powers.

era.zh: 早期铁器传入的驯鹿牧猎阶段
era.en: Early iron-contact reindeer herding-hunting stage

keyInnovations.zh: 驯鹿驯化与役用、桦树皮加工技术、经草原贸易获得的少量铁器、复合弓制作、可拆卸帐篷系统。
keyInnovations.en: Reindeer domestication and use as draft animals, birch-bark processing technology, limited iron tools obtained through steppe trade, composite bow construction, and portable tent systems.

strengths.zh: 驯鹿牧猎技术的成熟掌握、对森林与严寒环境的适应、毛皮贸易的持续价值。
strengths.en: Mastery of reindeer herding-hunting skills, adaptation to forest and extreme cold, and the enduring value of the fur trade.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、政治碎片化、技术水平受限、缺乏对外部大规模入侵的抵抗能力。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, political fragmentation, limited technological level, and vulnerability to large-scale external invasion.

outlook.zh: 通古斯诸部将在此后数百年内逐步向南扩展，部分群体最终演化为靺鞨、女真等对东亚历史产生深远影响的民族。
outlook.en: Tungusic peoples would gradually expand southward over the following centuries, with some groups eventually evolving into the Mohe and Jurchen peoples who profoundly shaped East Asian history.

description.zh: 5世纪的通古斯诸部散布于贝加尔湖以东至黑龙江上游的广阔森林中，以驯鹿畜牧和狩猎为生。各氏族自治，萨满教信仰深植于生活之中。他们已通过草原商路获取少量铁器，但整体仍处于部族社会阶段。毛皮贸易——尤其是珍贵的貂皮——使他们与南方草原帝国保持着间接联系。这些半游牧的驯鹿民族是数百年后建立渤海国和金朝的女真人的远祖。
description.en: In the 5th century, the Tungusic peoples were scattered across the vast forests east of Lake Baikal to the upper Amur, subsisting on reindeer herding and hunting. Each clan was self-governing, with shamanic belief deeply embedded in daily life. They had obtained limited iron tools through steppe trade routes, but remained overall at a tribal stage of social organization. The fur trade — especially in prized sable pelts — maintained their indirect connection with the steppe empires to the south. These semi-nomadic reindeer peoples were the distant ancestors of the Jurchen, who would later found the Bohai state and the Jin dynasty.

name.zh: 印度尼西亚群岛早期印度化政权
name.en: Early Indianized Polities of the Indonesian Archipelago

ruler.zh: 多位地方首领与早期国王（如呾卢摩国国王）
ruler.en: Multiple local chiefs and early kings (such as the rulers of Tarumanagara)

dynasty.zh: 呾卢摩王统（爪哇西部）及苏门答腊早期港口政权
dynasty.en: Tarumanagara royal line (western Java) and early Sumatran port polities

capital.zh: 多中心——爪哇西部的呾卢摩城与苏门答腊东南岸港口聚落
capital.en: Polycentric — Tarumanagara in western Java and port settlements on the southeastern coast of Sumatra

governmentForm.zh: 多个印度教-佛教影响的酋邦与早期王国并存，各港口和河流腹地首领维持半独立地位
governmentForm.en: Multiple Hindu-Buddhist influenced chiefdoms and nascent kingdoms coexisting, with semi-independent chiefs controlling ports and river hinterlands

socialStructure.zh: 社会以航海贸易精英、印度教婆罗门与佛教僧侣、地方酋长、稻作农民、渔民和手工业者为核心。印度文化影响正在自上层向下渗透，但基层社会仍保留浓厚的南岛传统。
socialStructure.en: Society was built around maritime trading elites, Hindu Brahmins and Buddhist monks, local chiefs, rice-farming peasants, fishermen, and artisans. Indian cultural influence was percolating downward from the top, but grassroots society still retained strong Austronesian traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 印度化国王与贵族、婆罗门祭司、大商人
rulingClass.en: Indianized kings and nobility, Brahmin priests, and prominent merchants

succession.zh: 王位在王室家族内传承，受印度教王权神授观念影响，但实际继承仍依赖地方势力支持和贸易网络控制。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal house, influenced by Hindu concepts of divine kingship, though actual succession depended on local power support and control of trade networks.

structure.zh: 政体多元：爪哇西部的呾卢摩已具备早期印度化王国行政特征，设有宫廷官员和碑铭制度；苏门答腊东南岸的早期港口政权尚处于酋邦向王国过渡阶段。各政权间无统一行政。
structure.en: Government was pluralistic: Tarumanagara in western Java already showed early Indianized kingdom administrative features with court officials and inscription systems; early port polities on southeastern Sumatra were still transitioning from chiefdoms to kingdoms. No unified administration existed between polities.

function.zh: 管理王室仪式、宫殿事务与王令传达（主要在呾卢摩）。
function.en: Managed royal rituals, palace affairs, and transmission of royal orders (mainly in Tarumanagara).

function.zh: 监管港口贸易、征收关税、管理外来商人和船只。
function.en: Supervised port trade, collected customs duties, and managed foreign merchants and vessels.

function.zh: 维护印度教和佛教寺庙、主持王室祭祀、管理寺庙地产与宗教人员。
function.en: Maintained Hindu and Buddhist temples, conducted royal rituals, and managed temple estates and religious personnel.

function.zh: 协调河流腹地与海岸聚落的贡赋征收和劳役调配。
function.en: Coordinated tribute collection and labor allocation across river hinterlands and coastal settlements.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖河流流域和港口的地方首领网络。呾卢摩国王对核心区域控制较强，但远离中心的岛屿和海岸聚落享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance relied on networks of chiefs along river basins and ports. The Tarumanagara king maintained stronger control over core areas, while distant islands and coastal settlements enjoyed broad autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以印度教法典（dharmashastra）原则与当地习惯法并行为基础，由国王、婆罗门和地方首领裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on parallel Hindu dharmashastra principles and local customary rules, adjudicated by kings, Brahmins, and local chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、稻米实物贡赋、劳役和贸易商品抽成为主。寺庙享有部分免税地产。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came primarily from port customs, rice tribute in kind, labor service, and levies on trade goods. Temples enjoyed some tax-exempt estates.

religion.zh: 印度教（湿婆派与毗湿奴派并存）与佛教正在传入，同时保留强烈的南岛万物有灵信仰与祖灵崇拜
religion.en: Hinduism (both Shaiva and Vaishnava) and Buddhism were being adopted, while strong Austronesian animistic beliefs and ancestor worship persisted

culturalAchievements.zh: 石刻碑铭（如呾卢摩七碑，约5世纪早期）、印度教神庙建筑的开端、精湛的远洋航海技术、稻田灌溉系统、金银器加工、梵文文化的本土化吸收。
culturalAchievements.en: Stone inscriptions (such as the seven Tarumanagara inscriptions, c. early 5th century), the beginnings of Hindu temple architecture, masterful deep-sea navigation, irrigated rice systems, gold and silver metalwork, and the localized absorption of Sanskrit culture.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系（马来-波利尼西亚语支），梵文用作宗教和宫廷铭文语言
languageFamily.en: Austronesian (Malayo-Polynesian branch), with Sanskrit used as the language of religious and court inscriptions

philosophy.zh: 印度教宇宙论和佛教教义正与本土南岛精神传统融合，王权被赋予神圣属性，以湿婆或毗湿奴的化身自居成为新兴的统治合法性来源。
philosophy.en: Hindu cosmology and Buddhist doctrine were merging with indigenous Austronesian spiritual traditions. Kingship was being invested with sacred attributes, and identification with Shiva or Vishnu was emerging as a new source of political legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 梵文石刻碑铭用于王室记录和宗教文献；早期南岛地方文字（帕拉瓦系文字）正在发展中。
writingSystem.en: Sanskrit stone inscriptions were used for royal records and religious texts; early local Austronesian scripts derived from Pallava-type writing were in development.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业和海上贸易为双支柱。群岛位于印度洋至南海贸易航线的关键节点，香料、黄金、树脂和锡矿等出口品具有高附加值。港口聚落是区域性商业中心。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on twin pillars of rice agriculture and maritime trade. The archipelago sat at a critical node on the Indian Ocean–South China Sea trade route, with high-value exports including spices, gold, resins, and tin. Port settlements served as regional commercial hubs.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、海上贸易与转口运输、香料采集、金矿开采、渔业、造船
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, maritime trade and entrepôt shipping, spice collection, gold mining, fishing, and shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 香料（丁香、豆蔻、胡椒）、黄金、樟脑、龙脑香、锡、珍贵木材、树脂；输入品包括印度纺织品、中国丝绸和陶器、铁器、玻璃珠。
tradeGoods.en: Spices (cloves, nutmeg, pepper), gold, camphor, benzoin, tin, precious timber, and resins; imports included Indian textiles, Chinese silk and ceramics, iron tools, and glass beads.

name.en: Gold and silver granules alongside barter

householdWealth.zh: 港口商人家庭积累了可观的贸易品和黄金；农村家庭财富以稻田、渔具和手工艺品为主。
householdWealth.en: Port merchant households accumulated significant trade goods and gold; rural household wealth was measured in rice fields, fishing gear, and craft products.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 农业-贸易双元经济：内陆以自给稻作和首领再分配为主，沿海港口以国际转口贸易为核心驱动力。印度和中国商人的参与日益增多。
economicSystem.en: A dual agrarian-trade economy: the hinterland was based on subsistence rice cultivation and chiefly redistribution, while coastal ports were driven by international entrepôt trade. Participation of Indian and Chinese merchants was growing.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以黄金、贸易品和稻米储备形式保存。呾卢摩以产金闻名，黄金是王室权力的物质象征。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held mainly in gold, trade goods, and rice reserves. Tarumanagara was famed for its gold production, and gold served as a material symbol of royal power.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持港口吸引力、支持宗教建设和确保稻米储备为目标。贸易关税是收入支柱。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to maintain port attractiveness, support religious construction, and ensure rice reserves. Trade customs were the revenue backbone.

description.zh: 装备铁制武器和盾牌的精锐部队，负责保卫王宫和港口要地。
description.en: Elite troops equipped with iron weapons and shields, responsible for guarding the palace and key port areas.

description.zh: 以战舟为平台的海上作战力量，担负港口防御、海上巡逻和海盗打击任务。
description.en: Sea-borne fighting force operating from war boats, responsible for port defense, maritime patrol, and anti-piracy operations.

description.zh: 由各地方首领征发的步兵，装备矛、弓和竹盾，战时集结。
description.en: Infantry raised by local chiefs, equipped with spears, bows, and bamboo shields, mustered in wartime.

technology.zh: 铁制武器已较普及，造船技术先进，具备远洋航行能力。战舟是主要作战平台。象部队规模有限但在爪哇内陆具有威慑力。
technology.en: Iron weapons were fairly widespread, and shipbuilding technology was advanced with deep-sea navigation capability. War boats were the primary combat platform. Elephant forces were limited but served as a deterrent in the Javanese interior.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 海盗活动、邻近政权（如扶南、占婆）的海上竞争、内部酋邦间的领地争夺、以及印度和中国贸易势力的潜在干预。
threats.en: Piracy, maritime competition from neighboring polities (such as Funan and Champa), territorial rivalry between internal chiefdoms, and potential intervention from Indian and Chinese trade interests.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于爪哇岛和苏门答腊岛东南部，以河流平原稻作区和海岸港口为聚居核心。婆罗洲沿岸和小巽他群岛有较少人口分布。群岛内部山地和热带雨林中散布着更为原始的部族群体。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly on Java and southeastern Sumatra, centered on river-plain rice areas and coastal ports. Borneo's coasts and the Lesser Sunda Islands had smaller populations. The mountainous and rainforest interior of the archipelago was inhabited by more isolated tribal groups.

name.zh: 苏门答腊东南岸港口群

socialClasses.zh: 印度化王室与贵族、婆罗门祭司、大商人、地方酋长、稻作农民、渔民、手工业者、奴隶与依附者。
socialClasses.en: Indianized royalty and nobility, Brahmin priests, major merchants, local chiefs, rice-farming peasants, fishermen, artisans, slaves, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与印度商人集团和南亚佛教僧团网络保持密切联系。扶南王国是重要的贸易伙伴。
allies.en: Close ties maintained with Indian merchant communities and South Asian Buddhist monastic networks. The Funan kingdom was an important trade partner.

enemies.zh: 无固定敌国，但与邻近海上政权（占婆等）存在贸易竞争，群岛内部酋邦间偶有武装冲突。
enemies.en: No fixed enemies, but trade competition existed with neighboring maritime polities (such as Champa), and armed conflicts occasionally erupted between chiefdoms within the archipelago.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护贸易航线安全、吸引印度和中国商人、通过印度教-佛教文化提升国际声望为核心。呾卢摩国王曾遣使至中国。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing trade routes, attracting Indian and Chinese merchants, and enhancing international prestige through Hindu-Buddhist culture. Tarumanagara kings reportedly sent envoys to China.

era.zh: 早期印度化海洋王国阶段
era.en: Early Indianized maritime kingdom stage

keyInnovations.zh: 双桅帆船和舷外浮杆船的先进造船术、印度洋与南海远洋航行技术、铁器冶炼、稻田水利灌溉、梵文碑铭和早期地方文字、印度教-佛教寺庙建筑。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced shipbuilding with double-mast sailing vessels and outrigger canoes, Indian Ocean and South China Sea deep-sea navigation, iron smelting, irrigated rice cultivation, Sanskrit inscriptions and early local scripts, and Hindu-Buddhist temple architecture.

strengths.zh: 扼控国际贸易航线的战略地理位置、丰富的香料和黄金资源、先进的航海技术、正在形成的印度化政治合法性。
strengths.en: Strategic geographic position controlling international trade routes, abundant spice and gold resources, advanced maritime technology, and an emerging Indianized political legitimacy.

weaknesses.zh: 政治碎片化、各港口和酋邦缺乏统一权威、内陆控制力薄弱、尚未形成成熟的中央行政体系。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, lack of unified authority among ports and chiefdoms, weak inland control, and the absence of a mature centralized administrative system.

outlook.zh: 群岛的贸易地位将持续上升。苏门答腊东南岸的港口政权在未来两个世纪内将演化为强大的室利佛逝海洋帝国，而爪哇将涌现出更多印度化王国。海上丝绸之路的繁荣将使这一地区成为连接印度与中国的关键枢纽。
outlook.en: The archipelago's trade position would continue to rise. The port polities on southeastern Sumatra would evolve over the next two centuries into the powerful Srivijaya maritime empire, while Java would see the emergence of further Indianized kingdoms. The flourishing of the maritime Silk Road would make this region a critical hub linking India and China.

description.zh: 476年的印度尼西亚群岛正处于文明转型的关键时期。爪哇西部的呾卢摩国（约400年建立）已是一个具备梵文碑铭传统和印度教王权观念的早期王国，其国王以毗湿奴化身自居，留下了七块著名石刻碑铭。苏门答腊东南岸的马来港口聚落正在从部落贸易站发展为区域性海洋政权——这些就是未来室利佛逝帝国的雏形。群岛扼控着印度洋至南海的贸易咽喉，香料、黄金、樟脑和龙脑香等高附加值商品通过这条航线流向印度和中国。印度文化——包括印度教、佛教、梵文、王权观念和寺庙建筑——正在深刻改变上层政治结构，但稻作农村和渔村的基层社会仍保留着浓厚的南岛传统。这一地区正处于从海洋酋邦向印度化王国过渡的上升轨道。
description.en: In 476 CE, the Indonesian archipelago stood at a pivotal moment of civilizational transformation. Tarumanagara in western Java (established c. 400 CE) was already an early kingdom with a tradition of Sanskrit inscriptions and Hindu royal ideology, whose king styled himself as an incarnation of Vishnu and left behind seven famous stone inscriptions. The Malay port settlements on southeastern Sumatra were developing from tribal trading posts into regional maritime polities — the embryonic forms of the future Srivijaya empire. The archipelago controlled the trade chokepoint between the Indian Ocean and the South China Sea, with high-value commodities including spices, gold, camphor, and benzoin flowing to India and China along this route. Indian culture — including Hinduism, Buddhism, Sanskrit, concepts of kingship, and temple architecture — was profoundly reshaping upper-level political structures, while the grassroots society of rice-farming and fishing villages still retained strong Austronesian traditions. The region was on a rising trajectory, transitioning from maritime chiefdoms to Indianized kingdoms.

dynasty.zh: 斯拉夫诸部落联盟时期
dynasty.en: Era of the Early Slavic Tribal Confederations

governmentForm.zh: 以部落首领、长老与民众集会为核心的松散部落联盟制度，各族群自治，仅在战时和祭祀中临时联合。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal confederation governed by chiefs, elders, and popular assemblies, with each group self-governing and uniting temporarily only in wartime and for ritual observance.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族与村社为基本单元，自由农民、武装男丁构成人口主体。上层为部落首领与战士贵族，下层包括普通农户、依附者与少量战俘奴仆。各部落间通过婚姻、互助和季节性集会维持联系。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans and village communes, with free peasants and armed men forming the bulk of the population. The upper tier comprised tribal chiefs and warrior elites, while ordinary farmers, dependents, and a small number of captive slaves stood below. Inter-tribal ties were maintained through marriage, mutual aid, and seasonal assemblies.

rulingClass.zh: 各部落首领、战士随从集团与长老会议。权威来源于血统声望、战功和财富再分配能力。
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs, warrior retinues, and elder councils. Authority derived from lineage prestige, military prowess, and capacity for wealth redistribution.

succession.zh: 继承通常在首领家族内部以推举方式进行，长老和战士集团可改立有威望者，非严格长子制。
succession.en: Succession generally occurred by election within chiefly families, with elders and warrior groups able to replace a chief with a more capable candidate; strict primogeniture did not apply.

structure.zh: 政务组织简单，以部落首领、长老会和自由民大会为核心。日常事务由各村社自行处理，跨部落事务通过季节性集会协商，战时临时推举军事领袖。
structure.en: Government was simple in structure, centered on tribal chiefs, elder councils, and assemblies of free men. Daily affairs were handled at the village level, inter-tribal matters negotiated at seasonal assemblies, and a military leader was chosen ad hoc in wartime.

function.zh: 裁决部落纠纷、安排婚盟、分配土地与组织祭祀活动。
function.en: Adjudicated tribal disputes, arranged marriage alliances, allocated land, and organized ritual activities.

function.zh: 保卫首领、征召战士、组织边境防御和劫掠行动。
function.en: Guarded the chief, mustered warriors, organized frontier defense and raiding expeditions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理以村社自治为主，氏族长者主持耕地、水源与林地分配。各聚落独立性强，仅在战时和贡赋征收时听从首领调度。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was primarily through village self-administration, with clan elders presiding over the allocation of arable land, water, and forests. Settlements were highly autonomous, deferring to chiefly authority mainly in wartime and for tribute collection.

legalSystem.zh: 以口传习惯法为主，强调赔偿与血亲复仇原则。杀伤、盗窃与婚姻纠纷多通过实物赔偿、宗族调解或神明裁判解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily oral customary practice, emphasizing compensation and kin-based vengeance. Homicide, theft, and marriage disputes were typically resolved through material indemnity, clan mediation, or divine ordeal.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，首领通过实物贡赋、战利品分配和劳役义务维持权威。集市和渡口处偶有临时征收。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; chiefly authority was sustained through tribute in kind, division of war spoils, and labor obligations. Occasional levies were collected at markets and river crossings.

religion.zh: 斯拉夫多神教
religion.en: Slavic polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 斯拉夫部落发展了独特的木构聚落建筑、丰富的口头史诗与神话传统、精美陶器与织物、以及季节性农业祭典和祖灵崇拜仪式。
culturalAchievements.en: The Slavic tribes developed distinctive timber-built settlements, rich oral epic and mythological traditions, fine pottery and textiles, and seasonal agricultural festivals and ancestor veneration rituals.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系斯拉夫语族（原始斯拉夫语阶段）
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Slavic branch (Proto-Slavic stage)

philosophy.zh: 斯拉夫多神信仰与自然万物有灵观念，祖先崇拜维系共同体凝聚力
philosophy.en: Slavic polytheistic belief and animistic worldview, with ancestor veneration sustaining communal solidarity

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本族文字体系，依靠口述传统、刻痕符号与记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system existed; reliance was on oral tradition, notched symbols, and memorized knowledge.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以刀耕火种与定耕农业、畜牧、狩猎、采集和养蜂为主。部分河流沿岸地区参与毛皮、蜂蜡和琥珀的远距离交换。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on slash-and-burn and settled agriculture, herding, hunting, foraging, and beekeeping. Some riverine areas participated in long-distance exchange of furs, beeswax, and amber.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、狩猎、养蜂、木材加工、陶器
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, herding, hunting, beekeeping, timber working, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、琥珀、木材、皮革、粮食
tradeGoods.en: Furs, honey, beeswax, amber, timber, hides, grain

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备和铁制工具为核心。首领家庭拥有更多武器、马匹和随从。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth centered on land-use rights, livestock, grain reserves, and iron tools. Chiefly households possessed more weapons, horses, and retainers.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给和部落贡纳为主的部落经济，辅以少量远程交换。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on village subsistence and chiefly tribute, supplemented by limited long-distance exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 无集中国库，财富分散于各部落首领家庭、祭祀场所和谷物仓储中。可快速动员的储备有限。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; wealth was dispersed among chiefly households, ritual sites, and grain stores. Readily mobilizable reserves were limited.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维系首领威望与战士忠诚为核心的再分配体制，无制度化征税。
fiscalPolicy.en: A redistributive system focused on maintaining chiefly prestige and warrior loyalty, without institutionalized taxation.

description.zh: 各部落首领直属核心战士，装备相对较好，承担护卫和突击任务。
description.en: Core warriors attached directly to tribal chiefs, relatively better equipped, serving as guards and shock troops.

description.zh: 由自由民临时征召组成的步兵，装备矛、斧、木盾与弓箭。
description.en: Temporary infantry levied from freemen, armed with spears, axes, wooden shields, and bows.

description.zh: 少量骑手用于联络侦察，独木舟和木筏用于河上机动与运输。
description.en: A small number of horsemen for scouting and communication, with dugout canoes and rafts for riverine mobility and transport.

commanderInChief.zh: 战时由诸部落首领共推军事统帅
commanderInChief.en: A war chief elected from among tribal leaders in times of conflict

technology.zh: 以步战为主，配合木栅寨防御和河流机动。铁制矛头和斧头已普及，但盔甲稀少，攻城能力极弱。
technology.en: Warfare centered on infantry combat, supported by palisaded defenses and riverine mobility. Iron spearheads and axes were widespread, but armor was scarce and siege capability was minimal.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自匈人残余势力、日耳曼邻族、阿瓦尔游牧入侵以及部落间内战。缺乏统一指挥使其在面对组织化国家力量时较为脆弱。
threats.en: Major threats came from Hunnic remnants, Germanic neighbors, Avar nomadic incursions, and inter-tribal warfare. The absence of unified command made them vulnerable when confronting organized state forces.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于从维斯瓦河到第聂伯河的广阔森林、沼泽与河谷地带，以小型村落聚居。高出生率和高死亡率并存，疫病、饥荒和战争对人口波动影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the vast forests, marshes, and river valleys from the Vistula to the Dnieper, living in small village clusters. High birth rates coexisted with high mortality, and disease, famine, and warfare caused significant fluctuations.

name.zh: 第聂伯河中游设防聚落
普里皮亚季沼泽边缘聚落

socialClasses.zh: 部落首领与战士贵族、祭司与长老、自由农民、依附者与奴仆
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and warrior elites, priests and elders, free peasants, dependents, and slaves

allies.zh: 以临时部落同盟为主，偶尔与波罗的海和芬-乌戈尔族群建立联姻和互助关系。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly temporary tribal coalitions, with occasional marriage ties and mutual aid arrangements with Baltic and Finno-Ugric groups.

enemies.zh: 与日耳曼迁徙部族、匈人残余势力及其他竞争性斯拉夫部落时有冲突。
enemies.en: They periodically clashed with migrating Germanic peoples, Hunnic remnants, and rival Slavic tribal groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略注重保护耕地与迁徙空间、控制河道贸易节点，并通过贡纳和婚盟避免被强邻吞并。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on protecting farmland and settlement space, controlling riverine trade nodes, and using tribute and marriage alliances to avoid absorption by stronger neighbors.

era.zh: 民族大迁徙时期东欧部落技术阶段
era.en: Migration Period Eastern European tribal technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与武器、木栅聚落防御工事、独木舟与河运网络、蜂蜡加工
keyInnovations.en: Iron agricultural tools and weapons, palisaded settlement defenses, dugout boats and riverine transport networks, beeswax processing

strengths.zh: 人口扩张潜力强大，适应森林与河谷生态环境，部落动员灵活迅速。村社韧性高，能在频繁战乱中维持人口增长和领土扩展。
strengths.en: Strong demographic expansion potential, excellent adaptation to forest and river-valley ecology, and rapid flexible tribal mobilization. Village communities were highly resilient, maintaining population growth and territorial expansion even amid frequent conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散，无文字与正式行政体系，财政和军事制度化程度极低。面对组织化国家军队时防御力不足。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, no literacy or formal administrative apparatus, and extremely low institutionalization of finance and military. Defensive capability was inadequate against organized state armies.

outlook.zh: 斯拉夫部落正处于大规模迁徙扩张的早期阶段，未来数世纪将遍布东欧、东南欧和中欧，并在接触拜占庭、法兰克与日耳曼文化后逐步形成国家。
outlook.en: The Slavic tribes were in the early phase of massive migratory expansion. Over the following centuries they would spread across Eastern, Southeastern, and Central Europe, gradually forming states through contact with Byzantine, Frankish, and Germanic cultures.

description.zh: 公元476年前后，早期斯拉夫部落正从其原始故地向四方扩散，以村社和氏族为基本单位在东欧森林与河谷中建立定居点，虽然政治分散但人口增长迅速。
description.en: Around 476 CE, the early Slavic tribes were spreading outward from their homeland, establishing settlements in the forests and river valleys of Eastern Europe in clan and village units. Though politically fragmented, their population was growing rapidly.

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心、教会与地方酋长辅助的基督教君主制王国。
governmentForm.en: A Christian monarchy centered on the king, supported by the Church and local chieftains.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、教会神职人员、自由农牧民、城镇工商业者及奴隶与依附人口构成。王廷与教会在首都索巴形成行政核心，地方由部族酋长和地区首领治理。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal family and nobility, Christian clergy, free farmers and pastoralists, urban merchants and artisans, and slaves and dependents. The royal court and Church formed an administrative nucleus at the capital Soba, while tribal chiefs and regional headmen governed the provinces.

rulingClass.zh: 王室家族、教会高层与地方有力酋长
rulingClass.en: The royal family, senior churchmen, and powerful local chieftains

succession.zh: 王位在王室内部继承，继承顺序受贵族支持与教会认可影响，常以母系血统为重要参考因素。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal house, with succession influenced by noble support and Church approval, and matrilineal descent often serving as an important factor.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕索巴王廷运作，通过任命地方长官和维持附庸关系治理领土。基督教会在行政、教育和外交中发挥重要辅助作用。
structure.en: Central authority revolved around the court at Soba, governing territory through appointed officials and tributary arrangements. The Christian Church played an important auxiliary role in administration, education, and diplomacy.

function.zh: 管理王室事务、发布命令、接待外使与监督贡赋征收。
function.en: Managed royal affairs, issued edicts, received foreign envoys, and supervised tribute collection.

function.zh: 负责宗教事务、文书记录、教育和对外通信。
function.en: Handled religious affairs, record-keeping, education, and external correspondence.

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、地方征召和边防巡逻。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, provincial levies, and frontier patrols.

function.zh: 管理尼罗河贸易、黄金与象牙交换、地方贡品接收与仓储。
function.en: Managed Nile trade, gold and ivory exchange, receipt of local tribute, and storage.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖部族首领与地区代理人。偏远牧区控制松散，核心农业区与贸易据点管理较密。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on tribal chiefs and regional agents. Remote pastoral zones were loosely controlled, while core agricultural areas and trade posts were more tightly managed.

legalSystem.zh: 法律融合努比亚习惯法与基督教教会法，王廷充当最高裁判机构。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Nubian customary practice with Christian canon law, with the royal court serving as the highest judicial authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 以地方贡赋、牲畜税、尼罗河贸易税和黄金象牙利润为主要收入来源。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived mainly from local tribute, livestock levies, Nile trade duties, and profits from gold and ivory exchange.

religion.zh: 基督教（科普特传统）
religion.en: Christianity (Coptic tradition)

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿洛迪亚继承麦罗埃和库施文明遗产，发展了教堂建筑、壁画艺术、古努比亚语文学以及独特的陶器和织物工艺。索巴城以其多座教堂和花园闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Alodia inherited the legacy of Meroitic and Kushite civilizations, developing church architecture, mural painting, Old Nubian literary production, and distinctive pottery and textile crafts. Soba was renowned for its multiple churches and gardens.

languageFamily.zh: 尼罗-撒哈拉语系努比亚语族（古努比亚语）
languageFamily.en: Nilo-Saharan, Nubian branch (Old Nubian)

philosophy.zh: 以基督教教义为精神核心，融合努比亚传统王权神圣观念
philosophy.en: Christian doctrine as the spiritual core, fused with Nubian traditions of sacred kingship

writingSystem.zh: 使用古努比亚文（基于科普特与希腊字母改造），兼用希腊文和科普特文进行宗教记录。
writingSystem.en: Old Nubian script (adapted from Coptic and Greek alphabets), supplemented by Greek and Coptic for religious records.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以尼罗河谷灌溉农业、畜牧业为基础，辅以黄金、象牙和铁器贸易。索巴作为区域商业中心连接上埃及与东非内陆。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on Nile valley irrigated agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by trade in gold, ivory, and iron goods. Soba served as a regional commercial hub linking Upper Egypt to the East African interior.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、畜牧业、黄金开采、铁器锻造、陶器与纺织
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, gold mining, iron smithing, pottery, and textile production

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、象牙、铁器、牲畜、皮革、香料、谷物
tradeGoods.en: Gold, ivory, iron goods, cattle, hides, spices, grain

name.zh: 实物交换与外来金银币并用
name.en: Barter combined with imported gold and silver coins

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以耕地、牲畜和谷物储备为主要财富。贵族和商人家庭可拥有黄金饰物、进口织物和大量牛群。
householdWealth.en: Common households measured wealth in land, livestock, and grain. Noble and merchant families might possess gold ornaments, imported textiles, and large cattle herds.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以尼罗河谷农业为基础、贡赋与区域贸易为辅的王国经济。
economicSystem.en: A kingdom economy based on Nile valley agriculture, supplemented by tribute and regional trade.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 贸易税与尼罗河通行税
source.en: Trade duties and Nile transit taxes

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模不大，主要由王室黄金储备、谷物仓和牲畜构成。教会财产也构成国家稳定资源的一部分。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was modest, consisting mainly of royal gold reserves, grain stores, and cattle. Church holdings also formed part of the state's stable resource base.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 注重维持尼罗河贸易通道安全和教会建设投入，财政偏保守。
fiscalPolicy.en: Focused on maintaining the security of Nile trade routes and investing in Church infrastructure, with a conservative fiscal approach.

description.zh: 常备精锐部队，驻守索巴及重要通道，由王室直接供养。
description.en: Standing elite force garrisoned at Soba and key routes, directly maintained by the crown.

description.zh: 由地方酋长召集的步兵，装备矛、弓和盾。
description.en: Infantry mustered by local chiefs, armed with spears, bows, and shields.

description.zh: 用于沙漠边缘巡逻、通信与追击的机动力量。
description.en: Mobile forces for desert-fringe patrols, communication, and pursuit.

commanderInChief.en: The king served as supreme military commander

technology.zh: 以步兵和弓箭手为核心，骆驼骑兵用于沙漠巡逻。铁制武器较成熟，但大规模攻城技术有限。利用尼罗河与其支流进行水路运输。
technology.en: Centered on infantry and archers, with camel cavalry for desert patrols. Iron weaponry was well developed, but large-scale siege technology was limited. The Nile and its tributaries were used for waterborne transport.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南部游牧与尼罗特族群侵袭、北方马库里亚可能的竞争以及沙漠贝贾部落的劫掠。
threats.en: Key threats included raiding by southern pastoralist and Nilotic groups, potential rivalry from Makuria to the north, and Beja tribal incursions from the desert.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于蓝白尼罗河交汇区域和周边灌溉农地，部分居民散布于半干旱牧区。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated around the Blue and White Nile confluence and surrounding irrigated farmland, with some inhabitants dispersed across semi-arid pastoral zones.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、教会神职人员、自由农牧民、工匠与商人、奴隶与依附民
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobility, Christian clergy, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans and merchants, slaves and dependents

allies.zh: 与北方马库里亚王国保持基督教兄弟关系，并通过尼罗河贸易与埃及科普特教会保持联系。
allies.en: Maintained a Christian fraternal relationship with Makuria to the north, and kept ties with the Egyptian Coptic Church through Nile trade.

enemies.zh: 与南部尼罗特族群和沙漠游牧部落存在间歇性冲突。
enemies.en: Intermittent conflict with southern Nilotic groups and desert nomadic tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护尼罗河贸易通道、巩固基督教同盟和防御游牧入侵为对外政策核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on safeguarding Nile trade routes, reinforcing the Christian alliance, and defending against nomadic incursions.

era.zh: 后麦罗埃努比亚基督教王国技术阶段
era.en: Post-Meroitic Nubian Christian kingdom technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 灌溉农业（萨基亚水车）、铁器锻造、教堂石砌建筑、古努比亚文书写
keyInnovations.en: Irrigated agriculture (saqiya waterwheel), iron smithing, stone church architecture, Old Nubian script

strengths.zh: 基督教信仰提供了强大的文化凝聚力，尼罗河谷提供稳定的农业基础，与北方努比亚诸国的同盟关系增强了安全。
strengths.en: Christianity provided strong cultural cohesion, the Nile valley ensured a stable agricultural base, and alliance with northern Nubian states enhanced security.

weaknesses.zh: 偏远且人口稀少，国际联系有限，军事规模较小，容易受到南部游牧力量的持续骚扰。
weaknesses.en: Remote and sparsely populated, with limited international connections, a small military, and vulnerability to sustained pressure from southern pastoral forces.

outlook.zh: 阿洛迪亚将继续作为南努比亚基督教文明的堡垒存续数百年，但未来面临伊斯兰化压力和南方族群迁徙的长期挑战。
outlook.en: Alodia would endure for centuries as a bastion of southern Nubian Christian civilization, but would face long-term challenges from Islamization and southward population movements.

description.zh: 公元476年的阿洛迪亚王国以索巴为都城，是蓝白尼罗河交汇处的基督教努比亚王国，承继库施和麦罗埃文明遗产，控制着上尼罗河重要贸易通道。
description.en: In 476 CE, the Kingdom of Alodia, with its capital at Soba near the Blue and White Nile confluence, was a Christian Nubian kingdom inheriting Kushite and Meroitic traditions, controlling vital Upper Nile trade routes.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族长老和地方酋长为核心的分散部落自治体制，部分地区出现小型酋邦。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system of tribal self-governance centered on clan elders and local chiefs, with small chieftaincies emerging in some areas.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族和世系群为基础，酋长、长老和铁匠等专业人士地位较高。普通农民和牧民构成人口主体，奴隶和战俘处于社会底层。年龄组织和成人礼在部分族群中具有重要社会功能。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans and lineage groups, with chiefs, elders, and specialists like smiths enjoying higher status. Ordinary farmers and herders formed the majority, while slaves and captives occupied the lowest tier. Age sets and initiation rites served important social functions in some groups.

rulingClass.zh: 世系酋长、氏族长老和有声望的铁匠、祭司
rulingClass.en: Lineage chiefs, clan elders, and prestigious smiths and ritual specialists

succession.zh: 酋长职位通常在特定世系内继承，兼有父系与母系传统，具体因族群而异。
succession.en: Chieftaincy typically passed within specific lineages, with both patrilineal and matrilineal traditions varying by ethnic group.

structure.zh: 政治组织以村落和氏族为基本单元，酋长和长老会议处理本地事务。缺乏统一的上层政治结构，部落间通过婚姻联盟和贸易网络维持联系。
structure.en: Political organization was based on villages and clans, with chiefs and elder councils handling local affairs. There was no unified overarching structure; inter-tribal connections were maintained through marriage alliances and trade networks.

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、分配土地、主持仪式和协调村际事务。
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, allocated land, presided over rituals, and coordinated inter-village affairs.

function.zh: 保卫酋长、征集贡赋、组织对外防御与劫掠。
function.en: Protected the chief, collected tribute, organized external defense and raiding.

localAdmin.zh: 以村社自治为主，各聚落独立管理耕地、牧场与水源。酋长在争端调解和战时动员中起协调作用。
localAdmin.en: Governance was primarily through village self-administration, with each settlement independently managing cropland, pasture, and water. Chiefs served a coordinating role in dispute resolution and wartime mobilization.

legalSystem.zh: 以口传习惯法为主，强调赔偿、调解和长老裁决。严重犯罪可能导致放逐或血亲报复。
legalSystem.en: Primarily oral customary law, emphasizing compensation, mediation, and elder arbitration. Serious offenses could result in banishment or kin retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，酋长通过收取贡品（谷物、牲畜、铁器）和劳役义务维持权威。
taxationSystem.en: No formal tax system; chiefly authority was maintained through tribute (grain, cattle, iron goods) and labor obligations.

religion.zh: 班图传统宗教（祖先崇拜与万物有灵论）
religion.en: Bantu traditional religion (ancestor veneration and animism)

culturalAchievements.zh: 东非班图族群发展了精湛的冶铁技术、独特的陶器风格、口头史诗传统、复杂的音乐与舞蹈、以及梯田和灌溉等农业技术。
culturalAchievements.en: East African Bantu peoples developed sophisticated iron-smelting techniques, distinctive pottery styles, oral epic traditions, complex music and dance forms, and agricultural innovations including terracing and irrigation.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语族
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Bantu branch

philosophy.zh: 祖先崇拜、万物有灵信仰和以共同体和谐为核心的社会伦理
philosophy.en: Ancestor veneration, animistic belief, and a social ethic centered on communal harmony

writingSystem.zh: 无本族文字系统，知识与历史通过口述传统、歌谣和象征符号传承。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; knowledge and history were transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and symbolic markers.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业（高粱、小米、薯类）、畜牧和铁器锻造为基础，辅以区域贸易网络交换盐、铁和牲畜。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence agriculture (sorghum, millet, root crops), pastoralism, and iron smithing, supplemented by regional trade networks exchanging salt, iron, and livestock.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、冶铁、陶器、编织
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, iron smelting, pottery, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、盐、牲畜、兽皮、谷物、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Iron goods, salt, livestock, hides, grain, pottery

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以牲畜数量、耕地面积、谷物储备和铁制工具为衡量。酋长家庭拥有更多牛群和铁器。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured in cattle holdings, cropland, grain stores, and iron tools. Chiefly households possessed larger herds and more iron goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给农业为核心、辅以区域贸易网络的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on village subsistence agriculture, supplemented by regional trade networks.

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 无集中金库，财富分散储存于各酋长家庭的牲畜围栏、谷仓和铁器储存中。
treasuryDescription.en: No centralized treasury; wealth was dispersed in chiefly cattle kraals, granaries, and iron stores.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持酋长权威和社区稳定为目标的再分配体制。
fiscalPolicy.en: A redistributive system aimed at maintaining chiefly authority and community stability.

description.zh: 各酋长直属的精锐战士，配备较好的铁制武器。
description.en: Elite warriors attached to individual chiefs, armed with better iron weapons.

description.zh: 由自由男丁组成的临时征召步兵，使用矛、弓、投掷棍和盾。
description.en: Temporary levy of free men, armed with spears, bows, throwing clubs, and shields.

description.zh: 善于丛林和草原环境中的侦察、追踪和伏击。
description.en: Skilled in reconnaissance, tracking, and ambush in bush and savanna environments.

commanderInChief.zh: 各酋长独立指挥本部战力，大型冲突时临时推举统帅
commanderInChief.en: Individual chiefs commanded their own forces, with a war leader chosen ad hoc for larger conflicts

technology.zh: 以步兵为主，铁制矛头和箭头为主要武器。战术重视伏击、包围和利用地形。筑防以荆棘篱笆和土围为主。
technology.en: Warfare centered on infantry, with iron spearheads and arrowheads as primary weapons. Tactics emphasized ambush, encirclement, and terrain exploitation. Defenses relied on thorn hedges and earth enclosures.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与先住狩猎采集族群的冲突、部落间领土争夺以及干旱和疫病带来的人口压力。
threats.en: Major threats came from conflict with indigenous hunter-gatherer populations, inter-tribal territorial competition, and demographic pressure from drought and disease.

populationDescription.zh: 人口散布于大湖地区和东非高原的农牧定居点，聚落沿河谷、湖岸和肥沃高地分布。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across farming and pastoral settlements in the Great Lakes region and East African highlands, with settlements along river valleys, lakeshores, and fertile uplands.

name.zh: 维多利亚湖西岸聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与贵族世系、铁匠与专业工匠、自由农牧民、依附者与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Chiefly and noble lineages, smiths and specialist artisans, free farmers and herders, dependents and slaves

allies.zh: 以婚姻联盟和贸易伙伴关系为主的部落间合作网络。
allies.en: Inter-tribal cooperation networks based mainly on marriage alliances and trade partnerships.

enemies.zh: 与先住狩猎采集族群和敌对班图氏族存在领土竞争。
enemies.en: Territorial competition with indigenous hunter-gatherer populations and hostile Bantu clans.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以扩展定居空间、控制铁矿和盐源、维系贸易网络为对外策略核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign strategy focused on expanding settlement space, controlling iron ore and salt sources, and maintaining trade networks.

era.zh: 非洲铁器时代早中期
era.en: Early-to-middle African Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 高温冶铁（含碳钢）、梯田农业、陶器烧制、班图独木舟与内河航运
keyInnovations.en: High-temperature iron smelting (carbon steel), terraced agriculture, pottery firing, Bantu dugout canoes and inland waterway navigation

strengths.zh: 强大的人口扩张能力、先进的冶铁技术赋予军事与农业优势、高度适应多样化生态环境。
strengths.en: Powerful demographic expansion, advanced iron-smelting technology conferring military and agricultural advantages, and high adaptability to diverse ecological environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散，缺乏跨区域协调能力，无文字记录系统，面对组织化入侵力量时难以形成统一抵抗。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, lack of cross-regional coordination capacity, no writing system, and difficulty forming unified resistance against organized invasive forces.

outlook.zh: 班图扩张将在未来数世纪覆盖东非和南非大部分地区，并在大湖地区最终形成复杂的王国体制。
outlook.en: Bantu expansion would cover most of Eastern and Southern Africa over the following centuries, eventually forming complex kingdom systems in the Great Lakes region.

description.zh: 公元476年前后，东非班图族群正以冶铁技术和农耕优势持续向大湖地区和东非高原扩展，在与先住狩猎采集人群互动的过程中建立越来越多的定居社区。
description.en: Around 476 CE, East African Bantu peoples were continuing their expansion into the Great Lakes region and East African highlands, leveraging iron technology and agricultural prowess to establish growing numbers of settled communities amid interactions with indigenous forager populations.

governmentForm.zh: 控制青藏高原东北部和河西走廊的游牧汗国
governmentForm.en: A nomadic khaganate controlling the northeastern Tibetan Plateau and Hexi Corridor

socialStructure.zh: 鲜卑慕容王族统治羌、氐等各族牧民和定居农民。
socialStructure.en: The Xianbei Murong royal clan ruled over Qiang, Di, and other pastoralists and settled farmers.

rulingClass.en: Murong royal clan and Xianbei aristocracy

succession.zh: 可汗位在慕容家族内传承。
succession.en: The Khagan title passed within the Murong family.

title.zh: 奥多亚塞废黜末代西罗马皇帝
title.en: Odoacer Deposes the Last Western Roman Emperor

description.zh: 日耳曼将领奥多亚塞废黜年幼的西罗马皇帝罗慕路斯·奥古斯都，将皇冠送往东罗马帝国君士坦丁堡。这一事件标志着西罗马帝国的正式终结，虽然当时许多人并未意识到其历史意义。
description.en: Germanic general Odoacer deposed the young Western Roman Emperor Romulus Augustulus and sent the imperial regalia to Constantinople. This event marked the formal end of the Western Roman Empire, though many contemporaries did not grasp its historical significance.

title.en: Fall of Liu Song, Rise of Southern Qi

description.zh: 南朝刘宋末帝被废，萧道成建立齐朝，成为南朝第二个王朝。南朝虽然政权更迭频繁，但在建康（今南京）维持了汉文化正统和江南经济的持续发展。
description.en: The last Liu Song emperor was deposed and Xiao Daocheng established the Qi dynasty, the second of the Southern Dynasties. Despite frequent regime changes, the Southern Dynasties maintained Chinese cultural orthodoxy and continued economic development of Jiangnan at Jiankang (modern Nanjing).

title.zh: 西格里乌斯领地——最后的罗马统治
title.en: Syagrius' Domain — Last Roman Rule in Gaul

description.zh: 高卢北部的罗马将领西格里乌斯维持着最后一块罗马控制区——苏瓦松领地，被称为"罗马人之王"。这片领土在蛮族汪洋中如孤岛一般，是西罗马帝国在高卢的最后残迹。
description.en: Roman general Syagrius maintained the last Roman-controlled territory in northern Gaul—the Domain of Soissons—and was called "King of the Romans." This territory stood as an island in a sea of barbarian kingdoms, the last vestige of Western Roman authority in Gaul.

title.zh: 克洛维击败西格里乌斯

description.zh: 法兰克国王克洛维一世在苏瓦松战役中击败西格里乌斯，吞并了高卢最后的罗马领土。法兰克王国由此控制了高卢北部大部分地区，为墨洛温王朝的扩张奠定了基础。
description.en: Frankish King Clovis I defeated Syagrius at the Battle of Soissons, absorbing the last Roman territory in Gaul. The Frankish kingdom thus gained control of most of northern Gaul, laying the foundation for Merovingian expansion.

description.zh: 在东罗马皇帝芝诺的默许下，东哥特国王狄奥多里克率领族人入侵意大利，与奥多亚塞展开争夺。经过数年战争，狄奥多里克最终于493年杀死奥多亚塞，建立东哥特王国。
description.en: With the tacit approval of Eastern Roman Emperor Zeno, Ostrogothic King Theoderic led his people to invade Italy, fighting Odoacer for control. After years of warfare, Theoderic finally killed Odoacer in 493 CE and established the Ostrogothic Kingdom.

title.en: Emperor Xiaowen's Sinicization Reforms in Northern Wei

description.zh: 北魏孝文帝拓跋宏推行全面汉化政策，将都城从平城迁至洛阳，要求鲜卑贵族改穿汉服、说汉语、改汉姓。这些改革促进了胡汉民族融合，但也加剧了保守鲜卑贵族的不满。
description.en: Northern Wei Emperor Xiaowen implemented comprehensive Sinicization policies, relocating the capital from Pingcheng to Luoyang and requiring Xianbei nobles to adopt Chinese clothing, language, and surnames. These reforms promoted Hu-Han ethnic integration but also intensified resentment among conservative Xianbei nobles.

description.zh: 法兰克国王克洛维一世在妻子克洛蒂尔德的影响下受洗皈依天主教，而非日耳曼各族普遍信奉的阿里乌斯派。这一决定使法兰克人获得了罗马教廷和高卢罗马人的支持，为法兰克王国的崛起提供了关键的政治优势。
description.en: Frankish King Clovis I, influenced by his wife Clotilde, was baptized into Catholic Christianity rather than the Arianism prevalent among Germanic peoples. This decision won Frankish support from the Roman papacy and Gallo-Roman population, providing a crucial political advantage for the kingdom's rise.

description.zh: 来自中亚的嚈哒（白匈奴）人不断入侵印度北部，严重削弱了已经衰落的笈多帝国。嚈哒人的入侵加速了笈多帝国的瓦解和南亚政治版图的碎片化。
description.en: The Hephthalites (White Huns) from Central Asia repeatedly invaded northern India, severely weakening the already declining Gupta Empire. Hephthalite invasions accelerated the disintegration of the Gupta Empire and the fragmentation of South Asia's political landscape.

description.zh: 萨珊波斯帝国与东罗马帝国之间的长期对抗在502年再次爆发为全面战争，双方在美索不达米亚和高加索地区激烈交战。这场战争持续至506年，最终以双方议和告终。
description.en: The long-standing rivalry between the Sasanian Persian Empire and the Eastern Roman Empire erupted into full-scale war again in 502 CE, with fierce fighting in Mesopotamia and the Caucasus. The war lasted until 506 CE, ending in a negotiated peace.

title.zh: 盎格鲁-撒克逊人征服不列颠

description.zh: 盎格鲁人、撒克逊人和朱特人从北海对岸持续迁入不列颠岛，逐渐控制了英格兰大部分地区。不列颠的罗马-凯尔特文明在日耳曼民族的浪潮中日益退缩至威尔士和西南部。
description.en: Angles, Saxons, and Jutes continued migrating from across the North Sea into Britain, gradually gaining control of most of England. Romano-Celtic civilization in Britain retreated increasingly to Wales and the southwest under the Germanic tide.

title.zh: 蒂卡尔与卡拉克穆尔争霸

description.zh: 玛雅低地的蒂卡尔和卡拉克穆尔两大城邦在5世纪末至6世纪初展开激烈的政治和军事竞争，各自建立庞大的同盟网络。这一时期的争霸奠定了玛雅古典时代"超级大国"对抗的基本格局。
description.en: Maya lowland superpowers Tikal and Calakmul engaged in fierce political and military competition from the late 5th to early 6th century, each building extensive alliance networks. This rivalry established the basic pattern of "superpower" confrontation during the Classic Maya period.

title.zh: 伏埃战役——法兰克击败西哥特
title.en: Battle of Vouillé — Franks Defeat Visigoths

description.zh: 克洛维一世率法兰克军队在伏埃战役中决定性地击败西哥特国王阿拉里克二世，将西哥特人逐出高卢大部分地区。法兰克王国由此成为后罗马时代西欧最强大的日耳曼国家。
description.en: Clovis I led the Franks to a decisive victory over Visigothic King Alaric II at the Battle of Vouillé, driving the Visigoths from most of Gaul. The Frankish kingdom thus became the most powerful Germanic state in post-Roman Western Europe.

title.zh: 狄奥多里克治理下的意大利繁荣

description.zh: 东哥特国王狄奥多里克大帝以拉文纳为都城，推行罗马-哥特并治政策，保留罗马行政体系和元老院，同时维持哥特军事贵族的地位。意大利在其统治下享受了数十年的和平与繁荣。
description.en: Ostrogothic King Theoderic the Great, ruling from Ravenna, implemented a Romano-Gothic dual governance system, preserving Roman administration and the Senate while maintaining the Gothic military aristocracy. Italy enjoyed decades of peace and prosperity under his rule.

title.zh: 高句丽与百济争夺朝鲜半岛
title.en: Goguryeo-Baekje Struggle for the Korean Peninsula

description.zh: 高句丽在5世纪末至6世纪初与百济、新罗在朝鲜半岛展开激烈的领土争夺。高句丽作为东北亚最强大的政权之一，南下扩张的企图与百济、新罗的联合抵抗构成了三国时代的基本对抗格局。
description.en: Goguryeo engaged in fierce territorial contests with Baekje and Silla on the Korean Peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century. As one of Northeast Asia's most powerful states, Goguryeo's southward expansion met with combined Baekje-Silla resistance, establishing the basic confrontational pattern of Korea's Three Kingdoms period.

title.zh: 查士丁一世登基东罗马
title.en: Justin I Ascends the Eastern Roman Throne

description.zh: 出身低微的查士丁一世在宫廷政变中成为东罗马皇帝，结束了阿纳斯塔修斯家族的统治。查士丁提拔其侄子查士丁尼进入权力核心，为后者日后的辉煌统治铺平道路。
description.en: The humble-born Justin I became Eastern Roman Emperor through a palace coup, ending the Anastasian dynasty. Justin elevated his nephew Justinian into the power structure, paving the way for the latter's illustrious future reign.

title.zh: 阿努拉德普拉佛教繁盛

description.zh: 斯里兰卡的阿努拉德普拉王国在公元5-6世纪继续作为上座部佛教的重要中心。国王们修建大型舍利塔和寺院，吸引了来自东南亚各地的朝圣者和学者，巩固了锡兰在佛教世界的崇高地位。
description.en: The Anuradhapura kingdom of Sri Lanka continued as a major center of Theravada Buddhism in the 5th-6th centuries. Kings constructed great stupas and monasteries, attracting pilgrims and scholars from across Southeast Asia, consolidating Ceylon's elevated status in the Buddhist world.

description.zh: 北非的汪达尔王国在盖塞里克去世后（477年）逐渐走向衰落，宗教迫害引发了与本地罗马人和柏柏尔人的矛盾。汪达尔的海上力量也开始减弱，为后来查士丁尼的征服创造了条件。
description.en: The Vandal Kingdom in North Africa gradually declined after the death of Gaiseric (477 CE), with religious persecution provoking conflicts with local Romans and Berbers. Vandal naval power also began to weaken, creating conditions for Justinian's later reconquest.

title.zh: 阿克苏姆入侵希木叶尔

description.zh: 东非阿克苏姆王国出兵渡过红海入侵南阿拉伯的希木叶尔王国，推翻了其犹太教统治者，建立了基督教附庸政权。这次远征反映了基督教阿克苏姆与犹太教希木叶尔之间的宗教对抗。
description.en: The Aksumite Kingdom of East Africa dispatched forces across the Red Sea to invade the Himyarite Kingdom in southern Arabia, overthrowing its Jewish rulers and establishing a Christian vassal regime. This expedition reflected the religious confrontation between Christian Aksum and Jewish Himyar.

title.zh: 查士丁尼即位东罗马皇帝
title.en: Justinian I Becomes Eastern Roman Emperor

description.zh: 查士丁尼一世继承其叔查士丁一世的帝位，开始了东罗马帝国最雄心勃勃的统治时期。查士丁尼将致力于收复西罗马帝国故土、编纂法典和修建圣索菲亚大教堂。
description.en: Justinian I succeeded his uncle Justin I as Eastern Roman Emperor, beginning the most ambitious reign in the empire's history. Justinian would devote himself to reconquering former Western Roman territories, codifying law, and constructing the Hagia Sophia.

description.zh: 查士丁尼下令编纂系统化的罗马法典集——《民法大全》，由法学家特里波尼安主持。这部法典整理了千年罗马法律传统，成为后世欧洲法律体系的基石，影响延续至今。
description.en: Justinian ordered the compilation of a systematic codification of Roman law—the Corpus Juris Civilis—overseen by jurist Tribonian. This codex organized a millennium of Roman legal tradition and became the cornerstone of European legal systems, with influence continuing to this day.

title.zh: 本笃创建蒙特卡西诺修道院

description.zh: 努西亚的本笃在意大利中部蒙特卡西诺山上创建修道院，制定了《本笃会规》。本笃修道制度成为中世纪西欧修道主义的基础，修道院成为黑暗时代知识保存和文化传承的重要场所。
description.en: Benedict of Nursia founded a monastery atop Monte Cassino in central Italy and established the Rule of Saint Benedict. Benedictine monasticism became the foundation of Western European monastic life in the Middle Ages, with monasteries serving as vital centers for knowledge preservation and cultural transmission in the Dark Ages.

title.en: Decline of the Kingdom of Funan

description.zh: 东南亚的扶南王国在6世纪初开始走向衰落，高棉人建立的真腊政权逐渐崛起并蚕食扶南领土。扶南的衰落标志着东南亚政治格局的重大转变，高棉文明即将取而代之。
description.en: The Kingdom of Funan in Southeast Asia began declining in the early 6th century as the Khmer-founded Chenla polity gradually rose and encroached on Funan's territory. Funan's decline marked a major transformation in Southeast Asian political geography, with Khmer civilization poised to take its place.
